================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SpecBot - Automated Code Parallelization Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for SpecBot, an automated tool that converts sequential C/C++ code to parallel code with performance optimization based on specific hardware specifications.

1.2 Scope
SpecBot will be available as a Visual Studio Code extension and a standalone website. It analyzes C/C++ code, examines hardware specs, identifies parallelizable segments, generates data dependency graphs, and automates code parallelization.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AST: Abstract Syntax Tree
- API: Application Programming Interface
- JSON: JavaScript Object Notation
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SpecBot aims to close the gap between sequential code and modern multi-core processors by automating the parallelization process based on hardware capabilities, improving performance for computationally demanding tasks.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Hardware specification extraction.
- Static code analysis (AST generation).
- Data dependency analysis.
- Thread enhancement.
- Parallelization algorithm (OpenMP/OpenACC).
- VSCode Integration.
- Web-based deployment.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- High-Performance Computing Centers: Speed up big computational tasks.
- Data Scientists: Make ML training faster using fewer resources.
- Research Institutes: Improve algorithm and simulation performance.
- Programming Library Maintainers: Boost performance of C/C++ libraries.

2.4 Operating Environment
VSCode extension and standalone website.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Hardware Specification Module
FR1.1: The system shall extract and analyze hardware information from Unix-based systems (CPU cores, threads, available RAM).
FR1.2: The system shall provide a mechanism to extract system information using the browser for web-based deployments.
FR1.3: The system shall store and utilize the extracted hardware information for optimizing code parallelization.

3.2 Static Code Analysis Module
FR2.1: The system shall generate Abstract Syntax Trees (AST) for input C/C++ code.
FR2.2: The system shall analyze the AST to identify regions suitable for parallelization.
FR2.3: The system shall determine whether to use static or dynamic AST analysis based on code complexity and size.

3.3 Data Dependency Analysis Module
FR3.1: The system shall identify shared data structures within the input code.
FR3.2: The system shall analyze dependencies between identified data structures.
FR3.3: The system shall highlight data structures that must be shared across threads for proper parallelization.

3.4 Thread Enhancement Module
FR4.1: The system shall develop algorithms to determine optimal thread size for each parallelizable loop.
FR4.2: The system shall implement mechanisms for dynamic thread adjustment during runtime.
FR4.3: The system shall investigate and implement methods for prioritizing process threads in the system queue.

3.5 Parallelization Algorithm Module
FR5.1: The system shall integrate libraries for parallel computing (OpenMP and OpenACC).
FR5.2: The system shall identify code regions independent of data dependencies and suitable for parallelization.
FR5.3: The system shall manage data-dependent regions by determining efficient data-sharing mechanisms.
FR5.4: The system shall select between OpenMP and OpenACC libraries based on code structure and hardware capabilities.

3.6 Deployment and Integration Module
FR6.1: The system shall be developed as a Visual Studio Code extension.
FR6.2: The system shall be deployed as a standalone website for broader accessibility.
FR6.3: The system shall benchmark the parallelized code output against competitor solutions.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have an MTBF of at least 720 hours.
NFR2: In the event of failure, the system shall automatically save the current state of code analysis and notify the user.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The VSCode extension shall allow users to initiate code parallelization with no more than three mouse clicks or keyboard shortcuts.
NFR4: The web interface shall be intuitive, requiring no more than 10 minutes for a new user to understand.
NFR5: SpecBot shall provide clear, concise error messages and suggestions for resolution.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: SpecBot shall complete analysis and parallelization of code snippets up to 1000 lines within 30 seconds on a standard desktop computer (quad-core processor, 8GB RAM).
NFR7: The system shall be capable of handling multiple simultaneous user requests on the web platform, with a maximum response time of 5 seconds for initiating the parallelization process.

4.4 Security
NFR8: All user-submitted code and generated parallel code shall be encrypted during transmission and storage using industry-standard encryption (e.g., AES-256).
NFR9: The system shall implement user authentication for the web platform, requiring strong passwords.
NFR10: The system shall isolate and sandbox code execution environments to prevent potential security breaches.

================================================================================

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: VerseCraft - AI-Assisted Writing Workflow Management Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document describes the requirements for VerseCraft, a web-based writing workflow management tool that aims to streamline the creative writing process and provide tools for collaboration, productivity, and AI-assisted content creation.

1.2 Scope
VerseCraft offers structured writing tools, collaboration features, and workflow management. It includes chapter management, outline bars, notes, version control, and language options (English/Urdu). It provides character/plot development windows, AI-powered grammar checking, and writing suggestions.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- UI: User Interface
- API: Application Programming Interface
- PDF: Portable Document Format

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
VerseCraft addresses common writer challenges like creative blocks, disorganized workflows, and limited grammar proficiency by providing a collaborative, structured environment with AI assistance.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Workspace Dashboard (Chapter organization, collaboration, notes, version control, language options, character/plot windows, publishing).
- AI-Analysis Tools (Grammar checker, writing assistant chatbot).

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Writers: Create and manage stories/novels.
- Administrators: Manage platform operation, user accounts, and system security.
- Developers: Develop and maintain the platform.
- Testers: Test the application for bugs and usability.
- Documentation Writers: Create user guides and tutorials.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Workspace Dashboard
FR1.1: Users shall be able to manage their chapters separately, focusing on their content.
FR1.2: Chapters shall be reorderable using a drag-and-drop feature.
FR1.3: Multiple users shall be able to collaborate on the same project with role-based access and real-time changes.
FR1.4: Users shall be able to customize project settings (themes, content styles).
FR1.5: Users shall be able to create and organize notes alongside their content.
FR1.6: Users shall be able to manage and track different project versions.
FR1.7: Users shall be able to create content in either English or Urdu, with dashboard elements changing accordingly.
FR1.8: Users shall be able to create and manage characters, ensuring coherence with project content.
FR1.9: Users shall be able to create and manage main plots and subplots.
FR1.10: Users shall be able to publish their content with different privacy options (private/public).

3.2 AI-Analysis Tools
FR2.1: The system shall provide an AI language model specifically designed to enhance written content by improving flow and coherence.
FR2.2: A highly accurate grammar-checking tool shall identify errors and improve the overall readability of the content.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system must minimize failures and maintain high reliability.

4.2 Usability
NFR2: The system shall allow users to retrieve their previous work with a single interaction.

4.3 Performance
NFR3: 95% of webpages generated by the system shall load completely within 4 seconds for users with a 20Mbps or faster Internet connection.

4.4 Security
NFR4: The system must protect against unauthorized access.

================================================================================

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: ImpairAssist - Audio-Visual Speech Recognition for Hearing-Impaired
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document is a project proposal for ImpairAssist, an application that uses audio-visual speech recognition (AVSR) to generate real-time captions, helping audibly impaired people communicate with the general public.

1.2 Scope
The project focuses on combining audio (speech) and visual (lip movements) inputs to create a more robust speech-to-text system. It involves using separate models for audio and visual inputs and then fusing them.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AVSR: Audio-Visual Speech Recognition
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- WER: Word Error Rate
- CER: Character Error Rate

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
ImpairAssist addresses the shortcomings of traditional speech recognition systems that ignore visual cues, which are crucial for accurate communication, especially for the hearing impaired.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Audio and visual input processing.
- Model fusion (combining audio and visual speech recognition models).
- Real-time caption generation.
- Noise reduction and error rectification.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Hearing-Impaired People: Primary users who will use the app to communicate.

2.4 Operating Environment
Application for real-time captioning.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Proposed Solution/Method
FR1.1: The system shall receive input in the form of a video.
FR1.2: The system shall extract the visual (frames) and audio aspects of speech.
FR1.3: The visual input (lip movement) shall be fed to a visual speech recognition module.
FR1.4: The audio input shall be fed to an audio speech recognition module.
FR1.5: The results of both modules shall be passed to a cross-attention module that will combine them.
FR1.6: The cross-attention module shall use model fusion, a bidirectional transformer, or a multi-modal architecture to combine results.
FR1.7: The system shall perform preprocessing on both inputs to remove noise.
FR1.8: The system shall generate a text caption for the speech.
FR1.9: The system shall be evaluated using Word Error Rate (WER), Character Error Rate (CER), and Latency.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have a high Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF).
NFR2: The system shall maintain reliability in generating accurate transcriptions.
NFR3: The system shall detect and rectify errors.

4.2 Performance
NFR4: The system shall handle transcription efficiently, even with background noise.
NFR5: The system shall ensure high accuracy in speech-to-text transcription, particularly in medical terminology (if applicable).
NFR6: The system shall handle speaker diarization efficiently.

4.3 Usability
NFR7: The system shall be user-friendly.

4.4 Security
NFR8: The system shall implement strong user authentication mechanisms.
NFR9: The system shall define user roles and permissions.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: AI Fashion Design Studio - AI-Powered Fashion Design and Marketing Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the objectives, features, and overall vision for the AI Fashion Design Studio, a system that uses generative AI to expedite and enhance the fashion design process.

1.2 Scope
The platform converts sketches to digital images, creates 3D models, enables virtual try-on, generates new designs via a crossover mechanism, and automatically produces marketing materials (image and video ads). It focuses on adult clothing.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- GUI: Graphical User Interface
- API: Application Programming Interface
- 3D: Three-Dimensional

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The AI Fashion Design Studio aims to revolutionize the fashion industry by streamlining the long development cycle and enabling rapid experimentation and customization through AI.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Template Management.
- Sketch to Image Conversion (using Stable Diffusion).
- 3D Modeling and Visualization.
- Virtual Try-On.
- Crossover Design Mechanism.
- Marketing Material Generation (image and video ads).

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Fashion Designers: Primary users who use the system to improve and optimize the design process.
- E-commerce Retailers: Use the platform to create online catalogs and provide virtual try-on experiences.
- Customers: Benefit from personalized shopping experiences via virtual try-on and custom design.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Template Management
FR1.1: Users shall be able to browse and select from a provided list of fashion templates.
FR1.2: The system shall display the selected template, ready for further customization.

3.2 Sketch to Image Conversion
FR2.1: The system shall convert hand-drawn sketches into detailed 2D digital images using a fine-tuned Stable Diffusion model.
FR2.2: High-resolution digital images that closely resemble the original sketches shall be produced.

3.3 3D Modeling and Visualization
FR3.1: The system shall transform 2D digital images into 3D models that are rotatable and viewable from any angle.
FR3.2: A fully interactive 3D model of the clothing item shall be available for detailed inspection.

3.4 Virtual Try-On
FR4.1: The system shall simulate how clothing items look on virtual avatars using a VITON model.
FR4.2: A visualization of the garment as worn by the avatar shall be provided, offering realistic representations of fit and style.

3.5 Crossover Design Mechanism
FR5.1: The system shall allow for the fusion of elements from two different garments to create a new, unique design.
FR5.2: A new garment design that incorporates the selected elements from the original garments shall be produced.

3.6 Clothing Image Ads
FR6.1: The system shall automatically generate multiple marketing images featuring models wearing the designed clothes in various settings.
FR6.2: Professionally styled images suitable for marketing purposes shall be displayed.

3.7 Video Ads
FR7.1: The system shall create short video clips showcasing the clothes in dynamic custom scenes.
FR7.2: A 5-10 second marketing video that highlights the garment features dynamically shall be produced.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have an MTBF of no less than 100 hours.
NFR2: The system will implement regular data backups, automated recovery processes, and fail-over mechanisms.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The system shall allow users to complete common design tasks within a few steps.
NFR4: The system shall comply with web accessibility standards.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: The system shall generate high-resolution digital images from user-uploaded sketches within a minute.
NFR6: 3D models from 2D digital images shall be rendered within a minute.
NFR7: Virtual try-on simulations shall generate results within 50 seconds of input.
NFR8: Marketing Content Generation module will generate content within 3-4 minutes.
NFR9: The system must handle up to 300 concurrent users without noticeable degradation.
NFR10: Uploading and processing images under 50MB shall happen in less than a minute.

4.4 Security
NFR11: The system must implement robust authentication and authorization mechanisms to ensure only registered users can access the design studio.
NFR12: The system shall comply with GDPR, CCPA, and other relevant data protection regulations.
NFR13: Compliance with copyright and intellectual property laws must be ensured.

================================================================================

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: CommPulse - Real-Time Meeting Quality Monitoring System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the objectives, background, and technical details for CommPulse, a desktop application that monitors the quality of online meetings in real-time and post-meeting.

1.2 Scope
The system monitors audio streams from Microsoft Teams to track metrics like sentiments, active participation, clarity, and agenda adherence. It provides real-time feedback and post-meeting analysis through a user-friendly interface.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- API: Application Programming Interface
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control
- TLS: Transport Layer Security

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
CommPulse fills the gap in quality monitoring tools for online meetings by providing a streamlined system to track important communication traits and give actionable feedback to improve effectiveness.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- UI/UX design for meeting management, real-time and post-meeting graphs.
- Front-end development (ElectronJS, ReactJS) for user management, audio capture, meeting history.
- Back-end meeting quality monitoring (sentiment, participation, clarity, agenda adherence).
- Meeting history database (SQL/NoSQL).
- Integration of front-end and back-end.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Admin: Can track live meetings, perform post-meeting analysis, manage user roles.
- Regular User: Can analyze past meetings and evaluate metrics after the meeting has ended.
- Guest User: External participant with limited access.

2.4 Operating Environment
Desktop application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 UI/UX design
FR1.1: The system shall provide user management screens for registration, login, and profile management.
FR1.2: The system shall present a home screen with options for starting or joining meetings, accessing meeting history, and user settings.
FR1.3: The system shall display real-time graphs and figures representing meeting quality metrics.
FR1.4: The system shall provide a post-meeting screen with summarized and detailed meeting statistics.
FR1.5: The system shall generate post-time graphs showing key metrics such as clarity, agenda adherence, and speaker engagement.
FR1.6: The system shall display a meeting history screen where users can view data from previous meetings.

3.2 Front-end development
FR2.1: The system shall provide user management and authentication functionality.
FR2.2: The system shall display a home screen with options to manage meetings, user profiles, and settings.
FR2.3: The system shall include a start meeting screen, capturing necessary meeting details.
FR2.4: The system shall capture audio data from the user's device through an API for real-time analysis.
FR2.5: The system shall allow the user to manage ongoing meetings, including displaying real-time participant data and metrics.
FR2.6: The system shall display a post-meeting page showing results, statistics, and summary.
FR2.7: The system shall generate and display post-time graphs.
FR2.8: The system shall maintain a meeting history page.

3.3 Meeting Quality Monitoring System Back-End
FR3.1: The system shall use an API to identify and distinguish speakers.
FR3.2: The system shall analyze and display real-time sentiment metrics.
FR3.3: The system shall monitor active participation and display engagement scores.
FR3.4: The system shall monitor adherence to the meeting agenda and provide feedback.
FR3.5: The system shall generate time-stamped highlights.

3.4 Meeting History Database
FR4.1: The system shall store session-level data (start time, end time, participants, agenda).
FR4.2: The system shall store speaker-level data (sentiment, engagement, clarity, highlights).

3.5 Integration of Front-End and Back-End
FR5.1: The system shall capture audio from the front-end and transmit it to the back-end in real-time.
FR5.2: The system shall display real-time plots of meeting quality metrics.
FR5.3: The system shall generate and display post-time plots.
FR5.4: The system shall retrieve and display meeting history data.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The MTBF of the system shall be 1,000 operational hours.
NFR2: The system shall minimize storage loss of meeting data to less than 1 minute during failure.
NFR3: Within one minute of failure, error logs shall be created, and recovery will take place in less than five minutes.
NFR4: If the system fails, it shall recover automatically with the user not needing to pause ongoing meetings.

4.2 Usability
NFR5: Users shall reach post-meeting reports via the dashboard in just 5 clicks.
NFR6: A user shall initiate live meeting tracking as soon as the meeting has started.
NFR7: Help documentation and tooltips shall be present on every screen.
NFR8: Within 30 minutes of engagement, users shall execute basic analysis tasks.

4.3 Performance
NFR9: For up to two-hour meetings, analysis shall be completed in five minutes.
NFR10: Up to 100 users shall be handled simultaneously with stable performance.
NFR11: The system shall ensure instant feedback in 10 seconds during live meeting tracking over a 20 Mbps or faster connection.
NFR12: The system shall perform real-time monitoring with a maximum delay of 15 seconds for meetings with up to ten attendees.

4.4 Security
NFR13: The system shall require multiple authentication methods for all admin users.
NFR14: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) shall be implemented.
NFR15: The system shall support a connection protocol of TLS 1.2 or greater.

4.5 Maintainability
NFR16: Changes in one module shall affect fewer than 10% of the other modules.
NFR17: Codebase updates shall be noted within 2 days.
NFR18: No system downtime for users shall be allowed during any meetings.

================================================================================

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: CrimeSight - Real-Time Crime Detection and Reporting System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for CrimeSight, a system that aims to revolutionize traditional security by detecting and reporting crimes live, allowing for timely action by authorities.

1.2 Scope
The system detects and classifies crimes (fire, fight, weapon, destruction of property) within indoor environments. It alerts users and authorities via WhatsApp and mobile app, provides person tracking, remote feed viewing, clip sharing, and analytics.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- CCTV: Closed-Circuit Television
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
CrimeSight shifts from a reactive crime reporting system to a proactive one by using machine learning to continuously monitor and instantly report suspicious activities.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Multi-Crime Classification and Detection (Fire, Fight, Weapon, Destruction).
- Crime Analytics and Statistics.
- Crime Reporting via WhatsApp and app.
- Suspicious Activity Tracking.
- Clip Sharing.
- Remote Feed Viewing.
- Emergency Contacts.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Property Owner: Primary user who owns the property and uses the system to watch activities and receive alerts.
- Law Enforcement Users: Authorized users from agencies who are alerted and receive video evidence.

2.4 Operating Environment
Desktop and mobile applications.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Multi Crime Classification And Detection
FR1.1: The system shall detect and classify crimes into four categories: Fire Detection, Fight Detection, Weapon Detection, and Destruction of Property.
FR1.2: The system shall distinguish between normal and crime scenarios.
FR1.3: The system shall process video feeds from multiple cameras simultaneously.

3.2 Crime Analytics
FR2.1: The system shall display statistics of crime occurrences on a day-to-day, month-to-month, and year-to-year basis.
FR2.2: The system shall generate patterns in crime timings, types, and frequency.
FR2.3: The system shall provide safety advice based on the top crimes being committed.

3.3 Crime Reporting
FR3.1: The system shall automatically report to relevant authorities using WhatsApp.
FR3.2: The system shall automatically report to the Camera Setup owner using the Mobile Application.
FR3.3: The system shall have options to choose alerts to authorities based on the type of crime.
FR3.4: All crime reports shall include video evidence.

3.4 Suspicious Activity
FR4.1: The system shall allow the user to select an individual through the Desktop Application and track them through the multiple camera setup.
FR4.2: The system shall allow the user to set the time limit to track based on past available camera feeds.
FR4.3: The system shall provide the user with the combined clips from multiple cameras of the steps taken by the individual.

3.5 Tracking Analysis
FR5.1: The system shall provide the user with a relevant summary of what the tracked individual did.
FR5.2: The system shall provide analytics relevant to the individual tracked on both Desktop and Mobile Applications.

3.6 Clip Sharing
FR6.1: The system shall allow the user to combine multiple clips from a single feed.
FR6.2: The system shall allow users to share camera feeds with other users.
FR6.3: The system shall allow the user to specify the number of times the shared clips can be downloaded.

3.7 Remote Feed
FR7.1: The system shall allow users real-time access to camera feeds remotely on both Mobile and Desktop Applications.
FR7.2: The system shall allow the user to simultaneously view feeds from multiple cameras.

3.8 Emergency Contacts
FR8.1: The system shall provide the user with relevant emergency contacts for relevant authorities.
FR8.2: The system shall allow the user to customize emergency contact lists.
FR8.3: The system shall provide the user with immediate and easy access to contact emergency services.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system would be highly reliable, covering discrepancies in the model.
NFR2: The system would achieve an average MTBF of 600 hours.
NFR3: The system would manage crashes by sending proper logs and trying to recover within 5 minutes.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: The camera feeds will be available on the home page requiring no extra button clicks.
NFR5: Pressing on any Emergency Contact will result in a call being made directly.
NFR6: The system will be user-friendly for new users.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: The system shall classify activity type for 2 cameras simultaneously within a second.
NFR8: Alerts will be notified to relevant authorities within 5 seconds of finalizing crime detection.

4.4 Security
NFR9: The application will use industry standard encryption algorithms for all personal user data and security feeds transmission.
NFR10: The system will secure users using two-factor authentication.
NFR11: The system will make sure the shared clips are only available for a specific period of time.
NFR12: The system will manage a log of all audits.

================================================================================

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: ScholarChain - AI-Driven Interest-Free Student Loan Platform on Blockchain
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document is a project proposal for ScholarChain, an AI-driven student loan system on the blockchain that provides interest-free loans to students by connecting them with social welfare organizations through a crowd-sourcing model.

1.2 Scope
The system uses blockchain for transparency and security, smart contracts for automation, and an LLM for risk assessment, personalized repayment plans, and personal assistance. It includes interfaces for students, donors, and administrators.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- RAG: Retrieval Augmented Generation
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
ScholarChain aims to address the problems of high tuition fees, complex application processes, and lack of transparency in existing financial aid systems by providing an automated, interest-free, and transparent platform.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- AI Module: Risk assessment, personal assistant (RAGbot), personalized repayment plans, AI-driven customer support.
- Blockchain Module: Immutable ledger, smart contracts for automation.
- Web Application: Interfaces for students and donors, audit trails, automated reports, reminders.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Students: Primary users applying for interest-free loans.
- Parents: Act as co-signers and monitor repayment.
- Social Welfare Entities (Donors): Provide funding.
- System Administrators: Verify and approve risk assessment and repayment plans.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Student Interaction Module
FR1.1: Students shall be able to submit loan applications by providing personal, academic, and financial information.
FR1.2: The system shall assess the current financial situation of the user and generate a personalized repayment plan.
FR1.3: Students shall be able to track their upcoming payments and view payment history.
FR1.4: Students shall be able to interact with an AI-powered chatbot to address queries.

3.2 Funding and Loan Management Module
FR2.1: Donors shall be able to contribute funds to the platform via secure payment gateways.
FR2.2: The system administrator shall review the AI-assessed application and make the final decision on approval.
FR2.3: Funds shall be automatically disbursed via smart contracts once applications are approved.

3.3 Administrative Tools
FR3.1: The AI shall evaluate each application and assign them a loan risk score.
FR3.2: System administrators shall verify that an application's terms and conditions have been accepted by the student.

3.4 Notifications and Reminders
FR4.1: Automatic notifications shall be sent to students to remind them of upcoming payment deadlines.
FR4.2: The system shall notify students about the current status of their loan application.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have an MTBF of no less than 500 hours.
NFR2: The system should not experience more than one failure per month, with recovery period not more than 1 hour.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: A new user should be able to easily navigate the platform within a few minutes.
NFR4: Common errors such as incomplete forms should be clearly highlighted.
NFR5: All tasks such as submitting an application should be completable within 5 steps.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: 90% of all pages must be loaded completely within 3 seconds over a 20Mbps connection.
NFR7: The platform must support almost 5000 concurrent users without degradation.
NFR8: The AI should be able to process data for the application process within 30 seconds of submission.

4.4 Security
NFR9: All user data must be encrypted at all times.
NFR10: Different users must have different access rights to ensure unauthorized users cannot access sensitive information.

================================================================================

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Napalm - The Web Creation Kit (No-Code Website Builder)
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document describes the requirements for Napalm, a web development tool that facilitates drag-and-drop website building, AI-powered content generation, and real-time collaboration.

1.2 Scope
Napalm provides a no-code platform with exportable, production-ready code and customizable AI integrations. It targets freelancers, startups, and small enterprises who need flexible and open-source solutions.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- UI: User Interface
- API: Application Programming Interface
- MVC: Model-View-Controller

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Napalm overcomes the limitations of proprietary platforms like Webflow and Wix by providing an open-source alternative that offers complete control over the website creation process and exportable code.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- UI Design, Editor, and Templating Module (drag-and-drop).
- AI Content Generation Module (text, images).
- User Authentication, Management, and Database Module.
- WebSocket Module (real-time collaboration).
- Third-Party Deployment Module (Vercel, Netlify).
- Testing and Optimization Module.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- User: Regular users who create and manage websites.
- Admin: Manages platform settings, user access, and templates.
- Project: Represents an individual project.
- Template: Pre-designed website structures.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

Project: EDA - Enhanced Driver Assist System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for the Enhanced Driver Assist (EDA) system, which aims to improve road safety by assisting drivers with real-time alerts and monitoring.

1.2 Scope
The EDA system integrates three primary modules: driver attention monitoring (via in-cabin camera), lane departure monitoring (via road camera), and driving pattern analysis (via OBD-II data). It provides voice alerts and is designed for highways and motorways in Pakistan.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- EDA: Enhanced Driver Assist
- OBD-II: On-Board Diagnostics II
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures
- TPU: Tensor Processing Unit

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
EDA aims to reduce traffic accidents caused by driver fatigue, distraction, and lane departure by providing timely alerts and feedback, especially on highways.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Driver Attention Monitoring (drowsiness detection).
- Lane Detection and Departure Warning.
- Driving Pattern Monitoring (via OBD-II).
- Voice Alerts.
- Hardware Integration (Raspberry Pi/Nvidia Jetson Nano, Coral TPU, dashcams).

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Driver: Primary user who receives alerts and feedback.
- Fleet Manager: Oversees multiple vehicles and reviews aggregated data.

2.4 Operating Environment
In-vehicle hardware (Raspberry Pi/Nvidia Jetson Nano) with connected cameras and OBD-II sensor.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Driver Attention Monitoring
FR1.1: The system shall detect driver drowsiness based on eye closure duration.
FR1.2: The system shall issue an alert if the driver's attention is diverted for more than 5 seconds.

3.2 Lane Departure Monitoring
FR2.1: The system shall detect lane markers on highways and monitor the vehicle's position.
FR2.2: The system shall warn the driver when the vehicle departs from its lane.

3.3 Driving Pattern Analysis
FR3.1: The system shall analyze OBD-II data for speed, acceleration, and rpm.
FR3.2: The system shall generate reports for unsafe driving behavior (harsh acceleration, over-speeding).

3.4 Voice Alert
FR4.1: The system shall deliver voice alerts for potential hazards and driver assistance.
FR4.2: The system shall process alerts locally on edge devices for minimal response time.

3.5 Hardware Implementation and Integration
FR5.1: The system shall integrate OBD-II sensors for vehicle data collection.
FR5.2: The system shall stream videos from in-cabin and out-cabin cameras to the models.
FR5.3: The system shall run lightweight models locally on the device.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall maintain an MTBF of at least 1000 hours.
NFR2: The system shall perform regular self-checks and log errors.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The system shall allow a driver to access driving behavior feedback.
NFR4: The system shall enable fleet managers to generate reports on driving patterns.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: The system shall analyze OBD-II data and provide feedback within 4 seconds of data collection.
NFR6: Voice alerts shall be processed and delivered within 4 seconds of detection.
NFR7: Lane detection warnings shall have an approximate accuracy of 80% or higher.

4.4 Security
NFR8: The system shall limit access to sensitive data to authorized personnel only.
NFR9: The system shall log all access attempts to sensitive data.

Project: Predictive Maintenance System - IoT Anomaly Detection

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This project aims to develop a Predictive Maintenance System using anomaly detection in sensor data to identify potential equipment failures for CARE Pvt. Ltd.'s IoT devices (3-Phase Current Sensor, Thermistor, Vibration Sensor) attached to motors of different equipment.

1.2 Scope
The project deals with data from vibration, temperature, and current sensors on CARE's machinery. Features include: data preprocessing (cleaning, handling missing values), anomaly detection using statistical and machine learning techniques, real-time visualization with green/red light indicators, and predictive maintenance scheduling based on historical patterns.

- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

The system supports Pakistani industry in sustaining machinery, extending equipment lifespan, and avoiding catastrophic failures through early detection of faults.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data preprocessing (combine datasets, alter time, handle missing values)
- Model training (LSTM on labeled time series data)
- Hybrid approach (statistical modeling + machine learning)
- Equipment failure prediction
- Model visualization

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Users: Access collected data, login, select city/device, manage API configuration
- System: Import CSV files, combine files, provide preprocessing options
- Users: Train models, visualize results in graphs

Jupyter notebooks, Python environment, cloud data storage.

Project: LeafSpec - AI-Powered Plant Species Identification from Leaf Images
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for LeafSpec, a mobile application that uses a custom-trained CNN to recognize plant species from leaf photos, helping botanists, researchers, and enthusiasts.

1.2 Scope
The project focuses on developing a mobile app (Flutter) that accurately classifies plant species from leaf images. It aims to be better than existing competitors in terms of accuracy, intuitiveness, and ease of use.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CNN: Convolutional Neural Network
- UI: User Interface
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
LeafSpec automates the tiring and tedious task of manual leaf classification, reducing errors and increasing efficiency for professionals and enthusiasts.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Preprocessing (resizing, augmentation).
- Model Training (CNN).
- App Development (Flutter) with admin/user dashboards.
- Model testing and improvement.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- End User: Botanists, researchers, or plant enthusiasts who use the app for species identification.
- Developer: Creates and maintains the app and models.
- Testers: Ensure the application is working properly.

2.4 Operating Environment
Multi-platform mobile application (iOS, Android).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Preprocessing
FR1.1: The leaf image provided by the user shall be resized to a standard resolution accepted by the CNN.
FR1.2: Data cleaning shall be done to improve the model's accuracy.

3.2 Model Training
FR2.1: The system shall train the model on a preprocessed dataset of 10-15 species.
FR2.2: Model's testing and validation accuracy along with confidence scores shall be used to judge its overall accuracy.
FR2.3: The updated model shall be saved to the database.

3.3 User Interface (UI) development
FR3.1: The user interface shall be friendly, easier to use, and have intuitive navigation.
FR3.2: Output shall contain the identified specie along with confidence score and description.

3.4 Admin and User Dashboards
FR4.1: Admin Dashboard shall allow admins to add, remove, or delete species or perform maintenance.
FR4.2: The application shall have a user interface for identification.

3.5 Integration of Model with Application
FR5.1: The application shall be integrated with the CNN model so leaf images can be sent for classification.

3.6 Testing the Model
FR6.1: The model shall be regularly evaluated using a testing dataset.
FR6.2: An error message shall be provided by the system if the confidence score is less than 20%.

3.7 Improvement of Model
FR7.1: The model's accuracy should be improved by catering to more species and enhancing the dataset.
FR7.2: The system shall use feedback to improve the application.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system will have an MTBF target of 500+ operation hours.
NFR2: A failure is defined as whenever uploading images failed, leaf classification failed, or a user did not get a result after 2 minutes.
NFR3: If an error is detected, re-processing of the image will be done.
NFR4: If a failure happens, the system shall automatically restart and indicate to the development team.

4.2 Usability
NFR5: The interface shall require a minimum number of clicks (approximately 3-5) to upload leaf images.
NFR6: Application buttons must be clearly labeled.
NFR7: The feedback mechanism shall not appear as a pop-up every time; there shall be an option.
NFR8: The application shall be accessible on iOS, Android, and Web.

4.3 Performance
NFR9: The custom-trained CNN shall be able to classify a specie within a few seconds.
NFR10: Data retrieval from the database shall be done within (5-10 seconds).
NFR11: The system shall store feedback submission right after the user clicks "Send Feedback".
NFR12: The system shall be fast enough to retrieve previous classification data within 5-10 seconds.
NFR13: Error messages shall be displayed as soon as the issue is identified.

4.4 Security
NFR14: The system shall encrypt all sensitive user data (passwords, email, user history).
NFR15: The software would be locked for the user for approximately 10-15 minutes if a user unsuccessfully tries to login more than 10 times.
NFR16: A captcha system shall be implemented to avoid bots.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: BlackGate - Blockchain-Based Identity & Authentication System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for BlackGate, a stateless decentralized identity and access management (SD-IAM) system that leverages blockchain technology for secure and efficient identity management.

1.2 Scope
The system provides passwordless login, Single Sign-On (SSO), Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), user context analysis for anomaly detection, and dashboards for admins and employees. It supports LDAP, APIs, and Federated Identity Management (FIM).

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- IAM: Identity and Access Management
- DID: Decentralized Identifier
- VC: Verifiable Credential
- SSO: Single Sign-On
- MFA: Multi-Factor Authentication

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
BlackGate addresses the complexity, security risks, and inefficiencies of traditional IAM systems by providing a decentralized, blockchain-enabled solution that enhances security, scalability, and user privacy.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User and Mobile Authentication Interface (passwordless, MFA, FIM).
- Administrative Dashboard.
- AI Contextual Monitoring Engine (trust score, anomaly detection).
- Blockchain-Based Identity Management (BDIM).

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- End Users: Manage personal data and verifiable credentials.
- Identity Issuers: Generate, manage, and revoke verifiable credentials.
- Service Providers: Request identity verification and manage access.
- Identity Verifiers: Authenticate user credentials on the blockchain.
- Administrators: Monitor sessions and configure policies.
- Compliance & Audit Professionals: Ensure adherence to regulations.
- Regulatory Authorities: Set policies and standards.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web and mobile interfaces.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Registration and Authentication
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to register by entering a valid email address or phone number.
FR1.2: The system shall generate a DID and VC upon successful registration.
FR1.3: The system shall send a confirmation link or code for verification.
FR1.4: The system shall require users to authenticate using their DID and VC.
FR1.5: The system shall verify the submitted DID and VC against the blockchain.
FR1.6: The system shall provide an authentication token upon successful verification.
FR1.7: The system shall support secure user authentication using DIDs and VCs.
FR1.8: The system shall implement FIM to facilitate user access through SAML/OAuth/OpenID Connect.
FR1.9: The system shall provide a mobile-friendly authentication interface.
FR1.10: The system shall offer real-time notifications and dedicated dashboards.
FR1.11: The system shall provide a passwordless login option.
FR1.12: The system shall generate and send a one-time code for authentication.
FR1.13: The system shall authenticate users based on the verification of the one-time code.

3.2 Administrative Dashboard
FR2.1: The system shall enable administrators to access a monitoring dashboard displaying real-time user session data, access logs, and blockchain transaction logs.
FR2.2: The system shall allow administrators to filter logs by date, user, action, or transaction type.
FR2.3: The system shall generate analytics and reports on user activity and blockchain actions.

3.3 AI Contextual Monitoring Engine
FR3.1: The system shall continuously monitor user behavior and contextual data to calculate a trust score.
FR3.2: The system shall automatically revoke user access if their trust score falls below a specified threshold.
FR3.3: The system shall notify users when their access is revoked due to a low trust score.
FR3.4: The system shall leverage AI algorithms to continuously improve the accuracy of trust score assessments.

3.4 Blockchain-Based Identity Management
FR4.1: The system shall record user identity updates on the blockchain to ensure data integrity and security.
FR4.2: The system shall prevent unauthorized modifications to identity records on the blockchain.
FR4.3: The system shall log identity management actions and generate an audit trail.

3.5 User Account Management
FR5.1: The system shall allow users to view and update their account information securely.
FR5.2: The system shall validate user-submitted updates before applying changes to the blockchain.
FR5.3: The system shall notify users when their account information has been successfully updated.
FR5.4: The system shall allow users to view their recent account activity logs, including blockchain transactions.
FR5.5: The system shall enable users to filter their activity logs.
FR5.6: The system shall provide an account recovery option.
FR5.7: The system shall send a recovery link or code for resetting authentication.
FR5.8: The system shall securely record the account recovery process on the blockchain.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 concurrent user sessions.
NFR2: The system shall respond to user authentication requests within 2 seconds.
NFR3: The system shall process identity verification requests and provide feedback within 5 seconds.
NFR4: 95% of identity verification requests involving blockchain transactions shall be confirmed within 4 seconds.

4.2 Scalability
NFR5: The system shall be designed for horizontal scalability.
NFR6: The system shall allow for seamless integration of new identity providers and authentication methods.

4.3 Reliability
NFR7: The system shall achieve an uptime of 99.9%.
NFR8: The system shall have a robust disaster recovery plan.
NFR9: Reliability metrics shall be defined, including MTBF for blockchain operations.

4.4 Usability
NFR10: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface requiring minimal training.
NFR11: The system shall offer comprehensive help documentation and tooltips.
NFR12: Users shall be able to retrieve previous authentication attempts with a single interaction.

4.5 Maintainability
NFR13: The system should be designed for easy maintenance, with each module as a standalone subsystem.

4.6 Capacity
NFR14: The system should have the capability to store vast amounts of user data, including blockchain transaction records.

4.7 Security
NFR15: The system shall implement stringent security measures to protect user data.
NFR16: Security metrics shall be defined in terms of the effort required to breach the system.
NFR17: The system shall employ advanced authentication methods and data protection techniques.
NFR18: Regular security assessments and audits shall be conducted.

1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the problem and solution for automating the driving license testing process using sensors, cameras, and AI models to ensure a more fair, accurate, and efficient testing process.

1.2 Scope
The system automates driver's license exams using sensors and AI. Features include driver performance monitoring (traffic laws, following distance, mirror use), assessment of reverse and parallel parking, processing test data via image processing models, automated decision-making, and web/desktop interfaces for candidates and administrators.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations

- CNIC: Citizens National Identity Card
- AI: Artificial Intelligence

2.1 Product Perspective
The system replaces subjective human examiners with an objective, AI-driven assessment process, utilizing hardware sensors and cameras integrated via a Raspberry Pi.

- Data processing and cleaning.
- Model training for traffic sign recognition, driver attention, seat-belt usage, and mirror checking.
- Output processing to determine pass/fail and generate feedback.
- Admin panel for managing tests and reviewing results.
- User panel for viewing test results.

FR1.3: The system shall record video from interior cameras to assess driver compliance (seatbelt, attention, hand position).

3.2 Data Processing
FR2.1: The system shall clean, preprocess, and standardize data from all sensors and cameras.
FR2.2: The system shall synchronize data from multiple sensors and cameras for cohesive analysis.
FR2.3: The system shall preprocess video frames for smooth image analysis.

3.3 Model Training and Evaluation
FR3.1: The system shall use image processing and object detection models to observe driver behaviors.
FR3.2: The system shall utilize object detection ML models for traffic sign detection and driver response analysis.
FR3.3: The system shall evaluate candidate parking performance using sensor and video data.

3.4 Output Processing
FR4.1: The system shall automatically determine if a candidate passes or fails based on set criteria.
FR4.2: The system shall generate a detailed feedback report for each candidate on specific tasks.

3.5 Admin Panel Interface

FR6.2: The system shall enable administrators to respond to user feedback.

3.6 User Panel Interface
FR5.1: The user shall be able to view a detailed report of the test and feedback via a web panel.

3.7 Hardware Integration

FR7.2: The system shall manage communication between hardware components and the central server.

FR2.1.1: The system shall allow users to create profiles using a valid email and password.
FR2.1.2: The system shall allow users to update personal information (name, contact, address).
FR2.1.3: The system shall allow users to permanently delete their profiles.
FR2.1.4: The system shall allow users to leave ratings and reviews for transactions.
FR2.1.5: The system shall allow users to reset passwords via secure email verification.

3.2 Product Listings
FR2.2.1: The system shall allow users to add new product listings (title, description, price, duration, condition).
FR2.2.2: The system shall allow users to modify existing product details.
FR2.2.3: The system shall allow users to remove their product listings.
FR2.2.4: The system shall allow users to upload multiple images per listing.
FR2.2.5: The system shall allow users to search for products based on categories, price, and location.

3.3 Identity Verification
FR2.3.1: The system shall verify user identities by scanning and analyzing CNIC documents.
FR2.3.2: The system shall utilize facial recognition to match user faces with their CNIC photos.
FR2.3.3: The system shall securely store verified identities in blockchain wallets.
FR2.3.4: The system shall provide users with access to their verification status history.
FR2.3.5: The system shall complete the identity verification process within 5 minutes.

3.4 Real-Time Communication
FR2.4.1: The system shall allow renters and owners to initiate and maintain real-time chat conversations.
FR2.4.2: The system shall send notifications for new messages.
FR2.4.3: The system shall allow users to share images and documents during chats.
FR2.4.4: The system shall allow users to access their chat history.
FR2.4.5: The system shall enable users to block or report other users.

3.5 Real-Time Tracking of Renters
FR2.5.1: The system shall track the geographical location of renters during product pickup and return.
FR2.5.2: The system shall provide real-time updates on location and estimated time of arrival (ETA).
FR2.5.3: The system shall maintain a history of tracking data for future reference.
FR2.5.4: The system shall allow users to enable or disable location tracking at any time.

3.6 Blockchain-Based Proof of Ownership
FR2.6.1: The system shall verify product ownership using blockchain technology.
FR2.6.2: The system shall securely store all transaction-related data on the blockchain.
FR2.6.3: The system shall allow users to transfer product ownership via a secure blockchain transaction.
FR2.6.4: The system shall allow users to view the complete ownership history of a product.
FR2.6.5: The system shall provide a mechanism for resolving disputes regarding product ownership.

3.7 Rent-to-Own Model
FR2.7.1: The system shall allow users to set up payment plans for rent-to-own transactions.
FR2.7.2: The system shall allow users to transfer ownership after fulfilling rent payments.
FR2.7.3: The system shall allow users to view their remaining balance and payment history.
FR2.7.4: The system shall send reminders for upcoming payments and confirmations upon successful payments.
FR2.7.5: The system shall provide multiple payment options (credit cards, digital wallets).

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: RentEase must ensure high availability with a target uptime of 99.9%.
NFR2: The platform must handle large volumes of concurrent transactions without downtime.
NFR3: The system should provide backup and recovery solutions to prevent data loss.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: The system should provide an intuitive interface accommodating users with limited technical skills.
NFR5: Clear instructions and tooltips must guide users through key features.
NFR6: The application must support accessibility features to assist users with disabilities.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: Pages should load within 2 seconds for users with a 20Mbps connection.
NFR8: Real-time communication and tracking data should update within 1 second.
NFR9: The system must support at least 10,000 concurrent users without performance degradation.

4.4 Security
NFR10: RentEase must ensure user data protection through encryption during transmission and storage.
NFR11: The application must comply with relevant data protection regulations (e.g., GDPR).
NFR12: The system should implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for user accounts.
NFR13: Regular security audits must be conducted to identify vulnerabilities.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: VoiceArchi - Voice-Controlled Architectural Drawing Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document describes the requirements for VoiceArchi, a web/mobile application that transforms spoken ideas into detailed architectural drawings using voice-controlled interfaces, speech recognition, and natural language processing.

1.2 Scope
The system allows users to describe desired floorplans in natural language. Key features include voice command input converted to text via Whisper, constraint extraction using a fine-tuned GPT model, and 2D floorplan generation via a constraint satisfaction algorithm. The scope includes developing user-friendly web and mobile interfaces.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- GPT: Generative Pre-trained Transformer
- 2D: Two-Dimensional

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
VoiceArchi simplifies the complex process of floorplan creation by using AI to interpret spoken descriptions and generate accurate architectural drawings, making it accessible to users without technical expertise.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Voice Command Input: Describe floorplans using natural language.
- Real-time Text Conversion: Convert voice to text using Whisper.
- Constraint Extraction: Extract architectural constraints (room types, sizes, adjacency) from text.
- 2D Floorplan Generation: Produce detailed floorplans based on extracted constraints.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Architects: Professionals using the platform for quick initial sketches.
- Interior Designers: Use the tool to visualize room layouts based on client requirements.
- Homeowners and Clients: Communicate ideas effectively without formal design tool knowledge.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Client Web App
FR1: The system must allow the user to activate and provide voice input through the browser interface.
FR2: The system must display real-time transcriptions of the user’s voice commands using Whisper.
FR3: The system must render and display the generated 2D floorplan based on extracted constraints.

3.2 Client Mobile App
FR1: The system must allow the user to provide voice input via the mobile interface, maintaining web app functionality.
FR2: The system must adapt and display the generated 2D floorplan on mobile devices, ensuring responsiveness.

3.3 Backend Processing
FR1: The system must convert voice input into text using the Whisper model with high accuracy.
FR2: The system must extract architectural constraints (room types, sizes, adjacency requirements) from the transcribed text.
FR3: The system must generate a 2D floorplan based on the extracted constraints, ensuring all user-specified conditions are satisfied.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have an MTBF of at least 1000 hours of continuous operation.
NFR2: In case of failure, the system must automatically attempt recovery without data loss.
NFR3: The system shall implement error detection mechanisms to identify issues in real-time.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: The system interface must be simple and intuitive.
NFR5: The system shall have a minimum 90% accuracy in recognizing voice commands.
NFR6: The system must comply with accessibility standards (screen readers, adjustable text size).

4.3 Performance
NFR7: 95% of voice commands shall be processed and transcribed within 3 seconds.
NFR8: The system shall generate a 2D floorplan within 5 seconds after receiving all required constraints.
NFR9: The mobile app should respond to user input with less than 200 milliseconds delay.

4.4 Security
NFR10: All user data (saved floorplans, personal information) shall be encrypted (AES-256) during storage and transmission.
NFR11: The system must support multi-factor authentication (MFA) for secure login.
NFR12: The system should be resistant to penetration attempts, requiring at least 48 hours of sustained effort from a highly skilled attacker to breach.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SparkQuest - An AI-Powered Educational Web App for Primary-Grade Students
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for SparkQuest, an interactive educational web app targeting primary-grade students (1-5) to enhance learning in English, Maths, and Science through Generative AI and RAG.

1.2 Scope
SparkQuest focuses on making education fun and engaging. Core functionalities include a multi-modal question-answering module (text, images, videos), text-to-audio support, bilingual support (Urdu/English), and a parental/guardian role for monitoring progress.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- CNIC: Computerized National Identity Card (not applicable, but mentioned in user description)
- QA: Question Answering

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SparkQuest addresses the gap in existing educational tools by combining audio-visual aids, an interactive environment, and bilingual support to engage young learners, especially in regions like Pakistan.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Two Modes: User Mode (guided learning, ask questions from content) and Evaluation Mode (test-based self-assessment).
- Question Answering in User Mode: Text-based and visual (images/videos) QA via RAG.
- Question Answering in Evaluation Mode: Dynamic question generation, answer evaluation, timed modules.
- Parental/Guardian Role: Monitor student progress and interactions, view conversation logs.
- Audio Input and Output: Process audio input and provide audio output.
- Dual Language Support: Urdu speech-to-text, text-to-speech, and context understanding.
- Database: Store user details, questions, answers, and track progress.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Primary Grade Students: Students (grades 1-5) who use the app for self-learning and guided learning.
- Parents/Guardians: Adults who oversee a student's learning and monitor activity.
- Developers: Responsible for building and maintaining the system.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Modes of the Web Application
FR1-001: The system shall provide a User Mode for learning assistance where students can upload materials and receive summaries/visualizations.
FR1-002: The system shall provide an Evaluation Mode where students can take quizzes (short-answer, MCQs) based on studied content.
FR1-003: The system shall allow parental controls for monitoring student progress and receiving activity logs.

3.2 Question Answering (QA) in User Mode
FR2-001: The system shall allow students to ask text-based questions related to uploaded/selected content and provide answers using RAG.
FR2-002: The system shall allow students to ask questions using visual content (videos, images) and provide answers based on analysis using RAG.

3.3 Question Answering in Evaluation Mode
FR3-001: The system shall provide an interface for students to enter Evaluation Mode and take quizzes.
FR3-002: The system shall generate questions dynamically, segmented by subject (English, Math, Science) and difficulty (easy, medium, hard).
FR3-003: The system shall evaluate student answers and provide instant feedback.
FR3-004: The system shall allow for timed assessments where students are given a set time to complete the test.

3.4 Parental/Guardian Role in Evaluation Mode
FR4-001: The system shall provide parents/guardians with access to a monitoring interface to view student progress and chatbot interactions.
FR4-002: The system shall generate and display complete logs of conversations between the student and the chatbot.

3.5 Audio Input and Output
FR5-001: The system shall allow students to ask questions using audio input, which will be processed to provide answers.
FR5-002: The system shall allow students to receive audio output responses.
FR5-003: The system shall provide default settings for managing audio input and output preferences.
FR5-004: The system shall integrate audio input/output functionality across all relevant modules.

3.6 Dual Language Support
FR6-001: The system shall support Urdu speech-to-text conversion.
FR6-002: The system shall provide Urdu text-to-speech functionality.
FR6-003: The system shall have context understanding capabilities for Urdu to English.
FR6-004: The system shall provide translations between Urdu and English.

3.7 Database
FR7-001: The system shall store detailed student profiles (ID, name, grade, guardian reference).
FR7-002: The system shall store parent/guardian details (ID, name, email/phone).
FR7-003: The system shall store conversation logs between the student and chatbot.
FR7-004: The system shall store all questions asked by the user and corresponding student answers.
FR7-005: The system shall store all uploaded content (text, images, videos, PDFs) with metadata.
FR7-006: The system shall generate and store evaluation reports (test results, scores, feedback).

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Usability
NFR1-001: The system shall provide an intuitive interface, with 90% of users aged 6-10 navigating with minimal guidance.
NFR4-001: The system shall comply with WCAG 2.1 standards for accessibility.

4.2 Performance
NFR1-002: The system shall load content (text, images, videos, PDFs) within 3-4 seconds on average.
NFR5-002: The system shall process audio input and provide audio output responses within 4-6 seconds.
NFR2-003: The system shall provide answers within 3-5 seconds for typical queries using RAG.

4.3 Reliability
NFR1-003: The system shall ensure 99% uptime.
NFR7-002: The system shall ensure data integrity, with no data loss or corruption when storing and retrieving data.

4.4 Security
NFR3-001: The system shall use SQLite for securely storing student data.
NFR7-001: The system shall scale to handle up to 100,000 student and parent records.

4.5 Scalability
NFR2-002: The system shall handle up to 500 concurrent users without performance degradation.
NFR7-001: The system shall scale to handle up to 100,000 student and parent records.

4.6 Accuracy & Localization
NFR2-001: The system shall provide answers with at least 90% accuracy in text-based and visual question-answering.
NFR6-001: The system shall fully support both English and Urdu text, including right-to-left (RTL) support for Urdu.
NFR6-002: The system shall ensure at least 85% accuracy for Urdu and English speech-to-text and text-to-speech features.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: RoadInSight - Road Surface Detection Using Inexpensive Cell Phone Sensors
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for RoadInSight, a system that uses smartphone sensors (accelerometer, gyroscope, magnetometer, GPS) to detect road anomalies like potholes and bumps, providing a cost-effective alternative to specialized vehicles.

1.2 Scope
The project involves developing mobile and web applications. The mobile app collects data from smartphone sensors, visualizes it, and displays road surface condition/elevation maps. The web app visualizes this data and insights. Data collection is limited to main roads in Islamabad/Rawalpindi. A cloud-based inference module uses ML models (initially trained on collected data) for detection.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- GPS: Global Positioning System
- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- NHA: National Highway Authority

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
RoadInSight addresses the inefficiencies of manual road inspections and the high cost of specialized vehicles by leveraging crowdsourced data from common smartphones to monitor road conditions.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Mobile App: Data collection (sensors), user authentication, data aggregation, dashboard, real-time sensor graphs, road condition map, road elevation map.
- Web App: Visualization of road conditions and elevation on maps, user login, data dashboard.
- Cloud-Based Inference: Model training, cloud deployment, scheduled batch inference for anomaly detection.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Government Authorities (RDA, CDA, NHA): Monitor conditions, plan maintenance.
- Freight Companies: Avoid damage, optimize routes.
- Everyday Drivers: Avoid hazards, prevent damage.
- Emergency Responders: Plan fastest, safest routes.
- Tourists: Navigate unfamiliar roads safely.
- Data Contributors: Voluntarily share sensor data.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Mobile App Module
FR1-001: The system shall allow users to create accounts, log in, and manage their profiles.
FR1-002: The system shall aggregate sensor data from multiple users and store it in a central database.
FR1-003: The system shall provide a dashboard in the mobile app showing an overview of collected sensor data.
FR1-004: The system shall display real-time graphs of data from accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer sensors.
FR1-005: The system shall display a map highlighting road surface conditions based on collected data.
FR1-006: The system shall display a road elevation map.

3.2 Web App Module
FR2-001: The web app shall provide functionality for users to create accounts, login, and manage their profiles.
FR2-002: The system shall display a map on the web app visualizing road surface conditions.
FR2-003: The system shall display a map on the web app showing road elevation.
FR2-004: The system shall provide a dashboard offering insights and statistics from aggregated sensor data.

3.3 Cloud-Based Inference Module
FR3-001: The system shall train a machine learning model using user data to detect road anomalies (potholes, bumps, rough surfaces).
FR3-002: The system shall deploy the trained model to a cloud environment to perform inference on data.
FR3-003: The system shall schedule regular batch inferences using the trained model to analyze incoming data for road anomalies.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Accessibility
NFR1: The application shall be accessible with a responsive GUI across various devices (computers, tablets, mobile phones).

4.2 Adaptability
NFR2: The system shall employ techniques to improve its performance over time, aiming to reduce prediction errors by 10% annually.

4.3 Accuracy
NFR3: The system’s AI algorithm shall achieve an accuracy of at least 90% in predicting road surface conditions.

4.4 Availability
NFR4: The system shall maintain an uptime of 98% or above.

4.5 Response Time
NFR5: The system’s database response time shall be less than 1 second for data retrieval.

4.6 Data Security
NFR6: The system shall encrypt user data both in transit and at rest using industry-standard security protocols.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: AdvertEase - AI-Generated Poster and Video Advertisements
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for AdvertEase, a web application that automates the creation of poster and video advertisements using AI (LLM, image/video generation) to help businesses, especially small and middle-tier furniture businesses, market products easily and cost-effectively.

1.2 Scope
The system generates ads from a product URL. Features include web scraping, LLM script generation, poster image generation (with prompt-based editing), video ad generation with a human avatar (male/female) and enhanced emotional voiceover, and cloud deployment (AWS).

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- URL: Uniform Resource Locator
- AWS: Amazon Web Services

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
AdvertEase simplifies and accelerates the ad creation process, allowing businesses to input a product link and receive customized, compelling ads without needing specialized design or marketing teams.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- AI-generated poster ads from product URL (with web scraping, text preprocessing, LLM script generation, image generation).
- AI-generated video ads (LLM script, video generation via inpainting/control techniques, avatar generation, templates).
- Website development (MERN stack).
- Cloud deployment (AWS).

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- End User: Individuals/companies creating ads. They need an easy, fast process.
- Admin: Oversees platform operation, user management, system performance.
- Marketing Experts: Improve scripts, avatars, and templates.
- Developers: Maintain backend, deploy, integrate AI modules.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Authenticate User
FR1.1: The system shall provide a login page.
FR1.2: The system shall verify user credentials using a database.
FR1.3: The system shall grant access to the dashboard after a successful login.

3.2 Input Product URL
FR2.1: Users shall be able to enter a legitimate product URL.
FR2.2: The system shall verify the format of the entered URL.

3.3 Scrape Product Data
FR3.1: The system shall extract product data (photos, descriptions, features) from the given URL.
FR3.2: The system shall handle data scraping errors and alert the user.

3.4 Generate Poster Ads
FR4.1: The system shall use scraped product data to produce aesthetically appealing poster adverts.
FR4.2: Users shall be able to alter the poster advertisement's design aspects.
FR4.3: The user shall be given a preview of the generated poster advertisement.

3.5 Generate Advertisement Script
FR5.1: The system shall use product data to create an advertisement script.
FR5.2: Users shall be able to modify the generated script.

3.6 Edit Advertisement
FR6.1: The system shall allow users to edit the generated script and poster ad.
FR6.2: The system shall save user edits.

3.7 Select Avatar
FR7.1: The system shall let users choose a video ad avatar.
FR7.2: The system shall make a library of selectable avatars available.

3.8 Modify Script
FR8.1: After generation, users shall be able to edit the advertisement script.
FR8.2: The system shall validate the changes to ensure coherence.

3.9 Synthesize Speech from Script
FR9.1: The system shall employ text-to-speech technology to translate the commercial script into audio.

3.10 Enhance Emotional Tone in Speech
FR10.1: In accordance with the script's nature, the system shall make the synthesised speech sound more emotionally charged.

3.11 Generate Video Ads
FR11.1: The system shall combine the script, synthesised speech, and poster advertisement to produce a video advertisement.
FR11.2: The user shall be given a preview of the generated video.

3.12 Download Ads
FR12.1: Users shall be able to download produced video advertisements and posters in various formats.

3.13 Share Ads
FR13.1: Users shall have the ability to share created adverts on social media sites via system options.

3.14 Manage Users
FR14.1: The admin shall be able to view a list of registered users.
FR14.2: The admin shall be able to add, modify, and remove user accounts.
FR14.3: The system shall notify users when the administrator modifies their accounts.

3.15 Generate Reports
FR15.1: The system shall allow the administrator to provide reports on user behaviour and ad creation.
FR15.2: The system shall require customisation choices for report criteria (date range, user activity).
FR15.3: The admin shall be able to retrieve created reports in various forms (PDF, CSV).

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system must maintain an average MTBF of at least 1000 hours.
NFR2: Should a failure occur, the system will record the error and notify the administrator in five minutes.
NFR3: The system must have automated recovery methods to function again within two minutes.
NFR4: To guarantee data integrity, the system must regularly backup user data and configurations (once every 24 hours).

4.2 Usability
NFR5: The system must have an interface that lets users create advertising from the dashboard in only three clicks or less.
NFR6: The system will include tooltips and contextual help for all significant functions.
NFR7: With only one click, users will be able to access previously created advertisements from their user dashboard.
NFR8: The system must include accessibility features (keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility).

4.3 Performance
NFR9: The system will create a poster advertisement in no more than five seconds after the user enters the product URL and data.
NFR10: The system should be able to parse and scrape data from a product URL in 10 seconds.
NFR11: The system must guarantee that a video ad is created in a maximum of 5 minutes and poster ads in 10 seconds.
NFR12: The system must support a minimum of 100 concurrent users without compromising performance.

4.4 Security
NFR13: The system must encrypt all sensitive user data (login passwords, personal information) both in transit and at rest.
NFR14: Role-based access control (RBAC) will be implemented to limit access to various capabilities.
NFR15: The system must conduct routine security audits to find and fix vulnerabilities.
NFR16: The system must offer defence against SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF.
