SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: AutoEngage - AI-Powered Client Engagement and Sales Automation for Call Centers
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document describes the requirements for AutoEngage, an AI-powered client engagement and sales automation solution designed to replace human agents in call centers, managing high-volume client relations efficiently and cost-effectively.

1.2 Scope
The system automates call center operations using Speech-to-Text (STT), Large Language Models (LLMs) for response generation, and Text-to-Speech (TTS). It features an analytics dashboard for tracking performance and aims to increase client conversion rates and handle overseas clients across time zones.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- STT: Speech-to-Text
- TTS: Text-to-Speech
- LLM: Large Language Model
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
AutoEngage addresses the high costs, scalability issues, and management challenges of traditional call centers by providing a 24/7 automated AI agent that can handle diverse client interactions.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Speech to Text Model: Transcribes client speech.
- Intent Classification: Categorizes client intent.
- LLM Script Adaptability: Fine-tunes LLM, uses RAG for flexible script generation.
- Human-Like Speech Synthesis: Converts text responses to natural-sounding speech (male/female).
- Call Center Dashboard: Real-time analytics, voice recorder, API integration.
- Pipeline Integration and Testing: Integrates all components, conducts testing.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Call Centers: Organizations needing high-volume, cross-border client handling.
- Overseas Clients: Receive instant, appropriate responses despite time differences.
- Customer Support Teams: Use automation for common interactions, handle complex issues.
- Sales Teams: Automate initial client contact and lead generation.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Speech to Text Model and Basic UI
FR1: The system shall interface with an existing Speech-to-Text (STT) model to transcribe client speech.
FR2: The system must be operable via desktop and/or web interface with a microphone button.
FR3: The microphone button shall integrate with the STT model for real-time voice recognition.

3.2 Intent Classification Development
FR1: The system shall incorporate an intent classification model to categorize client input into pre-ordained categories.
FR2: The intent classification model shall assign the correct intent for each user's request.

3.3 LLM Script Adaptability and Fine-Tuning
FR1: The system must consist of a Large Language Model (LLM) suitable for call center use cases.
FR2: The selected LLM must be fine-tuned with call center type data.
FR3: The system shall integrate the LLM model with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for flexible dialogical interactions.

3.4 Human-Like Speech Synthesis
FR1: The system shall have a Text-to-Speech (TTS) synthesis model that generates human-like speech.
FR2: The TTS model must provide male and female voices that sound natural.

3.5 Call Center Dashboard and Application Integration
FR1: The system must offer a desktop/web application with a dashboard displaying call center numbers (calls, average length, satisfaction).
FR2: This application must integrate with a backend PostgreSQL database storing center statistics and client information.

3.6 Pipeline Integration and Testing
FR1: The whole process shall be linked to a desktop/web application.
FR2: There shall be automated UI testing to ensure proper operation.
FR3: The system shall be manually tested for different call scenarios.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system should have an uptime of 99.9% with no more than 5 minutes of downtime per month.
NFR2: Should there be system failure, recovery processes must be automated, and the system should come back online within one hour.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Operators managing the AI should have access to a clear, intuitive dashboard for viewing ongoing calls, metrics, and real-time issues.

4.3 Performance
NFR4: The STT model should maintain an accuracy rate of 90% or higher.
NFR5: The dashboard must load and display real-time call analytics data within 10 seconds of request.
NFR6: The TTS model should generate speech rated at least 4/5 in terms of naturalness.
NFR7: Intent classification model should correctly classify user intent 95% of the time.
NFR8: The fine-tuned LLM should generate responses in line with user intent or script with 95% accuracy.

4.4 Security
NFR9: Only authorized users should access system features, and all data should be securely stored.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Salon360 - All-in-One Salon Management and Virtual Hair Try-On System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for Salon360, a web application that integrates a virtual hair try-on system (hairstyles and colors) with a comprehensive salon management system.

1.2 Scope
The system consists of two main modules: Management Module (Admin Web App) for salon operations (POS, employee/customer records, bookings, automated deal generation, email reminders) and User Module (Client Web App) for virtual try-ons (hairstyles, hair colors, recommendations based on face shape) and booking integration.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- POS: Point of Sale
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- 2D: Two-Dimensional
- CRUD: Create, Read, Update, Delete

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Salon360 combines realistic virtual hair try-ons with backend salon management to create an efficient and unified experience for both salons and their clients.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Management Module: POS and management system, automated deal generation, email reminders for bookings.
- User Module: Hair color try-on (basic to advanced), hairstyle try-on, facial feature-based recommendations.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Salon Clients: Use virtual try-on and recommendations before booking.
- Salon Owners: Control managerial operations (bookings, employees, transactions) and integrate the system.
- Salon Employees (Receptionists, Stylists): Use management/POS system and virtual try-on to assist customers.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Management Module (Admin Web App)
FR1-001: The system shall provide login and signup authentication for employees and managers.
FR1-002: The system shall provide the admin with CRUD functionality to manage salon operations (customers, orders, bookings, employees, services) via a centralized database.
FR1-003: The system shall provide customers with a set of available services to select from and book appointments.
FR1-004: The system shall provide management staff with the functionality of processing customer orders and generating invoices.
FR1-005: The system shall provide the admin an option to generate monthly deals based on trending/most frequently purchased services.
FR1-006: The system shall generate and send an email 24 hours prior to a customer's upcoming booking as a reminder.

3.2 User Module (Client Web App)
FR2-001: The system shall allow users to upload a clear image without obstructions on face and hair.
FR2-002: The system shall allow users to virtually try on different hairstyles.
FR2-003: The system shall allow users to virtually try on different hair colors (basic to advanced shades like highlights).
FR2-004: The system shall analyze the uploaded image to provide personalized hairstyle recommendations based on facial features.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The recommendation system's success rate should be more than 90%.

4.2 Usability
NFR2: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface for clients to use the virtual try-on and booking system.

4.3 Performance
NFR3: The output of the virtual try-ons should be less than 10 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR4: The system shall protect user data by encryption (AES-256).
NFR5: The system shall provide user login functionality to ensure only authentic users have access.

4.5 Availability
NFR6: The system must be available to its users 100% of the time for a seamless flow.

4.6 Maintainability
NFR7: The source code must be modular and scalable with proper documentation.

4.7 Compatibility
NFR8: The system design must be compatible across multiple devices and browsers.

4.8 Scalability
NFR9: The system must be scalable to expand the user database and ensure a seamless experience during heavy traffic.

4.9 Accessibility
NFR10: The system must be accessible to all individuals, complying with standards to cater to individuals with disabilities.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Kisan Rabta - Agricultural Data Collection and Analysis Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for Kisan Rabta, a system that collects, processes, and analyzes agricultural data from Urdu-speaking farmers in real-time, enabling field workers to record conversations and providing actionable insights through a web interface.

1.2 Scope
The system includes a mobile app for field agents to record conversations with farmers (Urdu). Recordings are transcribed and analyzed using ML models to extract agricultural data (crop diseases, problems). An admin web interface allows querying, filtering, and visualizing this data on maps and dashboards.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- ML: Machine Learning
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- UI: User Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Kisan Rabta bridges the gap between farmers and decision-makers by providing a real-time platform to collect, analyze, and visualize agricultural issues, ensuring farmers' voices are heard and actionable insights are derived.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Mobile App: Secure login, profile management, voice recording of conversations, upload integration, audio file management.
- Admin Web App: Secure login, admin panel for user management, data visualization tools (graphs, charts, maps), data filtering and querying.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Field Agent (App User): Collects data from farmers via audio recordings or manual entry, with geotagging.
- Admin (Administrator): Manages users, reviews collected data, utilizes web portal for visualization, filtering, and querying.
- Farmer (Farmer Participant): Shares data on agricultural issues during conversation with the field agent; does not interact with the app directly.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Voice Recording Interface
FR1.1: The system must include a voice recording interface for call center agents to capture conversations between farmers and them in Urdu language.
FR1.2: The system should enable agents to manually start and stop the recording during the conversation to avoid capturing unwanted conversation.
FR1.3: The recorded files should be kept securely in the system.

3.2 Audio-to-Text Conversion
FR2.1: The system will use machine learning models to automatically transcribe recorded Urdu audio conversations into text.
FR2.2: The transcription accuracy will be high to ensure minimum data loss.

3.3 Agricultural Data Extraction
FR3.1: The system will automatically extract crucial agricultural information from the transcribed text, such as crop diseases, problems, and treatment approaches.
FR3.2: The system must classify extracted data by crop type, disease name, and farmer area.
FR3.3: Extracted data must be retained in a database for future analysis and reporting.

3.4 Data Analysis and Insights
FR4.1: The system will evaluate the retrieved data to discover patterns, such as crop diseases, farmers' problems, and other agriculture-related information.
FR4.2: The system will provide insights depending on area, demographics, and crop kind.
FR4.3: The analysis must accommodate large-scale datasets for future scalability.

3.5 Data Visualization
FR5.1: The system must include a web-based dashboard with data visualization features such as graphs and charts.
FR5.2: Users may pick and filter data according to area, crop type, disease name, and time range.
FR5.3: The system must show visual representations of data trends, such as bar charts, to aid decision-making.

3.6 Admin Dashboard and Reporting
FR6.1: The system must have an admin interface that allows administrators to query data using various filters (region, crop type, farmer demographic).
FR6.2: Administrators must be able to create reports based on the filtered data.
FR6.3: The system must enable reports to be exported in formats such as PDF or CSV.

3.7 User Authentication and Roles
FR7.1: The system must offer a login interface for all users (agents, administrators).
FR7.2: The system must allow role-based access control.
FR7.3: Users will be authorized using their login and password.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1.1: The system must support concurrent use of up to 15 field agents and administrators without significant degradation.
NFR1.2: Audio-to-text transcription should be processed within 5 minutes of uploading, even with large audio files.
NFR1.3: The system must handle storage and processing of large datasets, ensuring data retrieval queries for visualization take time between 30 - 60 seconds.

4.2 Scalability
NFR2.1: The system must be scalable to accommodate future growth, supporting up to 25 field agents.

4.3 Security
NFR3.1: The system must implement role-based access control (RBAC) with strict authorization checks.
NFR3.2: User authentication must be strong and include multi-factor authentication for administrators.

4.4 Usability
NFR4.1: The mobile and web interfaces must be user-friendly and intuitive, requiring minimal training.

4.5 Data Integrity
NFR5.1: The system must ensure that all data (audio recordings, transcriptions, reports) is stored and maintained in its correct form.

4.6 Maintainability
NFR6.1: The system architecture must allow for easy updates and maintenance with minimal downtime.
NFR6.2: Codebase should follow modular architecture patterns to allow individual components to be updated independently.

4.7 Compliance
NFR7.1: All audio recordings must adhere to consent-based data collection practices, with appropriate notifications provided to farmers before recording.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: PenTrack - Football Penalty Kick Analysis and Goalkeeper Simulation
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for PenTrack, a system that analyzes a football striker's body pose during penalty kicks using pose estimation and machine learning to predict shot outcomes and simulate a goalkeeper's reaction.

1.2 Scope
The system analyzes user-uploaded penalty footage using pose estimation (OpenPose/MediaPipe) to extract key body points. An LSTM model predicts shot direction and power. A 3D simulation then shows a pre-trained goalkeeper animation reacting to the predicted shot. The system includes a user-friendly mobile/web interface for uploading videos and viewing results.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- LSTM: Long Short-Term Memory
- CNN: Convolutional Neural Network
- UI/UX: User Interface/User Experience
- 3D: Three-Dimensional

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
PenTrack helps players, especially in regions with limited access to high-level training, improve their penalty kick technique by providing precise, pose-based insights and a realistic training environment.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Collection: Gather video data (YouTube, SoccerNet).
- Model Training: Process pose data, train/fine-tune LSTM model, validate performance.
- App Development: UI/UX design, application development, model/simulation integration.
- Simulation: Set up 3D environment, render goalkeeper animations based on model predictions.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Player: Individual wanting to improve penalty kicks using the app individually or with a trainer.
- Coaches: Use the app to analyze player posture and shot outcomes to give personalized feedback.
- Football Clubs: Standardize training, manage player analytics.
- Researchers: Use the app to learn workflow and bring further innovation to sports analysis.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Video Upload & Preprocessing
FR1: The app shall allow users to upload their recorded penalty kick videos.
FR2: The app shall preprocess uploaded videos to extract relevant frames for analysis.
FR3: The app shall validate uploaded video format and size to ensure compatibility.

3.2 Pose Estimation Module
FR1: The app shall extract body pose keypoints from the video frames.
FR2: The app shall store the extracted keypoints for subsequent processing.

3.3 Prediction Module
FR1: The app shall process video data to make predictions about shot outcomes (shot direction and power).
FR2: The app shall validate the predictions to ensure consistent results.

3.4 Simulation Module
FR1: The app shall render simulation results based on the model's predictions.
FR2: The app shall simulate the interaction between the penalty shot and a goalkeeper model reacting to the predicted outcome.
FR3: The app shall provide a visual representation of the goalkeeper's reaction alongside the simulation results.

3.5 App Module
FR1: The app shall provide a user-friendly interface for users to navigate through the app.
FR2: The app shall allow users to easily upload videos and view simulation results.
FR3: The app shall ensure that the insights provided on penalty kicks are easy to understand for non-technical users.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The app shall ensure that prediction results for different videos exhibit a consistency rate of at least 95% across multiple tests.
NFR2: The app shall guarantee that data is saved reliably, with a data retention rate of 99.9%.
NFR3: The app shall generate accurate simulations, achieving an accuracy rate of at least 80% when comparing predicted outcomes to actual outcomes based on historical data.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: At least 90% of users shall complete key tasks (uploading a video, viewing results) without assistance during usability testing.
NFR5: The insights provided on penalty kicks shall be rated as "easy to understand" or higher by at least 80% of users in post-interaction surveys.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: The model should be efficient enough to provide the penalty results within (10-20) seconds for a player's uploaded video.
NFR7: Users should be able to upload their videos successfully within 20 seconds.
NFR8: The simulation should run smoothly on at least 85% of devices without buffering or lag.

4.4 Security
NFR9: User's personal data (uploaded videos, insights) should be stored securely and only made visible to authenticated users.
NFR10: The app should ensure no unnecessary data is saved for enhanced security.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: ProbeXpert
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Small to medium-sized enterprises lack resources and expertise to implement efficient security controls, struggling to safeguard external assets due to complexity, cost, and resource-intensive nature of existing external scanning and vulnerability assessment solutions.

1.2 Scope
ProbeXpert is an automated, web-based platform for external asset scanning, vulnerability assessment, and compliance reporting for SMEs. Features include automated external scanning, real-time progress tracking, IP reputation evaluation, self-healing architecture, risk assessments, compliance reports, and dynamic updates as new risks emerge.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- SME: Small to Medium-sized Enterprise
- IP: Internet Protocol
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
ProbeXpert simplifies cybersecurity management for resource-constrained organizations through a user-friendly interface that identifies and ranks vulnerabilities in assets like IP addresses and domains.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- On-demand scans of external systems
- Customizable scan parameters (Deep, Shallow, Individual)
- Environmental variables analysis for risk assessment
- Risk factor calculation (probability of occurrence, impact)
- Quantitative score categorization
- Vulnerability prioritization
- Live scan results with completion percentage
- Alerts and notifications
- Detailed vulnerability reports with risk score visualization
- Exportable reports (PDF)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- System Administrator: Manage and configure platform, push new plugins, manage user access
- System (Plugin): Interact with external/internal APIs, ensure version tracking
- Website User: Manage infrastructure, perform scans, respond to vulnerabilities

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, Docker-based worker nodes, Kubernetes for load balancing.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 External Asset Scanning
FR1.1: Users shall initiate on-demand scans on IP addresses and domain names.
FR1.2: Users shall select from three scan types (Deep, Shallow, Individual) with customization.
FR1.3: Users shall add or remove specific scanning tools to tailor scans.
FR1.4: System shall generate list of detected vulnerabilities with severity levels.

3.2 Risk Factor Calculations & Vulnerability Detection
FR2.1: System shall calculate risk factor for each vulnerability based on environmental variables.
FR2.2: System shall categorize vulnerabilities based on quantitative score (severity, likelihood, impact).
FR2.3: System shall prioritize vulnerabilities, displaying most critical first.
FR2.4: Users shall trigger new scans to detect recent vulnerabilities and update risk scores.

3.3 Real-Time Progress Tracking
FR3.1: System shall provide real-time updates on active scan progress (percentage completion).
FR3.2: System shall display estimated completion times based on scan type and progress.
FR3.3: System shall send alerts and notifications when scans complete or errors occur.

3.4 Reporting
FR4.1: System shall generate detailed vulnerability reports including risk factors.
FR4.2: System shall offer risk score visualizations (bar charts, pie charts, heatmaps).
FR4.3: System shall provide summary of organization's overall risk posture.
FR4.4: System shall allow users to export reports in PDF format.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: Data integrity shall be preserved; no scan results lost in critical failure.

4.2 Readability
NFR2: Reports shall be understandable by IT team members and individuals with minimum IT knowledge.

4.3 Usability
NFR3: Tooltips and on-screen help shall reduce need for extensive training.
NFR4: System shall provide feedback for user actions within 2 seconds.
NFR5: Built-in trained chatbot shall answer user queries.

4.4 Performance
NFR6: Reports for completed scans shall generate within 10 seconds after scan concludes.
NFR7: Kubernetes shall provide automatic load balancing for best resource distribution.

4.5 Security
NFR8: Role-based access control (RBAC) with minimum 3 user roles (Administrator, Analyst, Viewer).
NFR9: All services shall operate independently in Docker containers for fault isolation.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SmoothFlow - Speech Therapy Assistant for People Who Stutter
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for SmoothFlow, a web-based application that assists English-speaking individuals with speech disfluencies (stuttering) through AI-powered detection, classification, and gamified speech therapy exercises, connecting them with Speech Language Pathologists (SLPs) as needed.

1.2 Scope
The system provides detection and multi-label classification of speech disfluencies (blocks, prolongations, repetitions). It offers four gamified speech therapy exercises (Reading Passage, Matching Syllables, Sound Mimic, Breathing Control). Features include establishing a baseline, progress tracking with feedback reports (PDF export), SLP consultation (text/video), and an information center (chatbot) about stuttering.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- SLP: Speech Language Pathologist
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- BPM: Beats Per Minute
- XP: Experience Points

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SmoothFlow provides a robust digital solution for individuals who stutter, offering science-backed exercises, AI analysis, and professional connection to improve speech fluency in a structured, motivating (gamified) environment.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Detection and Multi-label Classification: Detect and classify speech disfluencies in patient audio.
- Speech Therapy Exercises: Reading Passage, Matching Syllables, Sound Mimic, Breathing Control.
- Baseline of the Patient: Create profile, establish normal rate of speech.
- Progress Tracking and Feedback Reports: Provide feedback after exercises, view history, export reports (PDF).
- SLP Consultation: Connect with SLPs via text/video, view patient reports.
- Information Centre: Chatbot to answer questions about speech disfluencies and therapy.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Person Who Stutters (PWS): Primary end-user who uses the app to improve speech fluency.
- Speech Language Pathologist (SLP): Provides guidance and support to patients via consultation and monitoring.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Detection and Multi-label Classification of Patient's Speech
FR1.0.0: The system shall detect the presence of speech disfluency in the recorded audio of the patient.
FR1.1.0: The system shall recommend relevant speech therapy exercises based on the classification of the patient's speech.

3.2 Speech Therapy Exercises
FR2.0.0: Reading Passage Exercise: A passage shall be generated by LLM. The patient shall read it while their speech is recorded and transcribed.
FR2.0.1: Custom Passage Generation via LLM: The system generates a custom passage based on the patient's selected difficulty.
FR2.1.0: Matching Syllables Exercise: The patient selects a challenging word and practices pronouncing it with spaced-out syllables using a metronome.
FR2.1.1: BPM Syllable Practice: The patient shall be able to adjust the BPM of the metronome (options: 64, 92, 118, 144).
FR2.2.0: Sound Mimic: The patient listens to a pre-recorded sentence and then repeats it; the system records and transcribes their speech.
FR2.3.0: Breathing Control: The system displays a breathing animation; the patient follows it, then reads a sentence for recording and transcription.

3.3 Baseline of the Patient
FR3.0.0: The system shall provide a set of passages for the patient to read (starting high complexity, becoming simpler) to establish baseline.
FR3.0.1: Once the system detects no more speech disfluencies, it shall calculate the patient's normal rate of speech and store it.

3.4 Progress Tracking and Feedback Reports
FR4.0.0: After every speech therapy exercise, an analysis shall be conducted on the patient's speech to check disfluency severity.
FR4.1.0: Before every speech therapy exercise, an analysis shall be conducted to measure current state (read a passage).
FR4.2.0: Based on pre and post analysis, the system shall generate a feedback report (performance, difficulty, improvement, time, badges, XP).
FR4.3.0: Analysis on the patient's speech after completing the reading passage exercise (compare transcription with original passage).
FR4.4.0: Analysis on the patient's speech after completing the matching syllable exercise (compare pronounced syllables with actual word).
FR4.5.0: Analysis on the patient's speech after completing the sound mimic exercise (compare transcription with original).
FR4.6.0: Analysis on the patient's speech after completing the breathing exercise (provide reading passage, transcribe, analyze).

3.5 SLP Consultation
FR5.0.0: The patient shall be able to contact an SLP registered on the system through text message.

3.6 Information Centre
FR6.0.0: The patient can use the chatbot to ask questions about speech therapy and speech disfluencies; it will provide answers related only to these topics.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have a 99 percent uptime to ensure availability.
NFR2: The system shall have backups to make sure data is not lost in case of failure.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The system shall provide a clear and intuitive user interface for easy navigation.
NFR4: The system shall display instructions before each exercise.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: The system shall have a response time of less than 4 seconds.
NFR6: The system shall process and analyze speech audio within 10 seconds.
NFR7: The system shall be able to support up to 500 users.

4.4 Security
NFR8: The system shall use encryption while storing critical information in the database.
NFR9: The system shall have user authentication and authorization.
NFR10: The system shall have input validation.
NFR11: The system shall have role-based access control for administration.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SmartGuard - AI-Based Shoplifting Detection System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
According to National Retail Federation (NFR), retail theft recorded in 2017 was over $112 billion, projected to rise above $140 billion by 2025. Traditional CCTV surveillance systems are ineffective as humans are prone to failures, making it hard for retailers to respond in time.

1.2 Scope
In-Scope: Identify shoplifting behaviors including picking a product from shelf, examining a product, putting product back, placing product in bag/pocket/clothing without paying. Out-of-Scope: Physical aggression, property damage, verbal aggression, consumption of products in-store. Facial recognition requires clear visibility of face.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures
- AES: Advanced Encryption Standard
- NFR: National Retail Federation

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SmartGuard automates detection of shoplifting activities and alerts store management using computer vision to track behaviors in real-time.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Live feed monitoring
- Suspicious behavior detection
- Alert management
- Facial recognition
- Centralized watchlist across multiple stores
- Customer visit tracking
- Incident reporting and analytics
- Image-based search
- Store management

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Store Managers: Receive real-time alerts, access reports via mobile/web
- Store Employees: Monitor camera feed, respond to alerts
- System Administrators: Handle system, add/remove stores, provide technical support
- CCTV Technicians: Ensure integration, handle hardware maintenance

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile and web applications, Android devices (version 10+), modern browsers.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Real Time Monitoring and Detection
FR1.1: The system shall continuously monitor connected cameras for suspicious behavior.
FR1.2: The system shall detect suspicious behavior (picking item from shelf, putting in bag).
FR1.3: The system shall send notification to store management when suspicious behavior detected.

3.2 Facial Recognition and Centralized Watchlist
FR2.1: The system shall perform facial recognition on detected persons.
FR2.2: The system shall compare detected faces with common watchlist to detect known offenders.
FR2.3: The watchlist shall be accessible by all stores.
FR2.4: The system shall send notification when known shoplifter detected.

3.3 Customer Insights and Analytics
FR3.1: The system shall record number of customers visiting the store.
FR3.2: The system shall log shoplifting incidents (date, time, store location).

3.4 Advance Search and Store Management
FR4.1: The system shall allow store management to search a person by image-based input.
FR4.2: The system shall allow store managers to manage multiple stores from single interface.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 1,000 hours of operation.
NFR2: Data integrity shall be ensured during failures (no loss of recorded data, logs, alerts).

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Core functions accessible with ≤ 3 clicks.
NFR4: Users shall operate system after ≤ 2 hours of training.
NFR5: Mobile app compatibility with Android versions 10 and above.
NFR6: Web interface compatibility with Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: Live video feed latency < 8 seconds.
NFR8: Suspicious activity detection ≤ 10 seconds of event capture.
NFR9: System shall handle up to 50 concurrent users without noticeable lag.
NFR10: Facial recognition searches ≤ 5 seconds against watchlist.
NFR11: Alerts sent ≤ 10 seconds after detecting suspicious behavior.

4.4 Security
NFR12: Data encrypted at rest and in transit using ≥ 256-bit AES encryption.
NFR13: Account locked for 10 minutes after 3 failed login attempts.
NFR14: User actions logged with logs stored for 1 year.

4.5 Scalability
NFR15: System shall handle up to 2 stores, each with up to 3 cameras.
NFR16: Cloud infrastructure shall auto-scale when CPU utilization exceeds 70%.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Digi-Guard - Parental Control Application
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for Digi-Guard, a parental control mobile application that enables parents to manage and track their children's digital activities, providing real-time monitoring, location tracking, app restrictions, and screen time management.

1.2 Scope
To build a platform for parents to control and safeguard their child's digital presence. The app will block/limit indecent content, monitor real-time device usage, track location, and allow remote control of device features (e.g., microphone, camera) and keylogging. The scope is defined by functions like app restrictions, screen time management, location tracking, and real-time monitoring.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- GPS: Global Positioning System
- SOS: Emergency signal
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Digi-Guard is a comprehensive mobile solution for parents to ensure their children's online safety by providing detailed, real-time insights and controls.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Real-time device monitoring (live screen, activity logs, keylogging)
- Location tracking (GPS-based tracking, geofencing)
- App restrictions (app blocking, time-based restrictions)
- Screen time management (daily/weekly limits, automatic locking)
- Remote controls (remote device locking, app enable/disable)
- Notifications and alerts (geofence, screen time alerts)
- Emergency SOS feature (SOS button with location sharing)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Parent: Primary user who wants to monitor and control their child's device usage. May vary in tech-savviness.
- Child: Secondary user whose device is monitored. Has limited access, primarily receiving alerts.

2.4 Operating Environment
Android mobile application communicating with a backend service (Firebase/Google Maps API).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Real-Time Device Monitoring
FR1.1: The application shall allow the parent to see a live feed of the child's screen.
FR1.2: The application shall provide real-time indications of active applications.
FR1.3: The application shall log the child's app usage for review.
FR1.4: The application shall allow parents to access the activity log and review app usage history.
FR1.5: The application shall log keystrokes and provide reports to the parent.

3.2 Module 2: Location Tracking Feature
FR2.1: The application shall display the child's real-time location on a map.
FR2.2: The application shall use GPS to provide exact location tracking.
FR2.3: The application shall allow parents to define geofencing areas (safe zones).
FR2.4: The application shall notify the parent if the child leaves or enters a geofenced area.

3.3 Module 3: App Restrictions
FR3.1: The application shall allow parents to block specific applications on the child's device.
FR3.2: The application shall implement time-based restrictions for specific apps.
FR3.3: The application shall notify in real-time if the child attempts to use a restricted app.
FR3.4: The application shall record each blocked attempt for the parent to view.

3.4 Module 4: Screen Time Management
FR4.1: The application shall allow setting daily or weekly limits on screen time.
FR4.2: The application shall automatically lock the child's device when the screen time limit is exceeded.
FR4.3: The application shall provide an overview of the child's screen usage (daily/weekly).
FR4.4: The application shall alert the parent when the child approaches or exceeds the limit.

3.5 Module 5: Remote Controls
FR5.1: The application shall allow parents to remotely lock the child's device.
FR5.2: The application shall allow parents to remotely enable or disable specific applications.
FR5.3: The application shall notify the parent when a remote control action is activated.
FR5.4: The application shall log all remote control operations.

3.6 Module 6: Notifications and Alerts
FR6.1: The application shall send geofence alerts when the child leaves or returns to a safe area.
FR6.2: The application shall send screen time alerts when the child approaches or exceeds the limit.
FR6.3: The application shall alert the parent if the child breaches app restrictions or screen time limits.
FR6.4: The application shall provide a notification history.

3.7 Module 7: Emergency SOS Feature
FR7.1: The application shall provide an SOS button that, when pressed, sends an emergency signal with GPS coordinates to the parent.
FR7.2: The system shall automatically send the child's location to the parent at a specified interval (e.g., every minute) upon SOS activation.
FR7.3: The system shall display the child's location on a map in real-time upon SOS receipt.
FR7.4: The application shall automatically cease sending location updates after the parent reports the emergency is resolved.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall have an MTBF of at least 6 months.
NFR2: The system must automatically detect and classify failures (minor/major). For major failures, the parent shall be notified immediately.
NFR3: In case of a major failure, the system shall attempt to restart affected services within 10 seconds.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: First-time users shall accomplish simple tasks (e.g., setting screen time limits) within 10 minutes.
NFR5: Key features must be accessible from the home screen within 3 taps.
NFR6: The system shall confirm all critical operations (e.g., locking the device).
NFR7: The app shall display clear error messages and offer a way to correct them.
NFR8: The app shall allow parents to set app restrictions within 1 minute.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR9: Real-time monitoring latency shall be within 3 seconds.
NFR10: Location data shall be retrieved and displayed within 3 seconds, with accuracy within a 10-meter radius under optimal conditions.
NFR11: Geofencing alerts shall be sent within 5 seconds after the child crosses a boundary.
NFR12: App restrictions or device locks shall be enforced within 5 seconds of the parent's command.
NFR13: Data sync and backup shall occur every 15 minutes and complete within 2 minutes.
NFR14: Notifications and alerts shall be delivered within 5 seconds of the trigger event.
NFR15: The app shall load completely and be ready for use within 5 seconds.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR16: The system shall allow access only to authorized users, locking accounts after 5 failed login attempts.
NFR17: Sensitive data (location, activity logs) shall be stored and transmitted securely.
NFR18: Data related to a child shall only be accessible by the authenticated parent.
NFR19: Backup data shall be encrypted and require high-level authentication for access.

Project: CryptoTradeBot - AI-Driven Cryptocurrency Trading Bot
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the research and development of CryptoTradeBot, an AI-driven trading bot designed to assist retail investors in navigating the volatile cryptocurrency market by providing real-time, emotionless trade execution based on data-driven insights.

1.2 Scope
To develop an AI-powered cryptocurrency trading bot that integrates historical market data, social media sentiment analysis, and news trends. The bot will use machine learning algorithms (LSTM, Random Forest) to provide real-time insights, predictions, and automated trade execution. It will offer a dashboard for performance monitoring and support user-defined trading parameters.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- ML: Machine Learning
- LSTM: Long Short-Term Memory
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- API: Application Programming Interface
- ROI: Return on Investment

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
CryptoTradeBot is a software system that acts as an intelligent trading assistant for retail investors, bridging the gap between advanced analytical tools and retail trading needs.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User registration and authentication
- Real-time dashboard with cryptocurrency prices, trends, and bot performance
- AI model predictions based on technical analysis and sentiment analysis (from social media)
- Automated trade execution via exchange APIs
- Performance monitoring and analytics (ROI, win rate, profit/loss)
- Real-time notifications for trade execution, price alerts, etc.
- Integration with market data and social media APIs

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Retail Investors: The primary users who rely on the bot for data-driven trading support to reduce emotional trading. They have varying technical skills.
- Developers/System Maintainers: Responsible for building, maintaining, and updating the bot's architecture and models.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application, with backend services for data processing and AI model inference.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Management
FR1.1: New users shall be able to sign up using an email address and password.
FR1.2: Users shall be able to log in with their credentials and recover a forgotten password.

3.2 Dashboard
FR2.1: The dashboard shall present real-time cryptocurrency prices, market trends, and bot performance metrics.
FR2.2: It shall display historical price charts, technical indicators, and sentiment analysis results.

3.3 Trading Parameters
FR3.1: Users shall be able to set trading parameters including risk tolerance and investment amount.

3.4 AI Model Predictions
FR4.1: The system shall generate cryptocurrency price movement predictions using ML models (LSTM, Random Forest).
FR4.2: The system shall analyze real-time social media sentiment and integrate this into price predictions.

3.5 Automated Trading Execution
FR5.1: The system shall automatically execute buy and sell orders based on AI model predictions.
FR5.2: It shall integrate with third-party trading APIs (e.g., Binance, OKX) for executing trades.

3.6 Performance Monitoring and Analytics
FR6.1: The system shall provide comprehensive analytics on bot performance, including ROI, win rate, and total profit/loss.

3.7 Notification and Alert System
FR7.1: Users shall receive real-time notifications for critical events such as executed trades, price alerts, and significant market changes.

3.8 Real-Time Data Integration
FR8.1: The system shall integrate with market data APIs to fetch real-time price, volume, and other indicators.
FR8.2: It shall fetch and analyze sentiment data from social media platforms like Twitter.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance Requirements
NFR1: The system shall update market data and bot performance quickly, with minimal delays.
NFR2: Key actions like executing trades or fetching data shall happen in near real-time.

4.2 Scalability Requirements
NFR3: The system shall be built to handle moderate increases in user traffic and data.
NFR4: It shall be easy to expand with more data sources or new features.

4.3 Security Requirements
NFR5: Basic security, like user login and access control, shall be in place to protect user data.
NFR6: Sensitive information (passwords, API keys) shall be encrypted or stored securely.

4.4 Reliability Requirements
NFR7: The system shall run smoothly without crashes during demos and testing.
NFR8: Errors shall be handled gracefully with clear user messages and proper error logging.

4.5 Usability Requirements
NFR9: The interface shall be simple and intuitive, allowing easy navigation for core tasks.
NFR10: The design shall be user-friendly, making it easy for beginners to use.

4.6 Maintainability Requirements
NFR11: The code shall be well-organized, commented, and documented for easy modification or extension.

4.7 Compliance Requirements
NFR12: The system shall follow basic data privacy rules.

4.8 Interoperability Requirements
NFR13: The system shall work seamlessly with existing crypto exchanges and APIs.
NFR14: It shall be easy to connect with new APIs or data sources in the future.

Project: PulseViz - AI-Powered ECG Analysis for Heart Attack Detection
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document provides insight into the implementation and documentation of PulseViz, an AI-powered mobile application to analyze ECG scans and detect heart attacks in real-time, aiming to improve timely diagnosis and treatment in Pakistan.

1.2 Scope
To develop an AI-powered mobile application that automates ECG readings to detect heart attacks. It will generate personalized health reports, provide real-time alerts to healthcare professionals, and store patient data securely in the cloud, all with a user-friendly interface for medical staff.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- ECG: Electrocardiogram
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- SSN: Social Security Number (used as patient ID)

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
PulseViz is a mobile-based system designed to assist healthcare providers in hospitals by reducing diagnostic delays and enhancing the detection of heart attacks through automated ECG analysis.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- ECG scanning via camera (capture or upload)
- Heart attack detection using deep learning algorithms
- Personalized health report generation
- Real-time notifications and alerts for emergencies
- Viewing patient history and previous records
- Cloud-based data management and storage

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Nurse: Experienced in patient care, possesses basic technical knowledge, uses the app to scan images, view results, and save reports.
- Doctor: Experienced in healthcare, uses the app for scanning, viewing results, and receiving emergency alerts. Requires fast and efficient performance.

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application (likely Android/iOS) with cloud backend for data storage and processing.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: ECG Scanning
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to take pictures of ECG scans using the mobile camera within the app interface.
FR1.2: The system shall enable users to upload images of ECG scans from their mobile gallery.

3.2 Module 2: Heart Attack Detection
FR2.1: The system shall process the given ECG images and pass them to the deep learning model for detection.
FR2.2: The system shall analyze the processed images using a deep learning model to detect occurrences of heart attacks.

3.3 Module 3: Personalized Health Reports
FR3.1: The system shall display the results of analysis quickly, indicating whether a heart attack is detected.
FR3.2: The system shall generate a detailed report based on the detection and allow users to save it.

3.4 Module 4: Generate Notifications and Results
FR4.1: In case of a heart attack, the system shall automatically notify relevant doctors, sending them a report with the patient's location and scan results.

3.5 Module 5: View Patient History
FR5.1: The system shall let users store each patient's report against their respective ID (SSN) for future retrieval.
FR5.2: The system shall allow doctors to view the medical history of the patient using their ID.

3.6 Module 6: Cloud Integration and Data Management
FR6.1: The system shall allow nurses or doctors to register and log in.
FR6.2: The system shall allow users to edit their account information or delete their account.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall have an MTBF of at least 500 hours.
NFR2: In case of failure (app crash preventing scanning/detection), the system shall restart within 10 minutes to ensure smooth operation.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR3: The system shall allow users to scan images and view results within just 3 interactions.
NFR4: The system will confirm with the user before sending images for processing.
NFR5: In case of upload failure, the system shall inform the user within 10 seconds.
NFR6: The system shall allow doctors to view a patient's history with just 3 interactions.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR7: 95% of the time, the system shall generate a report within 10 seconds of submission under normal load.
NFR8: 99% of the time, the system shall notify doctors in case of emergencies within 3 seconds of report generation (given stable internet).
NFR9: The system shall maintain an uptime of 99.5% over a 30-day period.
NFR10: The system shall retrieve previous records within 3 seconds.
NFR11: The system shall handle 100 concurrent users with minimal response delay.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR12: The system shall only allow access to registered users.
NFR13: The system shall enforce role-based restrictions to ensure only desired people access private data.
NFR14: In case of 5 or more failed login attempts within 5 minutes, the system shall lock the system on that device.
NFR15: The system shall maintain logs to track activity.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: VirtuLectra - AI-Powered Interactive Online Tutoring Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document collects, analyzes, and defines the high-level needs and features of VirtuLectra, a web-based application designed to lessen the burden on teachers and provide interactive, engaging lectures for students.

1.2 Scope
To deliver a comprehensive AI-powered educational platform that supports real-time interaction, automated content creation, and student monitoring. It aims to reduce teacher workload by automating tasks like content generation and grading, while enhancing student engagement through real-time feedback.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- MCQ: Multiple Choice Question
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
VirtuLectra is a web-based platform that acts as an AI teaching assistant, automating course material creation and providing interactive learning experiences for students.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Real-time interaction (voice exchanges, Q&A sessions)
- Automated content creation (lecture scripts/notes, tailored quizzes)
- Student monitoring (engagement tracking via pop-up questions)
- Web portal for teachers (dashboards for management, review) and students (interface for learning materials, quizzes, lectures)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Teacher: Uses the platform to manage classrooms, generate content, and monitor student progress. Wants to decrease their workload.
- Student: Uses the platform to attend lectures, access materials, and participate in quizzes. Wants interactive and engaging learning.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application accessible via standard browsers.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Real-Time Interaction Module
FR1.1: Upon detecting voice input, the system shall process and convert it into text.
FR1.2: The system shall implement a time-out mechanism to stop listening after inactivity.
FR1.3: The system shall notify the student if voice input cannot be processed.
FR1.4: The system shall provide visual feedback when the microphone is activated.
FR1.5: The system shall convert the lecture script into synthesized speech.
FR1.6: The system shall deliver synthesized speech verbally to students.
FR1.7: The system shall convert generated text-based responses to student queries into synthesized speech.
FR1.8: The system shall ensure conversion occurs in real-time with minimal delay.
FR1.9: The system shall provide visual feedback when synthesized speech is delivered.
FR1.10: The system shall ensure synthesized speech outputs are clear.
FR1.11: The system shall detect and identify the main subject or topic of a student's question.
FR1.12: The system shall break down complex or multi-part questions into smaller components.
FR1.13: The system shall recognize key terms related to the ongoing lecture.
FR1.14: Based on identified topics, the system shall generate an appropriate answer using an LLM.
FR1.15: If a question is determined to be vague, the system shall prompt the student for clarification.
FR1.16: If a valid response cannot be generated, the system shall prompt the student to rephrase.
FR1.17: The system shall maintain a history of all questions asked during the lecture.

3.2 Automated Content Creation Module
FR2.1: The system shall extract key points and summaries for topics using an LLM.
FR2.2: The system shall structure extracted content into organized lecture notes.
FR2.3: The system shall automatically create headings for major sections.
FR2.4: The system shall use bullet points to list key points.
FR2.5: The system shall include numbering where appropriate.
FR2.6: Based on extracted content, the system shall generate a lecture script using an LLM.
FR2.7: The system shall provide smooth transitions between different sections or topics in the script.
FR2.8: The system shall format the script in a step-by-step manner.
FR2.9: The system shall ensure the script follows a logical flow from introduction to conclusion.
FR2.10: The system shall adjust the length and content of the script based on specified time duration.
FR2.11: Based on topics, the system shall create relevant MCQs using an LLM.
FR2.12: The system shall ensure the quiz covers a balanced mix of topics and key points.
FR2.13: For each MCQ, the system shall generate at least three plausible distractors.
FR2.14: The system shall ensure distractors are conceptually similar to the correct answer.
FR2.15: The system shall avoid overly obvious incorrect answers.
FR2.16: After quiz submission, the system shall compare the student's answers with correct answers.
FR2.17: Based on correct answers, the system shall calculate the total quiz score.
FR2.18: If necessary, the system shall allow teachers to manually override automated grades.

3.3 Student Monitoring Module
FR3.1: The system shall present pop-up questions to students periodically during live lectures.
FR3.2: The system shall allow the student to respond to pop-up questions.
FR3.3: The system shall log the student's response to pop-up questions.
FR3.4: The system shall calculate an engagement score based on response speed and accuracy.

3.4 Web Portal Module
FR4.1: The system shall allow users to register and log in.
FR4.2: The system shall verify email addresses during registration.
FR4.3: The system shall allow users to reset passwords via email.
FR4.4: The system shall allow users to log in using OAuth services.
FR4.5: The system shall allow users to update personal information.
FR4.6: The system shall allow users to delete their accounts permanently.
FR4.7: The system shall allow teachers to create a new classroom with a unique code.
FR4.8: The system shall allow teachers to update classroom information.
FR4.9: The system shall allow teachers to upload course material (PDF, DOCX) for AI processing.
FR4.10: The system shall allow teachers to specify topics or chapters within the material to focus on.
FR4.11: The system shall allow teachers to delete classrooms.
FR4.12: The system shall allow teachers to generate, edit, and delete quizzes.
FR4.13: The system shall allow teachers to access student records (quiz scores, participation).
FR4.14: The system shall allow teachers to schedule, reschedule, or cancel lectures.
FR4.15: The system shall allow students to join classrooms by entering a valid code.
FR4.16: The system shall allow students to leave a classroom.
FR4.17: The system shall provide students the ability to attend scheduled lectures.
FR4.18: The system shall present lecture notes during the lecture.
FR4.19: During lecture, the system shall allow students to request permission to speak.
FR4.20: When a student requests to speak, the system shall complete the current sentence before prompting.
FR4.21: The system shall offer the student to proceed with text or voice.
FR4.22: The system shall provide students the ability to take timed quizzes.
FR4.23: The system shall provide students feedback on quiz performance (scores, correct answers).
FR4.24: The system shall provide students access to past quizzes and scores.
FR4.25: The system shall notify students when a quiz is available.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall provide notification to administrators in case of downtime.
NFR2: The system shall ensure critical data is consistent and not lost during transfers.
NFR3: The system shall perform daily backups of all critical data.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The system shall provide a simple, intuitive user interface with self-explanatory elements.
NFR5: The UI shall be fully responsive across various devices and screen sizes.
NFR6: The system shall promptly acknowledge user actions with visual or auditory confirmation.
NFR7: The system shall provide access to a help section.
NFR8: The system shall communicate errors to users with actionable suggestions.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR9: User inputs (voice or text) shall be processed with minimal latency.
NFR10: Any dashboard page shall load in less than 3 seconds under normal network conditions.
NFR11: The system shall allow course materials of up to 100 MB to be uploaded.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR12: The system shall require users to create strong passwords.
NFR13: The system shall encrypt all sensitive data using industry-standard protocols.
NFR14: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC).
NFR15: The system shall implement automatic account lockout after multiple failed login attempts.
NFR16: The system shall implement protection against common security threats (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF).

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: unHinged - A Procedurally Generated Game for Mental Health Awareness
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for a procedurally generated video game that blends entertainment with raising awareness about mental health issues (Schizophrenia, Nyctophobia, Alzheimer's) by portraying how they disrupt daily life.

1.2 Scope
To create a 2D, top-down, single-player survival game set in a mental asylum. The player's goal is to escape. The game features adaptive difficulty, procedurally generated levels, and is designed with guidance from mental health professionals to simulate real-world difficulties without promoting violent stereotypes.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- NPC: Non-Player Character
- FPS: Frames Per Second

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
unHinged is an independent game that uses procedural generation to create unique levels and challenges each time, aiming to provide entertainment while fostering empathy and understanding for mental health struggles.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Procedurally generated levels (layout, challenges, objectives)
- 2D top-down perspective
- Survival gameplay (escaping asylum)
- Mental illness mechanics (challenges, hallucination/fear meters)
- Enemy NPCs with chasing behavior
- Inventory system with collectibles
- Main menu and settings
- Tutorial

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Mobile Players: Seek games playable in short bursts on mobile systems.
- Explorers: Want to explore the game environment to find secrets or easter eggs.
- Achievers: Want to earn achievements, collectables, or bragging rights.

2.4 Operating Environment
Desktop PC game, designed to be lightweight and run on low-end systems, developed using Godot Engine.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Game Story & Prototypes
FR1.1: The Game Design Document shall detail blueprints for the project.
FR1.2: A Figma prototype shall show how the first few screens of the game may look.
FR1.3: The game shall be designed using Godot.
FR1.4: Assets shall be created using Blender.
FR1.5: A backstory tying into the game's themes shall be created.

3.2 Module 2: Basic Game Structure
FR2.1: The game must use 2D assets.
FR2.2: The game shall feature animations using 2D assets.
FR2.3: The game shall feature a main menu.
FR2.4: The player can start a new game.
FR2.5: The player can continue an old game.
FR2.6: The game shall have adjustable settings.
FR2.7: Level 1 shall be created.
FR2.8: The game shall have a tutorial.
FR2.9: The player can turn a torch on and off.
FR2.10: The level shall be procedurally generated.

3.3 Module 3: Advanced Game Structure
FR3.1: The game shall have NPCs.
FR3.2: Enemy NPCs shall chase the player.
FR3.3: The player shall experience different effects based on mental parameters.
FR3.4: The game shall have an inventory.
FR3.5: The game shall have items to collect and use.
FR3.6: The game must have sound effects for the atmosphere.
FR3.7: The game must have 3 levels.

3.4 Module 4: Test & Publishing
FR4.1: The game must be completable.
FR4.2: The game shall be published online for free.
FR4.3: The game shall be marketed to players.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance Requirements
NFR1: The game shall run at a stable 30-60 fps.
NFR2: Load times between levels shall be no more than 5 seconds.

4.2 Atmosphere Requirements
NFR3: The game shall convey the feelings and troubles associated with mental health issues through consistent use of assets and sound effects to create an oppressive, dark setting.

4.3 Replayability Requirements
NFR4: Procedural generation shall ensure that the player gets a different experience every time they run the game, increasing its lifetime.

4.4 Portability Requirements
NFR5: The game must be able to run on any system regardless of processing power, achieved by using 2D assets and the lightweight Godot engine.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Traffix - AI-Powered Smart Traffic Management System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the development of Traffix, an innovative app that uses AI and advanced algorithms to solve traffic congestion problems by dynamically setting traffic signal timings based on real-time car density and providing emergency vehicle detection.

1.2 Scope
To develop a system using real-time CCTV footage and deep learning algorithms (YOLO) to identify vehicles, calculate car density, and adjust traffic signal timings dynamically. It also includes an emergency vehicle detection module, an automated challan generation system for violations, and an admin dashboard for management.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- YOLO: You Only Look Once (object detection)
- CCTV: Closed-Circuit Television
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Traffix is a web-based application and backend system designed to modernize traffic management by automating signal control and reducing reliance on manual traffic police, thereby improving travel efficiency and safety.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Traffic detection from CCTV using YOLO
- Traffic signal scheduling based on car density
- Emergency vehicle detection (ambulances, fire trucks)
- Automated vehicle challan generation for traffic violations
- Manual signal setting override for authorities
- Admin dashboard for user and system management

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Traffic Police Officer: Ensures system integration, monitors traffic, changes signal settings, and views challans/alerts at their location.
- Admin Officer: Manages traffic police officers and their locations, registers new users, and monitors/changes signal settings.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application for officers/admins, integrated with CCTV cameras and smart traffic controllers.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Detection of Vehicles
FR1.1: The system shall distinguish between different types of vehicles.
FR1.2: The system shall detect moving vehicles.
FR1.3: The system shall detect stationary vehicles.

3.2 Calculation of Car Density
FR2.1: The system shall calculate the total number of vehicles.

3.3 Traffic Signal Scheduling
FR3.1: The system shall prioritize lanes with more vehicle density.
FR3.2: The system shall adjust traffic signal timings based on real-time footage of vehicle density.
FR3.3: The system shall adapt to changing traffic conditions throughout the day.

3.4 Emergency Vehicle Detection
FR4.1: The system shall detect emergency vehicles in traffic using image detection.
FR4.2: The system shall modify traffic signal timings to provide a clear path for detected emergency vehicles.
FR4.3: The system shall notify traffic control when an emergency vehicle is detected.

3.5 Automated Vehicle Challan Detection System
FR5.1: The system shall identify traffic violations (e.g., breaking the signal).
FR5.2: The system shall record and store data on detected violations.

3.6 Admin Dashboard
FR6.1: The admin shall be able to register, view, update, and delete Traffic Officers.
FR6.2: The admin shall be able to view Traffic Officers.
FR6.3: The admin shall be able to monitor Traffic.
FR6.4: The admin shall be able to manage Signal Control.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall have a 24-48 hrs Mean Time Between Failures (MTFB), ensuring continuous operation except for scheduled maintenance.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR2: The system shall have a user-friendly interface, allowing Traffic Police to use the system efficiently.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR3: The system shall maintain its average response time of less than 5 seconds in various conditions.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR4: The system shall require user authentication for different tasks depending on the user's role.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: IntraSec - Client-less Network Risk Assessment and Asset Management Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document defines the requirements for IntraSec, a client-less risk assessment and asset management tool designed to provide real-time inventory of network assets and identify security risks without requiring agent installation on individual machines.

1.2 Scope
To develop and deploy a client-less system for internal networks that discovers and inventories devices, services, and software; continuously tracks assets; integrates with the NIST vulnerability database to assess risks; assigns risk scores; generates actionable reports; and automates patch deployment. It also includes continuous monitoring, real-time alerts, and notifications.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CVE: Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures
- CPE: Common Platform Enumeration
- NIST: National Institute of Standards and Technology
- API: Application Programming Interface
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control
- MFA: Multi-Factor Authentication

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
IntraSec is a centralized, automated security solution for internal networks, operating via microservices and RESTful APIs, designed to improve security posture and asset management.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Asset discovery and management (real-time inventory)
- Risk assessment (integration with CVE/CPE databases, risk scoring)
- Patch management (repository and deployment)
- Reporting (vulnerability summaries, recommendations)
- Alerts and notifications

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Network Security Team: Uses IntraSec to assess vulnerabilities and take proactive measures for threat detection and mitigation.
- IT Administrators: Oversee installation, configuration, and integration of IntraSec with existing tools.
- System Administrators: Manage the technical side, including patch deployments and addressing vulnerabilities.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application with backend microservices communicating via RESTful APIs, using Elasticsearch and PostgreSQL databases.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Assets Inventory
FR1.1: The system shall discover and display all devices, software, and services in real-time.
FR1.2: The system shall allow users to view, filter, and manage asset details through a front-end interface.
FR1.3: The system shall continuously track changes in asset details and update them in the PostgreSQL database.
FR1.4: The system shall integrate with Active Directory to centralize asset management.

3.2 Module 2: Risk Assessment
FR2.1: The system shall perform vulnerability scanning of assets using ElasticSearch.
FR2.2: The system shall continuously sync with the NIST Vulnerability Database API.
FR2.3: The system shall calculate and assign risk scores based on the severity of detected vulnerabilities.
FR2.4: The system shall provide a dashboard to display risk scores, vulnerability details, and remediation recommendations.

3.3 Module 3: Report Generation
FR3.1: The system shall generate standardized reports including identified vulnerabilities, severity, and risk score.
FR3.2: The system shall provide exportable reports for auditing and documentation.
FR3.3: The system shall generate detailed analysis reports with remediation recommendations.
FR3.4: The system shall allow users to view, filter, and export reports.

3.4 Module 4: Patch Deployment
FR4.1: The system shall automate the deployment of patches for identified vulnerabilities.
FR4.2: The system shall provide an interface to monitor the status of patch deployment.
FR4.3: The system shall notify users if manual intervention is required when automatic patch deployment fails.
FR4.4: The system shall report the progress and status of patch deployment.

3.5 Module 5: Alerts and Notifications
FR5.1: The system shall generate real-time alerts for detected vulnerabilities or security incidents.
FR5.2: The system shall allow users to customize alert thresholds for specific types of vulnerabilities.
FR5.3: The system shall provide an interface for users to view active alerts and notifications.
FR5.4: The system shall send notifications for vulnerabilities requiring immediate action.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall aim for an up-time of at least 95% during operational hours.
NFR2: In case of failure, the system shall log the event and notify administrators within 5 minutes.
NFR3: The system shall recover from critical failures and resume operation within 10 minutes.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: Users shall be able to perform key tasks such as viewing asset information and generating reports in 3 interactions or fewer.
NFR5: 80% of new users shall be able to navigate and complete common tasks without needing external documentation after 10 minutes.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR6: The system shall be able to generate an asset inventory report for up to 100 assets within approximately 15 minutes.
NFR7: The dashboard page shall fully load within 5 seconds for 80% of users on a >10 Mbps connection.
NFR8: Database queries shall return results within 5 seconds for typical operations.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR9: 80% of administrative users shall be required to use multi-factor authentication (MFA) for logging in.
NFR10: The system shall maintain logs of access to sensitive data for at least 3 months.
NFR11: The system shall lock user accounts after 5 consecutive failed login attempts, with notification sent to the administrator within 10 minutes.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SnapShop - AI-Powered Fashion Item Virtual Try-On and Ad Generation Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for SnapShop, a web-based application that uses AI to revolutionize the fashion industry by allowing customers to try on multiple clothing items virtually and enabling designers to generate video ads for their products.

1.2 Scope
To develop a web application that allows users to upload fashion item images, select poses and environments, and generate images/videos of models wearing those items. It also includes customer personalization features (using user's own image) and video ad generation for producers.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- UI: User Interface
- UX: User Experience
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SnapShop is a web-based platform that bridges the gap between fashion brands and customers by providing a virtual try-on and ad generation solution powered by AI (diffusion models, LoRA, Dreambooth).

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Custom image generation (single/multiple items, pose, custom environment)
- Customer personalization (single/multiple items on user's own image)
- Video ad generation (short videos, story generation)
- User authentication and profile management
- Real-time preview of generated images/videos

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Fashion Enthusiasts: Passionate about new styles, wants to make new outfits from imagination.
- Fashion Designers: Creates new clothing designs, provides design concepts.
- General Public: Occasionally buys items, provides consumer insights.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Custom Image Generation
FR1.1: The system shall allow the user to select a single or multiple fashion item images and generate an image of a human model wearing those items.
FR1.2: The system shall allow the user to select a desired image pose for the generated image.
FR1.3: The system shall allow the user to specify a custom environment via a text prompt for the generated image.

3.2 Customer Personalization
FR2.1: The system shall allow the customer to upload their own image and select a desired clothing item, generating an image of the customer wearing that item.
FR2.2: The system shall allow the customer to upload their own image and select multiple desired clothing items, generating an image of the customer wearing those items.
FR2.3: The system shall allow the customer to select a desired pose for the generated image based on their own photo.

3.3 Ad Generation
FR3.1: The system shall allow producers to generate a short video ad (5-8 seconds) for their fashion product.
FR3.2: The system shall generate a small storyline for the video ad to uniquely define the product.

3.4 User Management (Functional)
FR4.1: Users must be able to create an account and log in using email and password.
FR4.2: Users must be able to reset their password via email verification.
FR4.3: Users must be able to upload images of fashion items (JPG, PNG).
FR4.4: Users must be able to upload their own images (full-body or portrait).
FR4.5: The system must generate images showing the user wearing uploaded fashion items.
FR4.6: Users must be able to select from a predefined list of poses or provide a reference image for custom poses.
FR4.7: Users must be able to create video advertisements.
FR4.8: The application must provide a real-time preview of generated images/videos.
FR4.9: Users must be able to view and edit their profiles, including uploaded images and generated content.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance Requirements
NFR1: The application should generate images within a reasonable amount of time after uploading.
NFR2: The system should support multiple users at a time.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR3: The user interface must be intuitive and easy to navigate.
NFR4: The design should be responsive, ensuring a seamless experience.

4.3 Scalability Requirements
NFR5: The application must be scalable to accommodate increasing user loads and processing demands.

4.4 Maintainability Requirements
NFR6: The codebase should follow best practices for maintainability, allowing for easy updates and feature enhancements.
