SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: VocalCraft - AI-Driven Pamphlet Creation Through Voice Commands
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document clarifies the architectural and modular structure of VocalCraft, an AI-driven pamphlet creation application that helps users create promotional and menu pamphlets using voice commands, addressing issues of incorrect text generation in current AI models.

1.2 Scope
To develop a system that allows users to create print-ready pamphlets via voice commands. Features include: voice recognition and transcription, prompt engineering and NER, image generation and inpainting using diffusion models, segmentation for text/image placeholders, image resizing/optimization, a mobile app front-end, and cloud-based backend.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- NER: Named Entity Recognition
- ASR: Automatic Speech Recognition
- API: Application Programming Interface
- DBMS: Database Management System

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
VocalCraft is a mobile application that leverages multiple AI models (Whisper, Stable Diffusion) to convert user voice commands into professionally designed pamphlets with correctly placed text.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Voice input and transcription (using Whisper)
- Prompt engineering and NER for extracting design entities (colors, text, layout)
- Image generation and inpainting (using diffusion models)
- Segmentation and binary mask generation for text/image areas
- Image resizing and optimization for printing
- Mobile app front-end for voice input, editing, and printing
- Backend and cloud integration for processing and model inference

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- End User (Regular User): A person wanting to create posters for individual use, advertising, or aesthetics. May range from non-technical to designers.
- Admin: Manages the entire system, including performance, accounts, error handling, model updates, and security.

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application with cloud-based backend services for heavy processing.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Authentication
FR1.1: Users will sign up using their email, password, and username.
FR1.2: The system will authenticate the user.
FR1.3: The system shall allow users to log out of their accounts.

3.2 Voice-to-Text Processing
FR2.1: The system shall use the user's microphone for voice input.
FR2.2: The system shall convert voice input into text using a speech recognition model (Whisper).
FR2.3: The system shall display the text to the user for confirmation or editing.

3.3 Text Input
FR3.1: The system shall allow users to input text prompts for poster generation manually.
FR3.2: The system shall validate the text input to ensure it is not empty and has necessary info.

3.4 Poster Generation
FR4.1: The system shall generate a poster based on user's voice or text prompts using an image generation model (Stable Diffusion).
FR4.2: The system shall present the generated poster to the user for preview.

3.5 Poster Editing
FR5.1: The system shall allow users to edit the generated poster (e.g., move text, change colors, fonts, resize elements).
FR5.2: The system shall apply user edits to the poster in real-time.
FR5.3: The system shall allow users to undo changes made during the editing process.

3.6 Save and Load Prompts
FR6.1: The system shall enable users to save their voice or text prompts for later use.
FR6.2: The system shall allow users to retrieve previously saved prompts and reuse them.

3.7 Save and Share Posters
FR7.1: The system shall permit users to download generated or edited posters in conventional formats (PNG, JPEG).
FR7.2: The system shall allow users to share generated posters directly via social media, email, or messaging apps.

3.8 System Monitoring and Management (Admin)
FR8.1: The system shall enable an admin to monitor system performance (usage, user activity).
FR8.2: The system shall enable an admin to create, delete, and modify user profiles and access permissions.
FR8.3: The system shall log errors and enable the admin to view logs for troubleshooting.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The MTBF should not be below 1000 hours of uninterrupted usage.
NFR2: A system failure is defined as inability to generate a poster or respond to user input.
NFR3: The system must have automatic recovery mechanisms (retries, fallback strategies).
NFR4: The system must produce and store failure logs.
NFR5: The admin must be informed of consecutive failures.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR6: The system shall allow users to generate a poster from a prompt with no more than 3 interactions.
NFR7: The interface must allow users to preview and edit posters with drag-and-drop functionality.
NFR8: The system must provide real-time visual feedback during poster generation (progress bars).
NFR9: The system shall allow users to undo or reset any edits with a single interaction.
NFR10: The system must provide tips for first-time users.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR11: 95% of posters shall be completed within 30 seconds of the user's request.
NFR12: 90% of voice-to-text conversions shall be processed and displayed within 5 seconds.
NFR13: The system must support at least 100 concurrent users without noticeable degradation.
NFR14: The system shall allow the user to save or share posters within 5 seconds after confirmation.
NFR15: Under heavy load (>100 concurrent users), response time must be less than 60 seconds for 80% of requests.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR16: The system shall prevent unauthorized access to user data and poster generation history.
NFR17: The system shall detect and log unauthorized access attempts and notify the admin within 5 minutes.
NFR18: The system shall ensure user data is protected against unauthorized modifications.
NFR19: The system shall maintain the integrity of poster data during transmission and storage.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SprintCraft - Context Aware User Story Generator
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
In agile development, converting client requirements into actionable user stories is time-consuming and error-prone. Manual user story creation risks inconsistencies, incomplete coverage, and miscommunication between stakeholders and development teams.

1.2 Scope
The system automates user story generation from Software Requirement Specification (SRS) documents and prompts. It includes self-evaluation mechanisms (completeness, clarity, feasibility, value), interaction history, user-friendly interface, and fine-tuned LLMs.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- SRS: Software Requirements Specification
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- LLM: Large Language Model
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The system streamlines conversion of client requirements into actionable user stories, which are core components in agile development lifecycle, with evaluation against pre-defined thresholds.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- SRS document upload
- Prompt field for user story specification
- Input validation
- Requirement extraction
- Contextual understanding
- Auto-generation of user stories (functional/non-functional)
- User story editing
- Acceptance criteria addition
- Version history tracking
- Story comparison between versions
- Collaboration support
- User authentication and role-based access control

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Product Manager: Oversee product development, need detailed accurate user stories
- Software Developer: Implement features, rely on clear technical stories
- Business Analyst: Bridge stakeholders and development, refine SRS
- QA Tester: Create test cases, need clearly defined acceptance criteria
- Stakeholders: Review output at high level for alignment with project vision
- Project Supervisor: Monitor progress, ensure stories align with defined goals

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, backend engine with fine-tuned LLMs.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Input Module
FR1.1: Users shall upload SRS documents (.doc, .docx, .pdf).
FR1.2: Users shall browse local file system and select SRS document.
FR1.3: System shall notify users upon successful upload or failure with appropriate messages.
FR1.4: System shall provide prompt input field for user story specification.
FR1.5: System shall validate uploaded SRS document and prompt input for required format and structure.

3.2 Natural Language Processing (NLP) Engine
FR2.1: NLP engine shall extract key requirements from uploaded SRS document.
FR2.2: Engine shall identify functional and non-functional requirements, tag components (goals, constraints, assumptions).
FR2.3: Engine shall understand context of SRS document to accurately interpret content.
FR2.4: Engine shall interpret user prompt to generate tailored user stories.

3.3 User Story Generation Module
FR3.1: Module shall automatically generate user stories from extracted data (format: "As a [role], I want [goal], so that [benefit]").
FR3.2: Module shall generate user stories for both functional and non-functional requirements.
FR3.3: Module shall allow customization based on user prompt.

3.4 Story Review and Editing Module
FR4.1: Module shall allow users to review and edit generated user stories.
FR4.2: Module shall provide mechanisms for user feedback and suggestions for generation rule adjustments.

3.5 Version Control and Tracking Module
FR5.1: Module shall track all changes to user stories, capturing each version as separate entity.
FR5.2: Module shall provide functionality for users to compare different versions of user stories.

3.6 Security and Authentication Module
FR6.1: Module shall provide user authentication and login with multi-factor authentication.
FR6.2: Module shall implement role-based access control (RBAC).

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: Processing from input to scoring shall complete in 10-15 seconds.
NFR2: System shall allow simultaneous user activities without observable delay.

4.2 Scalability
NFR3: System shall support multiple concurrent users, adapt to increased workload via horizontal scaling.

4.3 Security
NFR4: All stored data shall be encrypted using AES-256.
NFR5: Secure user authentication following OAuth 2.0 standards.
NFR6: Role-based access control shall ensure appropriate access levels.

4.4 Usability
NFR7: User interface shall be intuitive and user-friendly.
NFR8: Compliance with WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards.
NFR9: Clear, actionable feedback for input corrections.

4.5 Reliability
NFR10: Service uptime ≥ 90%.
NFR11: Automatic failover mechanisms shall restore services within 1-10 minutes.
NFR12: Daily backups shall safeguard against data loss.

4.6 Maintainability
NFR13: Codebase shall adhere to SOLID design principles.
NFR14: Scheduled updates with minimal disruption (downtime < 1 hour).

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: OptiFit - Virtual Glasses Try-On System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for OptiFit, a virtual glasses try-on solution that allows users to try various glasses frames on their face using their device camera in real-time, providing an interactive and customized experience.

1.2 Scope
To develop an advanced virtual glasses try-on system that uses 3D face and glasses models. The project includes: face 3D model generation (from photos), glasses 3D model generation (from images/videos), 3D model integration, glasses customization (color, size), API development for retailer integration, and a client web application for demo and testing.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- 3D: Three-Dimensional
- API: Application Programming Interface
- CMS: Content Management System
- VR: Virtual Reality

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
OptiFit is a service-based model that allows online eyewear retailers to integrate a virtual try-on feature into their platforms, enhancing the customer experience and reducing returns.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Face 3D model generation (face detection, feature extraction, mesh generation, size estimation)
- Glasses 3D model generation (image segmentation, mesh generation via photogrammetry)
- 3D model integration (feature mapping, model integration)
- Glasses customization (color, size)
- API development for integration with retailer systems
- Client web application (CMS, 3D frame previewer, try-on functionality)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Customer: Uses the system to try on glasses frames virtually.
- System Administrator: Manages the system.
- Admin: Configures and manages the system.
- Retailer: Integrates the system into their e-commerce platform.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application and APIs, designed for integration with various e-commerce platforms.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Face 3D Model Generation
FR1.1: The system shall detect and identify faces in video input using facial features.
FR1.2: The system shall use facial landmarks to categorize and extract facial features.
FR1.3: The system shall generate a 3D mesh of the face using extracted features.
FR1.4: The system shall estimate the size of the user's face using depth estimation via the diameter of the iris.

3.2 Glasses 3D Model Generation
FR2.1: The system shall preprocess images to segment and separate glasses from the background.
FR2.2: The system shall create a 3D mesh of the glasses using multiple images from different angles through photogrammetry.

3.3 3D Model Integration
FR3.1: The system shall identify and extract common features from glasses and face.
FR3.2: The system shall map the features of the glasses to the features of the face for integration.
FR3.3: The system shall connect and integrate the glasses mesh with the face to create an integrated model.

3.4 Glasses Customization
FR4.1: The system shall allow users to select from a variety of colors for the glasses frame.
FR4.2: The system shall enable users to adjust the size of the glasses to fit the individual.

3.5 API Development
FR5.1: The system shall provide API endpoints for each functionality of the virtual glasses try-on system.
FR5.2: The system shall implement authentication on all API endpoints to ensure only authorized access.

3.6 Client Web Application Development
FR6.1: The web application shall allow users to view and manage a catalog of available glasses.
FR6.2: The web application shall include an interactive 3D previewer to visualize glasses from all angles.
FR6.3: The web application shall enable users to virtually try on all available glasses using the integrated API.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance Requirements
NFR1: The system shall process video input and provide output with a maximum latency of 500 milliseconds.
NFR2: The system shall support a frame rate of at least 30 fps for smooth video viewing.

4.2 Reliability Requirements
NFR3: The system shall have an uptime of 99% to ensure consistent availability.
NFR4: The system shall implement error handling to easily recover from failure without loss of data.

4.3 Usability Requirements
NFR5: The system shall provide an intuitive user interface, requiring no more than three clicks to access the virtual try-on feature.
NFR6: The application shall include tooltips to help users navigate the interface.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR7: Secure coding practices will be used to prevent vulnerabilities (e.g., SQL injection, XSS).
NFR8: User data stored in the system shall be encrypted to protect it from unauthorized access.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: InsuraSync - AI-Powered Insurance Claim Processing System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for InsuraSync, an AI-powered insurance claim processing system designed to handle car accident claims, increasing speed, providing real-time status updates, improving accuracy, and changing how claims are handled.

1.2 Scope
To develop an all-in-one insurance management application that includes: user registration/login, personalized policy recommendations, payment integration, document verification/storage, auto-issuance of policy documents, real-time incident reporting, fraud detection, real-time cost estimation, and claim categorization.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- MFA: Multi-Factor Authentication
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
InsuraSync is a platform that automates and simplifies the insurance claims process for both policyholders and insurance companies, using AI for fraud detection, cost estimation, and claim categorization.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Personalized policy recommendations using ML
- Automated claims processing (electronic submission, data processing)
- Discrepancy and fraud detection
- Automated repair cost estimation
- Claim categorization and severity prediction using NLP
- Customer self-service portal
- Automated email notifications
- Document management system
- Accident site verification

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Insurance Policy Holders: Individuals who purchase policies, file claims, and track status.
- Insurance Company Officers: Employees who administer claims, policies, and inquiries; have access to document management and fraud detection.
- Accident Site Officer: Personally visits the site to verify details for high-value cases to prevent fraud.
- System Administrator: Manages system maintenance, user roles, and monitors security/compliance.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Personalized Policy Recommendations
FR1.1: The system shall provide a form for users to enter essential information (age, income, family size, insurance type).
FR1.2: The system shall analyze user input using machine learning and filtering techniques to suggest relevant policies.
FR1.3: The system must provide various policy options according to user preferences.

3.2 Automated Claims Processing
FR2.1: The system shall provide the facility for filing insurance claims over the web application.
FR2.2: The system shall allow users to upload supporting documents (images, PDFs, scanned forms, FIR reports).
FR2.3: The system shall show a confirmation message after successful submission.

3.3 Discrepancy and Fraud Detection
FR3.1: The system must automatically compare claim details with user profiles and historical data to detect inconsistencies.
FR3.2: The system shall flag claims with mismatches between claims and supporting documents.
FR3.3: The system shall recognize and flag claims with high frequency as possible cases of fraud.

3.4 Automated Repair Cost Estimation
FR4.1: The system shall allow users to enter information about damaged parts of vehicles.
FR4.2: The system shall estimate repair costs for similar damages using historical data.
FR4.3: The system shall compare the repair cost estimate with the user's requested claim amount.

3.5 Claim Categorization and Severity Prediction
FR5.1: The system shall extract relevant information from claim descriptions using NLP.
FR5.2: The system shall classify claims into three severities: high, low, and general damage.
FR5.3: The system shall rank claims to be handled quickly according to severity level.

3.6 Customer Self-Service Portal
FR6.1: The system must allow users to manage policies, submit claims, and track status through an online portal.
FR6.2: The system shall allow access to user's insurance history and latest coverage details.
FR6.3: The system shall ensure easy and accessible document uploading for claims.

3.7 Automated Email Notifications
FR7.1: The system shall send an automatic email for each key event like claim submission or approval.
FR7.2: The system shall support customizable email triggers depending on claim or policy status.

3.8 Document Management System
FR8.1: The system must store all claim-related documents in a central, secure repository.
FR8.2: The system shall be able to search and grab documents based on metadata (claim number, date).
FR8.3: The system shall accept scanned documents related to the incident/claim.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system should have at least an MTBF of 500 hours.
NFR2: In case of failure, an automatic error detection mechanism should produce alerts within 5 seconds.
NFR3: The system must provide back-up and recovery capabilities that restore functionality within 15 minutes of a critical failure.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The system shall enable first-time users to submit claims within 5 minutes without training.
NFR5: The system shall make user prompts clear and concise to avoid errors during submission.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR6: The system should deal with at least 50% of claims in less than 10-20 seconds.
NFR7: NLP should complete categorization within 10-15 seconds for a 500-word claim description.
NFR8: Estimated repair cost should be displayed within 10-15 seconds after user inputs damaged parts.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR9: The system shall enforce two-factor authentication for all users before they log in.
NFR10: File storing shall be secure between user and the system, preventing third-party access.
NFR11: The system shall record all actions performed with claims and policy updates, retaining logs for at least 6 months.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Personix - Personalized 3D Avatar Creation and Animation Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This report outlines the development progress and requirements for Personix, a platform allowing users to create personalized 3D avatars from photos, customize them, and animate them with natural expressions for content creation.

1.2 Scope
To develop an Android application that enables users to create realistic 3D avatars from their own photos using computer vision and facial recognition. Features include: avatar customization (skin tone, hairstyle, facial structure), animation (text-to-speech, sentiment-based expressions), content creation (stickers, GIFs, short videos), cloud sync, and management of multiple avatars.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- 3D: Three-Dimensional
- TTS: Text-to-Speech
- GIF: Graphics Interchange Format
- UI: User Interface
- UX: User Experience

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Personix is a mobile application that empowers users to create a digital self-representation, enabling them to generate expressive content for social media while maintaining privacy.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Avatar creation from user photos (3D model generation)
- Avatar customization (facial features, skin tone, hairstyles)
- Animation and expression (real-time tracking, TTS with sentiment analysis, voice customization)
- Content creation and sharing (stickers, GIFs, videos with face filters)
- Cloud sync and storage (secure storage, cross-device sync)
- User profile and settings (registration, preferences, notifications)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- End User (Avatar Creator): Wants to create customized 3D avatars for personal or social media use; values privacy and creativity.
- Content Creator: Advanced user who frequently generates shareable content (videos, GIFs) for social media.
- Casual User: Interacts infrequently, may only use basic avatar creation features.
- Tester/QA: Responsible for testing app features to identify bugs.
- Developer: Responsible for technical development, updates, and bug fixes.

2.4 Operating Environment
Android mobile application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Avatar Creation and Customization
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to upload photos for 3D avatar generation.
FR1.2: The system shall generate a 3D avatar based on the uploaded photo.
FR1.3: The system shall provide tools for facial feature customization (e.g., hairstyle, facial hair, facial features).
FR1.4: The system shall allow users to save customized avatars for future use.
FR1.5: The system shall support creating multiple avatars per user.

3.2 Animation and Expression
FR2.1: The system shall allow real-time tracking of user facial movements and map them to the 3D avatar.
FR2.2: The system shall support text-to-speech conversion with avatar lip synchronization.
FR2.3: The system shall provide sentiment analysis to adjust facial expressions based on the tone of the speech.
FR2.4: The system shall allow voice customization, including pitch and tone adjustments.

3.3 Content Creation and Sharing
FR3.1: The system shall provide tools for users to create custom stickers using their avatars.
FR3.2: The system shall allow users to generate GIFs with avatar animations.
FR3.3: The system shall support recording videos with avatar face filters.
FR3.4: The system shall provide integration with social media platforms for sharing created content.
FR3.5: The system shall allow in-app sharing of content with other users.

3.4 Cloud Sync and Storage
FR4.1: The system shall securely store user avatars and content in the cloud.
FR4.2: The system shall provide synchronization of avatars and content across multiple devices.
FR4.3: The system shall allow users to manage cloud storage, including deleting avatars.
FR4.4: The system shall support automatic backup of avatars.

3.5 User Profile and Settings
FR5.1: The system shall support user registration and authentication.
FR5.2: The system shall allow users to customize their profiles, including avatar preferences.
FR5.3: The system shall enable users to configure application preferences (notifications, privacy).
FR5.4: The system shall allow users to manage notifications (email alerts, app notifications).

3.6 App Development and Deployment
FR6.1: The system shall provide a user-friendly and responsive interface for both mobile and web platforms (web platform optional).
FR6.2: The system shall implement core features defined in the use cases.
FR6.3: The system shall support app deployment and updates through the Android Play Store.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall achieve a mean time between failures (MTBF) of at least 1,000 hours during avatar creation processes.
NFR2: System failures during content sharing shall not result in data loss and shall automatically retry the operation.
NFR3: The system shall implement error detection mechanisms to log failures and alert users.
NFR4: In case of failure during avatar creation, the system shall save progress and allow resuming from the last saved state.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR5: The system shall allow a user to create and customize an avatar with no more than 3 interactions.
NFR6: The system shall provide tooltip guidance for all customizable features.
NFR7: The system shall allow users to recover from any customization mistake with a single undo action.
NFR8: The system shall be optimized for performance on devices with limited resources.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR9: 90% of avatar customization actions shall be reflected on-screen within 3 seconds of interaction.
NFR10: All content-sharing operations shall complete within 10 seconds over a 10 Mbps connection.
NFR11: The system shall handle up to 100 concurrent users customizing avatars without performance degradation.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR12: All user data, including avatar customizations and personal details, shall be encrypted using AES-256 during storage and transmission.
NFR13: The system shall implement a two-factor authentication (2FA) mechanism for accessing user accounts.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: PromoHive - AI-Powered Promotion Engine for E-commerce
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document serves as a mid-evaluation report for PromoHive, an AI-powered promotion engine developed in collaboration with UpStart Commerce and KDD Lab. It provides insights into the scope, problem statement, and progress.

1.2 Scope
To develop a promotion engine that helps sellers create, manage, and automate promotional offers for different customer segments. Key features include: a promotional calendar, customer segmentation, selection of promotion type, calculation of optimal discount value, automated emails, and monitoring/reporting dashboards.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- MACH: Microservices, API-first, Cloud-native, Headless
- A/B Testing: Comparing two versions to see which performs better

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
PromoHive is a headless, AI-driven engine that integrates with e-commerce platforms to automate the creation and execution of personalized promotions based on customer segmentation and behavioral analysis.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- AI-powered promotional calendar generation
- Customer segmentation based on purchase history and behavior
- Dynamic discount type classification (selecting the right qualifier)
- Dynamic discount value calculation (optimizing the amount)
- Automated promotion execution
- Email campaigns for personalized promotions
- Dashboard for monitoring promotion performance
- Offline A/B testing on existing data
- Marketer approval/rejection workflow for promotions

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Marketers and Retailers: Review and approve promotions, access dashboards for performance analysis.
- Development Team: Handles development, deployment, and maintenance of the engine.
- Customers: End users who receive personalized promotions.
- System Administrators: Responsible for overall system management.

2.4 Operating Environment
Cloud-native system designed for integration with various e-commerce platforms via APIs.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Promotion Creation
FR1.1: The system shall analyze available data and generate promotions specifically for each customer segment.
FR1.2: The system shall create a visually appealing and easy-to-understand promotional calendar showing promotion type and value for each segment.
FR1.3: The system shall select the discount type and optimize the discount value by considering customer preferences, behavior, and purchasing patterns.

3.2 Automated Emails and Analytics
FR2.1: The system shall be able to send promotional emails automatically to the right customers based on the generated, approved promotional calendar.
FR2.2: The system shall provide an insightful dashboard about each rolled-out promotion for marketer decision-making.

3.3 System Integration and Deployment
FR3.1: The system shall provide RESTful APIs for easy integration with numerous e-commerce platforms.
FR3.2: The system shall provide a flexible engine, handling input data of different formats.
FR3.3: The system shall be scalable, handling input data of different sizes/volumes.

3.4 Testing and Optimization
FR4.1: The system shall have the option for offline A/B testing, allowing users to run simulations on existing data before rollout.
FR4.2: The system shall make sure all blocks/functions work as expected before deployment.
FR4.3: The system shall allocate room for feedback-driven fine-tuning of AI models.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall constantly maintain an uptime percentage of 99.5% and ensure MTBF is minimally 5 minutes.
NFR2: The system shall automatically detect failures and recover with 0% data loss.
NFR3: The system shall maintain logs of all critical processes, notifying the dev team within 3 minutes of a failure.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The system shall allow the marketer to approve/disapprove any promotion with just a click.
NFR5: The system shall allow the marketer to change promotion content within 5 minutes.
NFR6: The system shall display KPIs in a user-friendly manner, understandable in under 30 seconds.
NFR7: The system shall provide guides so a new user can be proficient within 1 hour of training.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR8: The system shall process and store generated promotions in the database within 5 seconds.
NFR9: The system shall store changes made to promotions by marketers within 2 seconds of clicking 'save'.
NFR10: The email promotion system shall be able to push 50,000 emails within 10 minutes.
NFR11: The customer segmentation module shall process up to 10 million records and return results in under 8 minutes.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR12: Data in transit and at rest shall be encrypted using standard procedures (e.g., AES-256).
NFR13: The system shall use multi-factor authentication for marketers accessing sensitive information.
NFR14: The system shall maintain logs for everyone using it for the past 1 year.

4.5 Scalability Requirements
NFR15: The system shall be able to handle input data of up to 1 million records/entries without performance degradation.

4.6 Maintainability Requirements
NFR16: The system shall be designed so new modules can be added or updates made without requiring a complete restart.
NFR17: The code shall follow proper commenting procedures so other developers can contribute easily with an onboarding time of 3 days.

4.7 MACH Principles Requirements
NFR18 (Modularity): The system shall be divided into modules for easy addition/removal/updating.
NFR19 (API First): The system shall make use of well-documented RESTful APIs.
NFR20 (Cloud Native): The system shall be deployed on the cloud for easy scaling.
NFR21 (Headless): The system's backend implementation shall be decoupled for compatibility with multiple interfaces.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: BeAWarden - AI-Powered Community-Based Traffic Violation Detection System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for BeAWarden, an app that uses AI to detect traffic violations through registered users acting as virtual wardens, aiming to reduce human effort and capture violations missed by static cameras and traffic wardens.

1.2 Scope
To develop an app that allows registered users (virtual wardens) to record traffic violations using their mobile phones. The app will use AI models to detect violations (e.g., illegal number plates, HTV in the first lane), identify license plates via OCR, and notify authorities to issue e-challans.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- OCR: Optical Character Recognition
- HTV: Heavy Transport Vehicle
- LTV: Light Transport Vehicle
- FPS: Frames Per Second

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
BeAWarden is a mobile application that transforms community members into active participants in traffic law enforcement by using AI to automatically detect and report violations.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- App development (camera recording, voice command for auto-recording, UI/UX)
- License plate detection (OCR)
- Illegal number plate detection (custom dataset, model training)
- HTV first lane detection (vehicle classification, model training)
- Violation reporting and notification to authorities

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Virtual Warden: Registered user who records violations via the app after passing a test. They use a voice command to save violation footage.

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application communicating with a centralized server for AI processing and reporting.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 App Development & License Plate Detection
FR1.1: The app shall continuously record video through the mobile fixed on the dashboard.
FR1.2: The app shall save a chunk of the video being recorded using a voice command by the user.
FR1.3: The recorded video shall be saved locally until uploaded to the server.
FR1.4: The system shall detect specific voice commands (e.g., "Violation") to save recorded video chunks.
FR1.5: The app shall have a user-friendly interface for recording and uploading.
FR1.6: The app shall give voice feedback on saving video and starting/stopping.
FR1.7: The app shall have a dashboard for user records, analytics, and incentive tracking.
FR1.8: OCR shall be used to get the license plate number of the violator.

3.2 Illegal Number Plate Detection & HTV First Lane Detection
FR2.1: The system shall allow the collection of a custom dataset of images containing various types of illegal number plates.
FR2.2: The system shall support dataset updates with new images to improve model accuracy.
FR2.3: The system shall train an ML model to detect illegal number plates in video footage.
FR2.4: The system shall detect the HTV on the first lane by first detecting the first lane and then the HTV on it.
FR2.5: The system shall classify all vehicles using object detection to detect violations based on vehicle type.
FR2.6: The system shall train an ML model for HTV detection based on a custom dataset.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall have an MTBF of at least 200 hours of continuous operation.
NFR2: The system shall have a downtime of no more than 2 hours for maintenance.
NFR3: Recorded videos shall be uploaded without corruption.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The app shall have an appealing UI for easy recording and uploading.
NFR5: The voice command feature for recording shall have an accuracy rate of at least 90%.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR6: The mobile app shall function smoothly, maintaining at least 30 FPS for video recording.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR7: All personal data (videos, user information, violation reports) shall be secured.
NFR8: Unauthorized access to uploaded videos or violation data must be prevented.
NFR9: App access will be given only to authorized users.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: EmpoweredAI - Sense Beyond Limits (Assistive Technology for Visually Impaired)
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for Empowered-AI, a comprehensive accessibility application that uses AI to improve independence and quality of life for visually impaired individuals.

1.2 Scope
To develop a free, AI-powered mobile application that provides essential tools for daily tasks, including: real-time object detection, scene description, facial emotion recognition, and text recognition (OCR). All information will be conveyed to the user via voice feedback.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- OCR: Optical Character Recognition
- UI: User Interface
- UX: User Experience

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Empowered-AI is a standalone mobile application that integrates computer vision, NLP, and haptic feedback into one platform, eliminating the need for multiple devices or costly assistive technologies.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Vision Insights (object detection, scene understanding)
- Smart Interface (UI design, scene description, text-to-speech)
- Live Assist (app development, text recognition, voice feedback)
- Emotion Sense (facial emotion detection, testing)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Visually Impaired Individuals: The primary user group, who rely on assistive technology. They interact with the app through voice feedback.

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application (client) communicating with powerful servers (cloud) for AI processing.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Vision Insights
FR1.1: The system shall be able to detect and identify objects in real-time within the user's environment.
FR1.2: The system shall provide scene descriptions based on object detection and contextual understanding.

3.2 Smart Interface
FR2.1: The system shall describe scenes in a way that visually impaired users can understand via voice feedback.
FR2.2: The system shall integrate text-to-speech technology to facilitate easy interaction.

3.3 Live Assist
FR3.1: The system shall provide real-time assistance by recognizing printed and handwritten text using OCR.
FR3.2: The system shall provide voice feedback to users based on the text detected.

3.4 Emotion Sense
FR4.1: The system shall detect facial emotions in real-time using AI-based emotion recognition technology.
FR4.2: The system shall test all functionalities to ensure they provide accurate and reliable outputs.
FR4.3: The system shall conduct thorough quality assurance to ensure robustness across different environments.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall maintain an MTBF of at least 168 hours of continuous operation.
NFR2: The application shall have a maximum allowable downtime of no more than 1 day per month for maintenance and updates.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR3: The application shall allow users to access main features (object detection, scene description, text recognition) within three taps.
NFR4: New users shall learn core functionalities within 30 minutes of initial use.
NFR5: The application shall provide clear and concise audio instructions for all features.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR6: 90% of scene descriptions shall be processed and relayed to the user within 15 seconds of user input.
NFR7: Voice feedback responses shall have a latency of no more than 3 seconds from the time the user requests information.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR8: The system shall ensure all data is processed securely in-memory and discarded immediately after use, preventing unauthorized access.
NFR9: The application shall not store any personal or sensitive information locally or on external servers.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SIGNFUSION (RTSLConverter) - Real-Time Speech-to-Sign Language Converter
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document describes the requirements for SIGNFUSION, a Chrome extension that translates speech and text into animated sign language in real-time for multimedia content.

1.2 Scope
To develop a real-time software solution that converts speech and text into animated American Sign Language (ASL) for video content, primarily on YouTube. The system will be delivered as a Chrome extension, featuring: speech-to-text conversion, text-to-gesture mapping, animation rendering, avatar customization, and low-latency performance.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- ASL: American Sign Language
- STT: Speech-to-Text
- API: Application Programming Interface
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SIGNFUSION is a client-side Chrome extension that processes audio from videos and overlays animated ASL gestures, bridging the accessibility gap for the deaf and hearing-impaired community.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Speech-to-Text (STT) conversion
- Text-to-gesture mapping
- Animation rendering (with avatar customization)
- Backend communication for data retrieval/storage
- Chrome Extension API for audio capture and display
- Dataset research for ASL gestures
- Testing and debugging
- Deployment

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- General User: Deaf or hearing-impaired individuals using the extension to view sign language translations.
- Content Creator: Individuals who want to ensure their video content is accessible; may test the translation accuracy.
- Administrators: Manage the backend system, update gesture datasets, and monitor system performance.
- Testers: Ensure reliability and functionality across different environments.

2.4 Operating Environment
Chrome browser extension.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Speech-to-Text (STT) Module
FR1.1: The system shall convert speech from video content into text in real-time.
FR1.2: The system shall support various speech patterns, accents, and tones.
FR1.3: The system shall handle background noise and filter out non-speech audio.

3.2 Text-to-Gesture Mapping Module
FR2.1: The system shall map recognized text to appropriate ASL gestures with high accuracy.
FR2.2: The system shall support a comprehensive database of ASL signs.
FR2.3: The system shall ensure gestures align with text and are contextually accurate.

3.3 Animation Module
FR3.1: The system shall generate sign language animations in near real-time.
FR3.2: The system shall ensure animations are smooth and natural.
FR3.3: The system shall support avatar customization, allowing users to personalize the appearance.

3.4 Backend Communication Module
FR4.1: The system shall facilitate real-time communication between the Chrome extension and backend servers.
FR4.2: The system shall store and retrieve gesture data for accurate animations.
FR4.3: The system shall ensure low-latency data transmission.

3.5 Chrome Extension API Module
FR5.1: The system shall capture audio input from video content using the Chrome Extension API.
FR5.2: The system shall process audio and video input in real-time.
FR5.3: The system shall provide an interface for users to enable/disable the extension's features.

3.6 Dataset Research Module
FR6.1: The system shall collect and maintain a dataset of ASL gestures.
FR6.2: The system shall preprocess the ASL dataset for compatibility.
FR6.3: The system shall continuously update and improve the dataset.

3.7 Testing and Debugging Module
FR7.1: The system shall be tested in multiple environments.
FR7.2: The system shall identify and resolve issues related to transcription, gesture mapping, and animation.

3.8 Deployment Module
FR8.1: The system shall package the Chrome extension for easy installation.
FR8.2: The system shall provide an intuitive setup and configuration process.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall have an MTBF of at least 30 days.
NFR2: In case of failure, the system shall recover within 5 minutes.
NFR3: The system shall log all failures and performance issues.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The user interface shall be simple and intuitive, allowing users to enable/disable features with a single click.
NFR5: The system shall allow users to customize the avatar.
NFR6: The system shall offer clear instructions and error messages.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR7: The system shall process speech-to-text and gesture mapping within 500 milliseconds.
NFR8: The system shall support simultaneous translation of up to 5 video streams.
NFR9: 95% of sign language animations shall be rendered without noticeable lag.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR10: The system shall ensure no personal user data is collected or stored.
NFR11: The system shall prevent unauthorized access to the backend data storage.
NFR12: The system shall encrypt all communication between the extension and backend server.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: IntelliDatum - AI-Powered Unstructured Data Structuring Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document details the requirements for IntelliDatum, a smart data management web app that uses AI to automate the processing and structuring of unstructured data files into structured human-understandable dictionaries, developed in collaboration with Elixir Technologies.

1.2 Scope
To develop a user-friendly web application with a cloud-based backend that extracts structured information from heterogeneous data files (delimiter-separated, fixed-length, XML, line data). The AI model will learn patterns, infer field boundaries, and generate structured dictionaries. Additional features include schema validation, error detection, and a data mapping module to map data from dictionaries to document templates.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- CSV: Comma-Separated Values
- XML: Extensible Markup Language
- LLM: Large Language Model

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
IntelliDatum is a web-based platform that automates the manual process of extracting dictionaries from unstructured files, streamlining data analysis and management for enterprises.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- File Input (upload, type detection, metadata extraction)
- Delimiter Separated File Handler (delimiter detection, header detection, dictionary generation)
- Fixed Length File Handler (record length identification, dictionary generation)
- XML File Parser (parsing, dictionary generation)
- Line Data Handler (pattern recognition, heuristic-based field mapping)
- Web App (UI/UX, frontend/backend, integration)
- Data Mapping (message interpretation, template management, document generation)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Enterprise Users: Organizations with large amounts of incoming unstructured data (e.g., banks).
- IT Administrators: Oversee systems administration, user management, and data handling.
- Data Managers: Manage and organize data to make it usable.
- End Users (Clients): Users who want to convert data into specific formats with low technical skills.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application with a self-developed cloud-based backend service.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 File Input Module
FR1.1: The system shall allow the client to upload files in multiple formats (CSV, XML, fixed length, unstructured line data).
FR1.2: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface for file uploading.
FR1.3: The system shall display the progress of the file upload.
FR1.4: The system shall allow the user to upload at least 10 multiple files.
FR1.5: The system shall validate the size and format of the uploaded file.

3.2 Delimiter Separated File Handler Module
FR2.1: The system shall correctly identify the metadata of the file (single/multi record).
FR2.2: The system shall correctly identify the delimiter (e.g., ',', '|').
FR2.3: The system shall handle files with or without headers effectively.
FR2.4: The system shall generate tags of data by giving them user-friendly names.
FR2.5: The system should handle single/multiple records and documents effectively.

3.3 Fixed Length File Handler Module
FR3.1: The system shall extract the metadata of the file accurately.
FR3.2: The system shall process single and multiple records/documents effectively.
FR3.3: The system shall process fixed length based on inferred field lengths.
FR3.4: The system shall name identified fields with user-friendly names.

3.4 XML File Parser Module
FR4.1: The system shall process XML files using tags and attributes for effective tag generation.
FR4.2: The system shall give user-friendly names to generated tags.
FR4.3: The system shall support XML schema validation.

3.5 Line Data Handler Module
FR5.1: The system shall apply pattern recognition techniques to detect context and location of fields.
FR5.2: The system shall handle line data files without obvious structure.
FR5.3: The system shall use heuristic methods for field mapping.
FR5.4: The system shall give user-friendly names to detected fields.

3.6 Web App
FR6.1: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface for all functionalities.
FR6.2: The system shall allow easy navigation between different pages.
FR6.3: The system shall allow users to submit feedback for their actions.

3.7 Data Mapping
FR7.1: The system shall allow the user to input a message related to data to be interpreted and mapped onto a template.
FR7.2: The system shall support modification of the template.
FR7.3: The system shall allow the user to map extracted data fields to specific placeholders in the template.
FR7.4: The system shall automatically generate documents based on mapped data and selected templates.
FR7.5: The system shall allow the user to view the generated document before downloading.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: In case of failure, the system must roll back to the previous step and restore activity in less than 5 minutes.
NFR2: The system must implement backup logs for operations.
NFR3: When an error occurs during processing, the system shall generate a clear and detailed message and alert for any failures.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The interface shall be user-friendly with clear instructions and navigation.
NFR5: The application shall allow the user to view previous documents generated using a single interaction.
NFR6: The system shall provide clear and actionable steps in case of failure.
NFR7: The system will offer a responsive design to work on various device types.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR8: The system shall upload a file within 5 seconds for a size less than 10MB.
NFR9: Users can upload up to 10 files simultaneously.
NFR10: The system shall interpret the user's prompt within 10 seconds.
NFR11: The system shall map data to templates within 5 seconds.

4.4 Maintainability Requirements
NFR12: The system will use CI/CD pipelines for easy code maintenance and updates.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Quran Echo - AI-Powered Quranic Recitation Enhancement Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the technical and functional requirements for Quran Echo, an AI-powered tool for enhancing the user's pronunciation (Makharij) and style (Qira'at) of Quranic recitation through real-time analysis and feedback.

1.2 Scope
To build an AI-powered Quranic recitation tool that offers: real-time feedback on recitation, Makharij correction for individual letters (similar to Qaidah method), recitation style imitation of famous Qaris, a Hifz module for memorization tracking, error detection for common mistakes, personalized guidance, and progress visualization.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- Makharij: Points of articulation for Arabic letters
- Tajweed: Rules for Quranic recitation
- Hifz: Memorization of the Quran
- Qira'at: Different canonical methods of recitation
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Quran Echo is a web-based application that acts as a personal Quran tutor, using AI to provide accurate, personalized feedback to improve recitation skills.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User registration and profile management
- Makharij correction for individual letters (Haroof)
- Recitation recording and upload for detailed analysis
- Recitation style imitation of famous Qaris
- Hifz module for tracking memorization progress
- Real-time feedback on Tajweed and pronunciation
- Progress tracking and visualization

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Quran Learner: Limited knowledge of Tajweed and Makharij; relies on guided tools for improvement. Benefits from real-time feedback and personalized error correction.
- Advanced Reciter: Well-versed in Tajweed; uses the platform for fine-tuning phoneme duration and melody. Appreciates precise, technical feedback.
- Hifz Practitioner: Focuses on memorization; uses the Hifz module to track progress, identify areas for revision, and receive reminders.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Registration and Profile Management
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to create an account using email or social media.
FR1.2: The system shall enable users to log in, log out, and recover forgotten passwords.
FR1.3: Users shall be able to customize their profiles, including preferences for recitation tracking and Hifz progress.
FR1.4: The system shall store and display user recitation progress, past uploads, Makharij corrections, and Hifz tracking.

3.2 Makharij Correction for Individual Haroof
FR2.1: The system shall allow users to practice individual Haroof and receive real-time feedback on Makharij correction.
FR2.2: The system shall provide specific guidance on the correct articulation points (Makharij) for each letter.
FR2.3: Visual and audio feedback shall highlight mistakes in letter pronunciation and suggest corrections.
FR2.4: Users shall be able to repeat practice until correct pronunciation is achieved, with progress tracking.

3.3 Recitation Recording and Upload
FR3.1: The system shall allow users to upload recitation audio files (MP3, WAV).
FR3.2: The system shall analyze the uploaded audio and provide detailed feedback on errors and corrections.
FR3.3: Users shall receive a breakdown of Tajweed and pronunciation errors in a clear, structured format.

3.4 Recitation Style Imitation
FR4.1: The system shall allow users to select a Qari's recitation style to imitate.
FR4.2: The system shall compare the user's recitation with the selected Qari's style, focusing on pitch, tone, and rhythm.
FR4.3: The system shall track progress and provide feedback on improvements over time.

3.5 Hifz Module
FR5.1: The system shall allow users to select specific verses or surahs for Hifz.
FR5.2: The system shall track the user's memorization progress, highlighting sections needing improvement.
FR5.3: Users shall receive reminders and suggestions for revising previously memorized sections.
FR5.4: The system shall display progress tracking for Hifz completion.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system will have an MTBF of at least 1,000 hours.
NFR2: In case of failure, the user should be able to restart their session from the last saved progress.
NFR3: The system will include error detection strategies, such as logging errors and alerting the development team.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The system shall allow a user to upload a recitation file or begin a live recitation feedback session within three clicks.
NFR5: The platform will include clear instructions and on-screen error guidance.
NFR6: The interface will be designed for accessibility, including options for different text sizes and screen readers.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR7: The system will provide real-time recitation feedback within a few seconds after the user finishes a phrase.
NFR8: The audio file upload feature will support files up to 10MB and process them for feedback within 5 seconds.
NFR9: The user interface shall load fully in less than 3 seconds on a standard 5Mbps connection.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR10: The system shall prevent unauthorized access by implementing role-based access control (RBAC).
NFR11: All user data, including recitation audio, shall be encrypted during transmission and storage.
NFR12: The system will have measures in place to prevent unauthorized access, with an estimated time to breach of no less than 24 hours for a skilled attacker.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: CyberQuest - VR-Based Cybersecurity Awareness Game
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for CyberQuest, a 3D game-based intervention designed to increase cybersecurity awareness among teenagers and young adults in the Indo-Pak region by simulating realistic scenarios and teaching preventive measures.

1.2 Scope
To design, develop, and implement a 3D game (with VR integration) that simulates real-world locations (homes, universities, restaurants) and cyberattacks (phishing, social engineering, malware). The game features a social life storyline, reputation and mood bar system, in-game economy (coins), and educational content. It covers common cyberattacks but excludes others like DDoS and SQL injection.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- VR: Virtual Reality
- NPC: Non-Player Character
- UI: User Interface
- UX: User Experience

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
CyberQuest is an immersive educational game that uses VR and interactive storytelling to teach cybersecurity concepts, focusing on player decision-making and its consequences within a simulated daily life.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Game Mechanics (player movement, object interaction)
- Game Elements (visual design of environments, game objects)
- Assets Creation (environment, characters)
- Game Scenarios (storylines, narrative building, scenario integration)
- UI/UX (intuitive menus, game flow objects)
- VR Integration (VR-ready controls, optimization)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Students: Primary users (16-25) who will face cybersecurity threats in virtual real-world locations.
- Faculty Members: Cybersecurity professionals or experts who might use the game for training or evaluation.
- Cybersecurity Experts: Evaluate the game's accuracy and effectiveness in teaching concepts; may provide feedback.
- Game Administrator: Maintains the game, updates scenarios, and ensures VR platform functionality.

2.4 Operating Environment
PC game optimized for VR platforms, including Oculus Quest 2.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Interface (UI) & User Experience (UX)
FR1.1: The system shall provide a simple, intuitive, and consistent UI for navigating menus, settings, and tutorials.
FR1.2: The game shall offer real-time visual feedback in response to player actions.
FR1.3: The game shall allow the player to view basic information about their overall performance, level, and scores.
FR1.4: The system shall include a "Mood Bar" reflecting the player's social status with two states: "happy" and "dead inside".
FR1.5: The system shall guide players through challenges with contextual prompts.
FR1.6: The audio subsystem shall adjust ambient sounds and music based on player interactions.

3.2 Virtual Reality Integration
FR2.1: The system shall be compatible with VR headsets, including Oculus.
FR2.2: VR environments shall simulate real-world locations (buses, universities, restaurants).
FR2.3: The system shall allow players to interact with NPCs through dialogue and decision-making.
FR2.4: Player decisions in virtual environments shall dynamically change the scenario and storyline.

3.3 Cybersecurity Attack Simulation
FR3.1: The game shall simulate cyberattacks (phishing, malware, ransomware) in daily life scenarios.
FR3.2: The system shall provide educational content for each simulated attack, explaining its nature and preventive measures.
FR3.3: After a successful attack, the system shall provide players with damage control strategies.

3.4 Reputation & Social Points System
FR4.1: The system shall adjust the player's reputation based on their responses to social interactions and cyberattacks.
FR4.2: The game shall reward positive actions with social points and in-game currency (coins) and penalize negative actions.
FR4.3: The "Mood Bar" shall visually reflect the player's accumulated social points.
FR4.4: Losing all social points shall result in failure of the current level.

3.5 Educational Content & Tutorials
FR5.1: The game shall provide real-time feedback and hints to reinforce proper responses to cyberattacks.
FR5.2: After an attack, the system shall give preventive tips to avoid similar attacks in future levels.

3.6 Game Progression & Levels
FR6.1: The game shall consist of multiple levels, each representing a different phase of life (e.g., university, early career).
FR6.2: Players shall unlock new virtual environments by completing tasks and reaching milestones.
FR6.3: Each level shall present increasingly complex challenges requiring application of cybersecurity knowledge.

3.7 Coins & Rewards System
FR7.1: The game shall reward players with coins for making good decisions (e.g., protecting personal data, avoiding attacks).
FR7.2: Players shall use earned coins to unlock in-game items or progress.
FR7.3: The system shall provide visual and auditory feedback when coins are earned.

3.8 Decision-Making & Scenario Selection
FR8.1: The system shall present players with decisions that impact the game's storyline and future challenges.
FR8.2: The outcome of these decisions shall dynamically alter the next set of challenges.
FR8.3: Player decisions shall influence the behavior of NPCs.

3.9 Non-Player Characters (NPCs) Interaction
FR9.1: NPCs shall exhibit different behaviors based on the player's decisions.
FR9.2: NPCs shall act as attackers, mentors, or allies depending on the context.
FR9.3: The player shall interact with NPCs through dialogues and decision points.

3.10 Task & Milestone Management
FR10.1: The game shall feature milestones like reaching academic or personal goals.
FR10.2: The system shall present challenges and tasks in each level that must be completed to move forward.
FR10.3: Each task must integrate a learning component related to cybersecurity.

3.11 Level Failure & Restart Mechanism
FR11.1: Players shall lose a level if they reach the "dead inside" state on the mood bar.
FR11.2: The game shall provide checkpoints within levels.
FR11.3: The system shall offer tips or guidance when players fail a level.

3.12 Daily Life Simulation
FR12.1: The game shall simulate daily activities (e.g., traveling on a bus, attending classes).
FR12.2: Daily activities must be interwoven with cybersecurity education (e.g., using free Wi-Fi safely).

3.13 Level Save Progression Management
FR13.1: The game shall automatically save player progress at predefined checkpoints.
FR13.2: Players shall have the option to manually save their current progress.
FR13.3: The game shall allow players to load their saved progress.

3.14 Performance
FR14.1: The player shall be able to view their current performance.
FR14.2: The player shall be able to interact with the updated gaming based on their performance.

3.15 Ending
FR15.1: The game shall end once the player successfully reaches their office milestone.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The game shall recover from unexpected crashes within 10 seconds and restore progress to the last saved checkpoint.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR2: The VR system shall allow users to easily navigate menus using simple hand gestures or VR controllers.
NFR3: The game must be simple and have a user-friendly interactive interface.
NFR4: The game shall provide on-screen prompts to guide players.
NFR5: The game must display error messages related to gameplay.
NFR6: The game must allow users to pause, save, and resume at any time.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR7: The game must load scenarios within 10 seconds.
NFR8: System shall be responsive towards the user's actions.
NFR9: The system shall ensure that all in-game data processing is completed within 1 second.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR10: Profile of one user shall not be accessed by any other user.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Sehat-e-Tafseel - Centralized Healthcare Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Pakistan's healthcare system lacks a centralized patient database, causing inefficiencies: repeated history taking, duplicated tests, un-standardized patient care, lost medical records, no collaboration between hospitals, and lack of cutting-edge tools like AI chatbots.

1.2 Scope
Sehat-e-Tafseel is a comprehensive healthcare web application connecting doctors and patients through a centralized platform. Features include healthbot for symptom analysis and disease prediction, skin disease detection via image upload, appointment booking, secure centralized database with unique patient IDs based on CNIC, and admin interface for hospital data management.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CNIC: Computerized National Identity Card
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The system provides a unique reference ID for every patient associated with their CNIC, enabling access to complete medical history across any hospital network, with AI-powered healthbot and skin disease detection.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Patient registration with unique ID based on CNIC
- Healthbot for symptom analysis and disease identification
- Doctor recommendation
- Appointment booking
- Skin disease detection via image upload
- Centralized medical record storage
- Admin role and permission management
- Report generation
- Prescription generation
- Doctor schedule management

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Patients: Seek medical assistance, use healthbot, upload skin images, retrieve medical history, book appointments
- Doctors: Offer healthcare services, schedule appointments, access patient records, generate prescriptions
- Admin: Manage system operations, maintain data security, manage accounts, generate reports

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, cloud-based centralized database, mobile-responsive design.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Admin Module
FR1.1: The system shall allow admins to assign roles (Doctor, Patient) to users.
FR1.2: The system shall provide interface to modify or revoke user roles and permissions.
FR1.3: The system shall allow admin to update and delete patient records.
FR1.4: The system shall generate slips for patients with unique reference ID based on CNIC.
FR1.5: The system shall allow admins to manage forms (application, operation, discharge, consent).
FR1.6: The system shall allow admin to view and analyze medical data for predictive insights.
FR1.7: The system shall provide graphical reports for trends and analysis.

3.2 Patient Module
FR2.1: The system shall allow patients to view medical history (diagnoses, treatments, medications, prescriptions, lab reports, surgeries).
FR2.2: The system shall restrict access to only authenticated patient's medical records.
FR2.3: The system shall allow patients to interact with Healthbot for symptom entry.
FR2.4: The system shall provide possible disease diagnoses based on symptoms.
FR2.5: The system shall allow patients to search for doctors by specialty.
FR2.6: The system shall allow patients to view health blogs and articles.
FR2.7: The system shall allow patients to book appointments with doctors.
FR2.8: The system shall confirm booking and send appointment slip.
FR2.9: The system shall allow patients to upload skin images for analysis.
FR2.10: The system shall return possible skin disease/allergy diagnoses and recommend doctors.

3.3 Doctor Module
FR3.1: The system shall allow doctors to view medical records of assigned patients.
FR3.2: The system shall restrict doctor access to only assigned patients.
FR3.3: The system shall allow doctors to update patient records after consultation.
FR3.4: The system shall keep log of all changes for auditing.
FR3.5: The system shall allow doctors to add remarks or additional notes.
FR3.6: The system shall allow doctors to generate prescriptions.

3.4 Centralized Database Module
FR4.1: The system shall store comprehensive patient data (diagnoses, treatment, prescriptions, surgeries, lab reports).
FR4.2: The system shall ensure data integrity and consistency.
FR4.3: The system shall allow hospitals to access patient records based on permissions.
FR4.4: The system shall maintain real-time database for immediate access.
FR4.5: The system shall enforce strict access control mechanisms.
FR4.6: The system shall utilize encryption for data transmission and storage.

3.5 Healthbot Module
FR5.1: The system shall allow patients to input symptoms for analysis.
FR5.2: The system shall analyze symptoms and suggest possible diseases based on pre-trained models.
FR5.3: The system shall recommend doctors based on identified disease.

3.6 Skin Disease Detection Module
FR6.1: The system shall allow patients to upload images of skin conditions.
FR6.2: The system shall ensure secure storage of uploaded images.
FR6.3: The system shall use computer vision to analyze image and return possible diagnosis.
FR6.4: The system shall recommend doctors based on analysis results.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: Automatic logs/alerts shall identify false detection immediately to admin.
NFR2: Auto-recovery from minor failures within 2 minutes.
NFR3: Backup recovery system within 10 minutes for critical failures.
NFR4: Daily backup strategy to restore integrity.

4.2 Usability
NFR5: Users shall learn and navigate within 30 minutes.
NFR6: Comprehensive help documentation with tooltips.
NFR7: Real-time form input validation.
NFR8: WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility compliance.
NFR9: Doctors shall retrieve patient records within 3 clicks.

4.3 Performance
NFR10: Process user requests (view records, book appointments) ≤ 2 seconds.
NFR11: Skin disease detection analysis ≤ 20 seconds.
NFR12: System shall handle up to 10,000 concurrent users without degradation.
NFR13: Centralized database shall support > 1 million records in < 1 second.
NFR14: Real-time updates to patient records without noticeable delay (< 1 second).

4.4 Security
NFR15: Data encrypted (AES-256) for storage and transmission.
NFR16: Multi-factor authentication for all users.
NFR17: System logs for confidential data accessible to admin.
NFR18: Strict role-based access control (RBAC).
NFR19: Intrusion detection mechanisms within 1 minute of detection.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Hifz Quran - Intelligent Quran Memorization Assistant
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for an intelligent Quran memorization assistant application that adapts to individual learning preferences, pace, and achievement levels, making the memorization process more efficient and engaging.

1.2 Scope
To develop a mobile application that helps users memorize the Quran. Features include: user profile and progress management, automatic ayah segmentation for easier memorization, personalized learning paths, interactive memorization modes (guided, recitation, listening), detection of similar verses (Mutashabihat), real-time recitation feedback, and notifications/reminders.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- Ayah: Verse of the Quran
- Surah: Chapter of the Quran
- Mutashabihat: Similar verses in the Quran
- MVC: Model-View-Controller
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The application is a mobile-based, AI-powered assistant that provides a structured, personalized, and supportive environment for memorizing the Quran, leveraging speech recognition and adaptive learning techniques.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User Profile and Progress Management (registration, profile, tracking, goals)
- Ayah Segmentation and Memorization (automatic segmentation, guided practice)
- Personalized Learning Path (learning style analysis, adaptive repetition)
- Interactive Memorization Modes (guided, recitation, listening)
- Mutashabihat Detection and Management (detect, practice, differentiate)
- Real-Time Recitation Feedback (speech recognition, error detection, suggestions)
- Notifications and Reminders (daily reminders, progress alerts)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- User (Memorizer): Person involved in the memorization process. Manages personal info, goals, and progress.
- System: Manages the core functionality of the app.

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application (iOS and Android).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Profile and Progress Management
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to register and log in using email/password or Gmail.
FR1.2: The system shall support profile customization (profile picture).
FR1.3: The system shall track the user's memorized verses and review history.
FR1.4: The system shall display a personalized dashboard with analytics on performance and progress.
FR1.5: Users shall be able to set memorization goals.
FR1.6: The system shall send reminders based on these goals.

3.2 Personalized Learning Path
FR2.1: The system shall analyze users' learning styles (listening or reciting).
FR2.2: The system shall adjust the repetition strategy based on the user's performance.
FR2.3: The system shall generate a personalized memorization plan for each user.

3.3 Ayah Segmentation and Memorization
FR3.1: The system shall automatically segment Ayahs into manageable chunks according to user performance.
FR3.2: The system shall be able to follow all Quran rules (waqf rules/Ayah stopping rules).

3.4 Interactive Memorization Modes
FR4.1: The system shall offer a mode where users follow guided steps to memorize verses.
FR4.2: Users shall be able to recite verses in real time.
FR4.3: The system shall provide a listening mode with playback and repeat functions.
FR4.4: The system shall take a test to modify user performance.

3.5 Real-Time Recitation Feedback
FR5.1: The system shall use speech recognition to detect errors in real-time.
FR5.2: Users shall receive feedback on their recitation, with errors highlighted and corrections suggested.

3.6 Mutashabihat Detection and Management
FR6.1: The system shall detect similar verses that could confuse users.
FR6.2: The system shall offer a special practice for differentiating similar verses.

3.7 Notifications and Reminders
FR7.1: The system shall send daily, weekly, and monthly reminders for memorization goals.
FR7.2: Users shall receive alerts when they achieve their goals.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance Requirements
NFR1: The system shall provide real-time recitation feedback with minimal latency.
NFR2: The system shall handle multiple users concurrently without degradation in performance.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR3: The interface shall be intuitive and easy to navigate for users of all ages.
NFR4: The system shall be accessible for users with varying levels of technical proficiency.
NFR5: The system shall work on mobile (iOS and Android).

4.3 Reliability Requirements
NFR6: The system shall ensure that user data is always consistent and accurate.
NFR7: The system shall ensure availability for users at all times.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR8: User data, including personal information and progress, shall be encrypted.
NFR9: The system shall implement secure authentication.
