SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: GlaciArc - Glacier Risk Prediction and Monitoring Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for GlaciArc, a web application designed for monitoring glaciers in Gilgit Baltistan and predicting risks related to Glacier Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) through satellite imagery analysis.

1.2 Scope
To develop a user-friendly, web-based monitoring and predictive platform for glacier-related hazards. Features include: satellite image acquisition and processing, machine learning model training (e.g., U-Net) for glacier and infrastructure classification, risk zone identification, and interactive map visualization of predictions and risk assessments.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- GLOF: Glacier Lake Outburst Flood
- ML: Machine Learning
- RF: Random Forest
- U-Net: Convolutional neural network architecture
- YOLO: You Only Look Once

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
GlaciArc is a web-based analytical tool that leverages satellite data and machine learning to provide actionable insights for disaster preparedness in northern Pakistan.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Collection (satellite image acquisition, data processing)
- Model Evaluation (model selection, model training)
- Testing (performance evaluation, comparative analysis)
- Web Application Development (UI design, map visualization, integration of ML models)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Researcher: Analyzes glacier data and predictions for research purposes.
- Administrator: Manages user accounts, oversees system performance, ensures data integrity.
- End User: Views glacier data and predictions via the dashboard.
- Stakeholder: Individuals/organizations interested in the project's outcomes (government, NGOs).

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Collection
FR1.1: The system shall be able to acquire satellite imagery data for specified glacier areas in Gilgit Baltistan.
FR1.2: The system shall be able to preprocess the collected satellite imagery data to create a usable dataset.
FR1.3: The system shall be able to crop satellite images to focus on specific glacier regions.
FR1.4: The system shall be able to label the processed data.
FR1.5: The system shall store the processed dataset in an organized manner.

3.2 Model Evaluation
FR2.1: The system shall select appropriate ML models (U-Net, Random Forest, YOLO) aligning with project requirements.
FR2.2: The system shall prepare training data by organizing it into suitable formats (training/validation sets).
FR2.3: The system shall train the selected models on the prepared dataset.
FR2.4: The system shall monitor training progress by tracking parameters like loss and accuracy.

3.3 Testing
FR3.1: System shall compute the trained models on performance metrics (accuracy, precision) and provide visuals to compare models.
FR3.2: System shall analyze errors and allow parameter tuning or adjustments.
FR3.3: System shall generate a report to compare each model in terms of accuracy, precision, and computational efficiency.

3.4 Web Application Development
FR4.1: The web app shall be appealing and responsive. Users can access targeted glaciers to see satellite images, classified images, predictions (red/green zones), and reports for comparative analysis.
FR4.2: The system shall be able to map output georeferenced satellite images onto a Google Earth map.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall maintain an MTBF of at least 1,000 hours.
NFR2: Regular data backups and performance logging will be implemented.
NFR3: The system will employ robust exception handling and a rollback mechanism.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: Users shall be able to perform common tasks (e.g., downloading images, viewing predictions) within three clicks.
NFR5: New users should comprehend basic functionalities within 30 minutes.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR6: The image preprocessing module must process images within 5 minutes.
NFR7: The system shall respond to user interactions within 5 seconds for 95% of operations.
NFR8: The prediction module shall manage historical movement data from at least 5 years.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR9: The system shall ensure data protection by preventing unauthorized access.
NFR10: The system shall support role-based access control.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: TheWeatherMind - AI-Driven Localized Weather Forecasting System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for TheWeatherMind, an AI-driven weather forecasting application designed to provide accurate, localized predictions for Pakistan, reducing reliance on external models and providing a revenue model through data purchase.

1.2 Scope
To build a system with four major phases: 1) Developing an AI model (LSTM) trained on local weather station, radar, and satellite data for Islamabad, Lahore, and Karachi. 2) Developing a user-friendly front-end for web and mobile. 3) Creating a content generation module (text, audio, video) using real-time data. 4) Integrating blockchain-based smart contracts for secure purchase of weather data.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
TheWeatherMind is a localized weather intelligence platform that combines AI prediction, engaging content generation, and a blockchain-based data marketplace.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Model training for precise weather prediction (integrating station, radar, satellite data)
- Front-End Design (web and mobile apps for forecasts, news, data purchase)
- Weather News Reporting (text, audio, video generation using LLMs and avatars)
- Blockchain Based Transactions (smart contracts for secure data purchase, payment verification, audit trail)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Weather Enthusiasts: Interested in personal use (planning activities). Use mobile app.
- Agriculture Professionals: Farmers/consultants relying on accurate predictions for irrigation/harvesting.
- City Planners/Government Officials: Need forecasts for severe weather conditions. Likely use desktop web app.
- Weather Data Purchasers: Companies/organizations buying localized data. Use web app.
- Weather Reporters: Produce content for public. Use web/mobile app.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web and mobile applications with cloud-based backend.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Weather Prediction Engine
FR1.1: The system shall collect real-time data from weather stations and radar.
FR1.2: The AI model shall process and analyze data to generate accurate localized forecasts.
FR1.3: The model shall provide predictions for specific geographic locations (Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi).
FR1.4: The system shall update weather forecasts at hourly intervals.
FR1.5: The model shall allow for continuous training and improvement as new data is collected.

3.2 Forecast Interface
FR2.1: The front-end shall display real-time weather forecasts (temperature, precipitation, humidity, dew).
FR2.2: Users shall be able to select specific locations to view localized predictions.
FR2.3: The design shall incorporate weather updates in textual, audio, and video formats.
FR2.4: The design shall integrate blockchain-based transactions for data purchase.

3.3 Weather Content Generator
FR3.1: The system shall generate weather forecasts in text, audio, and video formats using real-time data.
FR3.2: Text prompts shall be automatically generated based on weather data to create human-readable content.
FR3.3: The audio and video generation shall provide accurate spoken forecasts using NLP.
FR3.4: The module shall allow users to switch between content formats.

3.4 Transaction System
FR4.1: The system shall allow users to securely purchase localized weather data using blockchain technology.
FR4.2: The blockchain shall record every transaction in an immutable ledger.
FR4.3: Users shall be able to verify the authenticity of weather data transactions.
FR4.4: The system shall provide a seamless and transparent process for data transactions.
FR4.5: The transaction system shall support payments in local currency.
FR4.6: The system shall notify users once transactions are successfully completed.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall aim for an MTBF of at least 600 hours.
NFR2: A failure is defined as providing inaccurate forecasts or failing to display information correctly.
NFR3: The system shall implement monitoring and diagnostics for proactive issue identification.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The system shall allow a user to access the localized weather forecast for their selected location within two clicks from the homepage.
NFR5: The interface shall provide clear visual feedback (e.g., loading indicators).
NFR6: The system shall enable users to switch to the weather news screen and generate a video within a minute.
NFR7: The application shall allow users to search for weather data for any specified location within 7 seconds.
NFR8: Users can select up to 5 specific weather parameters for a downloadable file and finalize selection within 3 clicks.
NFR9: The blockchain-based purchase process shall provide confirmation within 10 seconds of payment initiation.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR10: 95% of weather forecast data shall be generated and displayed within 7 seconds of the user's request.
NFR11: The system shall support at least 100 concurrent users without degradation.
NFR12: Real-time updates shall be processed and delivered within 1 minute of data collection.
NFR13: The system shall allow for weather data retrieval from the database with a maximum latency of 5 seconds.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR14: Robust security measures shall protect sensitive data and ensure user privacy.
NFR15: Regular security evaluations shall be conducted to identify vulnerabilities.

4.5 Scalability Requirements
NFR16: The system shall support up to 100 simultaneous users without degradation.
NFR17: The infrastructure shall allow for horizontal scaling.

4.6 Portability Requirements
NFR18: The system shall be accessible from various platforms (web browsers, iOS, Android) without significant code changes.
NFR19: The system shall work seamlessly on all modern web browsers.

4.7 Extensibility Requirements
NFR20: The system shall allow the integration of new weather data sources or third-party APIs with minimal modification.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Decentra (Pixel) - Decentralized Marketplace on Blockchain
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document defines the requirements for Decentra, a decentralized marketplace built on the Base blockchain that allows users to trade goods and services directly without intermediaries, ensuring transparency, privacy, and low transaction fees.

1.2 Scope
To develop a decentralized protocol (collection of smart contracts) and a client web application that allows users to interact with it. Key features include: cryptocurrency-based transactions, escrow functionality (direct, double-deposit, notary), dispute resolution mechanisms, IPFS-based storage for images/reviews, ratings and reviews system, and notary/voting pools for arbitration.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- IPFS: InterPlanetary File System
- P2P: Peer-to-Peer
- API: Application Programming Interface
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Decentra is a permissionless, anti-censorship platform that eliminates middlemen, enabling peer-to-peer trading secured by smart contracts on the Base blockchain.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Trade Protocol (smart contracts for direct payment, double-deposit escrow, notary escrow)
- IPFS for hosting product images and reviews (tamper-proof storage)
- Decentra Client Application (web app for merchants, buyers, notaries)
- Notaries and Voting Pools (arbitration services)
- Ratings and Reviews system (tamper-proof, permanent)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Individual Sellers: Small business owners, artisans. Value autonomy, control, and privacy.
- Buyers: Seek diverse products, competitive pricing, and a secure, private shopping experience.
- Marketplace Operators: Focus on establishing niche marketplaces; need tools for managing multiple vendors.
- High-Trust Individuals: Can provide escrow services, building trust within the marketplace.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application interacting with the Base blockchain and IPFS.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Trade Protocol
FR1.1: The system must allow buyers to make direct payments to sellers without escrow when trust is established.
FR1.2: The system must implement a 2-of-2 multisignature smart contract for double-deposit escrow.
FR1.3: The system must support a 2-of-3 or n-of-m multisignature smart contract involving a designated notary.
FR1.4: The system must allow for the definition of trade type and validity period.
FR1.5: The system must maintain identifiers such as network ID, account public key, and PGP public key for each transaction.
FR1.6: The system must store information related to the goods or services being traded.
FR1.7: The system must provide a mechanism for handling disputes between peers.

3.2 IPFS for Hosting Product Listing Images and Reviews
FR2.1: The system must store high-resolution images of products on IPFS.
FR2.2: The system must host user reviews on IPFS, with each review stored as a separate file accessible via unique content hashes.
FR2.3: The system must leverage IPFS's content-addressable system for retrieving product images and reviews.

3.3 Decentra Client Application (Web App)
FR3.1: The application must allow users to select roles (merchant, buyer, notary, arbiter) with tailored features.
FR3.2: Merchants must be able to set up stores, list products, and manage sales.
FR3.3: Buyers must be able to search, purchase, and manage payment transactions.
FR3.4: Notaries must provide arbitration services and validate user reviews.
FR3.5: The application must connect with smart contracts on the Base chain and access images on IPFS.
FR3.6: Users must be able to provide ratings and written reviews for merchants and products.

3.4 Notaries and Voting Pools
FR4.1: The system must allow notaries to provide arbitration and validation services.
FR4.2: Users must be able to manually select notaries or rely on suggestions, ensuring mutual agreement.
FR4.3: The system must enable the formation of voting pools to minimize collusion risk during arbitration.
FR4.4: The notary pool must be able to issue digital rulings and receive fees for moderating trades.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system must have a high level of reliability, ensuring minimal downtime.
NFR2: In case of failure, the system should ensure data is not lost and users are informed.
NFR3: Robust error detection and automated recovery strategies should restore normal operation within one hour.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The platform must be easy to use for both novice and experienced users, abstracting away blockchain complexity.
NFR5: Wallet authentication and order placement should not require more than a few straightforward steps.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR6: Transactions (purchasing, listing) should be processed within a few seconds, ideally no more than 5 seconds.
NFR7: Search operations should provide results in under 1 second.
NFR8: The platform should handle up to 10,000 concurrent users without noticeable decline in performance.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR9: All user interactions, including wallet-based authentication, must be secure against threats like phishing or man-in-the-middle attacks.
NFR10: Users must securely manage their private keys; there should be no centralized recovery options.
NFR11: The system should encrypt all sensitive data in transit and at rest (AES-256).
NFR12: The system must be resilient against DDoS attacks.
NFR13: All critical operations must be logged for auditing.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: BugLens - Automated Game Bug Detection and Classification from Reviews
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for BugLens, a system that uses AI to streamline bug identification and reporting for games on the Steam platform, automating the process of review collection and classification.

1.2 Scope
To develop a system that accepts a Steam game link or a CSV file of reviews, scrapes or processes the data, uses Transformer-based AI models to identify and classify bugs into 16 predefined categories, and generates detailed reports with visualizations. The system includes user profiles, subscription plans, and an admin module.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- CSV: Comma-Separated Values
- API: Application Programming Interface
- NLP: Natural Language Processing

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
BugLens is an automated analysis platform that helps game developers efficiently identify and prioritize bugs by processing user reviews through advanced machine learning models.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Dataset gathering and labeling (scraping Steam reviews for early access games, labeling by bug categories)
- Model fine-tuning (sentiment analysis, bug identification, classification into 16 categories)
- Report generation (data visualization, summaries)
- User Profile Management and Admin Module (subscription plans, report history, user management)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Admin: Manages users, system statistics, and user feedback.
- Users: Gamers or developers who want to know about bugs in specific games.
- FYP Team: Responsible for fine-tuning models, platform development, and deployment/maintenance.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application with backend AI processing.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Dataset Gathering and Labeling
FR1.1: The team shall manually scrape early access game reviews from Steam.
FR1.2: The team shall preprocess the data.
FR1.3: The team shall label the reviews into 16 predefined categories for semi-supervised learning.

3.2 Model Fine Tuning
FR2.1: The system shall perform initial sentiment analysis of reviews, classifying them as positive or negative.
FR2.2: The system shall identify whether the negative reviews have bugs or not.
FR2.3: The system shall binary classify the reviews into separate predefined categories.

3.3 Data Visualization and Report Generation
FR3.1: The system shall enable a user to input a link to a specific game on Steam to generate a bug report.
FR3.2: The system shall provide the user with the option to upload a CSV file containing game reviews.
FR3.3: The system shall enable users to generate reports having classification of reviews in different categories.
FR3.4: The system shall enable users to view data visualizations related to sentiment analysis.
FR3.5: The system shall enable users to view data visualizations related to bug identification.
FR3.6: The system shall enable users to export/download reports in multiple formats (e.g., PDF or Word).

3.4 User Profile Management and Admin Module
FR4.1: The system shall allow users to view and update their personal details securely.
FR4.2: The system shall provide users with access to their previously generated reports.
FR4.3: The system shall allow users to manage their subscription plans.
FR4.4: The system shall allow the admin to manage user accounts (deactivate/reactivate).
FR4.5: The system shall provide the admin with access to overall system statistics.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Security Requirements
NFR1: The system will use AES-256 encryption for storing user data.

4.2 Performance Requirements
NFR2: The system shall process, classify reviews, and generate a report within a span of 5-10 minutes.

4.3 Compatibility Requirements
NFR3: The system shall support file format like CSV for upload.
NFR4: The system shall be able to scrape data from STEAM directly from the link.

4.4 Usability Requirements
NFR5: The UI shall be evaluated using Miller's principle, ensuring it is Minimal, Intuitive, Consistent, Observable, and Learnable.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: IntelliDatum - AI-Powered Unstructured Data Structuring Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document details the requirements for IntelliDatum, a smart data management web app that uses AI to automate the processing and structuring of unstructured data files into structured human-understandable dictionaries, developed in collaboration with Elixir Technologies.

1.2 Scope
To develop a user-friendly web application with a cloud-based backend that extracts structured information from heterogeneous data files (delimiter-separated, fixed-length, XML, line data). The AI model will learn patterns, infer field boundaries, and generate structured dictionaries. Additional features include schema validation, error detection, and a data mapping module to map data from dictionaries to document templates.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- CSV: Comma-Separated Values
- XML: Extensible Markup Language
- LLM: Large Language Model

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
IntelliDatum is a web-based platform that automates the manual process of extracting dictionaries from unstructured files, streamlining data analysis and management for enterprises.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- File Input (upload, type detection, metadata extraction)
- Delimiter Separated File Handler (delimiter detection, header detection, dictionary generation)
- Fixed Length File Handler (record length identification, dictionary generation)
- XML File Parser (parsing, dictionary generation)
- Line Data Handler (pattern recognition, heuristic-based field mapping)
- Web App (UI/UX, frontend/backend, integration)
- Data Mapping (message interpretation, template management, document generation)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Enterprise Users: Organizations with large amounts of incoming unstructured data (e.g., banks).
- IT Administrators: Oversee systems administration, user management, and data handling.
- Data Managers: Manage and organize data to make it usable.
- End Users (Clients): Users who want to convert data into specific formats with low technical skills.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application with a self-developed cloud-based backend service.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 File Input Module
FR1.1: The system shall allow the client to upload files in multiple formats (CSV, XML, fixed length, unstructured line data).
FR1.2: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface for file uploading.
FR1.3: The system shall display the progress of the file upload.
FR1.4: The system shall allow the user to upload at least 10 multiple files.
FR1.5: The system shall validate the size and format of the uploaded file.

3.2 Delimiter Separated File Handler Module
FR2.1: The system shall correctly identify the metadata of the file (single/multi record).
FR2.2: The system shall correctly identify the delimiter (e.g., ',', '|').
FR2.3: The system shall handle files with or without headers effectively.
FR2.4: The system shall generate tags of data by giving them user-friendly names.
FR2.5: The system should handle single/multiple records and documents effectively.

3.3 Fixed Length File Handler Module
FR3.1: The system shall extract the metadata of the file accurately.
FR3.2: The system shall process single and multiple records/documents effectively.
FR3.3: The system shall process fixed length based on inferred field lengths.
FR3.4: The system shall name identified fields with user-friendly names.

3.4 XML File Parser Module
FR4.1: The system shall process XML files using tags and attributes for effective tag generation.
FR4.2: The system shall give user-friendly names to generated tags.
FR4.3: The system shall support XML schema validation.

3.5 Line Data Handler Module
FR5.1: The system shall apply pattern recognition techniques to detect context and location of fields.
FR5.2: The system shall handle line data files without obvious structure.
FR5.3: The system shall use heuristic methods for field mapping.
FR5.4: The system shall give user-friendly names to detected fields.

3.6 Web App
FR6.1: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface for all functionalities.
FR6.2: The system shall allow easy navigation between different pages.
FR6.3: The system shall allow users to submit feedback for their actions.

3.7 Data Mapping
FR7.1: The system shall allow the user to input a message related to data to be interpreted and mapped onto a template.
FR7.2: The system shall support modification of the template.
FR7.3: The system shall allow the user to map extracted data fields to specific placeholders in the template.
FR7.4: The system shall automatically generate documents based on mapped data and selected templates.
FR7.5: The system shall allow the user to view the generated document before downloading.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: In case of failure, the system must roll back to the previous step and restore activity in less than 5 minutes.
NFR2: The system must implement backup logs for operations.
NFR3: When an error occurs during processing, the system shall generate a clear and detailed message and alert for any failures.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The interface shall be user-friendly with clear instructions and navigation.
NFR5: The application shall allow the user to view previous documents generated using a single interaction.
NFR6: The system shall provide clear and actionable steps in case of failure.
NFR7: The system will offer a responsive design to work on various device types.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR8: The system shall upload a file within 5 seconds for a size less than 10MB.
NFR9: Users can upload up to 10 files simultaneously.
NFR10: The system shall interpret the user's prompt within 10 seconds.
NFR11: The system shall map data to templates within 5 seconds.

4.4 Maintainability Requirements
NFR12: The system will use CI/CD pipelines for easy code maintenance and updates.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Quran Echo - AI-Powered Quranic Recitation Enhancement Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the technical and functional requirements for Quran Echo, an AI-powered tool for enhancing the user's pronunciation (Makharij) and style (Qira'at) of Quranic recitation through real-time analysis and feedback.

1.2 Scope
To build an AI-powered Quranic recitation tool that offers: real-time feedback on recitation, Makharij correction for individual letters (similar to Qaidah method), recitation style imitation of famous Qaris, a Hifz module for memorization tracking, error detection for common mistakes, personalized guidance, and progress visualization.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- Makharij: Points of articulation for Arabic letters
- Tajweed: Rules for Quranic recitation
- Hifz: Memorization of the Quran
- Qira'at: Different canonical methods of recitation
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Quran Echo is a web-based application that acts as a personal Quran tutor, using AI to provide accurate, personalized feedback to improve recitation skills.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User registration and profile management
- Makharij correction for individual letters (Haroof)
- Recitation recording and upload for detailed analysis
- Recitation style imitation of famous Qaris
- Hifz module for tracking memorization progress
- Real-time feedback on Tajweed and pronunciation
- Progress tracking and visualization

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Quran Learner: Limited knowledge of Tajweed and Makharij; relies on guided tools for improvement. Benefits from real-time feedback and personalized error correction.
- Advanced Reciter: Well-versed in Tajweed; uses the platform for fine-tuning phoneme duration and melody. Appreciates precise, technical feedback.
- Hifz Practitioner: Focuses on memorization; uses the Hifz module to track progress, identify areas for revision, and receive reminders.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Registration and Profile Management
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to create an account using email or social media.
FR1.2: The system shall enable users to log in, log out, and recover forgotten passwords.
FR1.3: Users shall be able to customize their profiles, including preferences for recitation tracking and Hifz progress.
FR1.4: The system shall store and display user recitation progress, past uploads, Makharij corrections, and Hifz tracking.

3.2 Makharij Correction for Individual Haroof
FR2.1: The system shall allow users to practice individual Haroof and receive real-time feedback on Makharij correction.
FR2.2: The system shall provide specific guidance on the correct articulation points (Makharij) for each letter.
FR2.3: Visual and audio feedback shall highlight mistakes in letter pronunciation and suggest corrections.
FR2.4: Users shall be able to repeat practice until correct pronunciation is achieved, with progress tracking.

3.3 Recitation Recording and Upload
FR3.1: The system shall allow users to upload recitation audio files (MP3, WAV).
FR3.2: The system shall analyze the uploaded audio and provide detailed feedback on errors and corrections.
FR3.3: Users shall receive a breakdown of Tajweed and pronunciation errors in a clear, structured format.

3.4 Recitation Style Imitation
FR4.1: The system shall allow users to select a Qari's recitation style to imitate.
FR4.2: The system shall compare the user's recitation with the selected Qari's style, focusing on pitch, tone, and rhythm.
FR4.3: The system shall track progress and provide feedback on improvements over time.

3.5 Hifz Module
FR5.1: The system shall allow users to select specific verses or surahs for Hifz.
FR5.2: The system shall track the user's memorization progress, highlighting sections needing improvement.
FR5.3: Users shall receive reminders and suggestions for revising previously memorized sections.
FR5.4: The system shall display progress tracking for Hifz completion.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system will have an MTBF of at least 1,000 hours.
NFR2: In case of failure, the user should be able to restart their session from the last saved progress.
NFR3: The system will include error detection strategies, such as logging errors and alerting the development team.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The system shall allow a user to upload a recitation file or begin a live recitation feedback session within three clicks.
NFR5: The platform will include clear instructions and on-screen error guidance.
NFR6: The interface will be designed for accessibility, including options for different text sizes and screen readers.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR7: The system will provide real-time recitation feedback within a few seconds after the user finishes a phrase.
NFR8: The audio file upload feature will support files up to 10MB and process them for feedback within 5 seconds.
NFR9: The user interface shall load fully in less than 3 seconds on a standard 5Mbps connection.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR10: The system shall prevent unauthorized access by implementing role-based access control (RBAC).
NFR11: All user data, including recitation audio, shall be encrypted during transmission and storage.
NFR12: The system will have measures in place to prevent unauthorized access, with an estimated time to breach of no less than 24 hours for a skilled attacker.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: CyberQuest - VR-Based Cybersecurity Awareness Game
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for CyberQuest, a 3D game-based intervention designed to increase cybersecurity awareness among teenagers and young adults in the Indo-Pak region by simulating realistic scenarios and teaching preventive measures.

1.2 Scope
To design, develop, and implement a 3D game (with VR integration) that simulates real-world locations (homes, universities, restaurants) and cyberattacks (phishing, social engineering, malware). The game features a social life storyline, reputation and mood bar system, in-game economy (coins), and educational content. It covers common cyberattacks but excludes others like DDoS and SQL injection.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- VR: Virtual Reality
- NPC: Non-Player Character
- UI: User Interface
- UX: User Experience

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
CyberQuest is an immersive educational game that uses VR and interactive storytelling to teach cybersecurity concepts, focusing on player decision-making and its consequences within a simulated daily life.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Game Mechanics (player movement, object interaction)
- Game Elements (visual design of environments, game objects)
- Assets Creation (environment, characters)
- Game Scenarios (storylines, narrative building, scenario integration)
- UI/UX (intuitive menus, game flow objects)
- VR Integration (VR-ready controls, optimization)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Students: Primary users (16-25) who will face cybersecurity threats in virtual real-world locations.
- Faculty Members: Cybersecurity professionals or experts who might use the game for training or evaluation.
- Cybersecurity Experts: Evaluate the game's accuracy and effectiveness in teaching concepts; may provide feedback.
- Game Administrator: Maintains the game, updates scenarios, and ensures VR platform functionality.

2.4 Operating Environment
PC game optimized for VR platforms, including Oculus Quest 2.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Interface (UI) & User Experience (UX)
FR1.1: The system shall provide a simple, intuitive, and consistent UI for navigating menus, settings, and tutorials.
FR1.2: The game shall offer real-time visual feedback in response to player actions.
FR1.3: The game shall allow the player to view basic information about their overall performance, level, and scores.
FR1.4: The system shall include a "Mood Bar" reflecting the player's social status with two states: "happy" and "dead inside".
FR1.5: The system shall guide players through challenges with contextual prompts.
FR1.6: The audio subsystem shall adjust ambient sounds and music based on player interactions.

3.2 Virtual Reality Integration
FR2.1: The system shall be compatible with VR headsets, including Oculus.
FR2.2: VR environments shall simulate real-world locations (buses, universities, restaurants).
FR2.3: The system shall allow players to interact with NPCs through dialogue and decision-making.
FR2.4: Player decisions in virtual environments shall dynamically change the scenario and storyline.

3.3 Cybersecurity Attack Simulation
FR3.1: The game shall simulate cyberattacks (phishing, malware, ransomware) in daily life scenarios.
FR3.2: The system shall provide educational content for each simulated attack, explaining its nature and preventive measures.
FR3.3: After a successful attack, the system shall provide players with damage control strategies.

3.4 Reputation & Social Points System
FR4.1: The system shall adjust the player's reputation based on their responses to social interactions and cyberattacks.
FR4.2: The game shall reward positive actions with social points and in-game currency (coins) and penalize negative actions.
FR4.3: The "Mood Bar" shall visually reflect the player's accumulated social points.
FR4.4: Losing all social points shall result in failure of the current level.

3.5 Educational Content & Tutorials
FR5.1: The game shall provide real-time feedback and hints to reinforce proper responses to cyberattacks.
FR5.2: After an attack, the system shall give preventive tips to avoid similar attacks in future levels.

3.6 Game Progression & Levels
FR6.1: The game shall consist of multiple levels, each representing a different phase of life (e.g., university, early career).
FR6.2: Players shall unlock new virtual environments by completing tasks and reaching milestones.
FR6.3: Each level shall present increasingly complex challenges requiring application of cybersecurity knowledge.

3.7 Coins & Rewards System
FR7.1: The game shall reward players with coins for making good decisions (e.g., protecting personal data, avoiding attacks).
FR7.2: Players shall use earned coins to unlock in-game items or progress.
FR7.3: The system shall provide visual and auditory feedback when coins are earned.

3.8 Decision-Making & Scenario Selection
FR8.1: The system shall present players with decisions that impact the game's storyline and future challenges.
FR8.2: The outcome of these decisions shall dynamically alter the next set of challenges.
FR8.3: Player decisions shall influence the behavior of NPCs.

3.9 Non-Player Characters (NPCs) Interaction
FR9.1: NPCs shall exhibit different behaviors based on the player's decisions.
FR9.2: NPCs shall act as attackers, mentors, or allies depending on the context.
FR9.3: The player shall interact with NPCs through dialogues and decision points.

3.10 Task & Milestone Management
FR10.1: The game shall feature milestones like reaching academic or personal goals.
FR10.2: The system shall present challenges and tasks in each level that must be completed to move forward.
FR10.3: Each task must integrate a learning component related to cybersecurity.

3.11 Level Failure & Restart Mechanism
FR11.1: Players shall lose a level if they reach the "dead inside" state on the mood bar.
FR11.2: The game shall provide checkpoints within levels.
FR11.3: The system shall offer tips or guidance when players fail a level.

3.12 Daily Life Simulation
FR12.1: The game shall simulate daily activities (e.g., traveling on a bus, attending classes).
FR12.2: Daily activities must be interwoven with cybersecurity education (e.g., using free Wi-Fi safely).

3.13 Level Save Progression Management
FR13.1: The game shall automatically save player progress at predefined checkpoints.
FR13.2: Players shall have the option to manually save their current progress.
FR13.3: The game shall allow players to load their saved progress.

3.14 Performance
FR14.1: The player shall be able to view their current performance.
FR14.2: The player shall be able to interact with the updated gaming based on their performance.

3.15 Ending
FR15.1: The game shall end once the player successfully reaches their office milestone.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The game shall recover from unexpected crashes within 10 seconds and restore progress to the last saved checkpoint.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR2: The VR system shall allow users to easily navigate menus using simple hand gestures or VR controllers.
NFR3: The game must be simple and have a user-friendly interactive interface.
NFR4: The game shall provide on-screen prompts to guide players.
NFR5: The game must display error messages related to gameplay.
NFR6: The game must allow users to pause, save, and resume at any time.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR7: The game must load scenarios within 10 seconds.
NFR8: System shall be responsive towards the user's actions.
NFR9: The system shall ensure that all in-game data processing is completed within 1 second.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR10: Profile of one user shall not be accessed by any other user.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: InsightWire - News Aggregation with Bias Detection
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
In today's Information Age, abundance of news media has led to polarization caused by biased news sources. News companies often have their own agendas, controlling narratives for political and social plans. The layman blindly trusts these news sources.

1.2 Scope
InsightWire aggregates news articles from 18-20 political news sources, providing bias detection classification (left, center, right), contextual background with timelines, story comparison across outlets, news analytics, and an AI chatbot with RAG integration for user interaction and summarization.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
- LLM: Large Language Model
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
InsightWire provides a balanced view of current events, helping users identify and understand inherent biases while offering deeper understanding of news landscape through analytics.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- News aggregation from diverse sources
- Categorization and tagging
- Customization and personalization
- Local news highlighting
- AI chatbot with summarization
- User feedback and comments
- Bias detection classification
- Story comparison with tone/language highlighting
- Contextual background timeline
- News analytics for trend identification

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- General News Consumers: Seek reliable, balanced news sources
- Researchers and Analysts: Conduct in-depth research on news trends
- News Providers: Contribute content to reach broader users
- IT and Development: Responsible for development, maintenance, updates
- Potential Investors: Interested in growth and potential of InsightWire

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, distributed NoSQL database (MongoDB), Chrome browser compatible.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 News Aggregation, Categorization, and Personalization
FR1.1: The system shall retrieve and aggregate news articles from 18-20 sources.
FR1.2: The system shall only retrieve articles categorized as political.
FR1.3: The system shall group articles about the same event together.
FR1.4: The system shall tag articles with publication source, date, and political bias classification.
FR1.5: The system shall allow users to create profiles.
FR1.6: The system shall enable users to customize news feed by selecting interests and political balance levels.
FR1.7: The system shall determine user location to highlight relevant local news.

3.2 Chatbot, User Interaction, and Feedback
FR2.1: The system shall include a chatbot responding to user queries.
FR2.2: The chatbot shall summarize specific news articles.
FR2.3: The chatbot shall provide insights into themes/trends across minimum 3 sources for same story.
FR2.4: The system shall allow users to submit reviews on articles.
FR2.5: The system shall allow users to leave comments on articles.

3.3 Bias Detection and Story Comparison
FR3.1: The system shall analyze political bias, classifying into left, center, right.
FR3.2: The system shall, upon request, display articles from two sources on same topic with highlighted differences.
FR3.3: The system shall generate summary of detected bias for each story with single-click access.

3.4 Contextual Background and News Analytics
FR4.1: The system shall display timeline of related historical events for each story.
FR4.2: The system shall perform analytics on news articles, identifying trends or predictions mentioned in content.
FR4.3: The system shall allow users to filter contextual timeline by date.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: System uptime ≥ 99.9%.
NFR2: Aggregation shall continue even if up to 2 external sources fail.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Intuitive user interface with clear navigation paths.
NFR4: Users shall customize interface to enhance experience.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: System shall handle up to 200 concurrent users without degradation.
NFR6: Chatbot response time ≤ 10 seconds.
NFR7: News analytics retrieval ≤ 5 minutes.

4.4 Scalability
NFR8: Distributed NoSQL database (MongoDB) shall allow scaling as users and articles increase.

4.5 Portability
NFR9: System accessible on Chrome without additional plugins.
NFR10: Responsive design across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.

4.6 Security
NFR11: User authentication via HTTPS ensuring encrypted data transmission.
NFR12: Role-based access control (RBAC) for sensitive operations.
NFR13: Strong password requirements (≥ 8 characters, uppercase, number, special character).
NFR14: User input sanitization to prevent XSS attacks.

4.7 Availability
NFR15: 24/7 availability with planned maintenance ≤ 2 hours/month.
NFR16: Users notified of planned maintenance ≥ 24 hours in advance.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Hifz Quran - Intelligent Quran Memorization Assistant
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for an intelligent Quran memorization assistant application that adapts to individual learning preferences, pace, and achievement levels, making the memorization process more efficient and engaging.

1.2 Scope
To develop a mobile application that helps users memorize the Quran. Features include: user profile and progress management, automatic ayah segmentation for easier memorization, personalized learning paths, interactive memorization modes (guided, recitation, listening), detection of similar verses (Mutashabihat), real-time recitation feedback, and notifications/reminders.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- Ayah: Verse of the Quran
- Surah: Chapter of the Quran
- Mutashabihat: Similar verses in the Quran
- MVC: Model-View-Controller
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The application is a mobile-based, AI-powered assistant that provides a structured, personalized, and supportive environment for memorizing the Quran, leveraging speech recognition and adaptive learning techniques.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User Profile and Progress Management (registration, profile, tracking, goals)
- Ayah Segmentation and Memorization (automatic segmentation, guided practice)
- Personalized Learning Path (learning style analysis, adaptive repetition)
- Interactive Memorization Modes (guided, recitation, listening)
- Mutashabihat Detection and Management (detect, practice, differentiate)
- Real-Time Recitation Feedback (speech recognition, error detection, suggestions)
- Notifications and Reminders (daily reminders, progress alerts)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- User (Memorizer): Person involved in the memorization process. Manages personal info, goals, and progress.
- System: Manages the core functionality of the app.

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application (iOS and Android).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Profile and Progress Management
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to register and log in using email/password or Gmail.
FR1.2: The system shall support profile customization (profile picture).
FR1.3: The system shall track the user's memorized verses and review history.
FR1.4: The system shall display a personalized dashboard with analytics on performance and progress.
FR1.5: Users shall be able to set memorization goals.
FR1.6: The system shall send reminders based on these goals.

3.2 Personalized Learning Path
FR2.1: The system shall analyze users' learning styles (listening or reciting).
FR2.2: The system shall adjust the repetition strategy based on the user's performance.
FR2.3: The system shall generate a personalized memorization plan for each user.

3.3 Ayah Segmentation and Memorization
FR3.1: The system shall automatically segment Ayahs into manageable chunks according to user performance.
FR3.2: The system shall be able to follow all Quran rules (waqf rules/Ayah stopping rules).

3.4 Interactive Memorization Modes
FR4.1: The system shall offer a mode where users follow guided steps to memorize verses.
FR4.2: Users shall be able to recite verses in real time.
FR4.3: The system shall provide a listening mode with playback and repeat functions.
FR4.4: The system shall take a test to modify user performance.

3.5 Real-Time Recitation Feedback
FR5.1: The system shall use speech recognition to detect errors in real-time.
FR5.2: Users shall receive feedback on their recitation, with errors highlighted and corrections suggested.

3.6 Mutashabihat Detection and Management
FR6.1: The system shall detect similar verses that could confuse users.
FR6.2: The system shall offer a special practice for differentiating similar verses.

3.7 Notifications and Reminders
FR7.1: The system shall send daily, weekly, and monthly reminders for memorization goals.
FR7.2: Users shall receive alerts when they achieve their goals.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance Requirements
NFR1: The system shall provide real-time recitation feedback with minimal latency.
NFR2: The system shall handle multiple users concurrently without degradation in performance.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR3: The interface shall be intuitive and easy to navigate for users of all ages.
NFR4: The system shall be accessible for users with varying levels of technical proficiency.
NFR5: The system shall work on mobile (iOS and Android).

4.3 Reliability Requirements
NFR6: The system shall ensure that user data is always consistent and accurate.
NFR7: The system shall ensure availability for users at all times.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR8: User data, including personal information and progress, shall be encrypted.
NFR9: The system shall implement secure authentication.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: GlaciArc - Glacier Risk Prediction and Monitoring Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for GlaciArc, a web application designed for monitoring glaciers in Gilgit Baltistan and predicting risks related to Glacier Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) through satellite imagery analysis.

1.2 Scope
To develop a user-friendly, web-based monitoring and predictive platform for glacier-related hazards. Features include: satellite image acquisition and processing, machine learning model training (e.g., U-Net) for glacier and infrastructure classification, risk zone identification, and interactive map visualization of predictions and risk assessments.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- GLOF: Glacier Lake Outburst Flood
- ML: Machine Learning
- RF: Random Forest
- U-Net: Convolutional neural network architecture
- YOLO: You Only Look Once

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
GlaciArc is a web-based analytical tool that leverages satellite data and machine learning to provide actionable insights for disaster preparedness in northern Pakistan.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Collection (satellite image acquisition, data processing)
- Model Evaluation (model selection, model training)
- Testing (performance evaluation, comparative analysis)
- Web Application Development (UI design, map visualization, integration of ML models)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Researcher: Analyzes glacier data and predictions for research purposes.
- Administrator: Manages user accounts, oversees system performance, ensures data integrity.
- End User: Views glacier data and predictions via the dashboard.
- Stakeholder: Individuals/organizations interested in the project's outcomes (government, NGOs).

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Collection
FR1.1: The system shall be able to acquire satellite imagery data for specified glacier areas in Gilgit Baltistan.
FR1.2: The system shall be able to preprocess the collected satellite imagery data to create a usable dataset.
FR1.3: The system shall be able to crop satellite images to focus on specific glacier regions.
FR1.4: The system shall be able to label the processed data.
FR1.5: The system shall store the processed dataset in an organized manner.

3.2 Model Evaluation
FR2.1: The system shall select appropriate ML models (U-Net, Random Forest, YOLO) aligning with project requirements.
FR2.2: The system shall prepare training data by organizing it into suitable formats (training/validation sets).
FR2.3: The system shall train the selected models on the prepared dataset.
FR2.4: The system shall monitor training progress by tracking parameters like loss and accuracy.

3.3 Testing
FR3.1: System shall compute the trained models on performance metrics (accuracy, precision) and provide visuals to compare models.
FR3.2: System shall analyze errors and allow parameter tuning or adjustments.
FR3.3: System shall generate a report to compare each model in terms of accuracy, precision, and computational efficiency.

3.4 Web Application Development
FR4.1: The web app shall be appealing and responsive. Users can access targeted glaciers to see satellite images, classified images, predictions (red/green zones), and reports for comparative analysis.
FR4.2: The system shall be able to map output georeferenced satellite images onto a Google Earth map.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall maintain an MTBF of at least 1,000 hours.
NFR2: Regular data backups and performance logging will be implemented.
NFR3: The system will employ robust exception handling and a rollback mechanism.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: Users shall be able to perform common tasks (e.g., downloading images, viewing predictions) within three clicks.
NFR5: New users should comprehend basic functionalities within 30 minutes.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR6: The image preprocessing module must process images within 5 minutes.
NFR7: The system shall respond to user interactions within 5 seconds for 95% of operations.
NFR8: The prediction module shall manage historical movement data from at least 5 years.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR9: The system shall ensure data protection by preventing unauthorized access.
NFR10: The system shall support role-based access control.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: TheWeatherMind - AI-Driven Localized Weather Forecasting System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for TheWeatherMind, an AI-driven weather forecasting application designed to provide accurate, localized predictions for Pakistan, reducing reliance on external models and providing a revenue model through data purchase.

1.2 Scope
To build a system with four major phases: 1) Developing an AI model (LSTM) trained on local weather station, radar, and satellite data for Islamabad, Lahore, and Karachi. 2) Developing a user-friendly front-end for web and mobile. 3) Creating a content generation module (text, audio, video) using real-time data. 4) Integrating blockchain-based smart contracts for secure purchase of weather data.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
TheWeatherMind is a localized weather intelligence platform that combines AI prediction, engaging content generation, and a blockchain-based data marketplace.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Model training for precise weather prediction (integrating station, radar, satellite data)
- Front-End Design (web and mobile apps for forecasts, news, data purchase)
- Weather News Reporting (text, audio, video generation using LLMs and avatars)
- Blockchain Based Transactions (smart contracts for secure data purchase, payment verification, audit trail)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Weather Enthusiasts: Interested in personal use (planning activities). Use mobile app.
- Agriculture Professionals: Farmers/consultants relying on accurate predictions for irrigation/harvesting.
- City Planners/Government Officials: Need forecasts for severe weather conditions. Likely use desktop web app.
- Weather Data Purchasers: Companies/organizations buying localized data. Use web app.
- Weather Reporters: Produce content for public. Use web/mobile app.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web and mobile applications with cloud-based backend.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Weather Prediction Engine
FR1.1: The system shall collect real-time data from weather stations and radar.
FR1.2: The AI model shall process and analyze data to generate accurate localized forecasts.
FR1.3: The model shall provide predictions for specific geographic locations (Islamabad, Lahore, Karachi).
FR1.4: The system shall update weather forecasts at hourly intervals.
FR1.5: The model shall allow for continuous training and improvement as new data is collected.

3.2 Forecast Interface
FR2.1: The front-end shall display real-time weather forecasts (temperature, precipitation, humidity, dew).
FR2.2: Users shall be able to select specific locations to view localized predictions.
FR2.3: The design shall incorporate weather updates in textual, audio, and video formats.
FR2.4: The design shall integrate blockchain-based transactions for data purchase.

3.3 Weather Content Generator
FR3.1: The system shall generate weather forecasts in text, audio, and video formats using real-time data.
FR3.2: Text prompts shall be automatically generated based on weather data to create human-readable content.
FR3.3: The audio and video generation shall provide accurate spoken forecasts using NLP.
FR3.4: The module shall allow users to switch between content formats.

3.4 Transaction System
FR4.1: The system shall allow users to securely purchase localized weather data using blockchain technology.
FR4.2: The blockchain shall record every transaction in an immutable ledger.
FR4.3: Users shall be able to verify the authenticity of weather data transactions.
FR4.4: The system shall provide a seamless and transparent process for data transactions.
FR4.5: The transaction system shall support payments in local currency.
FR4.6: The system shall notify users once transactions are successfully completed.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall aim for an MTBF of at least 600 hours.
NFR2: A failure is defined as providing inaccurate forecasts or failing to display information correctly.
NFR3: The system shall implement monitoring and diagnostics for proactive issue identification.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The system shall allow a user to access the localized weather forecast for their selected location within two clicks from the homepage.
NFR5: The interface shall provide clear visual feedback (e.g., loading indicators).
NFR6: The system shall enable users to switch to the weather news screen and generate a video within a minute.
NFR7: The application shall allow users to search for weather data for any specified location within 7 seconds.
NFR8: Users can select up to 5 specific weather parameters for a downloadable file and finalize selection within 3 clicks.
NFR9: The blockchain-based purchase process shall provide confirmation within 10 seconds of payment initiation.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR10: 95% of weather forecast data shall be generated and displayed within 7 seconds of the user's request.
NFR11: The system shall support at least 100 concurrent users without degradation.
NFR12: Real-time updates shall be processed and delivered within 1 minute of data collection.
NFR13: The system shall allow for weather data retrieval from the database with a maximum latency of 5 seconds.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR14: Robust security measures shall protect sensitive data and ensure user privacy.
NFR15: Regular security evaluations shall be conducted to identify vulnerabilities.

4.5 Scalability Requirements
NFR16: The system shall support up to 100 simultaneous users without degradation.
NFR17: The infrastructure shall allow for horizontal scaling.

4.6 Portability Requirements
NFR18: The system shall be accessible from various platforms (web browsers, iOS, Android) without significant code changes.
NFR19: The system shall work seamlessly on all modern web browsers.

4.7 Extensibility Requirements
NFR20: The system shall allow the integration of new weather data sources or third-party APIs with minimal modification.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Decentra - Decentralized Marketplace on Blockchain
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document defines the requirements for Decentra, a decentralized marketplace built on the Base blockchain that allows users to trade goods and services directly without intermediaries, ensuring transparency, privacy, and low transaction fees.

1.2 Scope
To develop a decentralized protocol (collection of smart contracts) and a client web application that allows users to interact with it. Key features include: cryptocurrency-based transactions, escrow functionality (direct, double-deposit, notary), dispute resolution mechanisms, IPFS-based storage for images/reviews, ratings and reviews system, and notary/voting pools for arbitration.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- IPFS: InterPlanetary File System
- P2P: Peer-to-Peer
- API: Application Programming Interface
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Decentra is a permissionless, anti-censorship platform that eliminates middlemen, enabling peer-to-peer trading secured by smart contracts on the Base blockchain.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Trade Protocol (smart contracts for direct payment, double-deposit escrow, notary escrow)
- IPFS for hosting product images and reviews (tamper-proof storage)
- Decentra Client Application (web app for merchants, buyers, notaries)
- Notaries and Voting Pools (arbitration services)
- Ratings and Reviews system (tamper-proof, permanent)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Individual Sellers: Small business owners, artisans. Value autonomy, control, and privacy.
- Buyers: Seek diverse products, competitive pricing, and a secure, private shopping experience.
- Marketplace Operators: Focus on establishing niche marketplaces; need tools for managing multiple vendors.
- High-Trust Individuals: Can provide escrow services, building trust within the marketplace.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application interacting with the Base blockchain and IPFS.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Trade Protocol
FR1.1: The system must allow buyers to make direct payments to sellers without escrow when trust is established.
FR1.2: The system must implement a 2-of-2 multisignature smart contract for double-deposit escrow.
FR1.3: The system must support a 2-of-3 or n-of-m multisignature smart contract involving a designated notary.
FR1.4: The system must allow for the definition of trade type and validity period.
FR1.5: The system must maintain identifiers such as network ID, account public key, and PGP public key for each transaction.
FR1.6: The system must store information related to the goods or services being traded.
FR1.7: The system must provide a mechanism for handling disputes between peers.

3.2 IPFS for Hosting Product Listing Images and Reviews
FR2.1: The system must store high-resolution images of products on IPFS.
FR2.2: The system must host user reviews on IPFS, with each review stored as a separate file accessible via unique content hashes.
FR2.3: The system must leverage IPFS's content-addressable system for retrieving product images and reviews.

3.3 Decentra Client Application (Web App)
FR3.1: The application must allow users to select roles (merchant, buyer, notary, arbiter) with tailored features.
FR3.2: Merchants must be able to set up stores, list products, and manage sales.
FR3.3: Buyers must be able to search, purchase, and manage payment transactions.
FR3.4: Notaries must provide arbitration services and validate user reviews.
FR3.5: The application must connect with smart contracts on the Base chain and access images on IPFS.
FR3.6: Users must be able to provide ratings and written reviews for merchants and products.

3.4 Notaries and Voting Pools
FR4.1: The system must allow notaries to provide arbitration and validation services.
FR4.2: Users must be able to manually select notaries or rely on suggestions, ensuring mutual agreement.
FR4.3: The system must enable the formation of voting pools to minimize collusion risk during arbitration.
FR4.4: The notary pool must be able to issue digital rulings and receive fees for moderating trades.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system must have a high level of reliability, ensuring minimal downtime.
NFR2: In case of failure, the system should ensure data is not lost and users are informed.
NFR3: Robust error detection and automated recovery strategies should restore normal operation within one hour.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The platform must be easy to use for both novice and experienced users, abstracting away blockchain complexity.
NFR5: Wallet authentication and order placement should not require more than a few straightforward steps.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR6: Transactions (purchasing, listing) should be processed within a few seconds, ideally no more than 5 seconds.
NFR7: Search operations should provide results in under 1 second.
NFR8: The platform should handle up to 10,000 concurrent users without noticeable decline in performance.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR9: All user interactions, including wallet-based authentication, must be secure against threats like phishing or man-in-the-middle attacks.
NFR10: Users must securely manage their private keys; there should be no centralized recovery options.
NFR11: The system should encrypt all sensitive data in transit and at rest (AES-256).
NFR12: The system must be resilient against DDoS attacks.
NFR13: All critical operations must be logged for auditing.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Digi-Guard - Parental Control Application
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for Digi-Guard, a parental control mobile application that enables parents to manage and track their children's digital activities, providing real-time monitoring, location tracking, app restrictions, and screen time management.

1.2 Scope
To build a platform for parents to control and safeguard their child's digital presence. The app will block/limit indecent content, monitor real-time device usage, track location, and allow remote control of device features (e.g., microphone, camera) and keylogging. The scope is defined by functions like app restrictions, screen time management, location tracking, and real-time monitoring.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- GPS: Global Positioning System
- SOS: Emergency signal
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Digi-Guard is a comprehensive mobile solution for parents to ensure their children's online safety by providing detailed, real-time insights and controls.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Real-time device monitoring (live screen, activity logs, keylogging)
- Location tracking (GPS-based tracking, geofencing)
- App restrictions (app blocking, time-based restrictions)
- Screen time management (daily/weekly limits, automatic locking)
- Remote controls (remote device locking, app enable/disable)
- Notifications and alerts (geofence, screen time alerts)
- Emergency SOS feature (SOS button with location sharing)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Parent: Primary user who wants to monitor and control their child's device usage. May vary in tech-savviness.
- Child: Secondary user whose device is monitored. Has limited access, primarily receiving alerts.

2.4 Operating Environment
Android mobile application communicating with a backend service (Firebase/Google Maps API).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Real-Time Device Monitoring
FR1.1: The application shall allow the parent to see a live feed of the child's screen.
FR1.2: The application shall provide real-time indications of active applications.
FR1.3: The application shall log the child's app usage for review.
FR1.4: The application shall allow parents to access the activity log and review app usage history.
FR1.5: The application shall log keystrokes and provide reports to the parent.

3.2 Module 2: Location Tracking Feature
FR2.1: The application shall display the child's real-time location on a map.
FR2.2: The application shall use GPS to provide exact location tracking.
FR2.3: The application shall allow parents to define geofencing areas (safe zones).
FR2.4: The application shall notify the parent if the child leaves or enters a geofenced area.

3.3 Module 3: App Restrictions
FR3.1: The application shall allow parents to block specific applications on the child's device.
FR3.2: The application shall implement time-based restrictions for specific apps.
FR3.3: The application shall notify in real-time if the child attempts to use a restricted app.
FR3.4: The application shall record each blocked attempt for the parent to view.

3.4 Module 4: Screen Time Management
FR4.1: The application shall allow setting daily or weekly limits on screen time.
FR4.2: The application shall automatically lock the child's device when the screen time limit is exceeded.
FR4.3: The application shall provide an overview of the child's screen usage (daily/weekly).
FR4.4: The application shall alert the parent when the child approaches or exceeds the limit.

3.5 Module 5: Remote Controls
FR5.1: The application shall allow parents to remotely lock the child's device.
FR5.2: The application shall allow parents to remotely enable or disable specific applications.
FR5.3: The application shall notify the parent when a remote control action is activated.
FR5.4: The application shall log all remote control operations.

3.6 Module 6: Notifications and Alerts
FR6.1: The application shall send geofence alerts when the child leaves or returns to a safe area.
FR6.2: The application shall send screen time alerts when the child approaches or exceeds the limit.
FR6.3: The application shall alert the parent if the child breaches app restrictions or screen time limits.
FR6.4: The application shall provide a notification history.

3.7 Module 7: Emergency SOS Feature
FR7.1: The application shall provide an SOS button that, when pressed, sends an emergency signal with GPS coordinates to the parent.
FR7.2: The system shall automatically send the child's location to the parent at a specified interval (e.g., every minute) upon SOS activation.
FR7.3: The system shall display the child's location on a map in real-time upon SOS receipt.
FR7.4: The application shall automatically cease sending location updates after the parent reports the emergency is resolved.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall have an MTBF of at least 6 months.
NFR2: The system must automatically detect and classify failures (minor/major). For major failures, the parent shall be notified immediately.
NFR3: In case of a major failure, the system shall attempt to restart affected services within 10 seconds.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: First-time users shall accomplish simple tasks (e.g., setting screen time limits) within 10 minutes.
NFR5: Key features must be accessible from the home screen within 3 taps.
NFR6: The system shall confirm all critical operations (e.g., locking the device).
NFR7: The app shall display clear error messages and offer a way to correct them.
NFR8: The app shall allow parents to set app restrictions within 1 minute.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR9: Real-time monitoring latency shall be within 3 seconds.
NFR10: Location data shall be retrieved and displayed within 3 seconds, with accuracy within a 10-meter radius under optimal conditions.
NFR11: Geofencing alerts shall be sent within 5 seconds after the child crosses a boundary.
NFR12: App restrictions or device locks shall be enforced within 5 seconds of the parent's command.
NFR13: Data sync and backup shall occur every 15 minutes and complete within 2 minutes.
NFR14: Notifications and alerts shall be delivered within 5 seconds of the trigger event.
NFR15: The app shall load completely and be ready for use within 5 seconds.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR16: The system shall allow access only to authorized users, locking accounts after 5 failed login attempts.
NFR17: Sensitive data (location, activity logs) shall be stored and transmitted securely.
NFR18: Data related to a child shall only be accessible by the authenticated parent.
NFR19: Backup data shall be encrypted and require high-level authentication for access.
