================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: IntraSec - Client-less Network Risk Assessment and Asset Management Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document defines the requirements for IntraSec, a client-less risk assessment and asset management tool designed to provide real-time inventory of network assets and identify security risks without requiring agent installation on individual machines.

1.2 Scope
To develop and deploy a client-less system for internal networks that discovers and inventories devices, services, and software; continuously tracks assets; integrates with the NIST vulnerability database to assess risks; assigns risk scores; generates actionable reports; and automates patch deployment. It also includes continuous monitoring, real-time alerts, and notifications.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CVE: Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures
- CPE: Common Platform Enumeration
- NIST: National Institute of Standards and Technology
- API: Application Programming Interface
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control
- MFA: Multi-Factor Authentication

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
IntraSec is a centralized, automated security solution for internal networks, operating via microservices and RESTful APIs, designed to improve security posture and asset management.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Asset discovery and management (real-time inventory)
- Risk assessment (integration with CVE/CPE databases, risk scoring)
- Patch management (repository and deployment)
- Reporting (vulnerability summaries, recommendations)
- Alerts and notifications

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Network Security Team: Uses IntraSec to assess vulnerabilities and take proactive measures for threat detection and mitigation.
- IT Administrators: Oversee installation, configuration, and integration of IntraSec with existing tools.
- System Administrators: Manage the technical side, including patch deployments and addressing vulnerabilities.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based application with backend microservices communicating via RESTful APIs, using Elasticsearch and PostgreSQL databases.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Assets Inventory
FR1.1: The system shall discover and display all devices, software, and services in real-time.
FR1.2: The system shall allow users to view, filter, and manage asset details through a front-end interface.
FR1.3: The system shall continuously track changes in asset details and update them in the PostgreSQL database.
FR1.4: The system shall integrate with Active Directory to centralize asset management.

3.2 Module 2: Risk Assessment
FR2.1: The system shall perform vulnerability scanning of assets using ElasticSearch.
FR2.2: The system shall continuously sync with the NIST Vulnerability Database API.
FR2.3: The system shall calculate and assign risk scores based on the severity of detected vulnerabilities.
FR2.4: The system shall provide a dashboard to display risk scores, vulnerability details, and remediation recommendations.

3.3 Module 3: Report Generation
FR3.1: The system shall generate standardized reports including identified vulnerabilities, severity, and risk score.
FR3.2: The system shall provide exportable reports for auditing and documentation.
FR3.3: The system shall generate detailed analysis reports with remediation recommendations.
FR3.4: The system shall allow users to view, filter, and export reports.

3.4 Module 4: Patch Deployment
FR4.1: The system shall automate the deployment of patches for identified vulnerabilities.
FR4.2: The system shall provide an interface to monitor the status of patch deployment.
FR4.3: The system shall notify users if manual intervention is required when automatic patch deployment fails.
FR4.4: The system shall report the progress and status of patch deployment.

3.5 Module 5: Alerts and Notifications
FR5.1: The system shall generate real-time alerts for detected vulnerabilities or security incidents.
FR5.2: The system shall allow users to customize alert thresholds for specific types of vulnerabilities.
FR5.3: The system shall provide an interface for users to view active alerts and notifications.
FR5.4: The system shall send notifications for vulnerabilities requiring immediate action.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system shall aim for an up-time of at least 95% during operational hours.
NFR2: In case of failure, the system shall log the event and notify administrators within 5 minutes.
NFR3: The system shall recover from critical failures and resume operation within 10 minutes.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: Users shall be able to perform key tasks such as viewing asset information and generating reports in 3 interactions or fewer.
NFR5: 80% of new users shall be able to navigate and complete common tasks without needing external documentation after 10 minutes.

4.3 Performance Requirements
NFR6: The system shall be able to generate an asset inventory report for up to 100 assets within approximately 15 minutes.
NFR7: The dashboard page shall fully load within 5 seconds for 80% of users on a >10 Mbps connection.
NFR8: Database queries shall return results within 5 seconds for typical operations.

4.4 Security Requirements
NFR9: 80% of administrative users shall be required to use multi-factor authentication (MFA) for logging in.
NFR10: The system shall maintain logs of access to sensitive data for at least 3 months.
NFR11: The system shall lock user accounts after 5 consecutive failed login attempts, with notification sent to the administrator within 10 minutes.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: WorkSync - AI-Powered Video Conferencing Platform with Deepfake Detection
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for WorkSync, a next-generation video conferencing platform that integrates advanced AI technologies including facial recognition for secure authentication, real-time deepfake detection, AI-generated backgrounds, and built-in collaboration tools to enhance user experience and security in remote communication.

1.2 Scope
The platform provides high-quality video conferencing with integrated features: real-time deepfake detection to flag manipulated media, AI-generated seamless backgrounds, facial recognition for secure logins, built-in collaboration tools (real-time document sharing, live chat), meeting recording with transcription, adaptive video quality, and administration/analytics dashboards. It integrates with third-party tools like Google Workspace.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- CNN: Convolutional Neural Network
- GAN: Generative Adversarial Network
- BNN: Binary Neural Network
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
WorkSync addresses the growing need for secure, feature-rich video conferencing tools by integrating AI-driven security (deepfake detection, facial recognition) and productivity features (collaboration tools) into a single platform, overcoming limitations of existing solutions.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Facial Recognition-based Login: Secure authentication using facial data.
- Traditional Login/Signup: Fallback authentication method.
- Role-based Access Control: Admin, Organizer, User roles with different permissions.
- Real-time Deepfake Detection: Analyze video streams, flag manipulated content, log incidents.
- AI-Generated Backgrounds: Select or generate dynamic backgrounds.
- Video Conferencing: High-quality calls, scheduling, screen sharing.
- Document Sharing and Collaboration: Real-time editing of shared documents.
- Live Chat: Real-time text chat alongside video.
- Adaptive Video Quality: Dynamic adjustment based on bandwidth.
- Meeting Recording and Transcription: Record meetings and generate automatic transcriptions.
- User and Session Management: Admin manage accounts and sessions.
- Meeting Analytics: Insights on duration, participants, engagement.
- Data Encryption: End-to-end encryption for all communications.
- Integration with Collaboration Tools: Seamless integration with Google Workspace.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- (User classes are implied: Meeting participants, administrators, etc. The document focuses on functional and non-functional requirements.)

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Authentication and Authorization
FR1.1.1: The system shall allow users to log in using facial recognition for secure authentication.
FR1.1.2: The system shall capture and store user facial data during signup for identity verification.
FR1.1.3: The system shall reject access for non-verified users or unauthorized individuals.
FR1.2.1: The system shall provide traditional authentication options (email/password) as a fallback.
FR1.2.2: The system shall send a verification email upon registration.
FR1.3.1: The system shall support multiple roles (Admin, Organizer, User), each with different permissions.
FR1.3.2: Admin users shall be able to manage user roles and access rights.
FR1.3.3: The system shall provide a separate interface for enterprises to create private meetings and chats.

3.2 Deepfake Detection
FR2.1.1: The system shall analyze video streams in real-time to detect and flag deepfake videos.
FR2.1.2: The system shall notify the user and the session organizer if suspicious deepfake content is detected.
FR2.2.1: The system shall log incidents where deepfake content was flagged for audit and future analysis.

3.3 AI-Generated Backgrounds
FR3.1.1: The system shall provide users with the ability to select AI-generated backgrounds during video conferencing.
FR3.1.2: The system shall offer a library of pre-generated backgrounds or allow dynamically generated backgrounds based on user preferences.
FR3.2.1: The system shall allow users to customize backgrounds by uploading images or selecting from predefined backgrounds.

3.4 Video Conferencing and Collaboration Features
FR4.1.1: The system shall support high-quality video conferencing for individuals or groups.
FR4.1.2: The system shall allow users to schedule meetings, send invites, and share links for joining meetings.
FR4.1.3: The system shall support screen sharing during video calls.
FR4.2.1: The system shall allow users to share documents, spreadsheets, and presentations in real-time.
FR4.2.2: Users shall be able to collaboratively edit documents during a video call.
FR4.3.1: The system shall offer real-time text chat alongside the video stream.

3.5 User Experience Enhancement
FR5.1.1: The system shall adjust video quality dynamically based on the user's bandwidth.
FR5.2.1: The system shall allow users to record meetings, with the option to generate automatic transcriptions using AI.

3.6 Administration and Analytics
FR6.1.1: Admin users shall be able to manage user accounts, monitor active sessions, and terminate sessions if needed.
FR6.2.1: The system shall provide insights into meeting metrics (duration, participants, engagement levels).
FR6.2.2: The system shall generate reports on user activity, facial recognition accuracy, and deepfake detection events.

3.7 Security and Privacy
FR7.1.1: The system shall use end-to-end encryption for all video streams, document sharing, and chat communications.
FR7.2.1: The system shall automatically log out users after a period of inactivity.

3.8 Integration with Third-Party Tools
FR8.1.1: The system shall provide seamless integration with Google Workspace tools (Google Docs, Sheets, Slides) for real-time collaboration.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: The system shall have a maximum response time of 2 seconds for user actions (login, starting/joining a meeting).
NFR2: The system shall maintain video stream latency under 200 milliseconds for real-time video communication.
NFR3: The system shall scale to support at least 1,000 concurrent video conference sessions.
NFR4: The system shall handle a minimum of 50 participants per single video conferencing session.

4.2 Security
NFR5: The system shall enforce end-to-end encryption for all communication (AES-256).
NFR6: The system shall comply with global privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA).
NFR7: The system shall securely store facial recognition data using encryption techniques.
NFR8: The system shall ensure role-based access control (RBAC).

4.3 Usability
NFR9: The system's user interface shall be intuitive and easy to navigate.
NFR10: The system shall be compatible with all mainstream web browsers.
NFR11: The system shall support cross-device transitions without interrupting ongoing sessions.
NFR12: The system shall provide comprehensive user documentation, video tutorials, and tooltips.

4.4 Reliability
NFR13: The system shall maintain 99.9% availability.
NFR14: The system shall continue to function in the event of partial failures.
NFR15: The system shall automatically back up all critical data daily, with ability to restore within 1 hour.

4.5 Maintainability
NFR16: The system shall use a modular architecture (e.g., microservices) for independent updates.
NFR17: The system shall allow updates to be deployed with zero downtime.
NFR18: The system shall provide real-time monitoring and logging for performance metrics and system health.

4.6 Scalability
NFR19: The system shall support horizontal scaling, allowing additional servers to be added dynamically.
NFR20: The system shall utilize cloud-based infrastructure with auto-scaling features.

4.7 Interoperability
NFR21: The system shall allow external video conferencing tools (Zoom, Microsoft Teams) to be integrated for hybrid conferencing.

4.8 Compliance
NFR22: The system shall comply with industry-specific regulations such as FERPA for educational institutions.
NFR23: The system shall be certified to adhere to relevant security standards (ISO/IEC 27001, SOC 2).

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Safar - Tourism Platform with 3D Exploration and Gamification
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for Safar, a tourism platform for Pakistan that offers features like 3D-rendered environments of destinations, personalized trip planning, real-time chatbot assistance, and gamification elements (placing/collecting virtual crystals) to enhance travel exploration and planning.

1.2 Scope
The platform includes web and mobile applications. Features: 3D destination exploration (via 3D Gaussian Splatting), personalized itinerary generation (budget, preferences), gamification (place/collect crystals, leaderboards), AI-powered chatbot for assistance, and user/admin management for content.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- 3D: Three-Dimensional
- GPS: Global Positioning System
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Safar enhances travel planning by providing immersive 3D previews of destinations, personalized itineraries, and gamified engagement, helping users make informed decisions and enjoy a more interactive travel experience.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Web Application: User registration, dashboard, trip plan management, view crystals on 3D map.
- Mobile Application: GPS-based location tracking, crystal placement/collection, push notifications, offline mode.
- 3D Scene Generator: AI-powered 3D rendering of tourism spots, crystal visualization, integration with trip planner.
- Chatbot: AI-based assistance for locations, trip planning, FAQs.
- Trip Planner: Custom trip creation, itinerary generation, travel time/distance calculation, integration with maps.
- Gamification: Badges, rewards, challenges, leaderboards, achievement tracking.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Traveler: Uses the platform to plan trips, explore locations, track/collect crystals.
- Admin: Manages content (tourist locations, 3D environments), monitors user-generated content, handles user accounts.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Web Application Module
FR1.1: Users can register and login via the web interface using email and password.
FR1.2: Users can reset passwords through an email-based recovery process.
FR1.3: Users can view their trips, explore locations, and manage their account from the dashboard.
FR1.4: Admins can login and manage content (locations, user reviews).
FR1.5: Users can create, edit, and delete trip plans.
FR1.6: Users can see their collected crystals in their profile.
FR1.7: Users can view crystals placed by others on the web-based 3D map.
FR1.8: The system allows users to track which locations have crystals to collect.

3.2 Mobile App Module
FR2.1: The mobile app uses GPS to track users' real-time location when visiting destinations.
FR2.2: Users can place crystals at physical locations after visiting them in person.
FR2.3: The system verifies the user's presence at the location through GPS before allowing crystal placement.
FR2.4: Users can collect crystals placed by others by physically being at the crystal's location.
FR2.5: Users receive push notifications about upcoming trips, nearby crystals, and special offers.
FR2.6: The mobile app supports limited offline functionality (saved trip plans, downloaded 3D environments).

3.3 3D Scene Generator Module
FR3.1: The system generates 3D renderings of tourism spots using data from real-world images and GPS coordinates.
FR3.2: Users can explore locations in 3D, rotate the view, and zoom in/out.
FR3.3: Crystals placed by users are rendered in the 3D environment at their real-world locations.
FR3.4: The system updates the 3D scene in real-time as crystals are placed or collected.
FR3.5: The 3D module integrates with the trip planner to visualize selected destinations and routes.

3.4 Chatbot Module
FR4.1: The chatbot provides assistance by answering questions about locations, trip planning, and app functionality.
FR4.2: Users can ask the chatbot for recommended destinations based on their preferences.
FR4.3: The chatbot helps users create trip plans by suggesting destinations, travel routes, and activities.
FR4.4: The chatbot is integrated with a FAQ database.

3.5 Trip Planner Module
FR5.1: Users can create custom trip plans by selecting locations, dates, and preferred activities.
FR5.2: The system provides suggestions for nearby attractions and recommended routes.
FR5.3: The system generates suggested itineraries based on selected destinations and time constraints.
FR5.4: Users can edit the generated itinerary by adding or removing stops.
FR5.5: The trip planner calculates estimated travel times and distances between destinations.
FR5.6: Users can save trip plans for future use.
FR5.7: The trip planner integrates with Google Maps (or similar) for directions and navigation support.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The app should run for at least 1000 hours without breaking down.
NFR2: If an error occurs, the system should log it immediately and notify the team.
NFR3: In the event of failure, the system should restore user data within 5 minutes.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: After logging in, users should be able to explore a 3D location with no more than three taps or clicks.
NFR5: A tutorial should be available on first use.
NFR6: The app should function well on different screen sizes (mobile, tablets).
NFR7: Users should be able to retrieve their last planned trip with a single tap from the main screen.

4.3 Performance
NFR8: 95% of the app's pages should load within 3 seconds on a 10Mbps internet connection.
NFR9: The 3D spaces should load and become interactive within 5 seconds of selection.
NFR10: The app should support up to 1000 users at once without major slowdowns.
NFR11: Trip planning and saving actions should complete within 2 seconds on average.

4.4 Security
NFR12: All user data (account details, trip plans) should be encrypted with 256-bit AES encryption.
NFR13: Admin accounts should use two-factor authentication.
NFR14: After 5 failed login attempts within 10 minutes, the system should lock the account.
NFR15: All user and admin actions should be logged and stored securely for at least 1 month.

4.5 Availability
NFR16: The app should be available 99.9% of the time.
NFR17: Planned maintenance should notify users at least 24 hours before downtime.

4.6 Scalability
NFR18: The system should handle twice the expected number of users without performance degradation.
NFR19: New features should be easily integrated without major disruptions.

4.7 Maintainability
NFR20: The code should be structured so developers can add new features or fix issues within 2 days.
NFR21: Updates should be applied automatically without affecting ongoing user sessions.

4.8 Compatibility
NFR22: The app should support the latest versions of Android and iOS, as well as the two most recent major versions.
NFR23: The app should adapt to different screen sizes without losing functionality.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: GradeMate - Automated Handwritten C++ Quiz Grading
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Evaluation of handwritten programming quizzes in C++ is time-consuming and error-prone. Traditional manual grading results in variability and vagueness, leading to unfair evaluation. Teachers spend excessive time collecting, checking, and grading submissions, leaving less time for interactive teaching.

1.2 Scope
GradeMate automates grading of handwritten C++ programming quizzes. Features include handwriting recognition to convert handwritten C++ code to digital format, evaluation using predefined grading rubrics, AI-generated solutions for multiple correct responses, secure cloud storage, batch processing, and plagiarism detection.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- OCR: Optical Character Recognition
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- TA: Teaching Assistant
- UI: User Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
GradeMate automates grading, reduces manual effort, and provides fair evaluation by comparing student submissions against multiple correct solutions.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Handwritten quiz upload
- OCR conversion and text extraction
- Template matching
- Model training with hybrid datasets
- Automated solution generation
- Customizable grading rubrics
- Plagiarism detection
- Secure cloud storage
- Batch processing

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Instructors: Define grading criteria, approve rubrics, oversee grading process
- Students: Submit handwritten quizzes, receive evaluations
- Teaching Assistants (TA): Manage quiz submissions, review automated grading, make manual adjustments

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application with dashboard, cloud storage integration.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Collecting Dataset Module
FR1.1: Teachers and TAs shall upload handwritten quiz submissions in JPEG and PNG formats.
FR1.2: The system shall automatically extract text from handwritten submissions using OCR.
FR1.3: The system shall enable integration with external datasets (IAM, MNIST, InfityCBD-2) for model training.
FR1.4: The system shall track every quiz submission in database, associated with student ID and quiz number.

3.2 Model Training Module
FR2.1: The system shall enable use of hybrid datasets (EMNIST, student quizzes) to train machine learning models.
FR2.2: The system shall enable saving trained model for further use and upgrades.
FR2.3: The system shall allow modification of model parameters (learning rate, batch size, epochs).
FR2.4: The system shall produce performance metrics (accuracy, recall, F1-score) after training.
FR2.5: The system shall allow re-training using new datasets or student submissions.

3.3 Solution Generation Module
FR3.1: Teachers shall create custom grading guidelines and rubrics for specific questions.
FR3.2: The system shall compare student handwritten solution with model-generated solution using text similarity algorithm.
FR3.3: The system shall generate alternate proper solutions if students solved using different logic but reached correct answer.
FR3.4: The system shall record differences between student and generated solutions for later analysis.

3.4 Automated Grading Module
FR4.1: The system shall automatically evaluate each quiz submission using established rubrics.
FR4.2: The system shall enable teachers to grade assignments manually, modifying or overturning automatically generated grades.
FR4.3: The system shall link each grade to appropriate quiz submission in database.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 1,000 hours.
NFR2: System shall identify grading errors or mismatches with high accuracy.
NFR3: Teachers shall access student information and grading history from most recent checkpoint.
NFR4: Error logs shall record and notify system failures.

4.2 Usability
NFR5: UI shall be intuitive and easy to use for new users.
NFR6: Teachers shall upload quizzes within three clicks.
NFR7: Students shall view graded quizzes with one click on dashboard.
NFR8: Error alerts and help for common mistakes (e.g., wrong file format).

4.3 Performance
NFR9: Process handwritten quiz upload ≤ 60 seconds with faster internet.
NFR10: Automatically graded results for ≥ 20 submissions accessible within 10 minutes.
NFR11: System shall accommodate up to 100 students and 50 teachers concurrently without performance reduction.
NFR12: Each page/activity shall load < 2 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR13: All quiz data, student submissions, and grades shall be encrypted.
NFR14: User authentication for instructors and TAs.
NFR15: User activity logs for teachers, TAs, and students kept for 90 days.
NFR16: Access control shall ensure only authorized individuals access/edit quizzes, grades, or student information. Account locked after 3 unsuccessful access attempts.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: EcoDrive - Autonomous Waste Collection System with Smart Bins
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for EcoDrive, an innovative waste management system that uses smart bins with sensors and an autonomous vehicle (toy truck) to optimize waste collection routes and schedules, enabling on-demand collection when bins reach capacity.

1.2 Scope
The system includes smart bins (ultrasonic sensors, lid automation, communication subsystem), a central server for signal processing and task assignment, an autonomous vehicle (perception, navigation, decision-making), a web dashboard for monitoring and control, and safety/compliance modules. The initial deployment is in a controlled environment.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- IoT: Internet of Things
- LiDAR: Light Detection and Ranging
- RL: Reinforcement Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
EcoDrive addresses inefficiencies in traditional waste collection (fixed schedules) by enabling on-demand, optimized collection through smart bins and autonomous vehicles, reducing fuel consumption, labor costs, and environmental impact.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Smart Bin Module: Level sensors, lid automation, communication with central server.
- Central Server and Communication Module: Signal processing, data aggregation, task assignment.
- Autonomous Vehicle Module: Perception (cameras, LiDAR), navigation, decision-making (RL), communication.
- Dashboard and User Interface Module: Real-time monitoring, user control, data visualization.
- Safety and Compliance Module: ISO 26262 compliance, emergency handling.
- Maintenance and Scalability Module: System maintenance, scalability.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Municipal Corporations, Development Authorities, Educational Institutions, Waste Management Companies, Environmental Agencies, Local Communities (all stakeholders with goals related to efficiency, sustainability, and clean environment).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Smart Bin Module
FR1.1: The smart bin shall detect when it is approaching full capacity and trigger a notification when it reaches a pre-defined threshold (e.g., 90% full).
FR1.2: The bin's lid shall automatically open when someone approaches to dispose of waste and close after a designated period.
FR1.3: The bin shall transmit its capacity and operational status to the central server in real-time.

3.2 Central Server and Communication Module
FR2.1: The central system shall process signals from smart bins and determine which bins require collection.
FR2.2: Upon receiving a full-bin signal, the system shall assign the waste collection task to the autonomous vehicle, optimizing based on location and priority.
FR2.3: The system shall monitor communication with all smart bins and log their status.

3.3 Autonomous Vehicle Module
FR3.1: The vehicle shall autonomously navigate to designated bins using sensor inputs and predefined routes, avoiding obstacles dynamically.
FR3.2: The vehicle shall align with the smart bin and collect waste efficiently after receiving a command from the central system.
FR3.3: The vehicle shall continuously communicate its location and task status to the central system.

3.4 Dashboard/Monitoring Module
FR4.1: The dashboard shall display real-time status of all smart bins (fill level, operational state).
FR4.2: The dashboard shall display the location and status of the autonomous vehicle.
FR4.3: The system shall trigger alerts on the dashboard for full bins or system malfunctions.
FR4.4: The system shall generate periodic reports on waste collection performance, vehicle operation, and bin usage trends.

3.5 Data and System Management Module
FR5.1: The system shall securely store data from smart bins and the vehicle (operational status, capacity, logs).
FR5.2: The system shall implement role-based access control.
FR5.3: The system shall allow administrators to perform over-the-air updates to smart bins, vehicle software, and dashboard.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 200 hours.
NFR2: In the event of a software failure, the system shall log the failure details and notify the maintenance team within 5 minutes.
NFR3: The system shall implement automated error detection algorithms.
NFR4: The system shall adhere to safety standards for self-driving vehicles (e.g., ISO 26262).

4.2 Usability
NFR5: The web dashboard shall allow users to monitor bin status and truck navigation with a user-friendly interface requiring no more than two clicks.
NFR6: The system shall provide a tutorial for new users.
NFR7: The interface shall support accessibility features (screen reader compatibility, high-contrast mode).

4.3 Performance
NFR8: The system shall respond to bin capacity signals and relay information to the truck within 2 seconds.
NFR9: The autonomous vehicle shall process sensor data to make navigation decisions with latency of no more than 100 milliseconds.
NFR10: The system shall successfully complete 90% of waste collection operations without human intervention within the first 30 minutes of deployment.

4.4 Security
NFR11: The system shall use encrypted communication for data transmitted between smart bins, the central server, and the autonomous truck.
NFR12: The Jetson Nano shall have its own firewall configured to protect the autonomous truck.
NFR13: An end-to-end decision-making approach shall be employed to reduce the chances of a security breach.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: ArtiFlo - AI-Powered Business Process Modeling Tool for Education
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for ArtiFlo, an intelligent Business Process Modeling Notation (BPMN) tool that integrates AI to support educational use, enhancing the teaching and learning of BPMN concepts through real-time feedback, automated diagram generation, and performance analysis.

1.2 Scope
The system allows manual BPMN diagram creation with drag-and-drop, AI-powered generation from natural language descriptions, real-time BPMN validation (BPMN 2.0 compliance), real-time assistance and predictive suggestions, activity logging, performance reports, and export/import of diagrams. It targets students and instructors in academic settings.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- BPMN: Business Process Model and Notation
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- XML: Extensible Markup Language

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
ArtiFlo transforms BPMN education by providing an interactive, AI-assisted environment where students can learn by creating diagrams with real-time guidance and instructors can track progress and provide targeted feedback.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- AI-Powered BPMN Diagram Generation: Generate diagrams from natural language descriptions.
- Manual BPMN Diagram Creation: Drag-and-drop interface for creating BPMN diagrams.
- Real-Time BPMN Validation: Check diagrams against BPMN 2.0 standards.
- Real-Time Assistance and Feedback: AI-driven suggestions and error detection.
- Activity Logging and Performance Monitoring: Log user actions for instructor review.
- Performance Reports: Generate reports on student performance.
- Export/Import BPMN Diagrams: Export in PNG, SVG, XML, BPMN 2.0 XML; import existing diagrams.
- AI Chatbot Support: Contextual help for BPMN concepts.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Students: Primary users (undergraduate/graduate) learning BPMN, needing real-time feedback and assistance.
- Educators/Instructors: University professors/instructors using the tool to teach, create examples, monitor student progress, and provide feedback.
- Educational Institutions: Decision-makers adopting the technology.
- Business Process Analysts: Professionals using the tool for analysis and documentation.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 BPMN Diagram Creation
FR1: AI-powered BPMN generation: The system shall enable instructors to generate BPMN diagrams by inputting natural language descriptions of business processes.
FR1.1: The AI engine shall process input and recognize standard patterns to generate a BPMN-compliant diagram.
FR1.1.1: The AI engine shall handle ambiguous process descriptions by offering suggestions for clarification.
FR1.1.2: The engine shall support business processes across various domains.
FR1.2: The system shall provide users with an option to confirm or edit the auto-generated diagram.
FR1.2.1: Users shall be able to modify elements such as tasks, gateways, and events in the generated diagram.
FR1.2.2: The system shall provide recommendations for correcting errors or improving the process.
FR1.3: The system shall allow for saving generated diagrams in progress to continue later.
FR2: Manual BPMN creation interface: The system shall provide a drag-and-drop interface that allows students to manually create BPMN diagrams, including all standard BPMN elements.
FR2.1: The interface shall include a side panel with a library of BPMN elements (tasks, subprocesses, events, gateways).
FR2.1.1: The library shall be categorized into types of elements for easier access.
FR2.1.2: Hovering over elements shall provide a tooltip explaining its use.
FR2.2: Users shall be able to place elements onto a canvas and connect them using sequence flows.
FR2.2.1: The system shall snap elements to a grid to maintain alignment.
FR2.2.2: Users shall be able to click and drag to rearrange elements.
FR2.3: The system shall allow users to undo/redo actions during diagram creation (up to 50 actions).
FR3: Diagram modification tools: Users shall be able to edit, resize, and delete BPMN elements.
FR4: Real-time BPMN validation: The system shall validate BPMN diagrams in real-time against BPMN 2.0 standards.
FR4.1: Validation errors shall be displayed with a description and suggestions for correction.
FR4.2: Users shall be able to disable real-time validation if desired.

3.2 Real-Time Assistance and Feedback
FR1: Real-time error detection: The system shall detect structural errors in the BPMN diagram and notify users immediately with visual cues and explanations.
FR2: Predictive assistance: The system shall offer predictive assistance, suggesting the next logical step or BPMN element based on the user's current diagram state.
FR3: AI chatbot support: An AI-powered chatbot shall be available to provide contextual help related to BPMN elements, processes, and the system interface.
FR3.1: The chatbot shall respond to user queries within 2 seconds.
FR3.2: Users shall be able to ask the chatbot for specific help regarding BPMN notation and best practices.

3.3 Activity Logging and Performance Monitoring
FR1: Comprehensive activity logging: The system shall log all user actions (creating, modifying, deleting elements) with timestamps.
FR2: Time tracking: The system shall track the time spent on each diagram component by individual students.
FR3: Performance metrics: The system shall generate performance metrics based on logged activities, highlighting strengths and areas for improvement.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall maintain an uptime of 99.9% during peak usage times.
NFR2: System downtime (excluding planned maintenance) shall not exceed 10 minutes per month.
NFR3: The system shall perform automated backups daily.
NFR4: In case of system failure, recovery procedures shall restore services within 15 minutes.
NFR5: The system shall detect critical failures and trigger alerts to system administrators within 2 minutes.

4.2 Usability
NFR6: The interface shall be designed to allow students and instructors to navigate core features within 5 minutes of first use.
NFR7: An onboarding tutorial shall be provided to new users.
NFR8: The system shall support keyboard shortcuts for common actions.
NFR9: The system shall comply with WCAG 2.1 standards for accessibility.

4.3 Performance
NFR10: The system shall provide real-time feedback with a response time of 1 second or less for user interactions.
NFR11: Latency shall not exceed 2 seconds during peak usage, even with up to 1000 concurrent users.
NFR12: The system shall scale horizontally to support up to 5000 concurrent users without degradation.

4.4 Security
NFR13: All data transmission shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
NFR14: Data at rest shall be encrypted using AES-256 encryption.
NFR15: The system shall implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all users.
NFR16: Passwords shall be stored using a salted hash (e.g., bcrypt).
NFR17: The system shall comply with GDPR regulations for user data protection and privacy.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: HaloSight - Worker Safety and Compliance System for Construction Sites
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for HaloSight, a real-time worker monitoring web application for construction sites that uses video analytics and AI to detect Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) violations, analyze worker stress/fatigue via facial emotion recognition, and provide alerts and reports to improve safety and productivity.

1.2 Scope
The system uses object detection (PPE detection), facial recognition/emotion analysis (stress, fatigue), a reward system for compliance, data history and report generation, and a user-friendly web interface. It aims to enhance workforce management, reduce accidents, and improve overall site safety.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- PPE: Personal Protective Equipment
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control
- OSHA: Occupational Safety and Health Administration

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
HaloSight addresses the challenges of construction site workforce management by providing real-time, AI-driven monitoring of safety compliance (PPE) and worker well-being (stress/fatigue), enabling proactive interventions and data-driven improvements.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- PPE Detection and Alerts: Real-time video analytics for PPE detection, alerts to supervisors and workers, compliance status dashboard, historical logs.
- Facial Emotion and Stress Analysis: Real-time analysis of facial expressions to detect stress/fatigue, alerts to supervisors, personal dashboard for workers, historical data.
- Reward and Recognition System: Track compliance, assign reward points, allow supervisors to manage and distribute rewards.
- Data History and Report Generation: Collect and store historical safety and emotional data, automatically generate reports on trends.
- Web Interface Development: User-friendly web interface for workers and administrators, responsive design.
- System Integration and Performance: Integrate all components, optimize for real-time performance and low latency.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Admins/Supervisors: Oversee daily operations, receive alerts, monitor compliance and emotional health, manage rewards, generate reports.
- Construction Workers: Perform tasks, receive alerts, access personal emotional health data, participate in reward system.
- Construction Company: Deploys software, monitors safety compliance reports, manages administrative functions.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 PPE Detection and Alerts
FR1: The system shall use real-time video analytics to detect if a worker is missing any required PPE (helmets, gloves, etc.).
FR2: The system shall alert the supervisor immediately if a PPE violation is detected.
FR3: The system shall send a notification to the worker in case of a PPE violation.
FR4: The system shall display real-time PPE compliance status on the supervisor's dashboard.
FR5: The system shall maintain a historical log of all PPE violations and corresponding actions taken.
FR6: The system shall allow the supervisor to generate reports on PPE compliance trends over time.

3.2 Facial Emotion and Stress Analysis
FR1: The system shall analyze workers' facial expressions in real-time to detect signs of stress, fatigue, and discomfort.
FR2: The system shall send an automatic alert to the supervisor if high stress or fatigue levels are detected.
FR3: The system shall display the emotional state of workers on the supervisor's dashboard.
FR4: The system shall provide workers with access to their own emotional state through a personal dashboard.
FR5: The system shall store emotional state data in a historical log for trend analysis.
FR6: The system shall notify workers if their emotional state indicates high levels of stress or fatigue.

3.3 Reward and Recognition System
FR1: The system shall track each worker's compliance with safety protocols.
FR2: The system shall assign reward points to workers who consistently follow safety protocols.
FR3: The system shall allow supervisors to manage and distribute rewards based on compliance status.
FR4: The system shall provide workers with access to their compliance score and reward status.

3.4 Data History and Report Generation
FR1: The system shall collect and store historical data related to worker safety and emotional states.
FR2: The system shall automatically generate reports highlighting trends in PPE compliance and emotional health.
FR3: The system shall allow supervisors to view and customize historical data reports based on time periods, worker compliance, and safety trends.
FR4: The system shall allow workers to view their personal historical data.

3.5 Web Interface Development
FR1: The system shall provide a user-friendly web application interface for both workers and administrators.
FR2: The system shall ensure a responsive design that works seamlessly across desktops, tablets, and mobile devices.
FR3: The system shall allow users to access the system from any location via a web interface.
FR4: The system shall allow real-time updates on worker compliance, emotional health, and alerts.

3.6 System Integration and Performance
FR1: The system shall integrate all components into one unified platform.
FR2: The system shall ensure that all integrated components function in real-time.
FR3: The system shall be optimized for performance, ensuring low latency in real-time video processing, notifications, and data updates.
FR4: The system shall support scalability, allowing multiple construction sites and numerous workers to be monitored.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall operate with no less than 1,000 Mean Time Between Failure (MTBF) operational hours.
NFR2: Failure in one module shall not affect the performance of other modules.
NFR3: The system shall log failures automatically and resume work within 1 minute of failure detection.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: New users shall accomplish simple operations within five minutes of first use.
NFR5: The interface shall be designed to minimize errors and allow recovery within three clicks.
NFR6: The system shall be available via mobile devices, tablets, and desktops with an adaptive layout conforming to WCAG 2.1 standards.
NFR7: Critical actions shall be performed in two or fewer mouse clicks.

4.3 Performance
NFR8: The system shall process PPE detection and facial emotion recognition with input-to-alert generation time of less than 2 seconds.
NFR9: The system shall support up to 1,000 concurrent users without noticeable performance degradation.
NFR10: The system shall generate a report for the past month in less than 5 seconds.
NFR11: The system shall be capable of streaming data from multiple construction sites simultaneously with less than 3% downtime over a year.

4.4 Security
NFR12: The system shall encrypt all user data, including video feeds, using AES-256 encryption.
NFR13: Admin accounts shall require multi-factor authentication.
NFR14: The system shall log all user activities with logs retained for 6 months.
NFR15: The system shall be resistant to security breaches, with a Time-to-Break (TTB) of at least 6 hours for a skilled White Hat hacker.
NFR16: The system shall lock accounts after three failed login attempts.

4.5 Availability
NFR17: The system shall have an availability of 99.9%.
NFR18: Critical services shall be accessible during working hours with no more than 1 minute of downtime.

4.6 Scalability
NFR19: The system shall scale to accommodate a 50% increase in workers and construction sites without major modifications.
NFR20: The system shall handle data from up to 50 construction sites simultaneously.

4.7 Maintainability
NFR21: The system shall allow updates to components while the system is operational, with updates taking no longer than 10 minutes.
NFR22: The system shall be modular, allowing individual components to be updated without impacting others.

4.8 Compliance
NFR23: The system shall comply with OSHA regulations.
NFR24: The system shall comply with GDPR standards for data storage, access, and deletion.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: DataTails - Reddit Data Analytics Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Reddit hosts extensive user-generated content across wide topics. Deriving meaningful insights poses challenges due to unstructured format. Conventional data analysis tools aren't well-suited for complex, context-dependent Reddit discussions where topics evolve rapidly and user sentiments are complex and multifaceted.

1.2 Scope
DataTails transforms unstructured Reddit data into structured and actionable insights using Large Language Models (LLMs), Knowledge Graphs, and Visualization Recommendation Engine. Features include web scraping, Kafka streaming, MongoDB storage, knowledge graph construction, topic modeling, RAG-based LLM for contextual responses, and automatic visualization recommendations.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- LLM: Large Language Model
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
- KG: Knowledge Graph
- NLP: Natural Language Processing

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
DataTails processes Reddit's unstructured data through multiple stages, transforming it into structured and actionable insights, providing sophisticated query functionalities and user-friendly visual representations.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Real-time Reddit data extraction
- Kafka streaming for data ingestion
- MongoDB storage for unstructured data
- Knowledge graph construction
- Topic modeling for theme extraction
- RAG-based LLM for contextual responses
- Automatic visualization recommendation
- Sentiment analysis
- Trend identification

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Developers: Configure system integrations, manage data extraction, tuning
- End Users: Access processed insights and reports
- Premium Users: Receive visualization recommendations, detailed knowledge graphs, personalized topic analysis
- Non-premium Users: Basic features (query submission, topic selection)
- System User: Overall maintenance, monitoring, configuration
- Data Source Component: Reddit for scraping unstructured data
- Kafka Integration: Streaming component for real-time data

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, React.js frontend, Flask backend, MongoDB, Kafka.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Scraping and Ingestion
FR1.1: The system shall scrape Reddit using PRAW, Selenium, or Beautiful Soup to extract posts, comments, user data.
FR1.2: The system shall use cron jobs to schedule scraping tasks at predefined intervals.
FR1.3: Kafka shall be used for real-time data ingestion from scraping module.
FR1.4: MongoDB shall store data with differentiation between premium and non-premium users.

3.2 Data Transformation and Knowledge Graph Construction
FR2.1: The system shall convert raw unstructured Reddit data to structured data using topic modeling and entity extraction.
FR2.2: The system shall search for and integrate suitable ontologies (SIOC, DCMI, FOAF) for KG structuring.
FR2.3: The system shall perform periodic Knowledge Graph reconstruction to update structure with new data.

3.3 Advanced Querying and Insights Generation
FR3.1: The system shall integrate RAG-based LLMs to respond to user queries extracting relevant knowledge from KG.
FR3.2: The system shall accept user queries via natural language interface and extract relevant information.
FR3.3: The system shall recommend appropriate visualizations based on user input and context.

3.4 System Integration and Testing
FR4.1: The system shall combine various APIs (data scraping, topic modeling, KG-based querying).
FR4.2: The system shall connect RAG-based LLMs with KG to handle user queries.
FR4.3: The system shall include visualization recommendation engine.
FR4.4: Premium users shall access expanded query limits, comprehensive visualizations, personalized topic modeling insights.
FR4.5: Non-premium users shall have basic functionality (limited queries, fewer visualizations, restricted KG access).
FR4.6: Continuous integration pipeline shall test updates and monitor performance.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 99.95% for premium users.
NFR2: System shall detect scraping/ingestion failures and retry within 5 minutes.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Users shall perform text-based search and receive insights with ≤ 3 interactions.
NFR4: Interface shall present recommended visualizations in drag-and-drop format.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: 95% of user queries generated within 3 seconds.
NFR6: Visualization loading ≤ 2 seconds for non-premium, ≤ 1 second for premium.
NFR7: Kafka shall ingest and process incoming data streams within 2 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR8: Role-based access control (RBAC) and OAuth2.0 for authentication.
NFR9: HTTPS encryption to protect user data during transmission.
NFR10: All user interactions logged for auditing; suspicious activity reported in real-time.

Project: ArchiVisionAI
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies requirements for ArchiVisionAI, a mobile and web application that merges architectural design (floor plan generation) with interior design and Augmented Reality (AR), providing a user-friendly tool for individuals with low or no knowledge of architectural and interior design.

1.2 Scope
The application integrates architectural design with interior design and AR for non-professional users. Features include: 2D floor plan generation from text/voice prompts, 3D model conversion, AI-powered interior furnishing, AR furniture visualization, space remodeling via prompts, and real-world object scanning. Generated floor plans are for visualization only, not professional use.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- AR: Augmented Reality
- NLP: Natural Language Processing

1.4 References
- RoomGPT [11]
- SmartDraw [33]

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
ArchiVisionAI is a new application combining architectural and interior design features, making them accessible through AI and voice commands.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User Authentication
- Floor Plan Generation (2D from prompts)
- 3D Model Generation
- AI-powered Interior Design
- AR Furniture Viewing
- Space Remodeling via Prompts
- Object Scanning/Visualization
- Voice-Based Commands

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Interior Designers: Professionals using system for client visualization
- Land Owners: Individuals visualizing future houses
- House Owners: Individuals remodeling existing spaces
- Furniture Businesses: Using AR for customer visualization

2.4 Operating Environment
Web browsers and mobile devices (smartphones) with AR capabilities.

2.5 Design and Implementation Constraints
- Web and mobile application development required
- AR features require camera and AR support (ARKit/ARCore)

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Architectural Design Implementation
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to create an account and access it with username/password.
FR1.2: The system shall generate a 2D floor plan based on user prompts with editing options.
FR1.3: The system shall generate a 3D model from the 2D floor plan with layout options.
FR1.4: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface for architecture design with easy navigation.

3.2 Interior Design Implementation
FR2.1: The system shall generate interior designs based on user-selected themes with multiple options.
FR2.2: The system shall allow users to capture or upload images of existing spaces for remodeling.
FR2.3: The system shall allow users to visualize furniture items in real-world space through AR.
FR2.4: The system shall allow users to add item images to space images and compare multiple options.
FR2.5: The system shall allow users to operate using voice-based prompts in addition to text.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance Requirements
NFR1: AR visualization response time ≤ 1 minute.
NFR2: User interaction response time (e.g., floor plan generation) ≤ 10 seconds.
NFR3: Complex tasks (3D model, interior design) ≤ 15 seconds.
NFR4: 85% of user interactions meet 10-second target.
NFR5: 90% of complex tasks meet 15-second target.
NFR6: 90% of AR tasks meet 1-minute target.

4.2 Security Requirements
NFR7: All user data shall be protected from unauthorized access.
NFR8: Brute-force attacks shall be prevented by limiting login attempts to 5.

4.3 Reliability Requirements
NFR9: MTBF ≥ 500 hours.
NFR10: System shall recover from failure within 15 minutes.

4.4 Usability Requirements
NFR11: Users shall learn to use the application within 15 minutes.
NFR12: User error rate < 10%.
NFR13: Users can change interior styling for the same model multiple times.
NFR14: AR furniture preview available within 1 minute after item selection.
NFR15: Voice commands shall work for design requests (e.g., "Show me modern living room").

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: TreeVerse - Global Tree Mapping and Monitoring System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for TreeVerse, a global tree mapping and monitoring system that uses machine learning, satellite imagery, and geospatial data to identify tree species, monitor deforestation trends, and provide actionable insights for sustainable plantation efforts.

1.2 Scope
The system processes satellite imagery to classify vegetation (trees, bushes, crops, barren land), monitors deforestation using change detection and time-series analysis, and provides reforestation suggestions. It includes data processing, tree classification models, deforestation monitoring, API development, client and admin web apps, and documentation.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- GIS: Geographic Information System
- UI: User Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
TreeVerse leverages satellite technology and AI to provide a comprehensive view of global forest changes, helping conservationists, researchers, and decision-makers make informed choices about forest management.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Processing: Gather, preprocess, enhance, and store satellite imagery.
- Tree Classification: Implement deep learning models (PyTorch) for tree species identification.
- Deforestation Monitoring: Detect forest cover changes, time-series analysis, generate alerts.
- API Development: RESTful endpoints for data access, authentication.
- Client Side Web App: Responsive UI with interactive maps, charts, search functionality.
- Admin Side Web App: User account management, role-based access, data import/export, ML model monitoring.
- Documentation: User manuals, API documentation.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- General User: Uses TreeVerse for general information and basic reports/visualizations.
- Admin: Responsible for system functioning, data management, account management, and monitoring.
- Government Agency/Environmental Agency: Accesses information on deforestation trends and vegetation types.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Processing
FR1: The system shall contain images from different sources and securely store them in a database.
FR2: The system shall preprocess the images and standardize them by removing noise.
FR3: The system shall integrate cloud storage services to store and manage satellite images.

3.2 Tree Classification
FR1: The system shall classify plant species into three categories: bushes, trees, and crops, based on satellite imagery.
FR2: The system shall use different algorithms to classify plants based on satellite images.
FR3: The system shall train the classification model on labeled satellite images and validate against a separate dataset.

3.3 Deforestation Monitoring
FR1: The system shall identify changes in forest cover using change identification algorithms over time.
FR2: The system shall perform a time-series analysis to keep track of deforestation trends.
FR3: The system shall validate the deforestation detection model using historical data.

3.4 API Development
FR1: The system shall provide RESTful API endpoints to the user to interact with the data.
FR2: The system shall implement authentication and authorization for secure access to the endpoints.
FR3: The system shall integrate properly with the front end for proper data communication.

3.5 Client Side Web App
FR1: The system shall support user authentication and management.
FR2: The system shall display important information on the dashboard of the user.

3.6 Admin Side Web App
FR1: The system shall allow administrators to create, manage, and delete user accounts, including assigning roles and permissions.
FR2: The system shall assign special roles to specific users for controlled data access.
FR3: The system shall allow user action monitoring and auditing.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system should have Mean Time Between Failure (MTBF) of at least 30 days.
NFR2: If the system fails, it will automatically start normal operation within 15 minutes.
NFR3: The system shall have a logging mechanism for failures and errors, visible to the administrator within 5 minutes.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: Visualizations for classification and deforestation trends should be available within 5 clicks after successful login.
NFR5: Visual feedback shall be given for all user actions within a 10-second timeframe.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: Visualizations should be displayed for any location queried within a 15-second timeframe.
NFR7: At a minimum, 100 concurrent users must be able to access and query the system.

4.4 Security
NFR8: All system data must be inaccessible to non-admin users.
NFR9: All sensitive data in TreeVerse should be encrypted using the AES-256 Standard.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: HumRahi - Carpooling Application
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Urban commuting is increasingly costly due to under-utilization of private vehicles, contributing to traffic congestion and environmental degradation. Existing carpooling applications face challenges: limited user adoption, inefficient ride matching, and security concerns. Public transportation suffers from overfilling, fixed routes, uncomfortable conditions.

1.2 Scope
HumRahi is a mobile carpooling application with advanced route-matching algorithms, dynamic pricing model, real-time vehicle tracking, safety measures, female-only ride options, enhanced privacy settings, and real-time ride analytics using Apache Kafka.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- ETA: Estimated Time of Arrival
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
HumRahi provides affordable, environmentally friendly, and safe urban commuting options through sophisticated algorithms for dynamic pricing, real-time safety monitoring, route matching, and personalized user experiences.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User sign-up/login with verification
- Profile management
- Ride offer creation with route details
- Ride request creation with destination/timing
- Optimal ride matching based on proximity, route, preferences
- Real-time notifications
- In-app messaging
- Route optimization and navigation with traffic updates
- Dynamic pricing model
- In-app payment processing
- Real-time ride tracking and location sharing
- User ratings and reviews
- Driver license and vehicle verification
- Analytics dashboard
- Environmental impact reporting

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Commuters: Primary users seeking affordable efficient transportation
- Drivers: Own vehicles, share rides to offset travel costs
- Female Commuters: Seek female-only ride options and enhanced privacy
- Administrators: Manage system functionality, safety compliance, user accounts

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application (Android/iOS), backend with Apache Kafka for real-time processing.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 UI/UX and Profile Management
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to sign up and log in using email, phone, or social media.
FR1.2: The system shall enable identity verification through email or phone.
FR1.3: The system shall provide profile editing and management.

3.2 Ride Matching and Booking
FR2.1: The system shall allow drivers to create ride offers (route details, availability, capacity).
FR2.2: The system shall allow passengers to create ride requests (destination, timing).
FR2.3: The system shall use algorithm to match ride offers with requests based on proximity, route, preferences.

3.3 Route Optimization and Navigation
FR3.1: The system shall provide route planning and navigation using mapping services.
FR3.2: The system shall provide real-time traffic updates and adjust routes.
FR3.3: The system shall calculate and display ETA and distance.

3.4 Dynamic Pricing Model
FR4.1: The system shall calculate estimated fare based on distance, demand, time before confirmation.
FR4.2: The system shall allow users to make payments using multiple options.
FR4.3: The system shall adjust pricing dynamically during peak times.

3.5 Safety and Security Features
FR5.1: The system shall allow real-time ride tracking and location sharing.
FR5.2: The system shall collect and display ratings and reviews.
FR5.3: The system shall verify driver licenses and vehicle information.

3.6 User Feedback and Support
FR6.1: The system shall provide in-app helpdesk and support options.
FR6.2: The system shall allow ride rating and feedback.
FR6.3: The system shall automatically analyze reviews using NLP for sentiment detection.

3.7 Notifications and Alerts
FR7.1: The system shall send push notifications for ride requests, confirmations, status updates.
FR7.2: The system shall send alerts for upcoming rides, cancellations, delays.
FR7.3: The system shall allow customizable notification settings.

3.8 User Analytics and Reporting
FR8.1: The system shall collect data on user preferences, ride patterns, feedback.
FR8.2: The system shall provide analytics dashboard for administrators.
FR8.3: The system shall generate reports on environmental impact and cost savings.

3.9 Real-time Ride Analytics and Processing Module
FR9.1: The system shall use Apache Kafka for real-time data collection on ride duration, distance, route deviations.
FR9.2: The system shall provide live analytics on traffic conditions during rides.
FR9.3: The system shall be scalable for increased live data volumes.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 1,000 hours.
NFR2: Server failure recovery mechanisms shall restore data within 5 minutes.
NFR3: Error logging with user notification within 1 minute of detection.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: New user sign-up < 2 minutes.
NFR5: Book a ride with ≤ 5 interactions from home screen.
NFR6: WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility compliance.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: Ride search results displayed within 2 seconds.
NFR8: System shall handle up to 1,000 concurrent users.
NFR9: Real-time traffic updates latency ≤ 5 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR10: User authentication with minimum 8-character password.
NFR11: Account locked for 30 minutes after 5 consecutive failed login attempts.
NFR12: Sensitive data (payment, personal identification) encrypted with AES-256.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: ApnaBudget
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Pakistan faces one of the worst economic crises, causing many people to struggle with managing finances. Popular apps like JazzCash and EasyPaisa cover 56% of transactions but offer limited features. Budgeting apps like Mint and YNAB require manual entry, which is tedious and inconsistent.

1.2 Scope
ApnaBudget is a cross-platform budgeting mobile application targeting Pakistani population. Features include automated SMS and notification reading for detecting transactions, AI integration, bill/receipt image upload with automatic processing, financial planning and forecasting, scenario planning, stock visualization, insurance/savings/retirement plan comparison, tax templates, and reminders.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- SMS: Short Message Service
- OTP: One-Time Password
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
ApnaBudget provides financial awareness by helping people budget, plan, visualize, and forecast their finances, empowering them to make better financial decisions.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User authentication (email/password, Google login)
- Automated SMS and notification parsing for transactions
- Manual transaction entry
- Transaction categorization
- Budget creation for transaction categories
- Image upload for bills/receipts
- Spending visualization
- Financial planning and goal setting
- Scenario planning with inflation adjustment
- Stock visualization
- Insurance/savings/retirement plan comparison
- Tax template generation
- Bill payment reminders

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Consumer: General population of Pakistan, track expenses and budget

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application (Flutter - cross-platform Android/iOS), SQLite for offline support, Firebase for remote updates, Kubernetes for microservices, MongoDB for storage.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: SMS and Notification Reading For Automated Transactions
FR1.1: The system shall allow new users to register with name, email, password with SMS verification.
FR1.2: The system shall allow login with email/password and Google accounts.
FR1.3: The system shall provide password reset option.
FR1.4: The system shall support two-factor authentication.
FR1.5: The system shall automatically detect and read SMS containing transaction details.
FR1.6: The system shall automatically detect and read notifications containing transaction details.
FR1.7: The system shall extract transaction information (amount, time, merchant, date).
FR1.8: The system shall categorize transactions based on predefined categories.
FR1.9: The system shall allow users to manually verify and edit transaction details.
FR1.10: The system shall provide interface for manual transaction entry.
FR1.11: The system shall allow users to select from predefined categories when adding transactions manually.
FR1.12: The system shall allow users to manually reassign transaction categories.
FR1.13: The system shall support creation of custom categories.
FR1.14: The system shall allow users to create custom budgets for specific categories with amount and time.
FR1.15: The system shall track spending against budget in real-time and notify when approaching/exceeding budget.

3.2 Module 2: Uploading Bills and Receipts via Images
FR2.1: The system shall allow users to upload images of bills and screenshots for automatic processing.
FR2.2: The system shall allow users to capture photos of receipts/bills using in-app camera.
FR2.3: The system shall allow users to share screenshots directly from Photos app via custom share extension.
FR2.4: The system shall display extracted information for confirmation before logging.
FR2.5: The system shall allow users to create financial scenarios based on variables (income changes, estimated spending).
FR2.6: The system shall allow users to simulate impact of potential tax law changes.
FR2.7: The system shall incorporate periodically updated inflation data.
FR2.8: The system shall visualize changes using graphs.
FR2.9: The system shall allow sorting of transactions by date, amount, merchant.
FR2.10: The system shall provide filtering options (date ranges, categories, amounts, merchants).
FR2.11: The system shall notify users when about to exceed budget.

3.3 Module 3: Planning and Forecasting
FR3.1: The system shall allow users to create financial plans and goals (savings amounts).
FR3.2: The system shall allow custom plans with predefined options (car, mobile, Hajj/Umrah).
FR3.3: The system shall provide progress tracking with percentage completion and time remaining.
FR3.4: The system shall allow users to modify or update plans and goals.
FR3.5: The system shall allow users to create different financial scenarios.
FR3.6: The system shall allow users to input custom inflation rates manually.
FR3.7: The system shall allow users to compare multiple scenarios side by side.

3.4 Module 4: Final Tweaks and Additional Features
FR4.1: The system shall fetch and display historical stock data (price trends, volume, market performance).
FR4.2: The system shall allow users to select specific stocks and visualize performance.
FR4.3: The system shall provide comparison between insurance, savings, and retirement plans.
FR4.4: The system shall generate tax template based on income, transactions, and applicable tax laws.
FR4.5: The system shall allow users to export tax template as PDF or Word.
FR4.6: The system shall allow users to set reminders for bill payments, cash withdrawals, and custom tasks.
FR4.7: The system shall notify users when reminder is due.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: Server failure definition: backend no responses due to pod issues, cluster problems, database availability.
NFR2: Backups in multiple zones.
NFR3: Disaster recovery and high availability servers.
NFR4: Failure detection using liveness probe provided by kubelet in Kubernetes.
NFR5: Rollback to previous deployment using Kubernetes control plane.

4.2 Usability
NFR6: Registration process ≤ 5 seconds after submission.
NFR7: Login response ≤ 3 seconds.
NFR8: SMS/notification transaction processing ≤ 1 minute.
NFR9: Parsing accuracy ≥ 95% for SMS/notifications.
NFR10: Data privacy with encryption at rest and in transit.
NFR11: Manual transactions saved ≤ 2 seconds.
NFR12: Manual transaction interface responsive for various screen sizes.
NFR13: Categorization process ≤ 10 seconds per transaction.
NFR14: Categorization accuracy ≥ 90%.
NFR15: Budget progress display ≤ 5 seconds.
NFR16: Image extraction accuracy ≥ 90%.
NFR17: In-app camera ≤ 3 taps to capture and process.
NFR18: Sorting/filtering ≤ 10 seconds for ≤ 1,000 transactions.

4.3 Security
NFR19: MD5 or SHA algorithm for encrypting transaction information.
NFR20: Strong password: ≥ 8 characters with uppercase letter and number/special character.
NFR21: AES algorithm for encrypting sensitive user information (passwords).
NFR22: External audits of security and data handling practices.
NFR23: Principle of least privilege for user data access.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: RefactoAi - AI-Driven Code Refactoring
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Traditional code refactoring processes require much manual effort, are time-consuming, and sensitive to human error. Research shows manual efforts result in inefficiencies, increased error rates, and lowered code quality and developer productivity.

1.2 Scope
RefactoAi is a web-based application automating refactoring of Python code using advanced Large Language Models (LLMs). Features include analyzing existing Python code, gathering user requirements, refactoring and regenerating code incorporating user requirements, automated error testing, performance optimization, and requirement-to-code mapping reports.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- LLM: Large Language Model
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The system automates the refactoring process, reducing manual effort and enhancing code quality by ensuring results match user-satisfying and project expectations.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Python code upload
- Code validation (syntax, indentation, coding standards)
- User requirement input
- User story generation using open-source LLM
- Code refactoring and regeneration using Code-LLaMA
- Automated testing using PyRat
- Performance optimization
- Requirement-to-code mapping report

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- User: Individual who uploads Python code for refactoring (software engineer, developer, or non-technical user)
- System (Automated): Performs all backend processes autonomously using LLMs

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, pre-trained dataset of 2 million Python scripts, file-based temporary storage.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Code Upload and Validation
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to upload Python scripts for analysis.
FR1.2: The system shall validate uploaded code for errors (syntax, indentation, etc.) before refactoring.
FR1.3: Upon error, the system shall ask user to upload a new file.
FR1.4: If no errors found, the system shall continue to next step.

3.2 Module 2: User Story Integration
FR2.1: The system shall allow users to input requirements for the code.
FR2.2: The system shall analyze requirements and translate them into user stories using open-source LLMs.

3.3 Module 3: Refactoring and Regeneration
FR3.1: The system shall refactor uploaded code to improve quality, performance, and maintainability.
FR3.2: The system shall regenerate code by embedding new user requirements into existing codebase.

3.4 Module 4: Testing and Reporting
FR4.1: The system shall validate refactored code using PyRat or other testing tools.
FR4.2: The system shall generate detailed report mapping user stories to refactored code.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: System uptime ≥ 90.5%.
NFR2: MTBF ≥ 100 hours.
NFR3: System shall log failures and automatically restart refactoring process upon interruption.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: Users shall upload and refactor code with ≤ 3 steps.
NFR5: Refactoring process completed within 5 minutes of interaction.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: Refactor code ≤ 5 seconds for < 200 lines, ≤ 15 seconds for ≤ 1,000 lines.

4.4 Security
NFR7: All user-uploaded code shall be encrypted while stored.
NFR8: System shall resist unauthorized access with intrusion detection mechanism.
