SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Emit - Insider Threat Detection SIEM Solution
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Organizations often prioritize external traffic monitoring, overlooking internal network threats. Insider threats, driven by disgruntled employees, are rising and considered more dangerous than external attacks. Since COVID-19, remote work has increased risk, with 73% of IT executives believing remote workers pose greater risk than on-site employees.

1.2 Scope
Emit focuses on monitoring and protecting internal networks. Features include agent creation for log consolidation, log preprocessing (Wazuh), web application for visualization, dataset creation for AI model training, static rule detection, and AI-based behavioral analysis for real-time insider threat detection.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- SIEM: Security Information and Event Management
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- SOC: Security Operations Center
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Emit detects insider threats both statically and using AI models, with agents configured according to identified insider threat behaviors.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Insider threat behavior identification
- Agent creation and configuration
- Log consolidation from end systems
- Dataset creation and labeling
- Static rule detection
- AI model training
- Real-time behavioral analysis
- Web application dashboard
- Alert visualization
- Incident management
- Role-based access control

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Security Analysts: Monitor system, respond to alerts, need real-time alerting and detailed logs
- IT Administrators: Install, configure, maintain system, need system management tools
- HR Personnel: Conduct employee conduct research, need simplified reports
- Compliance/Legal Officers: Ensure compliance with legal regulations, need audit trails
- Executive/Business Managers: Need high-level summaries and risk assessments

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, MongoDB for data storage, Kafka for log streaming, Wazuh for log preprocessing.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Agent Creation
FR1.1: The system shall provide environment for creating monitoring agents that collect/generate logs.
FR1.2: The system shall enable scripting agent development using Python and PowerShell.
FR1.3: The system shall provide templates and examples for developers.
FR1.4: The system shall provide configuration file/interface for parameters (log types, collection frequency, filters, thresholds).
FR1.5: The system shall permit definition of agents for polling or real-time deployment.
FR1.6: The system shall have methodology for testing agents in production environment.
FR1.7: The framework shall contain ability to run and create scenarios to test agents.
FR1.8: The system shall provide facility for deployment on multiple end systems via command-line scripts.
FR1.9: Automated deployment options using Ansible, Puppet.

3.2 Module 2: Logs Management
FR2.1: The system shall have tools for retaining logs generated by deployed agents in central repository.
FR2.2: The system shall support Classification Shelves and Dunnage Warehousing Systems for varying capacity logs.
FR2.3: Clean logs by removing non-essential or damaged data elements.
FR2.4: Standardize log entries to maintain global order.
FR2.5: System shall have module for log preprocessing to support audits.
FR2.6: System shall have tools to detect threats using rule set.

3.3 Module 3: Model Training
FR3.1: The system shall employ labeled dataset for machine learning model training.
FR3.2: The system shall implement methods to efficiently prepare and load dataset.
FR3.3: The system shall incorporate performance evaluation of classifier algorithms (decision trees, random forests, SVM).
FR3.4: The system shall provide facility for setting model parameters and hyperparameters.
FR3.5: The system shall introduce training infrastructure for carrying out model training process.
FR3.6: The system shall provide dataset partitioning into training, validation, and test sets.
FR3.7: The system shall perform evaluation metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC-ROC).
FR3.8: The system shall offer means of illustrating evaluation results.
FR3.9: The system shall allow adjustments based on evaluation results.
FR3.10: The system shall allow integration of AI model based detection engine into main application.

3.4 Module 4: User Dashboards
FR4.1: The system shall provide customizable dashboards displaying real-time security alerts and performance metrics.
FR4.2: The system shall allow rearrangement and configuration of dashboard widgets.
FR4.3: The dashboard shall include widgets for detected threats, system health status, historical trends.
FR4.4: The system shall provide options for users to choose displayed metrics.
FR4.5: The system shall provide interactive visualizations (charts, graphs, heatmaps).
FR4.6: Visualizations shall allow drill-down into specific alerts.
FR4.7: Users shall filter visualizations by severity, type, time range.
FR4.8: The system shall provide access to detailed logs.
FR4.9: Users shall view logs in structured format.
FR4.10: The system shall include search functionality for specific log entries.
FR4.11: Users shall filter logs by severity, event type.
FR4.12: The system shall provide export functionality (CSV, PDF).
FR4.13: The system shall include tools for tracking and managing security incidents.
FR4.14: The system shall provide workflow for escalating incidents based on severity.
FR4.15: Users shall document incident details (actions taken, evidence).
FR4.16: The system shall provide features for assigning incidents to specific personnel.
FR4.17: The system shall automate workflows (notifying relevant personnel).
FR4.18: The system shall integrate with static rule detection and AI-assisted detection systems.
FR4.19: Integration shall provide real-time data feeds.
FR4.20: The system shall aggregate alerts from both detection systems.
FR4.21: The system shall implement role-based access control.
FR4.22: The system shall allow administrators to define roles and permissions.
FR4.23: The system shall require user authentication (username/password, 2FA).
FR4.24: The system shall send alerts and notifications immediately when threat detected.
FR4.25: Users shall configure notification preferences (alert types, channels).
FR4.26: The system shall maintain notification history.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 6 months.
NFR2: Failure defined as inability to gather, process, or interpret logs, or positive/negative risk assessments impairing performance.
NFR3: Self-recovery: host-based agents reconfigured; manual trigger for persistent errors.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: Security analysts shall examine and evaluate generated alerts with ≤ 3 interactions.
NFR5: Web application shall provide real-time visualizations with ≤ 2 second response time.
NFR6: Security analysts shall view monitored user list within one interaction.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: Incoming logs processed and analyzed in real-time with ≤ 5 second processing limit.
NFR8: Threat warnings displayed ≤ 2 seconds after detection.
NFR9: Smooth log consolidation of all host-based agents.

4.4 Security
NFR10: System shall resist unauthorized external access for ≥ 8 hours.
NFR11: Host-based agents shall have encryption-in-transit and at-rest for confidentiality and integrity.
NFR12: RBAC shall prohibit unauthorized viewing, modification, or deletion of sensitive log data.
NFR13: Unauthorized access/modification attempts shall raise alarm to security team within < 5 seconds.

Project: NoduleNet - AI Based Multi Cancer Diagnosis System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
The project focuses on development of an AI-based system for detection and segmentation of cancer in lung, breast, and skin using higher-order deep learning techniques (CNN and Transformer) to accurately segment tumor regions from medical images (CT scans for lung, mammograms for breast, 2D images for skin).

1.2 Scope
The system estimates nodule/lesion size using radiomic features, predicts cancer progression risk, provides web-based interface with 3D visualization, collaboration features, secure patient record-keeping, encryption, and APIs for hospital management system/EHR integration.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CNN: Convolutional Neural Network
- CT: Computed Tomography
- EHR: Electronic Health Record
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The platform enhances diagnostic accuracy, streamlines workflows, and fosters collaboration among healthcare professionals.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Lung cancer detection and segmentation from CT scans
- Breast cancer detection and segmentation from mammograms
- Skin cancer classification and segmentation from 2D skin images
- Nodule/lesion size estimation
- Cancer severity prediction
- Cancer growth risk prediction
- Web dashboard for image upload and visualization
- 3D visualization tools
- Diagnostic report generation (PDF)
- Collaboration features
- Data encryption
- REST APIs for HMS/EHR integration

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Radiologists: Analyze image data, ensure tumor segmentation and classification accuracy
- Oncologists: Evaluate cancer severity, make treatment recommendations
- Medical Researchers: Collect and analyze data to improve detection algorithms
- Hospital Management: Integrate with HMS, ensure compliance
- Healthcare Providers: Manage patient records, align with health information workflows

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Cancer Detection and Segmentation
FR1.1: The system shall detect and segment lung cancer from CT scan images.
FR1.2: The system shall detect and segment breast cancer from mammogram images.
FR1.3: The system shall classify and segment skin cancers (melanoma, basal cell carcinoma) using 2D skin images.
FR1.4: The system shall allow users to upload medical images for processing.
FR1.5: The system shall estimate size of detected nodules or lesions.

3.2 Module 2: Cancer Severity and Risk Prediction
FR2.1: The system shall predict lung cancer severity based on nodule size and spread.
FR2.2: The system shall assess risk of lung cancer growth and spread from CT scans.

3.3 Module 3: Web Application Interface
FR3.1: The system shall provide dashboard for image upload and segmentation visualization.
FR3.2: The system shall support interactive 3D visualization of CT scan images.
FR3.3: The system shall allow users to download and share reports in PDF format.
FR3.4: The system shall allow users to collaborate by inviting other professionals.

3.4 Module 4: Reporting and Collaboration
FR4.1: The system shall generate diagnostic reports in PDF format summarizing segmentation and prediction results.
FR4.2: The system shall allow users to invite other professionals to collaborate on specific cases.

3.5 Module 5: Integration and Security
FR5.1: The system shall use data hashing methods for secure storage and transmission.
FR5.2: The system shall provide REST APIs for integration with HMS and EHRs.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Usability
NFR1: Medical image upload ≤ 2 interactions.
NFR2: Dashboard shall minimize errors with recovery options for failed uploads.

4.2 Performance
NFR3: Detection and segmentation tasks ≤ 1 minute.
NFR4: 3D visualization interface shall render CT scans < 10 seconds.

4.3 Security
NFR5: Multi-factor authentication for all medical professionals.
NFR6: All stored data encrypted using hashing algorithms.

Research shows average human attention span is 15-20 minutes while watching video, but decreases to only 5-8 minutes when reading textual content. Every 3 out of 4 students experience difficulty retaining focus while studying and need more concise, engaging learning materials.

Scholarly allows users to upload books and automatically generate personalized learning roadmaps with short video reels for each topic. Features include book upload, roadmap generation, textual summaries, short video reels (reels), progress tracking, and personalized dashboard.

- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

Scholarly revolutionizes how students comprehend and interact with learning content by breaking down complex topics into smaller, manageable sections with visual representations.

- Book upload (PDF format)
- Automatic roadmap generation
- Topic identification from book content
- Textual summary generation
- Short video reel generation
- User feedback system
- Progress tracking

Project: TrashCam - Real-Time Littering Detection
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document presents requirements for TrashCam, a real-time litter detection system using computer vision, machine learning, and IoT to automatically detect and report littering incidents, providing actionable information to authorities.

1.2 Scope
The system detects littering incidents in public spaces using AI technologies. Features include: real-time trash detection via object recognition, offender identification via facial recognition and license plate reading, behavior analysis to differentiate intentional littering from accidental disposal, automated report generation, fine issuance, and a citizen mobile app for reporting incidents.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- YOLO: You Only Look Once (object detection)
- OCR: Optical Character Recognition
- LLM: Large Language Model
- GPS: Global Positioning System

1.4 References
- Asian Development Bank Publication, 2022 [22]
- World Bank Report, 2020 [11]

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
TrashCam is a fully automated solution to fill the gap in large-scale littering monitoring and control, integrating with city databases and providing immediate notifications to authorities.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Real-time trash detection using YOLO
- Behavior analysis for intentional vs. accidental littering
- License plate reading via OCR
- Facial recognition for offender identification
- Report generation and fine issuing
- Citizen mobile app for incident reporting

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Municipal Authorities: Monitor public spaces, issue fines
- Law Enforcement Agencies: Trace offenders, take legal action
- Citizens: Report incidents via mobile app
- Waste Management Companies: Optimize collection routes
- Environmental Agencies: Promote responsibility, create awareness campaigns

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application for authorities, mobile application for citizens, cloud-based backend.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Litter Detection
FR1.1: The system shall detect litter objects (bottles, cans, papers) using YOLO.
FR1.2: The system shall detect incident location using Google Maps API.
FR1.3: The system shall classify litter into categories (plastic, glass, metal, organic waste).

3.2 Behavior Analysis
FR2.1: The system shall differentiate between intentional littering and accidental disposal.
FR2.2: The system shall detect vehicle-related littering by analyzing object throwing motion.

3.3 Number Plate Recognition
FR3.1: The system shall recognize vehicle license plates using OCR.
FR3.2: The system shall cross-reference plates with government databases.

3.4 Facial Recognition
FR4.1: The system shall capture and analyze facial features from video footage.
FR4.2: The system shall match faces with known offender databases.
FR4.3: The system shall trace relatives using public databases if direct identification fails.

3.5 Report Generation and Fine Issuing
FR5.1: The system shall generate detailed reports (litter type, location, time, offender identity).
FR5.2: The system shall include photographic/video evidence in reports.
FR5.3: The system shall cross-reference incident location with local zoning laws.
FR5.4: The system shall log and archive reports in a centralized database.
FR5.5: The system shall notify authorities in real-time via email/SMS/portal.

3.6 Fine Issuing
FR6.1: The system shall issue fines based on offense severity.
FR6.2: The system shall notify offenders via SMS/email with offense details and instructions.
FR6.3: The system shall include an analytics dashboard for authorities.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance Requirements
NFR1: The system shall detect and classify littering events within 5 seconds.
NFR2: The system shall generate reports within 10 seconds after detection.
NFR3: The system shall support up to 10 concurrent users without degradation.

4.2 Security Requirements
NFR4: Basic authentication shall protect sensitive data.
NFR5: Data shall be transmitted using secure HTTPS protocols.
NFR6: Personal data shall be anonymized to comply with privacy standards.
NFR7: Role-based access shall ensure only authorized users access specific features.

4.3 Reliability Requirements
NFR8: MTBF ≥ 200 hours.
NFR9: Incident data shall be stored in backup database to avoid data loss.
NFR10: The system shall handle up to 50 simultaneous detections without interruption.

4.4 Usability Requirements
NFR11: Web and mobile interfaces shall be intuitive and user-friendly.
NFR12: Mobile app shall allow one-click incident reporting.
NFR13: Clear visual elements shall support easy navigation.

================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
There exists no robust and real-time American Sign Language translation system bridging deaf and hearing communities. Without a solution, communication gap limits access to vital services, job opportunities, and educational advancements for 430 million people with disabling hearing loss, projected to reach 711 million by 2050.

1.2 Scope
ASLi is a conversational mobile app providing hearing-impaired individuals access to internet information. Features include real-time ASL-to-text translation using advanced ML models, text-to-ASL translation, 3D avatar for gesture display, and LLM integration for information retrieval.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- ASL: American Sign Language
- LLM: Large Language Model
- ML: Machine Learning
- NLP: Natural Language Processing

2.1 Product Perspective
ASLi enables smooth two-way communication between ASL and written language using gesture recognition, natural language processing, and 3D avatar presentations.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- ASL gesture recording via camera
- ASL-to-English translation using ML models
- Information retrieval via LLM
- Text-to-ASL conversion
- 3D avatar display of ASL gestures
- User-friendly interface

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Deaf and Hard-of-Hearing Individuals: Primary users communicating through ASL
- Educators and FYP Committee: Assess project quality and impact
- Testers and QA Team: Ensure app works smoothly, identify bugs

Mobile application, camera-enabled device, backend services with LLM integration.

FR1.4: The system shall send recorded gestures for further processing.
FR1.5: The system shall tell user to wait while results are generated.
FR1.6: The system shall convert generated text response back to ASL grammar.
FR1.7: The system shall map ASL grammar into appropriate ASL gestures and expressions.
FR1.8: The system shall provide visual feedback while converting text to gestures.
FR1.9: The system shall correctly map ASL gestures onto 3D Avatar Rig.
FR1.10: The system shall display converted ASL gestures using 3D avatar in real-time.
FR1.11: The system shall ensure 3D avatar remains responsive to user interactions.

3.2 Module 2: Mobile Back-End Services
FR2.1: The system shall automatically process recorded ASL gestures into corresponding ASL Grammar.
FR2.2: The system shall map ASL Grammar to English Grammar.
FR2.3: The system shall handle errors in gesture recognition and notify user.
FR2.4: The system shall send translated text to LLM for generating response.
FR2.5: The system shall ensure response is contextually correct.

NFR7: Users shall seamlessly use ASLi on any mobile phone device.

4.3 Responsiveness

NFR9: Visual feedback for all actions (recording, processing gestures).
NFR10: System shall handle user inputs without noticeable delays.

4.4 Reliability
NFR11: System uptime ≥ 99.9%.

NFR13: Error logging for future analysis.

4.5 Security
NFR14: New user login capability.
NFR15: Registered users sign-in easily.
NFR16: Compliance with data protection regulations (GDPR, HIPAA).

4.6 Accessibility for Deaf Users
NFR17: Visual cues and instructions for effective application use.
NFR18: Enhanced readability with accessible color schemes and fonts.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: IlmPath - Quranic Learning Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
There is no shortage of Quran apps, but many are outdated, outsized, with poor user interface, invasive ads, and unethical privacy practices. There is need for a quality app that facilitates the next generation in Learning and seeking answers in a modern, digital world.

1.2 Scope
IlmPath is a platform where users can learn to read Holy Quran with tajweed, get insights about the Holy book, interact with ulama and Scholars, use Quranic chatbot, recite verses to identify Surah/Ayah, bookmark verses, take notes, and track learning performance.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- Q&A: Question and Answer

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
IlmPath integrates technology with religious education to provide immersive and comprehensive learning experience for Muslims seeking to improve Quranic knowledge with limited access to qualified educators.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Quranic learning with tajweed from Qaidah
- Real-time audio feedback on pronunciation errors
- Progress tracking
- Quranic chatbot answering queries based on Quran
- Surah/Ayah finder via voice recitation
- Root meaning of words
- Note-taking on Surahs
- Bookmarking Surahs
- Ulama registration and verification
- Ulama-user chat system
- Payment-based appointments with Ulama

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Student/Learner: Primary user responsible for learning Quranic lessons, finding Surahs/Ayahs, making appointments with Ulama
- Ulama/Scholar: Authorized users offering Quranic teachings, answering inquiries, providing advice
- Payment Service Provider: External system actor for financial services
- Guest: Individuals accessing features without registration (Qirat test, Quranic chatbox, Surah finder)

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, React Native frontend, cloud database.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1 - Quranic Learning and Recitation
FR1.1: The system shall display Qaidah as initial learning resource for student.
FR1.2: The system shall automatically promote user to next lesson after successful completion.
FR1.3: The system shall allow user to browse or search for specific Surahs/Ayahs before starting recitation test.
FR1.4: The system shall prepare for voice input once Surah/Ayah selection is made.
FR1.5: The system shall allow users to initiate voice-based recitation test upon request.
FR1.6: The system shall record user recitation and begin analysis for pronunciation errors.
FR1.7: The system shall detect pronunciation errors or Tajweed mistakes.
FR1.8: The system shall mark detected mistakes for live feedback.
FR1.9: The system shall provide voice feedback based on detected errors.
FR1.10: The system shall offer voice assistance suggesting correct pronunciation.
FR1.11: The system shall allow students to practice Quranic recitation daily.
FR1.12: The system shall enable users to listen to recitation while reading along.
FR1.13: The system shall display text of selected Ayah with translation.
FR1.14: The system shall provide background information and context related to Ayah.
FR1.15: The system shall track daily listening time.
FR1.16: The system shall provide daily recitation progress tracking.
FR1.17: The system shall allow users to long-press Arabic word to search root meaning.
FR1.18: The system shall identify selected word and retrieve relevant linguistic data.
FR1.19: The system shall display root meaning and detailed definition.
FR1.20: The system shall provide context for both Arabic words and complex English terms.

3.2 Module 2 - Quranic Interaction and Search
FR2.1: The system shall verify user intent and prompt for Quranic-related queries.
FR2.2: The system shall log conversation and ensure appropriate Quranic references.
FR2.3: The system shall retrieve relevant Quranic information based on user query.
FR2.4: The system shall use database of Quranic texts and Tafsir for accurate responses.
FR2.5: The system shall provide accurate response with Quranic references.
FR2.6: The system shall offer follow-up questions or clarification options.
FR2.7: The system shall allow users to input Surah or Ayah verses by recording for search.
FR2.8: The system shall display relevant search results including matching Surahs and Ayahs.

3.3 Module 3 - User Engagement and Customization
FR3.1: The system shall allow users to add personalized notes to specific Ayahs.
FR3.2: The system shall save user notes for future reference.
FR3.3: The system shall allow users to bookmark specific Ayahs for easy access.
FR3.4: The system shall display bookmarked Ayahs in user study dashboard.

3.4 Module 4 - Ullama-Student Interaction
FR4.1: The system shall provide users option to book paid appointments with registered scholars.
FR4.2: The system shall offer chat option for connecting with scholars.
FR4.3: The system shall allow scholars to create profile and submit certification documents.
FR4.4: The system shall approve or reject registration based on background checks.
FR4.5: The system shall allow scholars to respond to user queries privately with Quranic guidance.
FR4.6: The system shall notify users when scholar has responded.
FR4.7: The system shall allow scholars to set time slots for paid consultations.
FR4.8: The system shall process payments through secure transactions before confirming appointments.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 500 hours.
NFR2: Real-time processing for Qirat test with fallback mechanisms.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: User interface simple and intuitive for all ages.
NFR4: Clear audio feedback and easy-to-navigate text for recitations.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: Real-time feedback for voice-based features with response time ≤ 10 seconds.
NFR6: Payment transactions smooth without errors.
NFR7: Data retrieval for progress tracking and analytics ≤ 5 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR8: Only logged-in and authorized individuals permitted access to notes and bookmarking features.
NFR9: Complete transparency for chats (no user or Ulama can see others' chats).

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: AdInsight - Ad Performance Analytics with Glance and Emotion Detection
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Advertisers lack critical insights into how users interact with ads: where users look, level of engagement, emotional responses. This gap leads to missed opportunities for optimizing ad content, targeting the right audience, and improving ROI.

1.2 Scope
AdInsight is a software platform integrating with web browsers via extension to detect video ads, track user interactions, and gather metrics. Features include ad detection (EasyList), gaze detection (WebGazer.js), emotion and demographic analysis (DeepFace), object detection in ads (YOLO), and analytics dashboard.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AR: Augmented Reality
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
AdInsight provides companies with comprehensive analytics to optimize advertising strategies through facial recognition, gaze detection, demographic analysis, and object detection.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Ad detection and localization on web pages
- User gaze tracking
- User emotion detection (happiness, sadness, surprise, anger, neutral)
- Demographic analysis (age group, gender)
- Object detection in video ads
- Heatmap generation based on gaze data
- Analytics dashboard for ad performance
- Report generation

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Advertising Companies/Marketers: Primary users interested in ad performance and user engagement analytics
- Data Analysts: Interpret raw data, identify trends, prepare performance reports
- Ad Creators/Content Designers: Design video ads based on insights
- End Users/Consumers: Individuals whose interactions are tracked

2.4 Operating Environment
Browser extension (Chrome, Opera, Edge), server module, analytics frontend.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Ad Detection
FR1.1: The browser extension shall identify and localize video ads in real-time.
FR1.2: The system shall detect video ads with ≥ 90% accuracy.
FR1.3: Detected video ads shall be linked to gaze and emotion tracking data.
FR1.4: Correlation shall include timestamps and metadata.

3.2 Gaze Detection
FR2.1: The browser extension shall track user eye movements.
FR2.2: Gaze detection shall start within 100 milliseconds after video ad starts playing.
FR2.3: The system shall track gaze at ≥ 30 FPS.
FR2.4: Heatmaps shall highlight areas users focus on most within ad.
FR2.5: Heatmaps shall refresh every 500 milliseconds.

3.3 Emotion and Demographic Analysis
FR3.1: The browser extension shall detect user emotions during ad playback.
FR3.2: Emotion detection shall occur in real-time and categorize into happiness, sadness, surprise, anger, neutral.
FR3.3: The system shall maintain emotion recognition accuracy ≥ 80%.
FR3.4: The system shall categorize age range (18-24, 25-34) and gender of users.

3.4 Object Detection in Advertisements
FR4.1: The server module shall detect objects within ad frames sent by browser extension.
FR4.2: Detected objects shall be stored with timestamps for future correlation.
FR4.3: The system shall detect objects with ≥ 60% accuracy.
FR4.4: Objects users focus on for ≥ 2 seconds shall be flagged as "objects of interest."

3.5 Data Storage and Processing
FR5.1: Browser extension shall send captured frames and data to server for storage.
FR5.2: Data shall be stored compliant with privacy regulations with encryption at rest.
FR5.3: Server module shall compile data on gaze tracking, emotion detection, demographics, objects of interest into report.
FR5.4: Reports shall include visual representations (heatmaps, charts).
FR5.5: Reports shall be exportable in multiple formats (PDF, Excel).

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: Ad detection and analysis ≤ 500 milliseconds of page load.
NFR2: Gaze detection latency < 100 milliseconds.
NFR3: Emotion detection from video frames ≤ 500 milliseconds.
NFR4: Server-side processing ≤ 5 seconds per video ad.

4.2 Scalability
NFR5: Platform shall support concurrent users, handling multiple ad interactions.

4.3 Availability
NFR6: System uptime ≥ 99.95%.
NFR7: Server/network failures shall retry and process incomplete/delayed frames within 20 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR8: Data exchanged between browser extension and server encrypted with ≥ AES-256.
NFR9: GDPR compliance; no PII leaves host machine.
NFR10: Multi-factor authentication for analytics dashboard access.

4.5 Usability
NFR11: Analytics dashboard shall render insights within 2 seconds.
NFR12: Extension shall function on ≥ 95% of modern chromium browsers.

4.6 Maintainability
NFR13: Modular architecture for new features and bug fixes within 2 hours.
NFR14: Documentation coverage ≥ 90%.

4.7 Data Integrity
NFR15: Graceful error handling with retries and logging within 5 seconds.

4.8 Interoperability
NFR16: Seamless integration with WebGazer.js, EasyList, DeepFace, YOLO without performance bottlenecks.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SmartTutor - Enhanced Learning System with Emotional Analytics
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Finding the right tutor for students according to preferences is difficult, leading to mismatch between learning style and teaching style. Traditional education lacks student engagement and fails to provide interactive learning environment. Inadequate parent involvement leads to uninformed progress.

1.2 Scope
SmartTutor provides effective matching of tutor based on student preferences. Features include tutor profiles with qualifications and availability, video calling with real-time emotional analytics, social learning communities, detailed reports for student progress, and feedback systems.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- UI: User Interface
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SmartTutor combines modern technology including real-time emotional analytics and video communication to provide an engaging and efficient learning environment.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User-friendly responsive interface
- User registration and profile management
- Role-based access control
- Tutor matching based on student preferences
- Video calling with screen sharing
- Real-time emotional analytics during video calls
- Social learning communities (group creation, shared resources)
- Report generation (progress reports, exportable PDF/PNG)
- Feedback forms

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Student: Find tutors based on preferences, attend video sessions, track progress, join communities
- Tutor: Create profile, manage schedules, conduct online sessions, monitor student engagement, generate reports
- Parent: Monitor child's progress, view academic reports, interact with tutors
- Admin: Manage user accounts, ensure security, assign roles, monitor system performance

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application (Three-tier architecture: Presentation, Application, Data), MySQL/PostgreSQL.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Interface (UI)
FR1.1: Easy to use API system using React for optimized UI dynamic rendering.
FR1.2: System shall automatically adjust layout based on screen size (mobile, tablet, laptop).

3.2 User Management
FR2.1: System shall provide admin Dashboard for managing user accounts (add, update, delete, view).
FR2.2: System shall provide visibility to follow user activities (login history, session data).

3.3 Authentication and Authorization
FR3.1: Authentication secured to create and authenticate users with encrypted credentials.
FR3.2: Fine-grained role-based access control for different user types.

3.4 Security and Privacy
FR4.1: End-to-end encryption for video calls.
FR4.2: All user data encrypted in transit and at rest.

3.5 Version Control and Data Backup
FR5.1: Automatic regular data backup.
FR5.2: Data redundancy for critical systems.

3.6 Real-time Synchronization
FR6.1: No delay in transmission of audio/video streams during video calling.
FR6.2: Real-time capture and transmission of Emotional Data.

3.7 Tutor Matching Based on Student Preferences
FR7.1: Students shall search for tutors based on subjects, availability, language.
FR7.2: System shall show extensive tutor profiles (qualifications, experience, subject matter).

3.8 Video Calling
FR8.1: Secure video and audio streaming for lag-free communication.

3.9 Real-time Emotional Analytics
FR9.1: System shall use emotion detection to recognize emotional state (satisfaction, confusion, frustration).
FR9.2: System shall give real-time emotional feedback to tutors.

3.10 Social Learning Communities
FR10.1: System shall enable students to interact with each other and tutors through joining groups.

3.11 Report Generation
FR11.1: Progress reports summarizing student learning outcomes, session attendance, emotional engagement.
FR11.2: Parents shall download reports in PDF/PNG format.

3.12 Feedback
FR12.1: System shall provide feedback form for parent and student suggestions and feedback on tutors.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 1,000 hours.
NFR2: Failure defined as inability to perform video calling, user authentication, or database content retrieval.
NFR3: Strong error handling and notification mechanisms.
NFR4: Input validation, regular code reviews, automated testing.

4.2 Usability
NFR5: Registration and login ≤ 3 interactions.
NFR6: Help available from every page.
NFR7: Clear navigation within 2 clicks from dashboard.
NFR8: User-friendly error messages with correction guidance.
NFR9: WCAG accessibility standards.
NFR10: Profile updates ≤ 3 interactions.
NFR11: Visible feedback for successful actions.

4.3 Performance
NFR12: 95% of video calls connected within 3 seconds with ≥ 20 Mbps internet.
NFR13: Tutor search results within 2 seconds for 90% of queries.
NFR14: Support up to 500 simultaneous users.
NFR15: Progress report generation ≤ 5 seconds.
NFR16: File upload (≤ 10 MB) ≤ 5 seconds.
NFR17: Emotional analytics feedback within 5 seconds.
NFR18: User interface load time ≤ 2 seconds for 90% of users.

4.4 Security
NFR19: System shall withstand 99.9% of unauthorized access attempts.
NFR20: Data leakage ≤ 0.01% in any breach.
NFR21: Failed login attempts and suspicious activity logged and reviewed within 1 hour.
NFR22: 100% of user data protected through encryption protocols in transit.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: LearnScape - VR Educational Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Online educational videos are not immersive enough; users don't get the feeling of sitting in a real classroom. Videos are not interactive enough and don't make the viewer feel part of the lecture. No educational platform allows users to interact with content in real-time and ask questions with relevant feedback.

1.2 Scope
LearnScape is a VR application providing immersive and interactive learning experience. Features include immersive 3D classroom environment, realistic teacher motions and emotions, real-time Q&A with virtual teacher, dynamic slide management, virtual note-taking, AI-generated interactive quizzes, multiplayer functionality, live teaching sessions, and user feedback system.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- VR: Virtual Reality
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- FPS: Frames Per Second

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
LearnScape changes the way current educational videos are perceived by creating an immersive and interactive educational environment using Virtual Reality technology.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Immersive VR classroom environment
- Teacher avatar creation with animations and emotions
- Realistic voice for teacher
- Dynamic slide management based on lecture flow
- Real-time Q&A with fine-tuned AI model
- AI-generated interactive quizzes (MCQ based)
- Virtual note-taking
- Multiplayer functionality (multiple students together)
- Live teaching sessions
- User feedback system
- User authentication

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Student: Attend lectures in virtual classroom, interact with quizzes, virtual notes, teacher model, learn alone or with others
- Teacher: Content creator, provide videos and transcripts, engage in live sessions
- Administrator: Oversee entire system, view published lectures, enrolled students, user feedback

2.4 Operating Environment
VR application (Oculus Rift compatible), Unity client, Python backend, Firebase authentication.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Authentication
FR1.1: System shall allow users to create accounts with email and password.
FR1.2: System shall provide login authentication using Firebase.
FR1.3: System shall ensure only authorized people access application.

3.2 Virtual Classroom Environment
FR2.1: System shall allow users to join virtual classroom environment to view lectures.
FR2.2: System shall provide environment where users can view virtual teacher and lecture content (slides).
FR2.3: System shall synchronize slides with teacher's voice and current content.
FR2.4: System shall allow students to ask questions in real-time during lecture.
FR2.5: System shall generate relevant response for student question and deliver via voice.

3.3 AI Generated Interactive Quizzes
FR3.1: System shall allow users to attempt MCQ-based AI-generated quizzes.
FR3.2: System shall display quizzes to students after each lecture completion.
FR3.3: System shall provide students with instant performance results.

3.4 Virtual Teacher Model
FR4.1: System shall use virtual teacher model to deliver lecture.
FR4.2: System shall feature realistic voice for teacher during lecture delivery.
FR4.3: System shall feature realistic actions and emotions for teacher.
FR4.4: System shall allow virtual teacher model to answer student questions using fine-tuned AI model.

3.5 Multiplayer
FR5.1: System shall allow multiple students to attend lectures simultaneously.
FR5.2: System shall allow multiple students to talk to each other for communication.
FR5.3: System shall allow students to attend live teaching session with teacher in person.

3.6 Virtual Note Taking
FR6.1: System shall allow students to take notes during lecture.
FR6.2: System shall allow students to save notes to view after lecture.

3.7 User Feedback System
FR7.1: System shall allow users to give feedback at end of each lecture.
FR7.2: System shall allow administrator to view all received feedback.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 500 hours.
NFR2: Connection loss recovery ≤ 10 minutes.
NFR3: Error logging to development team within 1 hour.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: Join classroom session ≤ 5 clicks.
NFR5: Users shall interact within classroom with VR controller within 10 minutes.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: Virtual classroom environment load ≤ 5 minutes with ≥ 20 Mbps internet.
NFR7: System shall handle 20 students simultaneously in multiplayer.
NFR8: Classroom environment rendering ≥ 20 FPS on standard VR equipment.

4.4 Security
NFR9: Only authenticated users shall access system using Firebase authentication.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: TerraVerse3D - VR Residential Society Tours
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
The real estate market in Pakistan relies on traditional property visualization methods (photos, 2D maps, occasional video tours). Individuals must physically visit each street and corner to get comprehensive understanding, which is time-consuming, physically demanding, and impractical for those with mobility limitations or busy schedules.

1.2 Scope
TerraVerse3D creates interactive VR application transforming video drone footage of residential housing societies into 3D models for virtual tours. Features include video stabilization and preprocessing, 3D model generation using NeRF, VR environment development in Unity, AI-powered Q&A system for society information, and web application for stakeholders.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- VR: Virtual Reality
- NeRF: Neural Radiance Fields
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- NLP: Natural Language Processing

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
TerraVerse3D bridges the gap in real estate industry by providing an innovative solution to visualize and explore residential societies using modern-day technologies.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Video stabilization and preprocessing
- 3D model reconstruction using NeRF
- 3D model optimization for VR performance
- VR environment development in Unity
- Smooth navigation controls (zoom, pan, rotate)
- AI-powered Q&A system for society information
- Web application for stakeholders to upload drone footage
- User-friendly interface

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Property Managers and Real Estate Agents: Virtually expose properties, manage multiple listings
- Potential Homebuyers and Investors: Browse properties, evaluate from distance
- System Administrators/Developers: Maintain and optimize platform
- Tourism Authorities/Policy Makers: Monitor tourist trends, make policy decisions

2.4 Operating Environment
VR application (Oculus Rift), web application, cloud storage.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Collection and Preprocessing
FR1.1: System shall provide utilities to stabilize and pre-process captured video output.
FR1.2: Video stabilization software shall remove unwanted movements and jitters.
FR1.3: Footage shall be preprocessed by correcting lens distortions, adjusting lighting, enhancing image quality.

3.2 3D Model Reconstruction
FR2.1: System shall generate accurate 3D model output from uploaded drone footage using NeRF.
FR2.2: System shall optimize 3D model for VR performance (reduce polygon count, optimize lighting, texture maps).
FR2.3: Model shall meet VR performance standards for smooth immersive experience.

3.3 VR Application Development
FR3.1: System shall support creation of VR environment using Oculus.
FR3.2: 3D model shall be imported into Unity with lighting, textures, interactive elements.
FR3.3: Smooth navigation controls for moving around VR environment.
FR3.4: Compatibility with Oculus Rift including mappings and input handling.

3.4 AI and NLP Integration
FR4.1: AI model shall process user queries regarding residential society and provide relevant answers.
FR4.2: AI shall answer questions about price ranges, commercial/residential areas, society rules, taxes.
FR4.3: NLP technologies shall develop AI model understanding and responding to queries.

3.5 Web Application
FR5.1: System shall share related video drone footage with users via intuitive web interface.
FR5.2: VR interaction shall include selecting points of interest and accessing further information.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Availability
NFR1: System uptime ≥ 99.99% for web application and VR features.

4.2 Reliability
NFR2: Latency in authenticating user and establishing network session ≤ 50 milliseconds.

4.3 Usability
NFR3: Interface intuitive and self-descriptive for learning VR navigation with minimal training.

4.4 Compatibility
NFR4: Compatible with major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, desktop).

4.5 Performance
NFR5: Process uploaded drone footage and generate 3D model ≤ 30 minutes for typical residential society (≤ 500 kanal).
NFR6: VR application frame rate ≥ 60 FPS for smooth navigation.

4.6 User Support
NFR7: Help and support feature with FAQs, tutorials, ticketing system.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: INSIGHTVISION - AI-Driven Video Summarization System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Manual review of pre-recorded CCTV footage is inefficient for security teams, causing time-consuming video analysis, missed critical events, increased labor costs, and delayed responses to security threats.

1.2 Scope
INSIGHTVISION is an AI system that automatically reviews pre-recorded video, detects motion, identifies important events, labels objects, and creates brief summaries with timestamps. Features include event-based video chunking, timestamp generation, object tracking, action recognition, summarized video compilation, and LLM-generated textual summaries.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- CCTV: Closed-Circuit Television

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
INSIGHTVISION automates event detection, reduces workload for security personnel, improves response times, and enhances overall effectiveness of surveillance operations.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Event-based video chunking (dividing long footage into manageable chunks)
- Timestamp generation for key events
- Action recognition and labeling (walking, running, sitting)
- Object identification and tracking
- Summarized video compilation
- Event identification and highlighting
- LLM-generated textual captions

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Security Personnel: Monitor surveillance footage, respond to incidents
- System Administrators: Manage system, oversee user access, software updates
- Testers: Test application for bugs, usability issues
- End Users: Managers or executives reviewing footage occasionally

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, backend processing with LLM integration.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Event Detection and Analysis
FR1.1: The system shall detect movements in video footage and classify them as events.
FR1.2: The system shall differentiate between significant and insignificant movements.
FR1.3: The system shall generate timestamps for each detected event (accurate to within 1 second).
FR1.4: The system shall recognize different actions based on object classification.
FR1.5: The system shall label identified actions (walking, running, sitting).
FR1.6: The system shall track moving objects within video footage.
FR1.7: The system shall maintain identity of tracked objects throughout footage.

3.2 Module 2: Summarization and Adaptive Reporting
FR2.1: The system shall compile detected events into summarized video.
FR2.2: Summary video shall condense footage based on key moments while maintaining chronological order.
FR2.3: The system shall identify and highlight significant events during playback.
FR2.4: Highlighted events shall be visually distinguishable from other parts.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF shall ensure consistent performance.
NFR2: System shall provide error logs and facilitate recovery within 1 hour.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Users shall navigate interface without prior training, functionalities accessible within 3 clicks.
NFR4: Contextual help and error messages for input errors.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: ≥ 95% of detected events accurately identified within first processing attempt.

4.4 Security
NFR6: All video uploads secured to prevent unauthorized access.
NFR7: User access restricted based on roles.
NFR8: System shall log all access attempts and modifications for audit trail.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: AIMaritimeAwareness - Maritime Pattern of Life Analysis and Predictive Operations
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Fleet operations management in commercial shipping poses significant challenges for efficient management. Fleet managers struggle to predict when vessels should be deployed, maintained, or kept running to avoid unplanned downtime, inadequate resource allocation, and increased operational costs.

1.2 Scope
The system analyzes historical datasets from commercial shipping operations (AIS, radar logs, maintenance records) to provide trends and predictive insights for deployment frequencies, operational statuses, maintenance activities, and other critical factors for managing large commercial shipping fleets.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AIS: Automatic Identification System
- ML: Machine Learning
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The system leverages machine learning models to deliver forecasts that help optimize fleet management, improve operational efficiency, and reduce costs.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data collection from AIS, radar logs, maintenance records
- Data preprocessing and cleaning
- Pattern recognition (deployment frequencies, vessel type correlations)
- Correlation analysis with external factors (weather, sea conditions)
- Predictive analytics (deployment patterns, operational status, maintenance schedules)
- Anomaly detection
- User interface with visualizations
- Report generation

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Fleet Manager: Oversee vessel operations, schedule deployments, optimize fleet performance
- Maintenance Team: Ensure vessels maintained, schedule repairs proactively
- System Administrator: Manage setup, data integration, system maintenance
- Commercial Shipping Companies: Primary beneficiaries seeking optimized efficiency

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, Streamlit server, machine learning models.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Integration and Preprocessing Module
FR1.1: The system shall collect historical data from multiple sources (AIS logs, radar data, maintenance logs).
FR1.2: The system shall clean and preprocess collected data to handle missing, incomplete, or erroneous data.
FR1.3: The system shall integrate and unify data into single structured format.

3.2 Pattern Recognition and Correlation Analysis Module
FR2.1: The system shall identify patterns in deployment frequencies based on historical data.
FR2.2: The system shall recognize correlations between operational statuses and external factors (weather, sea conditions).
FR2.3: The system shall visualize recognized patterns to assist decision-makers.

3.3 Predictive Analytics Module
FR3.1: The system shall develop predictive models to forecast future deployment patterns.
FR3.2: The system shall provide predictive insights on number of assets deployed based on vessel type, operational status, historical usage.
FR3.3: The system shall forecast upcoming maintenance needs based on historical logs and external conditions.

3.4 Anomaly Detection Module
FR4.1: The system shall alert fleet managers in advance of upcoming required maintenance.
FR4.2: The system shall identify and flag anomalies in vessel operation, maintenance schedules, or deployment patterns.
FR4.3: The system shall notify fleet managers of detected anomalies.

3.5 User Interface and Visualization Module
FR5.1: The system shall provide web-based interface for easy access.

FR5.3: Users shall interact with system through filters (vessel type, time range) to customize view.

3.6 System Integration and Testing Module
FR6.1: The system shall provide testing module for validating predictive models against historical datasets.
FR6.2: The system shall report on prediction accuracy, error rates, thresholds.
FR6.3: The system shall provide detailed reports on system performance, accuracy, improvement areas.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Scalability
NFR1: Must handle large datasets and scale to accommodate multiple fleets data.

4.2 Performance
NFR2: Near-time or real-time data processing with response times < 10 seconds.

4.3 Reliability and Availability
NFR3: 99.9% system availability with fault tolerance and reliable backup mechanisms.

4.4 Usability

4.5 Accuracy

4.6 Maintainability
NFR6: Easy to maintain and update with clear documentation.

4.7 Data Integrity

4.8 Extensibility
NFR8: Flexible architecture to allow future enhancements and integrations.

Eating right is hard because advice that works for one person might not work for another due to genetic differences. Most people don't know how to use genetic information to eat better.

- Daily food intake tracking
- Health goal setting and tracking

- Monthly reports with personalized advice

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SRX - Safe Route Xplorer
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Unexpected transportation dangers from landslides, floods, and earthquakes commonly affect travelers. Traditional guidance systems cannot promptly notify users of these hazards, leading to dangerous circumstances, traffic jams, and accidents. Typical GPS systems do not account for unexpected changes in road safety.

1.2 Scope
SRX is a Real-Time Road Safety Alert System using NLP to filter social media posts and news for relevant dangers, updating safety indexes of different routes in real-time. Features include route safety index calculation, real-time alerts, map-based interface, offline capability, and system administration for manual map management.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- GPS: Global Positioning System
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SRX improves traveler safety by providing early alerts about potential traffic risks from various disasters, integrating user-generated data from news channels and social media sites.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Safe route planning based on real-time social media data
- Real-time social media monitoring (Twitter, Facebook, Instagram) for potential hazards
- NLP processing to filter and extract hazard-related information
- Hazard severity classification
- Map updating and display with safety index
- User notifications for route updates and hazard alerts
- System administration for manual route management
- Offline capability with cached information

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Travelers (Regular Users): Use GPS-based navigation, fetch warnings on dangers
- Commuters: Daily travel, avoid hazards or traffic problems
- Road Safety Authorities: Monitor hazard frequency, send assistance, inform public
- System Administrators: Ensure backend working, manage data flow, oversee system uptime
- Data Analysts: Collect insights on hazard impact on travel behavior

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile and web applications, Firebase NoSQL database, real-time synchronization.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Safe Route Planning
FR1.1: The system shall allow user to enter starting location and destination.
FR1.2: The system shall show safest route based on received and processed data.
FR1.3: The system shall dynamically update map if road hazard occurs along route.
FR1.4: The system shall display estimated time to reach destination.

3.2 Real-time Social Media Monitoring for Potential Hazards
FR2.1: The system shall continuously fetch tweets, Facebook, Instagram posts in real-time based on keywords.
FR2.2: The system shall process and filter incoming data to extract relevant information.
FR2.3: The system shall identify severity of detected hazard.
FR2.4: The system shall identify location where potential road hazard occurred.
FR2.5: The system shall classify hazard severity and update map.

3.3 Map Updating and Display
FR3.1: The system shall update routes if new hazards are dynamically detected.
FR3.2: The system shall remove or not display hazardous routes as top route.

3.4 User Notifications
FR4.1: The system shall send notifications to user when route is updated.
FR4.2: The system shall send alerts when hazards are detected along planned route.

3.5 System Administration
FR5.1: The system shall allow administrator to manually remove paths that are potentially dangerous.
FR5.2: The system shall allow administrator to set severity levels and update map.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: Highly reliable ensuring safest route always shown.

4.2 Usability
NFR2: User-friendly, interactive, visually appealing, easy-to-navigate map.

4.3 Performance
NFR3: Smooth functionality during hazardous activity or high data traffic.

4.4 Security
NFR4: Maintain integrity; no unauthorized access to routes or data.

4.5 Availability
NFR5: Available at all times to detect hazardous activity and update routes accordingly.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: MalwareXplore - Automated PDF Malware Detection System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Malware analysis faces challenges due to fragmentation, manual dependency, and collaboration issues. Static and dynamic analysis used independently leads to incomplete results. Analysts must manually examine logs, investigate suspicious activity, and gather findings from various tools, which is inefficient and prone to human error.

1.2 Scope
MalwareXplore combines static and dynamic analysis into unified system for PDF malware detection. Features include web portal for PDF upload, automated static and dynamic analysis (Cuckoo sandbox), centralized and decentralized storage (Elasticsearch, Federated learning), visualization dashboard (Grafana), downloadable reports, and federated learning for continuous AI model improvement.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
MalwareXplore makes malware detection more efficient, accurate, and globally collaborative through AI automation and federated learning.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Web portal for PDF upload
- File format validation
- Static analysis (PDF structure, metadata)
- Dynamic analysis (execution in Cuckoo sandbox)
- Federated learning integration for local model training
- AI-driven learning for continuous improvement
- Visualization dashboard (Grafana) with charts
- Downloadable analysis reports
- Centralized (Elasticsearch) and decentralized storage
- User authentication

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Malware Analyst: Automate static and dynamic analysis, examine results, download reports
- System Administrator: Manage system infrastructure, ensure updates, monitor performance

2.4 Operating Environment
Web portal, Flask backend, Cuckoo sandbox API, Elasticsearch, Grafana.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Centralized and Decentralized Storage
FR1.1: The system shall store malware analysis results in centralized (Elasticsearch) and decentralized (Federated local models) ways.
FR1.2: The system shall make it easier to find and use information from different locations.

3.2 Data Privacy & Security
FR2.1: All PDF files, analysis results, and user information shall be encrypted in transit and at rest.
FR2.2: The system shall ensure analysis of uploaded PDFs only shown to that specific user.

3.3 Federated Learning Integration
FR3.1: The system shall enable local models to share malicious PDF data with global model for training without sharing sensitive data.
FR3.2: The system shall regularly update local model which will be sent to global model for refinement.

3.4 AI-Driven Learning
FR4.1: The system shall automatically update machine learning model on new features for accurate detection.
FR4.2: The system shall support continuous model improvement by adding new analysis data into training dataset.

3.5 Web Portal for PDF Upload
FR5.1: The system shall allow user to create accounts.
FR5.2: The system shall allow user to upload PDF files and reject any other format.

3.6 Automated Malware Detection
FR6.1: The system shall perform static analysis on uploaded files to extract metadata.
FR6.2: The system shall perform dynamic analysis by running files in virtual environment of cuckoo sandbox.

3.7 Visualization Dashboard
FR7.1: The system shall allow user to access analysis results on Grafana dashboard in form of charts.
FR7.2: The system shall allow user to download analysis results as report from Grafana dashboard.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: If system failure occurs during upload or analysis, system shall alert user and continue from last known state.
NFR2: Automated failure detection with notification during malware detection or federated learning updates.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Web portal interface user-friendly with obvious indicators for upload progress, analysis status, reports.
NFR4: Dashboard interface enables users to select charts and graphs for graphical display.
NFR5: Help section guiding through upload, analysis, reporting processes.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: Static and dynamic analysis on 10MB PDF shall complete quickly under normal load.
NFR7: Federated learning model updates processed and integrated within few minutes of receiving new data.

4.4 Security
NFR8: All uploads, downloads, communications between web portal and backend services end-to-end encrypted.
NFR9: Analysis data encrypted with strong algorithm.
NFR10: Backend services and databases accessible only by authorized personnel through authentication.
NFR11: Federated learning updates over secure environment without sharing sensitive data.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: BlockChainLMS - OneBlock Blockchain Student Portal
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Centralized systems for storing sensitive student data are vulnerable to cyber attacks, illegal alteration of data, and system failures. Educational institutions grapple with log obfuscation, slow response time to harmful actions, and leaking of crucial information.

1.2 Scope
OneBlock is a blockchain-based student portal providing tamper-proof log system with immutable records stored on blockchain. Features include real-time log collection from student activities, administration decisions, system changes; smart contracts for handling log changes; decentralized distribution for enhanced availability; data visualization (graphs, charts, tables); report generation; faculty announcements; and admin user management.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- LMS: Learning Management System
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
OneBlock ensures tamper-proof, decentralized data management enhancing trust and accountability through blockchain technology.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Secure login and authentication for faculty
- Dashboard for real-time monitoring and alerts
- Tamper-proof log viewing with filtering options
- Announcement feature for faculty updates
- Real-time log collection and storage on blockchain nodes
- Smart contracts for log changes
- Immutable data storage
- Decentralized distribution of logs
- Graphs and charts for visualizing logs and trends
- Detailed reports on alerts and system changes
- User management (roles, permissions)
- Node and network management

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Student: View academic logs (submissions, attendance, grades), receive announcements
- Faculty: Input and manage student data, post announcements, generate reports, monitor logs
- Administrators: Manage user accounts, monitor blockchain nodes, ensure system security
- Blockchain Network Participants (Validators): Verify and validate transactions
- Testers: Evaluate functionality, security, performance

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, Ethereum blockchain, smart contracts, Firebase.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Client Web Application
FR1.1: The system shall provide secure login and authentication for faculty members.
FR1.2: Faculty shall be able to post announcements through web application.
FR1.3: The system shall allow faculty to generate detailed reports on student activities, logs, anomalies.
FR1.4: Admin shall have filtering options for logs based on hashes, instructor name, or browser used.

3.2 Blockchain Log Collector
FR2.1: The system shall collect and store logs from student activities, faculty actions, administrative decisions in real-time using blockchain.
FR2.2: The system shall ensure all stored logs immutable (cannot be altered or deleted once recorded).
FR2.3: The system shall distribute logs across multiple blockchain nodes for data availability.
FR2.4: The system shall validate logs using smart contracts to detect and flag suspicious changes automatically.
FR2.5: The system shall provide decentralized access to logs.

3.3 Data Visualization and Reporting
FR3.1: The system shall provide graphical representations of log data (charts, graphs, tables).
FR3.2: The system shall enable real-time updates of visual data.
FR3.3: The system shall allow exporting reports in PDF, CSV, Excel formats.
FR3.4: The system shall provide detailed breakdown of anomalies or suspicious activities in visual format.

3.4 Admin Web Application
FR4.1: The system shall provide administrators with secure login and role-based access control.
FR4.2: Admins shall have access to audit trail detailing system changes, user permissions, administrative actions.
FR4.3: The system shall log all notifications and alerts for review.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 500 hours.
NFR2: Recovery from failure within 15 minutes.
NFR3: Automated error detection and correction mechanisms.
NFR4: Failover strategies for critical system components.

4.2 Usability
NFR5: Key tasks (log viewing, report generation) with ≤ 3 interactions.
NFR6: Faculty and students learn core functions ≤ 30 minutes.
NFR7: Clear error messages with error recovery rate ≥ 90% within single attempt.
NFR8: System responsiveness ≤ 2 seconds for any action.

4.3 Performance
NFR9: Handle up to 1,000 concurrent users without degradation.
NFR10: Critical transactions (blockchain log entry) processed within 5 seconds.
NFR11: Data retrieval actions response time < 3 seconds for 95% of queries.
NFR12: Support data visualization of up to 10,000 log entries without performance issues.

4.4 Security
NFR13: Blockchain technology guarantees 100% immutability of student and system logs.
NFR14: Unauthorized access attempts logged with alert to admin within 5 seconds.
NFR15: Resilient against DDoS attacks, maintaining 99% uptime.
NFR16: All sensitive data encrypted.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: CaseLink - AI-Based Legal Case Management System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Legal professionals in Pakistan face challenges: managing documentation for each case, scheduling case hearing dates, generating legal documents with specific formats, preparing strong arguments based on latest legal data. Law firms use social media groups for document sharing, manually sift through articles and judgments, clerks update lawyers on next hearing dates.

1.2 Scope
CaseLink is an all-in-one software solution using automation and AI to support law firms. Features include case management, client data storage, lawyer recommendation system, document generation, calendar with notifications, advisory AI for extracting relevant judgments, in-app chat for lawyer-client communication, and document sharing.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
CaseLink boosts law firm efficiency by saving lawyer's time through automation and AI-driven solutions.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Case management (case tabs, case cards, client details)
- Lawyer hub (lawyer info tracker, smart suggestion for case assignment)
- Scheduling and notification (calendar, notifications for hearing dates)
- Legal Convo (in-app chat with document support)
- DocuCraft (legal document generation from templates)
- LLM-based Legal Assistant (document insights, advisory AI for argument preparation)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Firm Manager: Lead firm, file and assign cases, build arguments, create/approve documents
- Lawyer (Associate): Work on assigned cases, create documents, build arguments
- Clerk: Track case dates, update case details
- Client: Contact law firms, receive case updates, provide documents

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application (Next.js frontend), microservices backend, MongoDB, AWS S3, Redis.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Case Management
FR1.1: The system shall provide daily case list view (case title, FIR number, client name).
FR1.2: Users shall check status of specific case (judge information, court information, advocates involved, case description, next date).
FR1.3: The system shall support storage and retrieval of evidence copies, ordersheets, plaints, written statements.
FR1.4: The system shall categorize cases into Criminal, Civil, and Family types.
FR1.5: The system shall store client information (name, address, contact number/email).
FR1.6: The system shall allow managers to decide whether to profile each client.

3.2 Lawyer Hub
FR2.1: The system shall support case assignment to Lawyers (Associates).
FR2.2: The system shall support user roles with specified access levels (Firm Manager: full access; Lawyer: full access; Clerks: access granted by manager; Clients: access granted by manager).
FR2.3: The system shall generate reports on number of new cases added, number of cases decided, outcome, lawyer-wise case analytics.

3.3 Scheduling and Notification
FR3.1: The system shall send email notifications to clients and assigned lawyers for case appearances (date, case number, title, court name).

3.4 Legal Convo
FR4.1: The system shall facilitate communication with clients (legal updates, requests for information).

3.5 DocuCraft
FR5.1: The system shall provide document generation capabilities from templates.
FR5.2: The system shall include approval workflow for document filing requiring validation from firm manager.

3.6 LLM Based Legal Assistant
FR6.1: The system shall provide document summarization capabilities.
FR6.2: The system shall assist in argument preparation by referencing previous relevant case judgments.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability and Availability
NFR1: System uptime ≥ 99.9%.
NFR2: Automatic backups and disaster recovery plans.
NFR3: Offline functionality in future iterations.

4.2 Accessibility
NFR4: Accessible via desktop and mobile devices.
NFR5: Responsive web application optimized for Chrome, Firefox, Edge.

4.3 Scalability
NFR6: Support ≥ 20 concurrent users without performance degradation.

4.4 Performance
NFR7: Response for most actions (loading case, retrieving documents) ≤ 4-5 seconds over ≥ 20 Mbps internet.

4.5 Security
NFR8: Single Sign-On (SSO) for user authentication.
NFR9: Account locked after 5 failed login attempts within 15 minutes.
NFR10: All documents treated as publicly accessible per firm policy.

1. INTRODUCTION

This document outlines objectives, scope, and features of RouteRover, a location-based app providing real-time information, navigation, safety alerts, and AI-driven recommendations with unique features like volunteer support and crowdsourced crime detection.

RouteRover is a mobile application providing real-time, community-driven insights for safer navigation. Features include: real-time traffic, event, and crime information; volunteer assistance; crowdsourced data; customizable notifications; multilingual support; local volunteer management; gamification; and predictive analytics.

- AI: Artificial Intelligence

RouteRover enhances travel experiences by providing personalized recommendations, real-time updates, and multilingual support, differing from existing map systems by offering volunteer support, crowdsourced crime detection, and AR for interactive navigation.

- Volunteer interaction (chat/phone support)
- Customizable alerts
- Crowdsourced data submission and validation
- Local business recommendations
- Points of interest discovery
- Gamification rewards

FR2.1: The system shall verify user-generated content (traffic, crime updates) before distribution.

FR2.3: The system shall ensure security and privacy of system and user data.

4.1 Reliability

4.2 Usability

4.3 Performance

This project proposal outlines the development of Fabricae, a web application helping independent and small-scale fashion designers overcome resource constraints and simplify their design process by providing AI-powered textile pattern generation and professional portfolio creation.

Fabricae enables designers to create textile patterns via AI prompts or sketch-to-2D conversion, customize patterns using editing tools, visualize designs on 3D clothing mock-ups with embellishments, create professional portfolios using customizable templates, and publish portfolios for networking with industry professionals.

- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- 3D: Three-dimensional

Fabricae helps designers create unique and high-quality design patterns without expensive tools, providing 3D mock-ups for visualization and a networking platform for career advancement.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)

- Sketch-to-2D motif conversion

- Portfolio creation with customizable templates
- Portfolio publishing and networking (chat feature)
- Pattern generation history

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Fashion Designer: Generate textile patterns, create portfolios, seek industry attention

Web application, cloud-based deployment, 3D rendering capabilities.

FR2.1: The system shall allow designers to upload pencil sketches from local machine.

FR3.1: The system shall allow designers to edit patterns by changing colors.

FR4.1: The system shall allow designers to select 3D clothing models to test patterns.

FR4.4: The system shall allow designers to apply customized patterns on 3D clothing models.
FR4.5: The system shall allow designers to export 3D designs in PNG, glTF, and OBJ formats.

3.6 Module 6 - Portfolio Publishing and Networking
FR6.1: The system shall allow designers to publish portfolios on the platform.

FR6.3: The system shall allow designers to add generated and edited patterns to design gallery.
FR6.4: The system shall allow designers to share portfolios via WhatsApp, LinkedIn, and Gmail.
FR6.5: The system shall allow designers to view notifications for message alerts.
FR6.6: The system shall allow industry professionals to view design galleries.
FR6.7: The system shall allow industry professionals to view designer portfolios.
FR6.8: The system shall allow industry professionals to send text messages to designers.
FR6.9: The system shall allow designers to send text messages once chat is initiated.

NFR4: User data shall be encrypted.
