NFR8: The system's MTBF shall be at least 30 days.
NFR9: The system shall automatically retry failed operations up to three times.

4.4 Scalability

4.5 Security

NFR14: Sensitive data (API tokens) must be safely saved and never exposed.

This document outlines the development of Visualytica, a web platform for simplified graph visualization, reducing technical complexity and improving interactivity for users to analyze large datasets and extract meaningful insights.

Visualytica provides a web platform for visual analysis of graph databases, allowing users to build sophisticated graph visualizations with an intuitive interface. It includes a recommendation engine suggesting appropriate visualizations based on graph structure (e.g., trees, force-directed graphs), interactive navigation, dashboard capabilities, and a domain-specific chatbot.

- CSV: Comma-Separated Values

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Visualytica simplifies graph visualization by reducing technical complexity and improving interactivity, enabling users to analyze large datasets easily.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Graph data input (Adjacency Matrix, Edge List, Adjacency List)
- Graph visualization (directed, undirected)
- Interactive graph navigation (zoom, pan, click nodes)
- Graph analysis (shortest paths, node centrality)
- Dashboard design with multiple chart views
- Graph export (PNG, SVG)
- Domain-specific chatbot for visualization concepts

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Developers: Build and maintain application, need detailed documentation
- Project Managers: Oversee progress, need high-level overviews

- Users: Primary audience for graph creation/visualization
- Testers: Check for bugs, ensure functionality
- FYP Panel: Assess quality, innovation, and usability

Web platform, compatible with major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Upload and Input Formats
FR1.1: The system shall support Adjacency Matrix, Edge List, and Adjacency List formats.
FR1.2: Users shall upload datasets via interface supporting CSV and JSON files.
FR1.3: The system shall validate uploaded dataset structure for format compliance.
FR1.4: Users shall receive error messages for invalid file formats.

3.2 Data Visualization
FR2.1: The system shall visualize graph data in directed and undirected formats.
FR2.2: Users shall interact with graphs (zooming, panning, clicking nodes).
FR2.3: The system shall allow users to apply filters based on node/edge attributes.

3.3 Dashboarding
FR3.1: The system shall provide a dashboard with key metrics and statistics.
FR3.2: The dashboard shall display graphs, charts, and tables representing graph properties.
FR3.3: Users shall customize dashboard by selecting displayed metrics and visualizations.
FR3.4: The dashboard shall automatically update in real-time with new data or changes.

3.4 Module 1 - Web Design/Plots/Area Graph
FR4.1: The system shall implement web interface and backend data processing for input validation and analysis (tree, DAG, simple graph detection).
FR4.2: The system shall generate and customize all graph types (trees, DAGs, other structures).

3.5 Module 2 - End-to-End Dynamic Dashboard
FR5.1: The system shall design dynamic dashboards for layout and presentation customization.
FR5.2: The system shall perform final frontend and backend integration with comprehensive testing.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: System uptime ≥ 99.5%.
NFR2: System shall automatically save user work and allow recovery within 30 seconds after failure.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Users shall generate a graph from data within 3 steps.
NFR4: Tooltips and tutorials shall explain graphing options.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: Graph visualization shall load within 5 seconds for datasets up to 10,000 nodes/edges.
NFR6: Users shall zoom, pan, and interact without noticeable lag.

4.4 Security
NFR7: All user data shall be encrypted during storage and transmission.
NFR8: User authentication shall be required for accessing saved graphs/dashboards.
NFR9: Unsuccessful access attempts shall be logged and administrators alerted.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: GrociDish - Personalized Grocery and Meal Planning App
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document provides an in-depth overview of GrociDish, a personalized grocery and meal planning app with nutritional management features, tailored for Pakistani cuisine and dietary habits.

1.2 Scope
The app features personalized grocery list generation based on budget and preferences, recipe suggestions based on available ingredients, nutritional recommendations, and daily caloric tracking for family members.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- BMI: Body Mass Index
- IBW: Ideal Body Weight
- JWT: JSON Web Token
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
GrociDish addresses the challenges of maintaining a healthy lifestyle in Pakistan by providing tools for budget-friendly, nutritionally balanced grocery planning and meal preparation, with a focus on local cuisine.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Personalized grocery list generation (preferences, budget, brands).
- Grocery list modification via chatbot.
- Ingredient-based recipe suggestions.
- Meal recommendation for health conditions.
- Calorie recommendation based on BMI/IBW.
- Daily caloric tracking.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Pakistani Families: Primary users looking for convenient, budget-conscious meal planning.
- Home Cooks: Need assistance in planning nutritionally balanced meals.
- Individuals with Dietary Restrictions: Require specialized recipes.
- Health-Conscious Individuals: Track daily caloric intake.

2.4 Operating Environment
Mobile application (cross-platform).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Manage Account
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to register with valid email, username, and password.
FR1.2: The system shall validate and securely store user registration data.
FR1.3: The system shall allow users to add family members and assign permissions.
FR1.4: The system shall confirm account creation and family member addition.
FR1.5: The system shall allow users to log in using their registered credentials.
FR1.6: The system shall allow users to update and delete their account.

3.2 Manage Profile
FR2.1: The system shall allow family members to view and edit their profile details.
FR2.2: The system shall allow users to change their password.
FR2.3: The system shall allow the Master User to update family member details and manage roles.

3.3 Personalized Grocery List Generation
FR3.1: The system shall allow authorized users to create personalized grocery lists based on preferences.
FR3.2: The system shall incorporate budget constraints to generate cost-effective lists.
FR3.3: The system shall provide options for brand preferences.

3.4 Grocery List Modification
FR4.1: The system shall allow users to add/remove items to their grocery list via a chat interface.
FR4.2: The system shall retrieve the current grocery list.
FR4.3: The system shall incorporate NLP to enhance user interaction with the chatbot.

3.5 Meal Recommendation
FR5.1: The system shall suggest recipes based on ingredients from the user's grocery list.
FR5.2: The system shall allow users to select meal suggestions by health conditions (diabetes, hypertension, obesity).
FR5.3: The system shall include alternative meal options for variety.
FR5.4: The system shall perform nutritional calculations (total calories).
FR5.5: The system shall provide access to a comprehensive database of pre-existing recipes.

3.6 Nutritional Recommendation
FR6.1: The system shall take user input (age, weight, height, gender, activity level).
FR6.2: The system shall calculate BMI and IBW.
FR6.3: The system shall use the Harris-Benedict equation to calculate daily calorie needs.
FR6.4: The system shall recommend daily caloric, carbohydrate, protein, and fat intake for each family member.

3.7 Daily Caloric Tracking
FR7.1: The system shall allow each family member to have a password-protected calorie tracker.
FR7.2: The system shall track daily caloric intake.
FR7.3: The system shall provide a visual breakdown of calorie sources.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall manage expected and unexpected errors efficiently, providing clear error messages.
NFR2: The system shall handle failures by using retries and backups for key processes.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The app interface must be intuitive and user-friendly.
NFR4: The system shall be designed to be accessible for all users.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: The system shall generate a personalized grocery list within 10 seconds.
NFR6: Updates to the grocery list shall be reflected in less than 10 seconds.
NFR7: The chatbot shall respond to user queries within 5 seconds.
NFR8: Meal plan suggestions shall be within 10 seconds.
NFR9: Calorie recommendations shall be processed within 5 seconds.
NFR10: BMI and IBW calculations shall achieve 95% accuracy.
NFR11: Calorie breakdowns shall be displayed within 5 seconds.
NFR12: Login requests shall be processed within 3 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR13: Only authorized users should generate grocery lists or select meal plans.
NFR14: The system should implement secure login with JWT, ensuring token expiration and revocation.

================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION

This document details the project vision, software requirements, and UML designs for Khiladi, an Android application that uses AI and ML to analyze cricket bowling videos and provide constructive feedback.

1.2 Scope
The app allows users to upload bowling videos, which are processed by AI to identify technical flaws and generate constructive feedback. It aims to help amateur and semi-professional cricketers improve their skills.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations

- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Khiladi empowers cricketers to self-assess and improve their bowling techniques by providing immediate, actionable feedback, filling the void left by conventional, lengthy coaching methods.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)

- Video upload functionality.
- AI-driven video analysis to identify mistakes.
- Personalized feedback generation.
- Progress tracking over time.
- Notifications and reminders.
- Connect with coaches (paid feature).
- Community support.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Users (Cricket Players): Primary users who will upload videos and receive feedback.
- Admin: Manages the portal, adds/removes users, adds incentives, adds coaches.

2.4 Operating Environment
Android mobile application.

3.1 Create Account

3.2 Customize Profile

3.8 Get Notifications

3.10 Connect with Coach

3.11 Provide Incentives

3.12 Create Events

3.13 Connect with other users
FR13.1: The system shall allow users to chat with other users, share feedback, and talk.

3.14 Manage Portal

NFR4: The application must handle multiple users simultaneously.

4.3 Compatibility
NFR6: The app will be compatible with Android devices running version 6.0 or higher.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: HireUp - Automated Hiring Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for HireUp, a web application designed to cut down the cost and time spent during hiring processes by automating CV filtration, video interview analysis, and test creation.

1.2 Scope
The system allows applicants to apply for jobs, search and receive recommendations, and schedule interviews/tests. Recruiters can post jobs and receive shortlisted candidates. It features CV filtration, video interview analysis, and knowledge-based tests.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CV: Curriculum Vitae
- MCQ: Multiple Choice Question
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
HireUp addresses the inefficiencies and biases in traditional hiring by automating the CV screening and initial assessment process, saving time and resources for companies.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- CV Filtration using AI.
- Video Interview Analysis (facial expressions, answer accuracy).
- Knowledge-based Test creation and scoring.
- Job Posting & Management.
- Job Application Submission.
- Job Search & Recommendations.
- Interview & Test Scheduling.
- Application Status Notifications.
- Application Tracking.
- Profile Management.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Applicant: Seeks a job and applies through the application.
- Recruiter: Finds most eligible candidates for a job opening.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 CV Filtration
FR1.1: The system shall provide a form to the applicant.
FR1.2: The system shall extract information such as skills, experience, etc., from the provided data.
FR1.3: The system shall verify experience through LinkedIn and GitHub.
FR1.4: The system shall score the CV based on the requirements of the job description.

3.2 Video Interview Analysis
FR2.1: The system shall provide an option to assess shortlisted applicants via an automated video interview.
FR2.2: The system shall take questions entered by the recruiter.
FR2.3: The system shall question the applicants.
FR2.4: The system shall record the interviews.
FR2.5: The system shall process and analyze emotions from facial expressions.
FR2.6: The system shall analyze the answers provided by the applicant.
FR2.7: The system shall score the applicant based on facial expressions and answer accuracy.

3.3 Test Creation
FR3.1: The system shall provide an option to recruiters to create an MCQ-based test.
FR3.2: The system shall score test answers based on the key given by recruiters.
FR3.3: The system shall show the test scores to recruiters.

3.4 Job Posting & Management
FR4.1: The system shall provide recruiters with an option to post a job opening.
FR4.2: The system shall show recruiters a history of all job openings they have posted.
FR4.3: The system shall allow recruiters to view the CVs, recorded interviews, or test scores of shortlisted candidates.

3.5 Job Application Submission
FR5.1: The system shall allow applicants to apply for a job vacancy.
FR5.2: The system shall allow applicants to upload CVs/resumes and other information.

3.6 Job Search & Recommendations
FR6.1: The system shall allow the applicant to search for jobs.
FR6.2: The system shall display jobs tailored to the applicant's specified field.
FR6.3: The system shall suggest jobs to applicants based on domain and location.

3.7 Interview & Test Scheduling
FR7.1: The system shall generate a schedule for applicants, listing all their interviews and tests with timings.

3.8 Application Status Notifications
FR8.1: The system shall notify applicants of any updates or changes in the status of their job applications.

3.9 Application Tracking
FR9.1: The system shall allow applicants to track the progress of jobs they have applied to.

3.10 Profile Management
FR10.1: The system shall provide access to recruiters or applicants through sign-up or login.
FR10.2: The system shall send a verification email after sign-up.
FR10.3: The system shall allow users to edit their profiles.
FR10.4: The system shall allow users to delete their profiles.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Security
NFR1: Only authenticated users can access the main functionalities.
NFR2: User data will be encrypted using AES-256 encryption.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Clear and informative error messages are provided.
NFR4: The UI will be user-friendly, following User-Centered Design principles.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: The application should respond to user actions within 2 seconds under normal load conditions.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: MedTrove - Affordable Medication Finder and Healthcare App
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document provides the requirements for MedTrove, a cross-platform application that helps users find alternative medications with similar ingredients at lower costs, along with other features like drug interaction checking and chatbot assistance.

1.2 Scope
The app allows users to search for medication, view detailed information and cheaper alternatives, compare substitutes, check drug interactions, manage profiles, use a chatbot (Medibot), make payments, donate to help others, and locate nearby pharmacies.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- DRAP: Drug Regulatory Authority of Pakistan
- BMI: Body Mass Index
- UI: User Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
MedTrove addresses the rising cost of medication in Pakistan by bridging the gap between patients and affordable healthcare, offering innovative solutions to find cheaper substitutes.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Substitute Drug Identifier.
- Smart Search and Recommendation.
- Drug Interaction Checker.
- Medication Reminder.
- Substitute Comparison.
- Profile Management.
- Medi-bot.
- Payment.
- Donation.
- Pharmacy Locator.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- User: Primary users who seek alternatives for prescribed medicines.

2.4 Operating Environment
Cross-platform (iOS/Android) mobile application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Substitute Drug Identifier
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to input the name of the prescribed medication.
FR1.2: The system shall access a drug database.
FR1.3: The system shall identify alternative medicines with similar ingredients.
FR1.4: The system shall compare alternative drugs based on price.
FR1.5: The system shall display a list of alternative drugs ranked by relevance.
FR1.6: The system shall display detailed information on each alternative drug.
FR1.7: The system shall give the option to buy the alternative drug.
FR1.8: The system shall display a disclaimer advising consultation with a healthcare professional.

3.2 Search and Recommendation
FR2.1: Users shall be able to search for medicines by entering its name.
FR2.2: The system shall provide auto-complete suggestions.
FR2.3: The system shall allow filtering of search results based on price.
FR2.4: The system shall give the option to buy the medicine.
FR2.5: The system shall provide recommendations to users based on previous searches.

3.3 Drug Interaction Checker
FR3.1: The system shall allow users to input the name of two medications.
FR3.2: The system shall access a drug interaction database.
FR3.3: The system shall classify interactions based on severity.
FR3.4: The system shall display whether the medications can be taken together.

3.4 Medication Reminder
FR4.1: The user shall be able to set a medication reminder.
FR4.2: The system shall allow users to input medication details.
FR4.3: The system shall provide timely reminders and notifications.
FR4.4: The system shall maintain a log of medication intake.

3.5 Substitute Comparison
FR5.1: The system shall allow users to input the name of the prescribed medication to compare substitutes.
FR5.2: The system shall compare alternatives based on price and potential side effects.
FR5.3: The system shall highlight key differences.

3.6 Profile Management
FR6.1: The system shall allow new users to register by providing personal details.
FR6.2: The user shall be able to login using their email and password.
FR6.3: The system shall allow users to update personal information.

3.7 Medi-bot
FR7.1: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface for initiating conversations with a chatbot.
FR7.2: The user shall be able to enter medical questions.
FR7.3: The system shall provide preliminary medical advice and information about medication.
FR7.4: The system shall guide users on when to seek professional help.

3.8 Payment
FR8.1: The system shall allow users to select a payment method (credit/debit cards, JazzCash).
FR8.2: The system shall provide an option to save payment information.
FR8.3: The system shall display an order summary before payment.
FR8.4: The system shall send a confirmation notification upon successful payment.
FR8.5: The system shall maintain a record of all user transactions.

3.9 Donation
FR9.1: The system shall allow users to make donations through various payment methods.
FR9.2: The system shall maintain a log for all donations.
FR9.3: The system shall provide users with a history of their donations.
FR9.4: The system shall allow allocation of donated funds to discount orders for underprivileged users.
FR9.5: The user shall fill a form to prove eligibility for discounts.

3.10 Pharmacy Locator
FR10.1: The system shall allow users to search for nearby pharmacies based on their current location.
FR10.2: The system shall display detailed information about nearby pharmacies.
FR10.3: The system shall provide directions to searched pharmacies.
FR10.4: The system shall allow users to save favorite pharmacies.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: The application shall load within 4 seconds.
NFR2: Medicine results shall be displayed within 5 seconds.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The user interface shall be easy to navigate.
NFR4: There shall be at least 2 helping screens between each frame.

4.3 Reliability
NFR5: The system shall have an uptime of 95%.
NFR6: Backups and recovery mechanisms shall be implemented to prevent data loss.

Project: Voyaige - AI-Powered Travel Planning Platform for Pakistan
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the problem statement, solution, scope, and modules for Voyaige, an AI-powered travel platform designed to simplify trip planning for travelers in Pakistan.

1.2 Scope
Voyaige offers personalized trip planning through a chatbot (Milo), a form-based system, and manual customization. It includes features like booking transportation/accommodation, viewing 360° panoramas, and a challenge/incentive system.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- ML: Machine Learning
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Voyaige addresses the overwhelming complexity of trip planning by providing an integrated platform with personalized itineraries, budget management, and booking options, all in one place.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- AI chatbot (Milo) for itinerary planning.
- Form-based AI trip planning.
- User-customized trip planning.
- Tourist attraction 360° panoramas.
- Personalized recommendation systems.
- Booking systems for transport/accommodation.
- Payment options.
- Challenges and incentives.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Traveler: Primary user who can choose how to plan their trip.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Chatbot
FR1.1: The user can communicate with Milo to customize a trip.
FR1.2: The user can provide vocal commands, which speech-to-text recognition converts to text.

3.2 AI Trip Planning Form
FR2.1: The user is provided with a form to fill out to help AI understand the user's preferences.

FR3.1: Users can customize their trip by selecting what to do each day.
FR3.2: The user can select transportation and lodging from a list.

3.6 Booking Systems

3.7 Payment Options

FR8.1: The system provides challenges to complete to earn discounts on future trips.

Project: TalkTracks - AI-Powered Audio/Video Processing Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for TalkTracks, a web application designed to process audio/video files by providing syntactic/semantic search, summary generation, noise reduction, and subtitle generation.

1.2 Scope
The tool allows users to upload audio/video files, perform syntactic/semantic searches, generate extractive/abstractive summaries, reduce noise, generate subtitles, and interact with a chatbot for file-related queries.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
TalkTracks addresses the lack of efficient tools for processing audio/video files by providing a single platform for search, summarization, and enhancement, helping users like students and hearing-impaired individuals.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Semantic Search.
- Syntactic Search.
- Summary Generation (extractive/abstractive).
- Noise Reduction.
- Subtitle Generation.
- Chatbot Assistant.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- End User: Uploads files and applies different processing.
- Admin: Oversees the system, manages packages, user details, and payments.
- Package: Various subscription plans.
- Payment: Handles billing.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Semantic Search
FR1.1: The system shall provide user to upload audio files (e.g., MP3, WAV) or video files (e.g., MP4).
FR1.2: The system shall accurately transcribe audio to text.
FR1.3: The system shall allow users to enter text queries for semantically searching within audio/video content.
FR1.4: The system shall highlight the timestamps in the multimedia file where the text is present.
FR1.5: The system shall allow the user to generate a report and download it as a PDF.

3.2 Syntactic Search
FR2.1: The system shall provide user to upload audio files (e.g., MP3, WAV) or video files (e.g., MP4).
FR2.2: The system shall accurately transcribe audio to text.
FR2.3: The system shall allow users to enter text queries for syntactically searching within audio/video content.
FR2.4: The system shall highlight the timestamps in the multimedia file where the text is present.
FR2.5: The system shall allow the user to generate a report and download it as a PDF.

3.3 Summary Generation
FR3.1: The system shall provide the user to upload the file (audio or video).
FR3.2: The system shall accurately transcribe audio to text.
FR3.3: The system shall allow the user to download the generated summary as a PDF.

3.4 Noise Reduction
FR4.1: The system shall provide the user to upload the file (audio or video).
FR4.2: The system shall allow the user to download the processed audio in MP3 format.

3.5 Subtitle Generation
FR5.1: The system shall provide the user to upload the file (audio or video).
FR5.2: The system shall accurately transcribe audio to text.
FR5.3: The system shall allow the user to download the generated subtitles as .srt.

3.6 Chatbot Assistant
FR6.1: The system shall allow the user to ask queries related to the multimedia input provided.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Usability
NFR1: Users shall be able to retrieve accurate results from the processed audios/videos.

4.2 Performance
NFR2: The system shall process a video of 3 minutes in 3 minutes.

4.3 Compatibility
NFR3: The system shall be compatible with audio formats MP3, WAV, and video formats MP4, MWAV.

4.4 Security
NFR4: Protecting data and granting access only to authorized users by verifying their identity through 2-Factor Authentication.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Sketch2Face - AI-Powered Sketch to Realistic Facial Image Generator
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document sets forth the specification for Sketch2Face, an AI program that recreates realistic human facial images from sketches or textual descriptions and allows manipulation of the generated image via prompts.

1.2 Scope
The system converts hand-drawn sketches and textual prompts into realistic facial images. It allows manipulation of facial features based on user prompts. It is suitable for forensic investigators and average users.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Sketch2Face aims to address the gap in generating high-quality, real-human images from sketches, which is critical for law enforcement and helpful for artists and content creators.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Sketch to Facial Image conversion.
- Text to Facial Image generation.
- Image manipulation via textual prompts.
- Real-time sketch conversion.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Law Enforcement Agencies: Generate images from witness sketches.
- Forensic Experts: Reconstruct faces from descriptions or partial images.
- Digital Artists: Transform sketches into realistic images.
- Government Agencies: Create visuals for public safety.
- General Public (Casual Users): Create images for personal projects.
- Content Creators: Generate realistic characters from sketches.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Sketch to Facial Images
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to upload or draw hand-drawn sketches and generate facial images from them.
FR1.2: The system shall generate high-quality facial images based on complete or partially drawn sketches.
FR1.3: The AI model shall accurately capture facial features from sketches to convert them into realistic images.
FR1.4: The system shall provide options for users to edit or refine sketches before generating the final image.

3.2 Facial Image Generation and Manipulation Using Text
FR2.1: The system shall allow users to input textual descriptions to generate facial images.
FR2.2: The system shall support text-based manipulation of facial images by allowing users to modify specific facial attributes.
FR2.3: The system shall provide feedback on changes and display real-time adjustments.

3.3 Web Application
FR3.1: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface that allows users to draw sketches and enter textual descriptions.
FR3.2: The system shall support real-time conversion of hand-drawn sketches into facial images.
FR3.3: The system shall integrate AI models for both sketch-to-image and text-to-image generation.
FR3.4: The system shall implement APIs for communication between AI models and the web application.
FR3.5: The system shall provide options to save facial images through the web application.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: In the event of system failure, recovery procedures shall be automatic, and services restored within 3 minutes.
NFR2: Any critical failure must trigger automatic logging.
NFR3: The system shall handle 5 user requests simultaneously without degradation.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: The system shall allow users to generate a facial image from a sketch with no more than three interactions.
NFR5: The system shall provide clear instructions at every step.
NFR6: A user should be able to undo any modifications with a single action.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: Facial image generation from a sketch shall be completed within 15 seconds.
NFR8: Facial image generation from text shall be completed within 20 seconds.
NFR9: The web application interface shall load within 5 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR10: The system shall require user authentication before accessing personal data or saved images.

Web application.

Project: EduVantage - Intelligent Graduate Recommender System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This project proposal describes the intended objectives, features, and technical architecture of EduVantage, a web-based platform to guide graduates in selecting appropriate universities for higher studies using AI to automate and customize university search.

1.2 Scope
The system focuses on top 150 universities in US, UK, and EU, providing personalized recommendations based on user profiles (academic achievements, exam results, career goals). Features include scholarship recommendations, real-time monitoring of university websites for updates, university comparisons (programs, fees, rankings), and interactive preference query system.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- NER: Named Entity Recognition
- LLM: Large Language Model
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation

1.4 References
- QS World University Rankings [1122]
- Node-mailer [88]
- Material-UI [33]
- Bootstrap [55]
- JWT [77]
- FilePond [99]
- Multer [22]
- PyPDF2/pdfplumber
- spaCy
- JOI.js [1100]
- Puppeteer [1111]
- Scikit-learn [66]

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
EduVantage automates the university search process using AI (NLP, NER, LLM) to provide personalized suggestions and explanations for why specific universities are suitable fits.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User registration and authentication
- Profile creation and management
- CV upload with auto-population
- Academic data scraping from university websites
- Smart data identification using NER
- NLP-based query analysis
- LLM precision tuning for fit score calculation
- Priority URL retrieval engine
- Application deadline tracking
- Database management (MongoDB)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Graduate Students: Seek admission to master's programs, use for tailored suggestions
- Academic Advisors: Guide students through application journey
- University Admissions Officers: Ensure accurate program representation
- Parents/Guardians: Support role, concerned with financial factors

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application (MERN stack), cloud deployment.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 UI/UX Development
FR1.1: Develop responsive user interface using React.js for web and mobile platforms.
FR1.2: Ensure smooth transitions between forms and pages with uniform consistency.
FR1.3: Implement ARIA-compliant features for accessibility.

3.2 User Information Portal
FR2.1: Permit users to establish profiles via CV upload (PDF) with automatic field extraction.
FR2.2: Permit users to manually provide extra information.
FR2.3: Provide profile update and deletion options.

3.3 Academic Data Scraper
FR3.1: Gather academic information by scraping 150 specific university domains.
FR3.2: Effectively manage pagination for multi-page data retrieval.
FR3.3: Schedule data recollection (weekly/monthly) for updates.

3.4 Smart Data Identifier (NER)
FR4.1: Use pre-trained NER model to extract key information (program details, costs, deadlines).
FR4.2: Validate extracted data for accuracy and format appropriately.
FR4.3: Ensure extracted structured data is accessible for further processing.

3.5 NLP-based Query Analysis
FR5.1: Analyze user queries, extract relevant keywords, integrate with profile data.
FR5.2: Order search results based on relevance to user criteria.
FR5.3: Offer options for quick results or detailed analysis.

3.6 LLM Precision Tuning
FR6.1: Collect and maintain data to fine-tune LLM for accurate matching.
FR6.2: Develop fit score calculation normalizing and weighing user data and requirements.
FR6.3: Dynamically generate and highlight university recommendations with real-time updates.

3.7 Priority URL Retrieval Engine
FR7.1: Respond to deep analysis requests by refining queries and conducting targeted searches.
FR7.2: Implement TF-IDF based system to rank URLs by relevance.
FR7.3: Use web crawling tools to efficiently locate and retrieve data from identified URLs.

3.8 Application Deadline Tracker
FR8.1: Periodically monitor university websites for application deadline updates.
FR8.2: Send automated notifications to users about impending deadlines.

3.9 Database Management
FR9.1: Design and implement flexible NoSQL schema for diverse data types.
FR9.2: Provide efficient data ingestion, retrieval, and management for CRUD operations.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: MTBF ≥ 300 operational days.
NFR2: Automatic recovery shall restore functionality within 5 minutes 95% of the time.
NFR3: Regular reviews and ongoing error tracking shall identify and fix problems early.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: User interface shall load in 2-4 seconds on all devices.
NFR5: Reusable UI components in React shall improve development efficiency.
NFR6: System shall be compatible with ARIA standards for accessibility.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: Data processing and profile population from CV shall complete in 10-15 seconds for 95% of actions.
NFR8: Web scraper shall finish scraping any university website in 30-60 seconds.
NFR9: Smart data identifier shall validate extracted entities with ≥ 85% accuracy.

4.4 Security
NFR10: Security breach probability ≤ 0.01% annually.
NFR11: Data integrity shall be maintained with 99.9% security effectiveness.

Project: PestInsight - AI-Driven Pest Detection System for Major Food Crops
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for PestInsight, an AI-driven system designed to detect pests in major food crops (wheat, rice, corn) using an ensemble model (YOLOv8, Faster R-CNN) and provide sustainable pest management advice.

1.2 Scope
The system focuses on 14 pest types in rice/wheat and 12 in corn using a subset of the IP-102 dataset. It uses a web app (Django, React, MySQL) where users upload images for pest detection, receive severity assessments, generate detailed reports, and interact with a chatbot for assistance.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- YOLO: You Only Look Once
- R-CNN: Region-based Convolutional Neural Network
- FAO: Food and Agriculture Organization

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
PestInsight automates pest detection and identification, providing farmers with fast, accurate, and actionable results to enable timely pest management and crop protection.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User Registration: Create accounts with unique username/password.
- Image Upload for Pest Detection: Upload and enhance images, detect and count pests.
- Generate Diagnosis Report: Compile pest types, counts, and severity into a downloadable report.
- View Diagnosis History: Access previously generated reports.
- Chat with Chatbot: Get assistance with pest-related queries and platform usage.
- Display Pest Control Instructions: Provide pest-specific management tips.
- Assess Pest Severity: Grade infestation as low, moderate, or severe.
- User Profile Customization: Update personal information.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Farmer: Primary user who uploads images, receives pest identification, diagnosis, and management tips.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Registration
FR1: The system shall allow users to register with a unique username, password, and personal information.
FR2: The system shall validate the personal information provided.
FR3: The system shall ask the user to re-enter details if the username is taken.
FR4: The system shall require password complexity rules.

3.2 Image Upload for Pest Detection
FR1: The system shall allow logged-in users to upload images of crops suspected of pests.
FR2: The system shall enhance the uploaded image for better pest recognition.
FR3: The system shall employ pest identification models (YOLOv8, R-CNN) to classify pests and provide counts.
FR4: The system shall support image formats like PNG and JPEG.

3.3 Generate Diagnosis Report
FR1: The system shall allow users to generate a detailed diagnostic report based on pest detection results.
FR2: The system shall compile pest types, counts, and severity levels into a report.
FR3: The system shall generate reports within 10 seconds.

3.4 View Diagnosis History
FR1: The system shall allow users to view previously generated diagnosis reports.
FR2: The system shall retrieve and display all previous diagnosis results in the "History" section within 5 seconds.

3.5 Chat with Chatbot
FR1: The system shall include a chatbot to assist users with queries.
FR2: The chatbot shall respond to user queries within 5 seconds.
FR3: The chatbot shall ask for clarification if it does not understand the query.

3.6 Display Pest Control Instructions
FR1: The system shall display pest control instructions after pests are identified.
FR2: The system shall retrieve instructions from a database.

3.7 Image Enhancement
FR1: The system shall upload photos after improving quality for accurate pest analysis.
FR2: The image enhancement process shall not exceed 10 seconds.

3.8 Assess Pest Intensity
FR1: The system shall grade infestation intensity as low, moderate, or severe based on pest types and count.
FR2: The system shall grade intensity within 10 seconds of pest detection.

3.9 User Profile Customization
FR1: The system shall allow users to update and change personal information.
FR2: The system shall validate updated profile information before saving.
FR3: The system shall update profile information securely in real time.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall log error messages with details for further analysis.
NFR2: Error detection mechanisms will be provided to monitor system health and report failures to administrators.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The application shall allow users to access past pest detection results through one interaction from the dashboard.
NFR4: The system shall offer an intuitive interface with clear buttons and instructions.
NFR5: The system shall require no more than three steps for uploading an image and getting pest detection.
NFR6: The system shall ensure accessibility for people with disabilities.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: The application shall upload images and respond with pest detection results within 10 seconds.
NFR8: 95% of generated reports shall be downloadable within 10 seconds of a user’s request.
NFR9: The system shall maintain fast data retrieval for prior reports.

4.4 Security
NFR10: The system will implement role-based access control to prevent exposure of sensitive data.
NFR11: The system shall undergo regular security audits and vulnerability assessments every 6 months.

SurgiGuide seeks to increase awareness during surgery, improve outcomes, and enhance surgical growth by providing contextually relevant data and corrective feedback.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Real-time identification of surgical phases.
- Prediction of the next surgical step.
- Identification and flagging of mistakes.
- Provision of corrective actions.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics

- Surgical Trainees: Who will benefit from real-time feedback as an educational tool.

2.4 Operating Environment
Integrates with operating room video streams.

3.2 Next Step Suggestion

3.3 Mistake Identification
FR3.1: The system shall compare actual surgical activities against a database of standards and mishaps.
FR3.2: The system shall flag deviations from standard procedures.

3.4 Remedy Provision
FR4.1: The system shall provide recommendations for corrective adjustments when a mistake is indicated.

Project: Amauton - Streamlining Amazon Seller Operations Through Advanced Automation and AI
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document specifies the requirements for Amauton, an automation system for Amazon Seller Accounts (focusing on the UK market) that helps sellers manage accounts, increase profitability, and make better decisions through automation and AI.

1.2 Scope
The system streamlines seller operations. Features include product discovery (niche-specific recommendations, market trend analysis), supplier sourcing (identifying suitable suppliers, AI consistency checks), profitability analysis and listing creation (auto-generate titles, descriptions, pricing), dynamic pricing and competitor analysis, and sales analytics dashboard.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- API: Application Programming Interface
- DB: Database

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Amauton automates time-consuming seller operations like product hunting, pricing comparison, and profit calculation, enabling sellers to focus on strategic growth.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Product Discovery and Extraction: Search by keywords, apply filters, extract product data, analyze market trends.
- Supplier Sourcing: Identify suitable suppliers, use AI to check product image/title consistency.
- Profitability Analysis and Listings: Calculate profit margins, automatically create product listings.
- Dynamic Pricing and Competitor Analysis: Real-time price adjustment and competitor monitoring.
- Sales Analytics: Data visualization, actionable insights dashboard.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Amazon Sellers: Manage product listings, pricing, supplier relationships. Use the system for product discovery, pricing, and market alerts.
- Data Analysts: Analyze sales data, profitability patterns, market dynamics using the system's analytics.
- Retailers and Suppliers: Provide product information to the system for seller sourcing.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Product Discovery and Extraction
FR1: System shall display current market trends in human-readable form with necessary information.
FR2: System shall allow user to enter product details and cater to any style of input.
FR3: System shall allow user to apply custom filters based on the options provided.
FR4: System shall correctly apply filters and move from generalization to specialization for efficient sourcing.
FR5: System shall search for the entered product on different stores and correctly retrieve required information.

3.2 Supplier Sourcing
FR1: System shall find the entered product on different stores specified in the scope.
FR2: System shall identify suitable suppliers by refining based on profit margins.
FR3: System shall retrieve required product information from selected suppliers.
FR4: System shall efficiently compare and match relevant information (title, price, images).

3.3 Profitability Analysis and Listings
FR1: System shall calculate profit margins leveraging different sales and analytics techniques.
FR2: System shall automatically manage the listing of products on the user's Amazon store.
FR3: System shall generate required product information (title, description, price, image) and place it correctly.

3.4 Dynamic Pricing and Competitor Analysis
FR1: System shall relentlessly look for changing competitors' prices.
FR2: System shall constantly adjust prices to be in the competitive race.
FR3: The system shall drop the product if it lacks profitability or sales activity.

3.5 Sales Analytics
FR1: System shall present sales and profit of different products in an interactive manner.
FR2: System shall generate effective insights from the user's sales history.
FR3: The system shall provide different visualizations for easy and better understanding.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: In case of failure (fetching data, listing product, product hunting cycle), system must rollback and try 2-3 times before logging the error.
NFR2: In case of continuous failure, system must notify the user or administrator through email.
NFR3: The system shall have a minimum MTBF of 30 days.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: System should be user-friendly and interactive, features easily accessible and understandable.
NFR5: System shall have a logical flow and each feature must perform what it is entitled to.
NFR6: The system shall record repetitive tasks to increase efficiency of interaction.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: Website must load within 10 seconds along with text and graphics.
NFR8: System shall perform specific tasks (pricing adjustments, product matching) in logical timeframes (e.g., within 2 minutes).

4.4 Security
NFR9: Website must be secure from attacks which might result in data leak.
NFR10: It must use strong and secure methods to store passwords in the database.
NFR11: User information must be handled efficiently, with no loss tolerance in case of a data breach.
NFR12: It must support multi-factor authentication and email confirmations to manage access.

The app aims to help cat owners manage their pet's health, diet, and overall care. Features include breed detection via image upload, personalized training and care recommendations (including special care for newborns/pregnant cats), health and nutrition management (diet tracking, symptom analysis, vet recommendations), a cat accessory finder, an AI-powered CatBot for quick questions, and a GPS tracker for locating cats via a custom collar.

- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- GPS: Global Positioning System
- ML: Machine Learning

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
PurrfectAssistant provides a personalized, all-in-one solution to help cat owners proactively manage their pets' health and well-being, integrating AI for personalized suggestions.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Breed Detection: Identify cat breed from uploaded pictures.
- Training and Care: Personalized recommendations for training, grooming, exercise, and special care.
- Health and Nutrition: Personalized nutrition plans, diet tracking, symptom analysis, vet clinic recommendations.
- Accessories: Web-scraped accessory listings with price filters.
- CatBot: AI-powered chatbot for quick answers.
- GPS Tracker: Real-time location tracking via a custom collar.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Cat Owners: Primary caregivers seeking personalized care solutions.
- Veterinarians: Medical professionals who can use the app for assistance and sponsor their clinics.
- Cat Accessory Sellers: Retailers who can sponsor and promote their products.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Breed Detection
FR1: The app shall allow users to use the built-in phone camera to capture cat images within the app.
FR2: The app shall allow users to upload cat images to the app.
FR3: The app shall provide near real-time breed detection from the uploaded cat images.

3.2 Training and Care
FR1: The app shall allow users to generate a personalized cat training plan.
FR2: The app shall provide personalized grooming tips.
FR3: The app shall allow users to generate a personalized exercise plan.
FR4: The app shall assist users to follow and track training and exercise plans.
FR5: The app shall provide specialized guidance regarding newborn cats, pregnant cats, and neutering.

3.3 Health and Nutrition
FR1: The app shall allow users to generate personalized nutrition plans.
FR2: The app shall allow users to track their cat’s daily nutrition intake.
FR3: The app shall allow users to input their cat’s health issues and symptoms.
FR4: The app shall provide symptom analysis reports and further guidance.
FR5: The app shall provide nearest veterinarian clinic recommendations.
FR6: The app shall provide developer contact info for veterinarians for potential sponsorships.

3.4 Accessories
FR1: The app shall allow users to choose from different categories of accessories.
FR2: The app shall provide users with a significant number of products and listings.
FR3: The app shall allow users to set a price filter for recommendations.
FR4: The app shall provide developer contact info for sellers for potential sponsorships.

3.5 Cat Bot
FR1: The app shall allow users to ask cat-care-related questions to an inbuilt chatbot.
FR2: The app shall provide short and reliable answers.

3.6 GPS Tracker
FR1: The app shall allow users to order a custom cat collar.
FR2: The app shall allow users to configure the custom collar with the app.
FR3: The app shall provide real-time GPS location of the cat collar.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The app shall have a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 500 hours.
NFR2: User data shall be backed up on the back-end database to avoid data loss.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The app shall provide a simple and straightforward user interface.
NFR4: The app shall use input validation in the chatbot to ensure cat-related questions are catered.
NFR5: The system shall allow users to complete primary functions (e.g., nutrition logging) within 3 clicks or less.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: The app shall identify the breed of uploaded images within 10 seconds.
NFR7: The app shall have an accuracy of at least 90% for correct breed detection.
NFR8: The app shall generate personalized nutrition, training, and exercise plans within 10 seconds.
NFR9: Symptom analysis should be accurate to at least 90%.
NFR10: The GPS tracker location should be accurate to within at least 15 meters.
NFR11: The app shall be able to handle at least 500 concurrent users initially.

4.4 Security
NFR12: The system shall manage user accounts with login IDs and passwords.
NFR13: The login and sign-up system shall be resistant to common vulnerabilities such as SQL injections.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: EDIConnect - EDI File Mapping and Conversion Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Companies using EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) must convert data into their own EDI standard and map that data into ERP systems. These mappings take significant time and human energy. The diversity of EDI standards and custom formats creates significant challenges for inter-company communication.

1.2 Scope
EDIConnect translates EDI data from one international standard or custom standard to another, and creates customizable mappings for EDI data into specific ERP system formats. Features include EDI standard identification, data preprocessing, standard-to-standard mapping, custom EDI-to-standard mapping, and EDI-to-ERP mapping.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- EDI: Electronic Data Interchange
- ERP: Enterprise Resource Planning
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- LLM: Large Language Model

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
EDIConnect reduces hindrances caused by differences in EDI standards and ERP systems, making it convenient for companies to apprehend messages and generate mappings for entering into respective ERP systems.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Identify EDI standard or custom EDI file
- Data preprocessing of EDI files
- Generate mappings across standards
- Generate mappings from custom EDI to EDI standard
- Generate mappings for EDI file to user's ERP system accepted format
- User-friendly interface

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Business Analytics: Manage business documents, need seamless system to map data
- IT Administrators: Maintain, configure, troubleshoot system
- Logistics Managers: Coordinate with suppliers, transportation providers, internal teams
- Finance Department Employees: Ensure accurate exchange of transactional data
- Warehouse Managers: Precisely describe information being exchanged
- End Users: Non-technical employees interacting with EDI documents

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application, cloud storage, MongoDB.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1 - EDI to EDI Mapping
FR1.1: UI shall allow users to input EDI file to generate mappings between different EDI standards.
FR1.2: UI shall ask user to enter mapping demand (EDI to EDI, non-EDI to EDI, or EDI to ERP).
FR1.3: UI shall ask for standards for converting files from and to.
FR1.4: If EDI file belongs to standards covered in scope, system shall give examples of standard-to-standard mappings based on predefined templates.
FR1.5: System shall automatically detect standard and pre-process data.
FR1.6: System shall start mapping from input standard to standard required by user.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: System shall generate clear error messages for upload issues or unsupported mappings.
NFR2: System shall allow users to manually correct or add data into generated final mappings.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Intuitive and user-friendly interface for input and visualization.
NFR4: Display file entered side-by-side with mapping result.
NFR5: Enable manual additions for missing information.

4.3 Security
NFR6: System shall secure user credentials for safe login and signup.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Decentrum - Decentralized Social Media Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document describes the requirements for Decentrum, a decentralized social media platform leveraging blockchain technology to provide users with control over their data, enhanced privacy, and freedom from centralized authority censorship.

1.2 Scope
The platform enables users to engage, communicate, and share content freely without third-party intervention. Key features include content creation/sharing/reaction, profile management with privacy controls, free content monetization, reward system (tokens for watching ads), community/group creation, private messaging, and KYC implementation to prevent bots.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- KYC: Know Your Customer
- IPFS: InterPlanetary File System
- Blockchain: Distributed ledger technology

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Decentrum addresses concerns about data privacy, censorship, and lack of user control inherent in centralized social media networks by using peer-to-peer and blockchain technologies.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Content Management: Creation, sharing, reaction (likes/comments).
- Profile Management: Personal info editing, account & privacy controls.
- Monetization and Rewards: Free content monetization, token rewards for viewing ads.
- Community Management & Interactions: Group formation, community forums, private messaging.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- General Users: Majority who interact with content, manage profiles, engage with others.
- Content Creators: Focus on producing original content (videos, images, text) and monetizing it.
- Community Builders: Create/manage groups and communities, facilitate discussions.
- Privacy Conscious Users: Prioritize security and control over communications and data visibility.
- Reward Seekers: Motivated by the token-based reward system, engage by watching ads.
- Moderators: Maintain platform safety, review reported content.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Content Management
FR1: The system shall allow content creation and posting in various forms (text, video, images, links to articles/blogs).
FR2: The system shall allow users to react to content through likes or comments.
FR3: The system shall enable users to share content posted on the platform with family and friends.
FR4: The system shall allow users to tag their friends on any post.
FR5: The system shall send reported content to 13 random users to verify if it is explicit.

3.2 Profile Management
FR1: The system shall allow users to make changes to personal information (profile/cover photos).
FR2: The system shall enable users to manage and customize their timeline.
FR3: The system shall allow users to change their passwords.
FR4: The system shall enable users to manage post visibility settings.
FR5: The system shall enable users to customize notification preferences.
FR6: The system shall implement KYC mechanism to cater to bots.

3.3 Monetization and Rewards
FR1: The system shall enable content creators to monetize their content without requiring any premium version.
FR2: Upon registration, the system shall give a reward token to new users to enable the transactional process.
FR3: The reward system shall enable rewards based on the user’s choice to view advertisements.

3.4 Community Management & Interactions
FR1: It shall give the opportunity to form groups with family and friends, sharing private interactions.
FR2: The system shall support forum creation based on the subject and notify participants when an event has occurred.
FR3: The system will offer messaging functionality where users will have direct private chats with their friends.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have 99.9% availability, excluding scheduled maintenance.
NFR2: The system shall ensure backup and recovery mechanisms in case of failure.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The interface shall be intuitive and easy to use by people of any age.
NFR4: The system shall include search functionality enabling at least an 80% chance of easily locating a friend, content, or group.
NFR5: The system shall provide users with preference options to display content (newest, most popular) with instantaneous selections.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: The system shall be able to complete posting and content creation operations within 5 seconds under normal circumstances.
NFR7: The system shall ensure that at least 1000 users are supported simultaneously without any decline in performance.

4.4 Security
NFR8: The system shall ensure that user passwords and sensitive data are encrypted both in transit and at rest.
NFR9: The system shall have role-based access control that restricts actions according to user roles.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: AtlasPredict - Real-Time Disaster Prediction System for Pakistan
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for AtlasPredict, a real-time disaster prediction system for Pakistan that integrates geospatial data from authoritative sources (NASA, NDMA) and applies advanced machine learning models to predict floods, heavy rainfall, and landslides.

1.2 Scope
The system ingests data from NASA and NDMA (with future plans for SUPARCO, PMD), preprocesses it, and uses ML models (Random Forest, CNN for initial flood prediction; LSTM, SVM planned) to provide localized predictions. Key features include real-time data streaming, automated alerts, an interactive web-based Pakistan Atlas for visualization, and community reporting.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- ML: Machine Learning
- CNN: Convolutional Neural Network
- LSTM: Long Short-Term Memory
- NDMA: National Disaster Management Authority (Pakistan)
- SUPARCO: Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission (Pakistan)
- PMD: Pakistan Meteorological Department

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
AtlasPredict bridges the technological gap in Pakistan's disaster management framework by providing a centralized, real-time platform that offers precise, timely warnings and localized risk analysis to enhance disaster preparedness and response.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Client Web Application: User dashboard with predictions and alerts, interactive Pakistan Atlas, alert notification system.
- Admin Web Application: Data management interface, machine learning model management, system monitoring.
- Machine Learning and Prediction Engine: Data preprocessing, predictive modeling (Random Forest, CNN), model retraining, real-time data integration.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Government Authorities (NDMA, PDMA): Need real-time data and forecasts for resource allocation, evacuation strategies.
- Emergency Responders (Rescue 1122): Need real-time alerts and predictive data to mobilize quickly.
- General Public: Citizens in disaster-prone areas who access region-specific alerts and visualizations.
- System Administrators: Manage data integrity, model training, and system performance.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Client Web Application
FR1: The web application shall display real-time disaster predictions (floods, heavy rainfall) based on satellite and weather data.
FR2: The system shall provide users with localized, customizable alerts.
FR3: The system shall allow users to access the interactive Pakistan Atlas for real-time data visualizations of high-risk areas.
FR4: Users shall receive region-specific advisories, including evacuation instructions based on risk classification.
FR5: The system shall allow users to review past predictions and advisories via an alert history.

3.2 Admin Web Application
FR1: The admin interface shall allow administrators to validate and integrate datasets from sources such as NASA and NDMA.
FR2: The system shall enable administrators to retrain machine learning models based on new data inputs.
FR3: The system shall allow administrators to monitor system performance, data integrity, and prediction accuracy in real-time.
FR4: Administrators shall be able to manually trigger alerts or override automated predictions when necessary.
FR5: The admin web application shall support multi-factor authentication for secure access.

3.3 Machine Learning and Prediction Engine
FR1: The system shall ingest geospatial datasets (satellite imagery, weather data) from NASA and NDMA, and preprocess the data for analysis.
FR2: The machine learning models (Random Forest, CNN) shall generate predictions of floods and heavy rainfall.
FR3: The system shall automatically retrain models based on new data for improved accuracy.
FR4: The prediction engine shall trigger alerts when the risk threshold for a disaster is crossed.
FR5: The system shall validate and incorporate community-reported data in real-time to refine predictions.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall maintain an availability of 99.9% with an MTBF of 12 months.
NFR2: Errors in data ingestion or model execution shall trigger alerts in the admin dashboard.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The system's user interface shall allow users to navigate to real-time disaster predictions and alerts within three interactions.
NFR4: The interface shall be mobile-compatible.

4.3 Performance
NFR5: The system shall process incoming satellite data within 10-30 seconds, updating predictions in real-time.
NFR6: The client web application shall display prediction results and visualizations within 5-10 seconds on a standard internet connection.

4.4 Security
NFR7: All data transmissions between the web application and backend servers shall be encrypted using SSL/TLS.
NFR8: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) shall be required for administrators accessing the data management and machine learning modules.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: KubeSecure - AI Driven Anomaly Detection in Kubernetes Cluster
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
Cyberattacks are becoming more frequent and sophisticated; estimated $12.5 billion lost to cyberattacks in 2023. Kubernetes clusters are prime targets for DDoS attacks and other malicious activities. Traditional detection methods cannot keep up with evolving cyber attacks.

1.2 Scope
KubeSecure is an AI-based anomaly detection system for Kubernetes clusters using deep learning models (Transformer-based, LSTM) to monitor network traffic and detect security risks. Features include real-time anomaly detection, automated threat mitigation (rate limiting, traffic redirection to honeypots), static rule writing for Cilium, YAML file misconfiguration scanning, and web/Android application for alerts.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- DDoS: Distributed Denial of Service
- LSTM: Long Short Term Memory
- YAML: YAML Ain't Markup Language
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
KubeSecure provides flexible and comprehensive protection by learning continuously from new attack patterns, recognizing both known and unknown attack vectors.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Kubernetes cluster monitoring with Prometheus/Grafana
- Network traffic collection and duplication to deep learning model
- Real-time traffic analysis
- Automated threat mitigation (rate limiting, Cilium rule updates)
- Honeypot for traffic analysis
- YAML file scanning for misconfigurations
- Admin panel for rule management
- Android app for alerts

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Security Team: Monitor cluster, respond to anomalies
- DevOps Engineers: Manage infrastructure, review YAML files
- Admin Users: Write Cilium rules, manage configurations

2.4 Operating Environment
Kubernetes cluster, Cilium for network policies, Prometheus/Grafana for monitoring, Android application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Anomaly Detection
FR1.1: Network traffic metrics shall be collected from Cilium.
FR1.2: Cluster and application metrics shall be collected from Prometheus.
FR1.3: Grafana dashboards shall show metrics from Prometheus and Cilium.
FR1.4: The system shall get streaming network traffic to deployed application.
FR1.5: Incoming traffic shall be duplicated to deep learning model pod.
FR1.6: Trained deep learning model pod shall be deployed for traffic analysis.
FR1.7: When malicious traffic batch detected, system shall issue alert.
FR1.8: Prometheus shall call webhook to run python script for rate limiting upon alert.
FR1.9: Python script shall dynamically update Cilium rules or call eBPF script.
FR1.10: Alerts shall be sent to Android application, Slack, and email.

3.2 Network Policies
FR2.1: The system shall detect simple DoS attacks (TCP Flooding) by tracking violations of static rules.
FR2.2: Upon recognizing rule violation, system shall rate limit incoming traffic.

3.3 Honeypot for Traffic Analysis
FR3.1: The system shall identify malicious traffic based on suspicious packet loads, aberrant traffic dynamics, attack origins.
FR3.2: The system shall forward malicious traffic batches to TPOT Honeypot for investigation.
FR3.3: Prior to forwarding, system shall feature metadata (source IP, detection time).
FR3.4: TPOT Honeypot shall generate enhanced analysis reports on traffic behaviors, attack trends, exploit vectors.

3.4 Yaml File Checker
FR4.1: The system shall scan uploaded Kubernetes .yaml files for configuration errors (RBAC misconfigurations, resource allocation issues, security statement errors, obsolete API versions).
FR4.2: The system shall identify misconfigurations by evaluating against industry standards.
FR4.3: The platform shall issue notifications (email, dashboard, system signals) for identified issues.

3.5 Web Application
FR5.1: The platform shall support web submissions of Kubernetes .yaml files for analysis.
FR5.2: Upon submission, system shall process and scan for misconfigurations.
FR5.3: Admin can view and setup Cilium rules via dropdown menu.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 User-Friendly
NFR1: System optimized for easy learning and operation.

4.2 Real-Time Data Processing
NFR2: System shall process real-time data with minimal delay.

4.3 Scalability
NFR3: System shall scale to support increased network traffic and monitoring needs.

4.4 Reliability
NFR4: System shall have redundancy and failover mechanisms for continuous operation.

4.5 Interoperability
NFR5: System shall be compatible with existing tools (CI/CD pipelines, monitoring systems, security tools).

4.6 Security
NFR6: Traffic analysis deep learning pod shall be isolated from external access.
NFR7: Under high-level DDoS attack, pod shall remain secure and stable.

Project: Legal Case Management System (Paralegal)
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document provides a comprehensive Software Requirements Specification for the development of a Legal Case Management System for Pakistan. It aims to streamline legal case management, reduce administrative burden, and improve efficiency.

1.2 Scope
The system provides functionalities for case tracking, document management, court scheduling, client communication, and reporting. It integrates with existing legal databases and supports multi-user access with role-based permissions.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- SRS: Software Requirements Specification
- MERN: MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js
- API: Application Programming Interface
- GDPR: General Data Protection Regulation

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The system is a comprehensive solution for law firms, courts, and government agencies, integrating modern technology with user-centric design to manage legal cases, documents, and communication.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Case Management: Creation, tracking, history.
- Document Management: Storage, version control, access control.
- Court Scheduling: Calendar, notifications, conflict management.
- Client Communication: Secure messaging, client portal, appointment scheduling.
- Reporting and Analytics: Customizable reports, data analytics.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Case Manager: Oversees case assignments, monitors progress, manages client interactions.
- Social Worker: Conducts client interviews, tracks progress.
- Client: Views case details, updates information, communicates.
- Administrator: Manages user accounts, configures settings.
- Supervisor: Monitors performance of case managers and social workers.
- Program Coordinator: Manages specific programs or initiatives.
- IT Support: Provides technical support and system maintenance.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application (modern browsers) and mobile application (Android, iOS).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Case Creation and Management
FR1.1: The system shall allow users to enter case details including title, description, priority, and status.
FR1.2: The system shall allow users to assign a case to a specific team or individual.
FR1.3: The system shall allow users to set deadlines and milestones for the case.
FR1.4: The system shall store and display case creation date and time.
FR1.5: The system shall display case details, history, and deadlines.
FR1.6: The system shall allow users to update case details (status, priority, assignment).
FR1.7: The system shall log all changes made to the case.

3.2 Case Assignment and Tracking
FR2.1: The system shall provide a list of available team members or departments for assignment.
FR2.2: The system shall allow users to assign or reassign cases.
FR2.3: The system shall notify assigned personnel of new case assignments.
FR2.4: The system shall provide a visual representation of case progress.
FR2.5: The system shall allow users to update progress of tasks and milestones.
FR2.6: The system shall generate reports on case progress.

3.3 Case Resolution and Closure
FR3.1: The system shall allow users to mark a case as resolved.
FR3.2: The system shall require users to provide a resolution summary or closure notes.
FR3.3: The system shall update the case status to "Resolved" and lock further modifications.
FR3.4: The system shall allow users to close a case once resolved.
FR3.5: The system shall archive case details and related documents upon closure.
FR3.6: The system shall generate a final report summarizing the case resolution.
FR3.7: The system shall allow users to review case history and final resolution.
FR3.8: The system shall provide tools for generating audit reports.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: Response time for user interactions shall be optimized to complete within 3 seconds.
NFR2: The system shall process and display search results for up to 10,000 cases within 5 seconds.
NFR3: The system shall handle up to 1,000 concurrent users while maintaining smooth interactions.
NFR4: CPU usage shall not exceed 70% during peak load.
NFR5: Average memory footprint shall not surpass 2 GB per active user session.

4.2 Safety
NFR6: The system shall incorporate transaction rollback and recovery procedures.
NFR7: The system shall provide detailed error messages and logging.
NFR8: The system shall maintain an uptime of 99.9%.

4.3 Security
NFR9: The system shall support multi-factor authentication (MFA).
NFR10: The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC).
NFR11: The system shall utilize industry-standard encryption algorithms (AES-256) for data in transit and at rest.
NFR12: The system shall maintain an audit trail of user actions and system events.
NFR13: Regular security assessments and vulnerability scans shall be conducted.

4.4 Software Quality Attributes
NFR14: The system shall offer an intuitive and user-friendly interface.
NFR15: The system shall be designed with a modular architecture for easy maintenance.
NFR16: The system shall be designed to support future enhancements without major modifications.
NFR17: The system shall be optimized for performance, executing operations efficiently.

4.5 Business Rules
NFR18: The system shall support case priority levels (Low, Medium, High, Urgent).
NFR19: The system shall enforce case assignment rules based on expertise and availability.
NFR20: The system shall ensure all required tasks are completed and approval is obtained before allowing a case to be closed.

Project: BTCConversa - Your Personalized Banking Assistant
================================================================================

This document outlines the requirements for BTC Conversa, a conversational AI-powered banking assistant designed to simplify and automate routine banking tasks through text-based conversations, offering a more personalized and user-friendly experience.

The system focuses on key functionalities: balance inquiry, account statement retrieval, money transfer, and branch information request. It supports multi-query interactions (e.g., "What is my balance, NTN, and e-mail?"). The system includes session storage for consistency across devices, personalized financial insights, and non-financial action handling (bill payments, adding payees). Future versions will add advanced analytics and advisory functions.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations

- RASA: Open-source machine learning framework for conversational AI
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
BTC Conversa is a conversational AI assistant that integrates with banking systems to provide secure, personalized, and efficient customer interactions, handling queries and actions via natural language.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Information Management: Access account details, transaction history, branch information.
- Action Handling: Perform financial actions (fund transfers, bill payments) and non-financial actions (card management, alerts).
- Analysis Module: Expenditure and payment analysis based on transaction history.
- Advisory Module: Personalized financial tips, budgeting, and savings advice.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Bank Customers: Manage accounts, query balances, initiate transfers, receive financial tips.
- Bank Staff: Manage customer interactions, debug issues, update backend info.
- IT Administrators: Manage infrastructure, security, API integrations.

FR2: The system shall allow users to view their transaction history for a specified period.

FR2: The system shall allow users to add new payees and manage payees.
FR3: The system shall allow users to pay bills through the chatbot interface.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability

NFR3: The chatbot shall support natural language queries in both English and Urdu.
NFR4: The chatbot shall allow users to query multiple account details in a single interaction (e.g., "What is my balance and my recent transactions?").

4.3 Performance
NFR5: The system should handle up to 1,000 concurrent users with no more than a 5% drop in response time.
NFR6: 95% of queries should be processed within 3 seconds of submission (assuming 10Mbps connection).

4.4 Security

NFR8: The system shall implement two-factor authentication (2FA).
NFR9: The system should log all access attempts and flag suspicious behavior for administrative review.
