SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: DataGenie - AI-Powered Synthetic Data Generation for Fabric Defect Detection
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for DataGenie, an AI-powered system for the textile industry that generates synthetic images of fabric defects to train defect detection models (YOLO), providing a cost-effective and efficient alternative to collecting large real-world datasets.

1.2 Scope
The system uses a Stable Diffusion model to generate synthetic defect images based on user prompts (processed by an LLM). It includes a YOLO model for real-time defect detection, a verification model to compare synthetic vs. real data, predictive analytics for defect trends, and a user-friendly web application.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- YOLO: You Only Look Once
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- UI: User Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
DataGenie addresses the challenge of obtaining sufficient and representative real-world defective fabric samples for training AI models by generating high-quality synthetic data, enabling more robust defect detection systems.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Dataset Annotation and Stable Diffusion Model Development: Manual annotation, train Stable Diffusion for synthetic image generation.
- NLP Model Training and Database Setup: Train LLM to interpret user prompts, organize datasets in database.
- YOLO Model Integration and Web App Development: Integrate and train YOLO for defect detection, optimize web app UI.
- Verification Model and Predictive Analytics: Verify synthetic data accuracy, implement predictive analytics for defect trends.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- AI Developer: Builds and deploys machine learning models (Stable Diffusion, YOLO, LLM).
- Quality Assurance Manager: Ensures synthetic datasets and defect detection models meet standards.
- Textile Manufacturer: Main user who uploads fabric images and uses defect detection results to improve quality control.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Synthetic Data Generation
FR1: The system must be able to generate synthetic fabric defect datasets using a Stable Diffusion model.
FR2: Users must be able to provide input through prompts to generate specific types of fabric defects.

3.2 Defect Detection
FR1: The system must integrate a defect detection model to perform real-time fabric defect detection according to given images.
FR2: The system must allow users to upload fabric images and receive detection results.

3.3 Prompt-Based Defect Generation
FR1: Users must be able to specify defect types and orientations through natural language prompts.

3.4 Model Verification
FR1: The system must include a verification model to compare generated synthetic datasets against real-world data to ensure accuracy.
FR2: The system must validate synthetic data accuracy by determining if the synthetic images resemble actual defects.

3.5 Predictive Analytics
FR1: The system must provide predictive analytics based on historical fabric defect data or production conditions (humidity, temperature).
FR2: Predictive analysis should help identify when and where defects are most likely to occur.

3.6 Database Management
FR1: The system must store annotated and synthetic datasets in a database for easy retrieval.
FR2: The system must support the storage of real-time detection results and user inputs.

3.7 Web Application Interface
FR1: The system must offer a user-friendly web interface for all functionalities (uploading images, entering prompts, viewing results).

3.8 Support for Multiple Defect Types
FR1: The system must allow generating and detecting a variety of fabric defects.

3.9 Training and Optimization
FR1: The system must allow for the training and optimization of AI models (Stable Diffusion, YOLO) to improve accuracy and efficiency.
FR2: The system should enable model retraining based on new data or feedback.

3.10 Data Collection and Pre-processing
FR1: The system must support data collection and pre-processing functionalities to properly annotate and label fabric defect images for model training.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system is required that the mean time between failures is at least 500 hours.
NFR2: Failures should be handled such that they spring back up without disturbing the process within 5 minutes.
NFR3: The system should have a capability to return to the previous successful configuration in case of error.
NFR4: The system should have a backup mechanism to back up datasets in the cloud.

4.2 Usability
NFR5: The web application will have an easy-to-use interface, allowing first-time users to complete synthetic dataset generation within 10 minutes.
NFR6: Feedback to prompts shall be within 2 seconds.
NFR7: The system should have recognizable error messages and supplied recovery.

4.3 Performance
NFR8: The Stable Diffusion model shall produce synthetic fabric defect dataset within 5 minutes for several images.
NFR9: The system should perform defect detection within 5ms per image on a high-performance GPU.
NFR10: The Large Language Model (LLM) shall reply to user prompts within 5 seconds.
NFR11: The system should support up to 500 concurrent users without decreasing efficiency.

4.4 Security
NFR12: All data transmissions between client and server must be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
NFR13: User sessions should be authenticated using JWT and support OAuth 2.0.
NFR14: The system shall enforce strong password policies.
NFR15: The system should automatically log out users after 15 minutes of inactivity.
NFR16: The system should ensure that only authorized users can access the application.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Khaka - Sketching Companion for Starters
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for Khaka, an AI-based web application that helps artists and designers (especially beginners) create hand-drawn sketches by transforming images into detailed sketch layers and providing step-by-step instructions.

1.2 Scope
The system allows users to upload an image, apply artistic stylizations, and then generates multiple sketch layers (outlines, basic shapes, shading, details). It provides step-by-step instructions (text/audio) for drawing each layer, tool recommendations, interactive feedback, and an art gallery for sharing. Features also include export/saving and user gallery management.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- SVG: Scalable Vector Graphics
- UI: User Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Khaka simplifies the sketching process for beginners and intermediates by breaking down complex images into manageable layers and guiding them through the creation process, providing a structured path to improve drawing skills.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Image Upload and Processing: Upload images, auto-adjust quality.
- Artistic Visualization and Previewing: Apply artistic styles (manual or AI), preview final sketch.
- Sketch Layer Generation: Generate outline, basic shapes, shading, detail layers.
- Layer Display and Navigation: Toggle layers, view progression.
- Step-by-Step Drawing Instructions: Provide text and audio guidance.
- Tool Recommendation System: Suggest pencils, pens, brushes for each layer.
- Interactive Feedback: Compare user's drawn sketch with the original and provide tips.
- Export and Sharing: Export individual layers or final result, share on social media.
- User Gallery: View and share sketches.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Beginner Artists: New to drawing, seeking guidance.
- Intermediate Artists: Looking to refine skills or try new techniques.
- Professional Artists: Need features for complex sketches.
- Art Educators: Use layered sketches for creating tutorials.
- Casual Users: Browse the gallery or explore functionalities.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Art Gallery Management
FR1: The system shall allow users to access their personal art gallery.
FR2: The system shall display all saved sketches in a grid format.
FR3: The system shall enable users to select an artwork to edit, update, or delete.
FR4: If a user has no artwork, the system shall display a message and provide an option to add new sketches.

3.2 Image Upload and Processing
FR1: The system shall allow users to upload images from their devices.
FR2: The system shall process the uploaded image and forward it to the layering system.
FR3: If the user cancels the upload, the system shall discard the image and return to the previous state.
FR4: If the uploaded image format is not supported, the system shall display an error message.
FR5: If the uploaded image exceeds the allowed size limit, the system shall display an error message.
FR6: The system shall support common image formats such as JPEG and PNG.

3.3 Artistic Stylization
FR1: The system shall allow users to apply artistic styles to the uploaded image.
FR2: The system shall prompt users to specify the artistic style they wish to apply.
FR3: The system shall display the stylized version of the image after processing.
FR4: If the user cancels the stylization process, the system shall revert to the previous image view.
FR5: If the selected artistic style fails to apply, the system shall display an error message.

3.4 Sketch Preview
FR1: The system shall allow users to preview the final sketch created from the stylized image.
FR2: The system shall retrieve and display the stylized image when the user requests a preview.
FR3: If the preview fails due to an error, the system shall display an error message.
FR4: The user shall have the option to return to the stylization options after previewing.

3.5 Layer Breakdown
FR1: The system shall convert the uploaded image into multiple sketch layers.
FR2: The system shall generate the Outline/Contour Layer, Basic Shapes Layer, Shading Layer, and Detail Layer.
FR3: The system shall save the generated layers in the user session.
FR4: If the image processing fails due to a system error, the system shall display an error message.

3.6 Layer Management
FR1: The system shall allow users to toggle between different sketch layers.
FR2: The system shall display a list of all available layers for the user.
FR3: If no layers are available, the system shall display an error message.

3.7 Instruction and Guidance
FR1: The system shall provide step-by-step instructions for the current layer.
FR2: The system shall deliver instructions in textual or auditory format based on user preference.
FR3: If no specific instructions are available, the system shall notify the user.

3.8 Tool Recommendations
FR1: The system shall analyze the current sketch layer and recommend specific tools.
FR2: The user shall have the option to accept the recommended tools or choose their own.

3.9 Feedback Mechanism
FR1: The system shall allow users to receive feedback on their current sketch.
FR2: The user shall upload an image of their sketch for analysis.
FR3: The system shall analyze the sketch and generate feedback based on various parameters.
FR4: If the system is unable to provide specific feedback, it shall display a general message encouraging the user to take another image with better light or dark background.

3.10 Export Functionality
FR1: The system shall allow users to export or save an individual sketch layer.
FR2: The user shall select a file format and destination for saving the layer.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: Image layer generation should be processed within 60 seconds, even for high-resolution images.
NFR2: File storage should be optimized to minimize load times.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: App's interface should be intuitive and user-friendly, guiding users through the sketching process with clear instructions.
NFR4: The app should be responsive and functional on all devices.

4.3 Security
NFR5: The app should implement strong authentication measures such as two-factor authentication.
NFR6: Uploaded images and sketches must be handled securely, with encryption for data in transit and storage.
NFR7: The app should include content moderation to prevent sharing of inappropriate material.

4.4 Scalability
NFR8: The app must use cloud storage solutions to handle growing data volume.
NFR9: Implement caching mechanisms to optimize frequently accessed content.
NFR10: Load balancing should be in place to distribute traffic evenly.

4.5 Reliability
NFR11: The app should be available 24/7 and be fault-tolerant.
NFR12: Backups should be created of users' uploaded images to protect against system failure.

4.6 Maintainability
NFR13: The app must be easy to maintain with modular code and thorough documentation.

4.7 Compatibility
NFR14: The app should be compatible with all types of browsers and be responsive for desktop and mobile viewing.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: ConvoShop - Conversational AI Shopping Assistant
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for ConvoShop, a conversational AI shopping assistant that helps users find and compare products across various e-commerce platforms through a text-based chat interface and a browser extension.

1.2 Scope
The system uses web scraping to collect real-time product data (prices, reviews, availability) from multiple platforms. A conversational interface (with LLM integration) understands user queries. Features include product comparison, sentiment analysis of reviews, review aggregation, price monitoring with alerts, personalized recommendations, and cross-platform analysis.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- LLM: Large Language Model
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- API: Application Programming Interface
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
- NLP: Natural Language Processing

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
ConvoShop automates the tedious process of searching and comparing products across multiple websites, providing users with a single, convenient conversational interface to find the best deals and make informed purchase decisions.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- LLM Interaction Module: Process user queries, handle ambiguities, communicate with other modules.
- Web Scraping Agent Module: Real-time retrieval of product info (prices, reviews, availability) from multiple platforms.
- Product Comparison Module: Aggregate and compare product data across sources.
- Sentiment Analysis Module: Analyze user reviews to determine overall sentiment.
- Review Aggregation & Analysis Module: Aggregate user and professional reviews.
- Price Monitoring & Alert Module: Track product prices and notify users of changes.
- Data Storage & Management Module: Store product data, user preferences, cached data.
- Security & Web Scraping Control Module: Ensure safe operation and compliance.
- User Management & Preferences Module: Handle user profiles, preferences, personalized suggestions.
- Error Handling & Notifications Module: Catch errors, inform users, retry logic.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- (User classes are implied: End-users who shop online. The document focuses on system functionality and modules.)

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 LLM Interaction Module
FR1: The module shall handle ambiguous or incomplete input by asking for clarification or providing suggestions.
FR2: It must integrate with LangChain to enhance conversational abilities.
FR3: The module shall send product data requests to other modules.

3.2 Web Scraping Agent Module
FR1: It must scrape from multiple sources such as Daraz, Amazon, and eBay.
FR2: The module shall retry scraping in case of timeouts or failures.
FR3: Scraped data must be passed to comparison, sentiment analysis, and UI modules.

3.3 Product Comparison Module
FR1: It must handle missing or outdated data and notify users of issues.
FR2: The module shall compare products based on price, reviews, and sales metrics.

3.4 Sentiment Analysis Module
FR1: The module shall aggregate sentiment ratings from multiple platforms and provide a comprehensive score.
FR2: It should continue to provide reviews in case of analysis failure while notifying the user.

3.5 Review Aggregation & Analysis Module
FR1: It must generate an overall product rating based on aggregated reviews.
FR2: The module shall handle scenarios where no professional reviews are available.

3.6 Price Monitoring & Alert Module
FR1: It shall notify users when there is a significant price drop or a change in product ratings.
FR2: The module shall store price history for future reference.

3.7 Data Storage & Management Module
FR1: It must handle large datasets using MongoDB and Vector DB for efficient retrieval.
FR2: The system shall cache data to prevent repetitive scraping.

3.8 Security & Web Scraping Control Module
FR1: It shall monitor security threats and handle rate-limiting or IP blocking.

3.9 User Management & Preferences Module
FR1: User accounts shall be secured, and preferences stored for personalized suggestions.

3.10 Error Handling & Notifications Module
FR1: The system shall catch and log errors across all modules.
FR2: The system shall notify users in case of delays, missing data, or partial results.
FR3: It must implement retry mechanisms for failed operations.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: The system should return search results and product comparisons within 2-3 seconds on average.
NFR2: Web scraping and sentiment analysis operations must complete within 5-10 seconds.

4.2 Scalability
NFR3: The system should scale to handle multiple concurrent user requests and web scraping operations.
NFR4: MongoDB and Vector DB should support large datasets with efficient indexing.

4.3 Security
NFR5: User data must be encrypted both at rest and in transit.
NFR6: Web scraping agents should avoid violating terms of service and respect rate limits.
NFR7: Implement secure authentication (OAuth or JWT).

4.4 Usability
NFR8: The conversational UI must be intuitive, guiding users through product searches and comparisons.
NFR9: The mobile app must have feature parity with the web app.

4.5 Reliability
NFR10: The system should have 99.9% uptime.
NFR11: Web scraping agents must have a fallback system to handle platform downtime.

4.6 Maintainability
NFR12: The system must have modular and well-documented code.
NFR13: Support automated tests and monitoring.

4.7 Compliance
NFR14: Ensure compliance with data privacy regulations such as GDPR.
NFR15: Web scraping agents should comply with website terms.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: Dexter’s Laboratory: A VR Laboratory Simulator
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This proposal describes the goals, characteristics, and driving force behind Dexter's Laboratory (Dex's Lab), a 3D virtual chemistry lab for O-level and 9-10 pupils, designed to improve learning experiences by providing a safe, immersive VR environment for experimentation.

1.2 Scope
The simulator is designed for chemistry education in O Levels and Pakistan Matric standards (classes 9-11). It covers experiments like cation/anion tests, acid-base titrations, pH testing, and exothermic/endothermic reactions. It focuses on providing virtual hands-on experience with accurate 3D models and realistic simulations, developed exclusively for the Meta Quest platform.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- VR: Virtual Reality
- 3D: Three-Dimensional
- SDK: Software Development Kit

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Dex's Lab bridges the gap between theoretical learning and practical application by offering a realistic virtual lab that replicates actual laboratory experiences in a secure setting, overcoming limitations of physical labs.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Asset Creation: 3D environment, equipment, textures, player movement, virtual hands.
- Mechanic Design: Chemical reaction simulation, equipment handling, liquid pouring, experiment management.
- Level Design: Implementation of curriculum-specific experiments.
- Application Design: Safety protocols, UI for instructions, tutorial system.
- Testing: Validate mechanics, performance, player movement.
- Deployment and Optimization: Performance optimization, graphics enhancement, Meta Quest integration.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Students: Primary users who engage in experiments and learning activities. They require guidance, navigation, and self-directed learning opportunities.
- (Other stakeholders like project managers, testers, documentation writers are also intended readers).

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Asset Creation
FR1: The system shall use interactive 3D modeling for a fully immersive environment.
FR2: The system shall fully support 3D equipment creation with precise textures and color schemes.
FR3: The system shall provide smooth player movement for easy navigation in the VR environment.

3.2 Mechanic Design
FR1: The system shall use a real-time engine to simulate chemical reactions (e.g., mixing acids and bases).
FR2: The system shall provide laboratory apparatus handling (burettes, pipettes, thermometers).
FR3: The system shall support pouring of liquid and measure mechanics, guaranteeing precise volume calculations.
FR4: The system shall manage sample tutorials and guidance for chemistry experiments.

3.3 Level Design
FR1: The system shall cover educational experiments on acids, bases, and salts for 9-10 and O-levels students.
FR2: The system shall teach students about acidity and alkalinity by enabling pH testing.
FR3: The system shall teach students about conducting cation and anion tests for substance analysis.
FR4: The system shall demonstrate heat exchange experiments by simulating exothermic and endothermic reactions.
FR5: The system shall conduct experiments on the physical states of matter (solid, liquid, gas).
FR6: The system shall perform experiments on chemical reactivity based on the set curriculum.
FR7: The system shall walk through basic organic chemistry concepts.

3.4 Application Design
FR1: The system must offer a virtual safety protocol (virtual safety equipment, hazard warnings).
FR2: The system shall have a user-friendly UI displaying real-time feedback and instructions.
FR3: The system shall walk users through tutorials and guidance for interacting with the virtual environment.
FR4: The main menu interface shall allow users to choose and launch various experiments.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The application shall store the user's progress and save the main phases of an experiment.
NFR2: The system should replicate chemical reactions as close to the real world as possible (max 2% difference).
NFR3: The system should handle a minimum of 500 users without delay or inaccuracy.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: Users should be able to begin a new experiment from the home screen with no more than five clicks.
NFR5: New users shall be able to complete the basic tutorial and a small experiment within the first fifteen minutes.
NFR6: The application shall allow users to switch between controller and hands using the settings icon.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: The system should respond to user interaction with the environment within 50 milliseconds.
NFR8: Experiments conducted by the user shall be calculated and displayed in real-time without noticeable delay.

4.4 Security
NFR9: The application shall have admin control to fully manage experiments (adding, deleting, modifying).
NFR10: The system shall not save any other personal information apart from basic requirements and progress tracking.

4.5 Compatibility
NFR11: The system must be fully compatible with Meta Quest 2 and utilize VR-based functionality across multiple platforms.

4.6 Scalability
NFR12: The application must be capable of storing and managing data for at least 500 individual users.
NFR13: The system must be able to accommodate future expansions without restructuring.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: CampusEye - Distributed Anomaly Detection for Campus Safety
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the framework for a real-time anomaly detection system to improve security on university campuses using CCTV footage.

1.2 Scope
The system aims to detect smoking, violence, and vandalism in real-time using machine learning. It uses distributed computing to spread the computational load across idle PCs on the university network, making it cost-effective and scalable.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CCTV: Closed-Circuit Television
- ML: Machine Learning
- MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The system automates surveillance to provide proactive security, allowing for faster reaction to incidents compared to traditional manual monitoring.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Real-time video analysis.
- Anomaly detection (smoking, violence, vandalism).
- Alert and notification generation.
- Distributed computing for load balancing.
- Dashboard for security personnel.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Security Personnel: Monitor live feeds and react to alerts.
- IT Administrators: Manage backend, nodes, and model updates.
- University Administrators: Utilize the system for campus safety insights.
- Maintenance Technicians: Oversee CCTV hardware and network.

2.4 Operating Environment
Integrates with existing university CCTV networks; uses idle PCs for distributed processing.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Dashboard & Monitoring Module
FR1.1: The system shall display live video feeds and detected anomalies.
FR1.2: The system shall provide options to view incident clips, analyze data, and generate reports.
FR1.3: The system shall allow configuration of system parameters and alert thresholds.

3.2 Video Input & Preprocessing Module
FR2.1: The system shall capture and process real-time video streams.
FR2.2: The system shall preprocess video data (resizing, normalization).
FR2.3: The system shall handle multiple camera inputs and synchronize feeds.

3.3 Distributed Computing Module
FR3.1: The system shall allocate tasks to idle machines for parallel video processing.
FR3.2: The system shall ensure load balancing across distributed nodes.
FR3.3: The system shall monitor and optimize computational resource usage.

3.4 Anomaly Detection Module
FR4.1: The system shall apply fine-tuned pre-trained models to classify anomalies (smoking, vandalism, violence).
FR4.2: The system shall support real-time analysis of streaming data.

3.5 Alert and Notification Module
FR5.1: The system shall generate instant notifications upon anomaly detection.
FR5.2: The system shall send alerts via email or a dedicated dashboard.
FR5.3: The system shall provide a timestamp and link to the detected video clip.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: The system ought to evaluate incoming video data promptly with little delay.
NFR2: Available resources must be evenly divided across machines.

4.2 Scalability
NFR3: The system shall accommodate new video streams by increasing nodes while maintaining performance.

4.3 Reliability
NFR4: The system shall have low downtime and distribute tasks to other nodes upon failure.
NFR5: The system needs to ensure maximum uptime at all times.

4.4 Usability
NFR6: The interface should be simple and intuitive for security personnel.
NFR7: Alerts should be informative and offer easy interaction.

4.5 Maintainability
NFR8: The system should allow for updating detection models without prolonged interruptions.

4.6 Compatibility
NFR9: The system needs to align easily with the current CCTV video setup.
NFR10: It should accommodate diverse hardware configurations.

4.7 Fault Tolerance
NFR11: The system needs to react to hardware/software breakdowns so that one failure does not affect overall efficiency.
NFR12: When a node fails, the system shall re-assign duties to usable nodes.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: PhysioVision - AI-Driven Physiotherapy Assistant for Arthritis
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for PhysioVision, an AI-driven application that provides personalized physiotherapy routines and nutrition plans for arthritis recovery.

1.2 Scope
The app combines a chatbot for personalized exercise/nutrition recommendations with a computer vision system for real-time posture correction and feedback. It generates session reports and tracks progress.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- CV: Computer Vision
- WHO: World Health Organization
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
PhysioVision addresses the limited access and high cost of physiotherapy in Pakistan by providing an at-home, AI-powered tool for arthritis management and recovery.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Vision Model: Real-time posture monitoring, correction, audio-bot, visual cues.
- Chat Bot: Personalized exercise and nutrition recommendations.
- Client Web App: User-friendly interface.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Patients: Individuals suffering from arthritis.
- Physiotherapist: Healthcare professionals who use the app to track patient progress.
- Caregiver: Family members assisting patients.
- AI/ML Researcher: Analyze algorithms used in the app.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Vision Model
FR1.1: The system shall generate a detailed report after each session, summarizing user performance, posture errors, and corrections made.
FR1.2: The system shall track user progress over time by comparing session reports.
FR1.3: The system shall provide visual graphs or data summaries on a weekly or monthly basis.
FR1.4: The system shall use a computer vision model to detect key body joints and positions in real-time.
FR1.5: The system shall analyze body positions to detect deviations from correct posture.
FR1.6: The system shall provide real-time feedback for incorrect posture via audio or on-screen text.
FR1.7: The system shall offer specific guidance to correct posture errors.

3.2 Chat Bot
FR2.1: The system shall allow users to input personal data (e.g., height, weight, arthritis severity).
FR2.2: The system shall collect feedback from users on exercise difficulty, pain level, and progress.
FR2.3: The system shall track exercise completion using data from the computer vision model.
FR2.4: The system shall generate personalized physiotherapy routines based on user inputs.
FR2.5: The system shall suggest tailored nutrition plans to support arthritis management.
FR2.6: The system shall generate weekly or monthly reports on user progress.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Multilingual Support
NFR1: The audio-bot and on-screen feedback must be available in English and Urdu.
NFR2: Users should be able to select their preferred language.

4.2 Performance
NFR3: The system must provide real-time posture analysis and feedback within a maximum delay of 200 milliseconds.

4.3 Compatibility
NFR4: The system must be compatible with smartphones, tablets, and computers across iOS, Android, and Windows.
NFR5: It should work seamlessly across different screen sizes.

4.4 Scalability
NFR6: The system should be scalable to accommodate future features (new exercises, languages).

4.5 Real-Time Interaction
NFR7: The chatbot must respond to user queries with minimal latency.

4.6 User-Friendly Interface
NFR8: The chatbot's UI must be intuitive, with easy navigation for all ages.

4.7 Accessibility
NFR9: The chatbot must support voice commands and text input for users with physical impairments.

4.8 Error Handling
NFR10: The chatbot should handle errors gracefully by providing appropriate feedback.

4.9 User Authentication
NFR11: Authentication must be secure, following industry standards (HTTPS, encryption).

4.10 Availability
NFR12: The Client Web Application must achieve high uptime.

4.11 Concurrent User Access
NFR13: The Client Web Application must support concurrent access by multiple users without performance degradation.

================================================================================
SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: BotNist - Automated Customer Service Chatbot for Websites
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for Bot-Nist, a web application that allows medium-scale website owners to create an advanced, AI-powered customer support chatbot by simply providing their website URL.

1.2 Scope
The system scrapes relevant data from the user's website, processes it using NLP, stores it in databases (standard and vector), and uses a fine-tuned LLM with RAG to answer customer queries. It generates an API key for easy integration. Features include analytics on popular queries and a calling feature (future scope).

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- LLM: Large Language Model
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
BotNist replaces basic script-based chatbots with an intelligent AI agent that can answer complex queries based on the specific content of the user's website, reducing reliance on human support agents.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Web Scraping: Extract relevant info (contact, services, legal, etc.) from provided URL.
- Data Processing and Structuring: Clean and organize raw data into structured format using NLP.
- Fine-Tuning and Model Training: Fine-tune a pre-trained LLM on the structured data.
- Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG): Fetch relevant data based on user query and generate contextual responses.
- API-Based Isolation: Provide unique API keys per user for integration, ensuring data separation.
- Web Interface: Simple UI for URL submission and API key generation.
- Query Handling and Response Generation: Process user queries and provide real-time responses.
- Security and Data Privacy: Ensure secure communication and data protection.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Website Owners: Integrate LLM-powered chatbot into their websites. Manage accounts, view analytics.
- Consumers (End Users): Interact with chatbots on websites.
- Admin (System Administrators): Manage users, monitor chatbot performance, respond to feedback.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Web Scraping Module
FR1: The module shall accept a user-provided URL.
FR2: The module shall extract all publicly available textual data including metadata.

3.2 Data Processing and Structuring Module
FR1: The module shall process the data to remove all unwanted text from scraped data.
FR2: The module shall structure the processed data to store in the RAG system.

3.3 Fine-Tuning and Model Training Module
FR1: The module shall allow for fine-tuning of the language model.
FR2: The module shall allow incremental training.

3.4 Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Module
FR1: The module shall allow querying of stored data to support quick response generation.
FR2: The module shall maintain context to give relevant responses based on previous conversation.

3.5 API-Based Isolation Module
FR1: The module shall generate API keys for users to access chatbot functionality.
FR2: The module shall limit the number of queries to prevent abuse.

3.6 Web Interface Module
FR1: The module shall allow the user to test the chatbot on our interface.
FR2: The module shall provide a user-friendly interface for the user to enter URL and get an API key.

3.7 Query Handling and Response Generation Module
FR1: The module shall respond according to the context of the current conversation.
FR2: The module shall get requests and respond to them in real time.

3.8 Security and Data Privacy Module
FR1: The module shall provide user authentication to limit access to data.
FR2: The module shall provide encryption for sensitive data like passwords.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system should ensure data integrity and accuracy during processing and response.
NFR2: The system should automatically try rescraping if a network failure or internal error occurs.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: Users should be able to enter the link and get an API key to integrate.
NFR4: Users should get real-time updates on the working of the link and see progress.
NFR5: Error messages should be clear.

4.3 Performance
NFR6: The system should be able to scrap data, process it, and provide the working API key within less than 30 minutes.
NFR7: The chat bot response time should be less than 2 seconds when it has 10,000 concurrent users.
NFR8: The system should be scalable with a response time degeneration of no more than 15% under high loads.

4.4 Security
NFR9: User accounts should be protected by multi-factor authentication.
NFR10: The login session should expire after 15 minutes of login to ensure security.
NFR11: The API Key should expire after every 12 months if not renewed by the user.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: GenFlex - AI-Powered Automated Examination and Evaluation Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for GenFlex, a digital platform designed to automate the complete examination process for educational institutions, from creation to grading, using AI technologies.

1.2 Scope
The system automates exam creation and evaluation. Features include digital exam paper generation (MCQs, essays, coding, math questions) using AI, lecture note generation from uploaded materials, automated grading using LLMs and specialized libraries, and a secure web interface for teachers and students.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- MCQ: Multiple Choice Question
- ITS: Intelligent Tutoring System (contextual, but not the primary focus)

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
GenFlex addresses the time-consuming and error-prone nature of manual exam processes by providing a seamless digital platform that automates exam creation, delivery, and grading, allowing teachers to focus more on instruction.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Teacher Module: Generate exam papers (MCQs, essay, coding) from uploaded materials, generate lecture notes.
- Student Module: Attempt exams digitally via web interface, receive quick grades.
- AI Evaluation Module: Use LLMs for essay evaluation, coding/math libraries for respective questions.
- Security and Authentication Module: Secure login, role-based access, data encryption.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Teacher: Creates exams, generates lecture notes, reviews automated evaluations, can adjust grades.
- Student: Attempts exams online, receives quick, accurate grades.
- IT Staff: Maintains system security, database, and resolves technical issues.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Teacher Module
FR1: The system shall allow teachers to create multiple-choice questions (MCQs).
FR2: The system shall enable teachers to create essay-type questions.
FR3: The system shall provide functionality for teachers to make coding-related questions.
FR4: The system shall allow teachers to upload PDF documents for course materials.
FR5: The system shall enable teachers to upload presentation slides for lecture notes.
FR6: The system shall allow teachers to create and manage an exam question bank.
FR7: The system shall provide facilities for teachers to generate lecture notes from uploaded materials.
FR8: The system shall offer a user-friendly interface for teachers to create exams.
FR9: The system shall allow teachers to compile exam papers from the question bank.

3.2 Student Module
FR1: The system shall provide a web interface for students to attempt exams digitally.
FR2: The system shall support various question displays (MCQs, essay-type, coding) in the exam.
FR3: The system shall provide quick grading/marks to students after they attempt exams.
FR4: The system shall display detailed feedback for each question type to students.

3.3 AI Evaluation Module
FR1: The system shall use Large Language Models (LLMs) to evaluate descriptive or essay-type questions after submission by students.
FR2: The system shall utilize coding libraries to evaluate coding-related question answers submitted by students.
FR3: The system shall support programming languages for coding evaluations.
FR4: The system shall use math libraries to deal with mathematical questions.

3.4 Security and Authentication Module
FR1: The system shall provide secure login functionality for user authentication.
FR2: The system shall implement role-based access control to show teachers and students their corresponding screens.
FR3: The system shall implement encryption for exam data during transmission when submitted by students.
FR4: The system shall ensure the security of stored exam questions and student responses in the database.
FR5: The system shall ensure and strengthen database security as it contains sensitive information.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 168 hours (7 days) during the initial prototype phase.
NFR2: In the case of failure, the system will automatically save all user progress made in the last 2 minutes.
NFR3: The system will keep track of errors and performance problems for manual review.
NFR4: If a failure occurs, the system will automatically recover and restore services within 30 minutes.

4.2 Usability
NFR5: The system shall allow teachers to create a standard exam with at least 20 questions within 60 minutes on their first use.
NFR6: Students shall be able to navigate and start an assigned exam within 5 clicks from the main dashboard.
NFR7: The system shall provide basic error messages if the user puts wrong inputs.
NFR8: The system will let users easily undo their last action in form fields.

4.3 Performance
NFR9: The system shall support up to 100 concurrent users simultaneously during the initial phase.
NFR10: 80 percent of all database queries shall complete in less than 1 second.
NFR11: The AI evaluation module shall complete grading of a standard 50-question exam (MCQs and short answers only) within 5 minutes.
NFR12: The system shall render the user interface for exam-taking within 5 seconds of a page request under normal load conditions.
NFR13: Auto-saving of responses during exams shall occur every 60 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR14: The system shall use TLS 1.2 or higher for data transmission, with AES-128 encryption for stored sensitive data.
NFR15: Teachers and administrators shall use single-factor authentication initially, with MFA planned for later.
NFR16: The system shall be designed to handle common security issues following OWASP Top 10 instructions.
NFR17: All user actions shall be held for audits.
NFR18: The system shall lock accounts after 10 failed login attempts within 30 minutes.
NFR19: The system shall enforce strong password policies (min 12 characters, mix of character types).

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: VirtuLectra - AI-Powered Interactive Learning Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the high-level needs and features of VirtuLectra, a web-based application geared towards teachers who want to lessen their burden and students who would benefit from interactive lectures.

1.2 Scope
The platform supports real-time interaction between the system and students, automated content creation (lecture notes, quizzes), and student monitoring (engagement tracking). It is built for Programming Fundamentals as a proof of concept.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- MCQ: Multiple Choice Question
- RBAC: Role-Based Access Control

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
VirtuLectra aims to provide a truly interactive learning experience by using AI to automate content creation and provide real-time feedback, reducing teacher workload and increasing student engagement.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Real-time voice interaction (Conversational Voice Exchanges, Q&A).
- Automated generation of lecture scripts and notes.
- Automated generation of MCQs and grading.
- Student engagement tracking via pop-up questions.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Teachers: Want to decrease their burden.
- Students: Want more interactive and engaging learning.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Real-Time Interaction
FR1.1: The system shall enable natural, interactive voice conversations between the system and students.
FR1.2: The system shall address student questions in real-time using an LLM.

3.2 Automated Content Creation
FR2.1: The system shall automatically generate comprehensive lecture scripts and notes from course content.
FR2.2: The system shall create customized quizzes (MCQs) aligned with the course material.
FR2.3: The system shall automate the grading of quizzes.

3.3 Student Monitoring
FR3.1: The system shall use LLM and scheduled pop-up questions to track student attention levels and participation.

3.4 Web Portal
FR4.1: The system shall provide a Teacher Dashboard with tools to manage lectures, review student progress, and generate content.
FR4.2: The system shall provide a Student Interface to access learning materials, quizzes, and real-time lectures.
FR4.3: The portal shall integrate all other modules.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall provide a notification to administrators in case of downtime.
NFR2: The system shall ensure data consistency during transfers.
NFR3: The system shall perform daily backups.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: The system shall provide a simple, intuitive UI with self-explanatory labels.
NFR5: The UI shall be fully responsive.
NFR6: The system shall provide visual/auditory confirmation for user actions.
NFR7: The system shall provide access to a help section.
NFR8: The system shall communicate errors with actionable suggestions.

4.3 Performance
NFR9: User inputs (voice/text) shall be processed with minimal latency.
NFR10: Any page within the dashboard shall load in less than 3 seconds.
NFR11: The system shall allow course materials of up to 100 MB to be uploaded.

4.4 Security
NFR12: The system shall require strong passwords.
NFR13: The system shall encrypt all sensitive data.
NFR14: The system shall enforce RBAC.
NFR15: The system shall implement automatic account lockout after multiple failed login attempts.
NFR16: The system shall implement protection against common security threats (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF).

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SmartGallery - AI-Powered Intelligent Image Management and Retrieval System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document provides a comprehensive understanding of Smart Gallery, an AI-powered image management and retrieval system that enhances user interaction with digital photo galleries using AI and Natural Language Processing.

1.2 Scope
To develop a mobile application (Android) with advanced features such as: person and category classification (age, emotion, objects, scenes), geo-location tagging, natural language-based search, reverse image search, prompt-based image editing (inpainting, style transfer), and caption generation.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- OCR: Optical Character Recognition
- LLM: Large Language Model
- SQL: Structured Query Language

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SmartGallery is an Android application that integrates state-of-the-art AI models (classification, diffusion, LLM) to provide an intelligent and intuitive image management experience.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Image Classification (person identification, age/emotion, object/scene recognition, geo-location tagging)
- Search and Retrieval (text-based search using natural language, reverse image search)
- Caption Generation (creative captions based on image content)
- Image Editing (prompt-based inpainting, style transfer, blurring)
- Mobile Application Integration (seamless UI/UX)

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Casual Users: Use smartphone for personal photos; benefit from categorized albums and natural language search.
- Professional Photographers: Manage large volumes; require advanced tools like categorization, reverse search, and prompt-based editing.
- Social Media Influencers: Need quick caption generation and editing tools for posts.
- Travelers: Benefit from geo-location tagging, landmark categorization, and location-specific captions.

2.4 Operating Environment
Android mobile application.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Image Classification
FR1.1: The system shall automatically detect and identify individuals in images, predicting attributes like age and facial expressions.
FR1.2: The system shall recognize objects (e.g., vehicles, animals) and classify them into relevant categories.
FR1.3: The system shall detect and classify environments (landscapes, urban, indoor).
FR1.4: The system shall generate descriptive, creative, and user-customizable captions based on image content.
FR1.5: The system shall enable users to view, edit, and delete images in their gallery.

3.2 Search and Retrieval
FR2.1: The system shall enable users to search for images using natural language prompts, supporting complex queries.
FR2.2: The system shall allow users to upload an image to find visually similar or related images in the gallery.
FR2.3: The system shall allow users to modify images by describing desired changes in text (e.g., object removal/replacement).
FR2.4: The system shall develop a simple, intuitive, and responsive UI for easy navigation across all features.
FR2.5: The system shall facilitate smooth, natural language interactions for searches, editing, and management.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance Requirements
NFR1: The system must load and process images quickly, ensuring smooth performance even for large galleries.
NFR2: AI-powered features like classification and caption generation shall deliver results in a timely manner.
NFR3: The app should handle a substantial number of images without noticeable degradation.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR4: The UI must be intuitive and easy to navigate, requiring minimal user training.
NFR5: Key features shall be accessible within 2 clicks/taps.
NFR6: The app should be designed for both beginner and advanced users.

4.3 Scalability Requirements
NFR7: The system must be scalable to support additional features without requiring a full redesign.
NFR8: The database and AI infrastructure must support adding new categories and classification criteria.

4.4 Compatibility Requirements
NFR9: The app must work on all modern Android devices and be adaptable for future versions.
NFR10: The system must be compatible with standard image formats (JPEG, PNG).

4.5 Reliability Requirements
NFR11: The system shall maintain a high level of availability for continuous access.
NFR12: All operations (uploads, classification, edits) must be accurately completed without data loss.
NFR13: A backup mechanism must ensure no user data is lost.

4.6 Privacy Requirements
NFR14: Users' images and interactions must be kept private, with clear terms of use.
NFR15: The app should allow users to control the level of sharing.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: ResQ - Multi-Agent Search and Rescue Robot System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document describes ResQ, a project focused on integrating affordance concepts into multi-agent robotic systems for search and rescue operations, particularly in unpredictable disaster environments like earthquakes.

1.2 Scope
The research aims to develop affordance-based concepts for multi-agent robots to operate in unpredictable environments. The robots will detect affordances of objects, update paths on global maps, and coordinate with each other to enhance life-saving operations.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- NAMO: Navigation Among Movable Obstacles
- RL: Reinforcement Learning
- CNN: Convolutional Neural Network
- A*: A* Pathfinding Algorithm

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
ResQ addresses the gap in search and rescue robotics where current systems rely on static maps and predefined paths, failing to adapt to dynamic, unpredictable environments.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Multi-agent coordination and communication.
- Affordance detection of objects (pushability, liftability).
- Dynamic path planning and updating.
- Simulation of rescue scenarios in Gazebo.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Rescue Teams: Intended end-users who will deploy the robots in real-world disaster scenarios.

2.4 Operating Environment
Simulated in Gazebo; intended for deployment in real-world disaster zones.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Multi-Agent Coordination
FR1.1: The system shall support the simultaneous operation of multiple robots in a simulated environment.
FR1.2: The robots shall be able to communicate and share environmental information.

3.2 Affordance Concepts
FR2.1: The robots shall be integrated with sensors to detect object affordances.

3.3 Path Planning
FR3.1: The system shall update the global path after detecting an object to facilitate rescue.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: The system must run efficiently and process real-time interactions.

4.2 Scalability
NFR2: The system must handle more complex environments.

4.3 Accuracy
NFR3: The simulation must provide accurate representations of real-world objects.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: ULingo - Voice Automated Conversational Assistant for Students (Urdu)
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for ULingo, a personalized Urdu voice assistant mobile app for students, leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) to provide meaningful and contextually rich interactions for accessing opportunities and managing finances.

1.2 Scope
The app provides voice-based interaction in Urdu. Key features include integration with LinkedIn for personalized job/internship opportunities, Namaz reminders based on location (Google Maps API), student finance budgeting, and a user-friendly mobile interface (Flutter). The system involves data collection, fine-tuning the LLaMA 3.1 model for Urdu, and testing.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- LLM: Large Language Model
- API: Application Programming Interface
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- Flutter: Google's UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
ULingo addresses the language barrier faced by Urdu-speaking students in accessing digital tools and opportunities by providing a voice assistant that understands and responds in their native language.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Collection: Gather Urdu datasets and user requirements.
- Model Fine-Tuning: Fine-tune LLaMA 3.1 for Urdu language processing.
- Personalized Opportunities: Integrate LinkedIn API for career listings, Google Maps API for location.
- Mobile App Development: Flutter-based app with voice interaction, dark/light modes, and text display.
- Testing: Unit, integration, and user acceptance testing.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Undergraduate Students: Tech-savvy, looking for academic/career opportunities.
- Graduate Students: Focused on advanced career opportunities and research.
- Educational Administrators: Oversee integration of university email systems.
- Career Counsellors: Guide students using the app's resources.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Retrieval
FR1: The system shall collect data from user inputs through the mobile app.
FR2: The system shall fetch tailored opportunities from LinkedIn based on user preferences.
FR3: The system shall retrieve the current location of the user through Google Maps API.

3.2 Model Finetuning
FR1: The system shall fine-tune the LLaMA 3.1 model to improve Urdu language understanding.
FR2: The system shall process user inputs and queries in Urdu, generating accurate responses based on the fine-tuned model.

3.3 Mobile App Development
FR1: The system shall authenticate the user during login.
FR2: The system shall converse with the user in Urdu (Nastaliq).
FR3: The system shall interact with the user via voice and display chat in text.
FR4: The system shall provide dark and light modes in the app.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall have a high degree of reliability, with logging and fallback mechanisms for failures.

4.2 Usability
NFR2: The app's usability will focus on ease of learning and interaction, especially for non-technical students.
NFR3: The UI/UX design will prioritize accessibility for Urdu-speaking users.

4.3 Performance
NFR4: 95% of responses shall be delivered within 3 seconds of user input, regardless of mobile platform.

4.4 Security
NFR5: Security measures will protect user data (LinkedIn credentials, email).
NFR6: The system must resist unauthorized access, with encryption used to secure sensitive data.
NFR7: The app will ensure proper API authentication and user session management.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: V-Escape - Procedurally Generated Horror Game
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document provides the skeleton of the V-Escape project, a horror game with procedural map generation and a compelling narrative, intended for all stakeholders (panel members, supervisors, designers, players).

1.2 Scope
To develop a horror game where players must solve puzzles to escape a procedurally generated environment. The game features: procedurally generated maps with diverse variations, puzzle generation (labyrinths, weight puzzles, memory puzzles), an AI agent that chases the player, difficulty scaling, and a narrative that unfolds as the player progresses.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- 3D: Three-Dimensional
- UI: User Interface
- ERD: Entity-Relationship Diagram
- EERD: Enhanced Entity-Relationship Diagram

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
V-Escape is a single-player horror game that uses procedural generation to create unique, replayable experiences, focusing on exploration, puzzle-solving, and evasion.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Diverse Map Variations (different settings, layouts, themes)
- Procedural Map Generation (random layout, logical coherence, item placement)
- Puzzle Generation (sequenced locked/unlocked rooms, item scattering)
- Narrative Creation (character story, player engagement)
- Player movement, object interaction, inventory system

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Casual Players: Prefer easy-to-understand mechanics and light entertainment.
- Hardcore Players: Seek deep, challenging experiences and complex puzzles.
- Puzzle Enthusiasts: Drawn to games with complex puzzle mechanics.
- Horror Enthusiasts: Seek tension, fear, and atmospheric settings.
- Speed Runners: Aim to complete games quickly, focusing on efficiency.
- Completionists: Aim to explore and achieve everything within the game.

2.4 Operating Environment
PC game developed in Unity.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Diverse Map Variations
FR1.1: The game shall generate a variety of maps and environments on each run.
FR1.2: The game shall not generate the same maps in consecutive runs.

3.2 Procedural Map Generation
FR2.1: The system will place objects and puzzles in a way that fits naturally into the environment.
FR2.2: The procedural generation algorithm must create maps with a logical structure that balances randomness with gameplay requirements, ensuring each map is solvable.

3.3 Puzzle Generation
FR3.1: The system will ensure the locking of doors is in a sequence.
FR3.2: The items to unlock them are placed in the 'at the time' explored rooms to ensure puzzles are solvable.

3.4 Narrative Creation
FR4.1: The narrative must be self-explanatory in terms of telling the players about the characters and the overall story.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability Requirements
NFR1: The system should gracefully handle errors and exceptions, providing informative messages without crashing.
NFR2: The game should automatically save player progress at regular intervals or upon level completion.

4.2 Usability Requirements
NFR3: The game's user interface should be easy to navigate.
NFR4: The game should support basic accessibility options and simple control schemes.

4.3 Portability Requirements
NFR5: The game should be optimized for the target platform but designed with considerations for future portability.

4.4 Difficulty Scaling Requirements
NFR6: The game should efficiently handle increasing complexity in procedural generation and level difficulty without significant performance degradation.

To develop a horror game where players must solve puzzles to escape a procedurally generated environment. The game features: procedurally generated maps with diverse variations, puzzle generation (labyrinths, weight puzzles, memory puzzles), an AI agent that chases the player, difficulty scaling, and a narrative that unfolds as the player progresses.

- UI: User Interface

V-Escape is a single-player horror game that uses procedural generation to create unique, replayable experiences, focusing on exploration, puzzle-solving, and evasion.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)

- Procedural Map Generation (random layout, logical coherence, item placement)
- Puzzle Generation (sequenced locked/unlocked rooms, item scattering)
- Narrative Creation (character story, player engagement)
- Player movement, object interaction, inventory system

- Hardcore Players: Seek deep, challenging experiences and complex puzzles.

PC game developed in Unity.

Project: FinXtract - Automated Financial Data Extraction and Analysis from PDF Reports
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document describes the requirements for FinXtract, a web-based software application that automates the extraction and analysis of financial data from PDF reports, reducing manual work and errors.

1.2 Scope
The system automates downloading financial PDFs, extracting data (using OCR for scanned PDFs), standardizing financial terms (e.g., "sales" vs "revenue"), and providing analysis via a chatbot (NLP for summarization, sentiment analysis). It includes front-end integration for data visualization.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- OCR: Optical Character Recognition
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- PDF: Portable Document Format

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
FinXtract eliminates the need for manual data extraction from financial reports, providing accurate, standardized, and ready-to-use data for analysts and investors, enabling data-driven decisions.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Scraping: Automated PDF downloading from company websites.
- Financial Data Extraction: Tabular and textual data extraction using OCR.
- Standardization: Reconstruct data into structured format and ensure consistency across companies/years.
- Chatbot: Interactive assistant for data querying, summarization, and sentiment analysis.
- Front-end Integration: Display data in tables and integrate the chatbot.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Financial Analysts: Need simplified data extraction to focus on analysis.
- Investors: Require accurate, timely data for capital allocation decisions.
- Accountants and Auditors: Need consistent data for verifying financial integrity.
- Corporate Executives (CFOs, CEOs): Require quick access to significant financial information.
- Regulators and Compliance Officers: Need precise data to look for irregularities.
- Financial Consultants: Need timely access to reliable data for client guidance.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Scraping
FR1: The system shall download PDF reports automatically from banking company websites.
FR2: The system shall support multi-level scraping from main pages and sub-pages.

3.2 Financial Data Extraction
FR1: The system, with the help of OCR, shall extract tabular and textual data from scanned/image-based PDF reports.
FR2: The system shall be able to extract data from multiple pages of a PDF document.

3.3 Standardization
FR1: The system shall convert raw unstructured data into a structured format.
FR2: The system shall be able to standardize financial data extracted from different companies or periods for consistency.

3.4 Chatbot
FR1: The system shall provide real-time answers to user queries related to financial data using a chatbot.
FR2: The system shall process financial reports by summarizing and carrying out sentiment analysis with an NLP module.

3.5 Front-end Integration
FR1: The system shall display As-Reported and As-Standardized extracted financial data in user-friendly tables.
FR2: The system shall integrate the chatbot into the user interface for easy access.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall be highly reliable, with an MTBF of at least 720 hours.
NFR2: The system shall recover from failures such as server crashes within 2 minutes.
NFR3: The system shall provide clear error messages and recover from uploading unsupported document formats without crashing.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: The system shall be user-friendly, enabling new users to perform basic tasks without external help.
NFR5: The system shall allow the user to view previously extracted data within 2 clicks.
NFR6: The system shall provide clear error messages for unsupported file types.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: The system shall respond to user inputs (data access, navigation) in no more than 5 seconds.
NFR8: The system shall be able to process large datasets efficiently without significant performance drop.
NFR9: Interactions with the backend (data queries, retrieval) shall be processed with minimal delay.

4.4 Security
NFR10: The system shall be designed to prevent unauthorized access.
NFR11: The system shall ensure data integrity during storage and transmission.
NFR12: The system shall protect sensitive financial data at all times.

4.5 Scalability
NFR13: The system shall be designed for deployment in a cloud environment (e.g., AWS).
NFR14: The system shall efficiently scale to handle an increased number of users.

Project: V-OR - Virtual Operating Room for Surgical Training
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for the Virtual Operating Room (V-OR), an advanced, immersive VR platform for medical students and professionals to practice complex surgeries (ASD closure, Pacemaker Implantation, CABG) in a realistic, risk-free environment.

1.2 Scope
The platform focuses on three cardiac surgeries. Features include a surgical simulation engine (interactive 3D models, step-by-step guidance, dynamic scenarios), virtual training mode (guided/independent practice), real-time feedback and performance evaluation, and a user management system (profile, progress tracking, instructor access).

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- VR: Virtual Reality
- ASD: Atrial Septal Defect
- CABG: Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting
- 3D: Three-Dimensional

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
V-OR provides an affordable, ethical, and repeatable alternative to traditional surgical training methods, overcoming limitations of physical simulators and cadaver availability.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Surgical Simulation Engine: Interactive 3D models, step-by-step guidance, dynamic scenarios.
- Virtual Training Mode: Practice with or without guidance, independent practice mode with evaluation.
- Real-time Feedback and Performance Evaluation: Real-time error detection, detailed performance reports.
- User Management System: Authentication, profile management, progress tracking, instructor monitoring.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Trainee: Medical students/first-year surgeons performing simulated cardiac surgeries (≈200-500 users).
- Doctor: Supervisor/instructor assessing trainees, adjusting scenarios (≈50-100 users).
- Simulation Admin: Technical staff managing the platform, setting up environments.
- 3D Model Designer: Develops and maintains high-fidelity 3D anatomical models.
- System Manager: Manages user verification, data organization, and access control.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 USER MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
FR1: The system shall allow users to login and manage their accounts.
FR2: User roles shall include students, surgeons, and administrators.
FR3: Users shall be able to update their profiles, view training progress, and feedback reports.
FR4: The system shall track and securely save the user's progress in different training modules.

3.2 SURGICAL SIMULATION ENGINE
FR1: The platform shall include interactive 3D models of organs, tissues, and surgical tools.
FR2: The system shall provide step-by-step guidance during training surgeries.
FR3: The dynamic scenarios generated by the system should mimic real-world complexities.

3.3 VIRTUAL TRAINING MODE
FR1: Users shall be able to take an assessment surgery with no help, receiving a performance evaluation.
FR2: Users shall be able to choose the surgery they want to perform from the 3 options (ASD, pacemaker, CABG).

3.4 REAL-TIME FEEDBACK & PERFORMANCE EVALUATION
FR1: The system shall be able to detect real-time errors during surgery and notify the user.
FR2: The system shall be able to provide detailed feedback and evaluate user performance using a predefined criterion.

3.5 USER INTERFACE
FR1: The system shall provide an intuitive dashboard for users to navigate to the selection of surgeries and track progress.

3.6 REPORTING AND ANALYTICS
FR1: The instructors shall be able to review the feedback and assessment reports of the students.
FR2: Instructors shall be able to provide feedback to those students' reports.

3.7 COMPATIBILITY
FR1: The system shall be compatible with Oculus VR headset and hand sensors.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Scalability
NFR1: The system will be scalable to accommodate additional complex surgeries in the future.

4.2 Compliance
NFR2: The system will comply with medical training standards.
NFR3: The system should comply with data protection regulations such as GDPR.

4.3 Data Integrity
NFR4: The system should ensure that all performance data is captured and stored without loss.

4.4 Maintainability
NFR5: The code should be modular and well documented.
NFR6: The system should include detailed documentation covering all major components.

4.5 Compatibility
NFR7: The VR environment should be compatible with multiple VR headsets, including Oculus Quest 2.
NFR8: The system should be compatible with major operating systems like Windows and Linux.

4.6 Security
NFR9: User authentication will be required for all access, with role-based access control.
NFR10: Sensitive data will only be accessible to authorised users.

4.7 Reliability
NFR11: The system will be highly reliable, with minimal downtime.

4.8 Availability
NFR12: It will be available 24/7.

4.9 Usability
NFR13: The interface should be user-friendly and intuitive for medical students and professionals.
NFR14: The VR experience should be immersive and realistic.

4.10 Performance
NFR15: The system should provide real-time feedback during surgeries without any noticeable delay.

Project: WattWiseAI - Intelligent Daily Electricity Demand Forecasting System
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for WattWiseAI, an intelligent system for accurate daily electricity demand forecasting using a hybrid deep-learning model (ANN, EDLSTM, ICEEMDAN) to optimize supply, reduce wastage, and enhance energy management sustainability.

1.2 Scope
The project involves collecting historical electricity consumption and weather data, preprocessing it, training a hybrid deep learning model, and deploying it. The system includes a user interface for real-time predictions, scenario analysis, and reporting. It targets electricity grid operators, energy managers, and utility analysts.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- ANN: Artificial Neural Network
- EDLSTM: Encoder-Decoder Long Short-Term Memory
- ICEEMDAN: Improved Complete Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition with Adaptive Noise
- UI: User Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
WattWiseAI addresses the limitations of traditional prediction models by using a hybrid deep learning approach that captures complex, nonlinear patterns in electricity demand data, leading to more accurate forecasts and improved grid stability.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Collection and Integration: Gather data from various sources, generate synthetic data.
- Data Processing and Analysis: Clean data, handle outliers and missing values.
- Model Training Module: Train hybrid model (ANN, EDLSTM, ICEEMDAN).
- Prediction Module: Create forecasts using the trained model.
- Model Deployment Module: Deploy model to real or simulated environment.
- User Interface and Visualization Module: Web interface for predictions, time-series plots, error distributions, scenario analysis.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Users (Electricity Grid Operators, Energy Managers, Utility Analysts): Monitor demand, view predictions, generate reports.
- Administrators: Oversee data operations, model training, and system performance; upload historical data; initiate model training.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Collection and Integration
FR1: The system should be able to gather data from different sources like government sites, public datasets, and utility companies.
FR2: The system should also be able to generate synthetic data to improve accuracy.

3.2 Data Pre-processing
FR1: The system should clean the data and handle outliers.
FR2: The system should fill in any missing values.

3.3 Hybrid Model Implementation
FR1: The system shall implement a hybrid deep-learning model combining ANN, EDLSTM, and ICEEMDAN for predicting electricity demand.
FR2: The system shall accurately decompose electricity demand data into high- and low-frequency components using ICEEMDAN.

3.4 Model Training and Validation
FR1: The system shall train the hybrid model using the processed data and validate its performance using cross-validation techniques.
FR2: The system shall optimize the model's hyperparameters to achieve the best prediction accuracy.

3.5 Prediction Module
FR1: The system shall provide daily electricity demand predictions based on the trained model.
FR2: The system shall visualize the prediction results in a user-friendly format via the web interface.

3.6 Web Application Interface
FR1: The system shall present prediction results and data visualizations through an interactive web interface built with React.js.
FR2: The system shall allow users to query past predictions and compare them with actual demand data.

3.7 Data Storage and Management
FR1: The system shall store historical data, model outputs, and user queries in a SQL database.
FR2: The system shall ensure secure and efficient retrieval of data for analysis and comparison.

3.8 Version Control and Collaboration
FR1: The system shall use GitHub for version control, enabling collaborative development and tracking changes in the codebase.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: The system should provide electricity demand predictions with a response time of less than 2 seconds after data input.
NFR2: The model training process should complete within a reasonable time frame, processing a typical daily dataset within 30 minutes.

4.2 Scalability
NFR3: The system should be able to handle increasing amounts of data without significant performance degradation.
NFR4: The system should be able to serve multiple users simultaneously.

4.3 Reliability
NFR5: The system should be up 98% of the time.
NFR6: The system should be able to recover from crashes efficiently and provide logs.

4.4 Usability
NFR7: The interface should be easy to use, with self-explanatory steps.
NFR8: The system should display appropriate messages to guide users in case of mistakes.

4.5 Security
NFR9: The system should ensure data security using protocols and passwords to verify that data is given only to authorized users.
NFR10: All communication should be secure and encrypted.
