Project: StoryAnimator - AI-Powered Character and GIF Creation Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for StoryAnimator, a chatbot web application that uses AI to simplify the process of character creation and animation, allowing users to generate images, convert them to editable formats, and create GIFs.

1.2 Scope
The platform provides core functionalities including AI image generation from text prompts, conversion of raster images to SVG (using contouring), background removal/noise reduction (using BRIA model), GIF generation from text prompts, and user chat history management. It aims to cater to both amateur and professional animators, designers, and content creators.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- SVG: Scalable Vector Graphics
- GIF: Graphics Interchange Format
- NLP: Natural Language Processing

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
StoryAnimator reduces the time, cost, and skill required to create and modify animated characters by automating many time-consuming operations (image generation, format conversion, background removal, GIF creation).

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Image Generation and Modification: Object tracing and separation, SVG conversion, background removal.
- AI Character Generator: Text-to-image feature with style options.
- Easy GIF Creation: Generate GIFs from text prompts.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Animation Studios: Generate and modify images without creating from scratch.
- Animators/Character Designers: Generate characters, download as SVG, use background removal, create GIFs.
- Content Creators: Make small videos/reels from generated GIFs.
- Game Development Studios: Generate sprite characters and use GIF generation for movement.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Authentication
FR1: A login page will be available where existing users will login.
FR2: A signup page will be available where new users will make an account.

3.2 Image Generation
FR1: The system shall use a suitable model (e.g., playgroundai/playground-v2.5) to generate cartoon characters from text prompts with good quality (>1080x720p).

3.3 Convert Image to SVG
FR1: The system shall convert raster images into SVG format using tracing and contouring techniques to make them editable.

3.4 Background Removal/Noise Reduction
FR1: The system shall effectively separate foreground from background and reduce noise using techniques like BRIA Background Removal.

3.5 GIF Generation
FR1: The system shall combine a text prompt with a generated or uploaded image to create animated GIFs.
FR2: The system shall detect verbs or actions in the text prompt and convert them into dynamic sequences.

3.6 Manage Chatbot History
FR1: The system shall manage and archive users' previous chatbot interactions using a database (e.g., MongoDB) for retrieval in future sessions.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 User Experience
NFR1: The platform shall be easy to use, allowing users to create animated GIFs and open bitmap images for use with other tools like Adobe Illustrator.

4.2 Industry Impact
NFR2: The project aims to significantly reduce the time and resources consumed in character creation.

4.3 Reliability
NFR3: The chatbot would guarantee availability for consumers by maintaining an uptime of at least 99%.
NFR4: Unexpected mistakes would be graciously handled by the system.

4.4 Security
NFR5: The technology would protect users' personal information, such as usernames and passwords.

4.5 Scalability
NFR6: The system will be able to handle added demand when more people or more complicated duties (like picture modification) are added.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: DriftGuard - Proactive Recurring Concept Drift Detection in Data Streams
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for DriftGuard, a research project focused on developing a framework for proactive detection and adaptation to recurring concept drift in data streams, particularly for domains like e-commerce and supply chain management.

1.2 Scope
The research aims to create a system that detects recurring drift patterns proactively, not just reactively. It includes a dashboard for visualizing real-time drift events, feature selection, historical drift data viewing, drift prediction alerts, customizable time windows, and report export. The core algorithm involves a temporal pattern graph, multi-modal drift handling, and adaptive forgetting mechanisms.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- ML: Machine Learning
- API: Application Programming Interface
- UI: User Interface
- CSV: Comma-Separated Values
- PDF: Portable Document Format

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
DriftGuard addresses the limitation of existing concept drift detection methods that focus on abrupt or gradual drifts but fail to handle recurring patterns (e.g., seasonal sales cycles), providing a proactive and scalable solution.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Real-Time Drift Visualization: Display drift events on dashboard with graphs.
- Feature Selection: User can select features to monitor drift patterns.
- Historical Drift Data: View historical drift data for comparison.
- Drift Prediction Alerts: Notify user when a drift is predicted.
- Drift Details & Concept History: Display details on current/previous concepts.
- Customizable Time Windows: Adjust monitoring time window.
- Export Drift Reports: Export reports in CSV or PDF format.
- Interactive Graphs and Tooltips: Hover for detailed drift information.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- (The primary "user" is the system administrator or data analyst who monitors the dashboard for drift events in their data streams, e.g., in supply chain or e-commerce.)

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Real-Time Drift Visualization
FR1: The dashboard should display real-time drift events based on the selected feature.
FR2: Provide graphical representations (line graphs, bar charts) to show concept drift occurrence over time.

3.2 Feature Selection
FR1: The user can select one or more features from a dropdown to monitor drift patterns.
FR2: Selected features will update visualizations accordingly.

3.3 Historical Drift Data
FR1: The dashboard should allow users to view historical drift data for comparison with the current stream.
FR2: Users can toggle between real-time and historical views.

3.4 Drift Prediction Alerts
FR1: The dashboard should notify the user when a drift is predicted to occur, based on the sequence predictor.
FR2: Alerts should include visual and textual notifications.

3.5 Drift Details & Concept History
FR1: The dashboard should display detailed information on current and previous concepts stored in the system.
FR2: Users should be able to see metadata for each drift occurrence (date, time, affected feature, drift type).

3.6 Customizable Time Windows
FR1: The user should have the ability to adjust the time window for which drift data is being monitored (e.g., last hour, last day).

3.7 Export Drift Reports
FR1: The dashboard should allow users to export reports on drift patterns, predictions, and adaptations in CSV or PDF format.

3.8 Interactive Graphs and Tooltips
FR1: Users should be able to hover over graphs to see detailed information about each drift point (confidence, timestamp).

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: The dashboard must process and display drift data with minimal latency (updates within 2 seconds).
NFR2: It should handle high-volume data streams efficiently without performance degradation.

4.2 Scalability
NFR3: The dashboard should scale with increasing numbers of features and drift events.
NFR4: It should accommodate multiple users monitoring different features simultaneously.

4.3 Usability
NFR5: The user interface should be intuitive and easy to navigate.
NFR6: The feature selection dropdown should provide search and filter functionality.

4.4 Reliability
NFR7: The dashboard must ensure continuous uptime with fallback mechanisms during data stream interruptions.
NFR8: It should maintain accurate drift data during temporary disruptions.

4.5 Security
NFR9: The dashboard should implement authentication to ensure only authorized users access the data.
NFR10: Drift reports and data streams must be encrypted and transmitted securely.

4.6 Compatibility
NFR11: The dashboard should be accessible on major web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) and responsive to various screen sizes.

4.7 Data Storage
NFR12: The system must optimize data storage, ensuring relevant drift patterns and historical data are retained while minimizing memory usage.

Project: IlmMate - WhatsApp-Based Educational Chatbot for Pakistani Students
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for IlmMate, a WhatsApp-based educational chatbot designed to empower Pakistani grade 9 and 10 students by providing curriculum-specific explanations, e-learning resources, and AI-generated quizzes to facilitate learning at their own pace and convenience.

1.2 Scope
The system uses OCR to digitize textbooks and stores content in vector databases. It processes user queries via WhatsApp API, shortens queries, retrieves relevant content, and uses an LLM to generate responses. Features include automated quiz generation, e-learning resource retrieval, progress tracking, and report generation. It aims to address educational inequality.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- OCR: Optical Character Recognition
- LLM: Large Language Model
- API: Application Programming Interface
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
IlmMate leverages the widespread use of WhatsApp in Pakistan to provide an accessible, platform-independent educational tool that helps students learn curriculum topics with detailed explanations and self-assessment, especially in under-served areas.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Backend Module: Digitize textbooks (OCR), store in vector databases, shorten user queries, retrieve relevant content, generate responses via LLM, track student performance.
- WhatsApp Chatbot: Automated assessment (generate questions), e-learning resource agent (retrieve video links), communicate via WhatsApp API.
- System Integration and Testing: Integrate components, set up guardrails, conduct testing.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Student: Main target user (grades 9-10) accessing curriculum content, quizzes, resources.
- Parent: Receives performance reports, monitors child's progress.
- Teacher: Tracks progress of students in their classroom.
- Educational Institution: Integrates IlmMate into existing curricula.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Backend Module: Content Management and Retrieval
FR1: The system should use OCR to convert textbooks into digital text and store it in a vector database.
FR2: The system must shorten user queries to focus on their main meaning.
FR3: The system should find and retrieve relevant content from the vector database.
FR4: The system should track student performance and generate progress reports.

3.2 WhatsApp Chatbot
FR1: The system should communicate with users through the WhatsApp API.
FR2: The system should automatically generate practice questions based on topics chosen by users.
FR3: The system must provide e-learning resources, such as video links, based on user requests.

3.3 System Integration and Testing
FR1: The system must integrate all parts of IlmMate.
FR2: The system must have safeguards to manage off-topic queries and reduce incorrect responses.
FR3: The system must perform testing to ensure everything works correctly.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system must achieve a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 48 hours.
NFR2: In case of failure, the system should implement automatic recovery mechanisms, sending alerts to developers.

4.2 Usability
NFR3: The chatbot interface will be intuitive and simple to navigate.
NFR4: The interface will provide clear feedback during response generation using filler sentences (e.g., "Let me get the answer to that for you.").

4.3 Performance
NFR5: The system must respond to user queries within 20 seconds, even during high traffic.
NFR6: The system will be optimized to support up to 50 concurrent users without performance degradation.
NFR7: The system aims for a 90% accuracy rate in LLM-generated responses, with continuous fine-tuning.

4.4 Security & Privacy
NFR8: Only authenticated users will have access to the system's features.
NFR9: The system could keep parents in sync with their child's performance, usage, and chat logs if possible.

Project: LUCY - Urdu-Speaking Virtual Banking Assistant
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for LUCY, a voice-based Urdu-speaking virtual banking assistant designed to automate common customer inquiries, reduce language barriers, and improve efficiency in the banking industry.

1.2 Scope
LUCY handles routine banking queries (balance checks, account opening info, card issues) via voice in Urdu. It uses NLP for speech recognition and query processing. Complex queries are escalated to human agents. The system aims to be a scalable SaaS solution.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- API: Application Programming Interface
- SaaS: Software as a Service

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
LUCY addresses the need for efficient, language-accessible customer service in banking by automating routine tasks and handling Urdu-speaking customers, reducing the workload on human agents and improving customer satisfaction.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Module 1: Customer Query Handling: Speech command capture and recognition in Urdu, real-time response for common queries, automated resolution of FAQs.
- Module 2: Escalation to Human Agent: Complex query detection, seamless handoff to human agent, integration with customer support system, notification system, agent assignment, feedback loop.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Banking Customers: Primary users (especially Urdu-speaking), use LUCY for routine banking queries.
- Banking Institutions: Implement LUCY to reduce operational costs and increase efficiency.
- Customer Support Agents: Handle complex queries escalated by LUCY.
- Development and Operations Team: Responsible for creation, deployment, and maintenance.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Module 1: Customer Query Handling
FR1: The system must capture and process voice commands in Urdu.
FR2: The system should utilize a speech recognition API to convert voice input to text.
FR3: The system should recognize predefined banking queries (checking account balance, retrieving transaction history, reporting lost/stolen cards, activating a card).
FR4: The system must also differentiate between simple and complex queries based on keywords.
FR5: The system should generate real-time responses for recognized queries.
FR6: The system should allow users to provide feedback on the accuracy of responses.
FR7: The system must handle unrecognized commands gracefully, prompting users to repeat or rephrase.
FR8: The system should maintain session context for follow-up questions.

3.2 Module 2: Escalation to Human Agent
FR1: The system must identify complex queries that require human intervention based on triggers or patterns.
FR2: The system must provide a seamless transition to a human agent, sharing user context.
FR3: The system must integrate with the bank's existing customer support infrastructure.
FR4: The system should notify users when their query is being escalated and provide an estimated wait time.
FR5: The system should assign escalated queries to available human agents based on expertise and availability.
FR6: After escalation, the system should prompt users to rate their experience with the human agent.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system should guarantee at least 99.5% uptime.
NFR2: The system must recover quickly from failures or crashes, with automatic recovery starting within 2 minutes.
NFR3: The system must maintain data integrity during transactions.

4.2 Usability
NFR4: The interface should be simple and intuitive, specifically designed for Urdu-speaking customers. Users should find information within three clicks or fewer.
NFR5: The system must meet accessibility standards for individuals with disabilities (text-to-speech, speech-to-text).
NFR6: Comprehensive help documentation should be available.

4.3 Performance
NFR7: The system should respond to 95% of user queries within 3 seconds.
NFR8: The system must be capable of handling up to 10,000 simultaneous users without performance drop.

4.4 Security
NFR9: Authentication will be implemented over the helpline along with security measures banks already use.
NFR10: Regular security audits should be conducted. The system should adhere to OWASP security best practices.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: WiVi - WiFi Vision: AI-Based Surveillance Using WiFi Signals
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for Wi-Fi Vision (Wi-Vi), a smart AI-based surveillance system that uses WiFi signal strength (Channel State Information) and deep learning to detect and classify human presence and movements, offering a cost-effective alternative to traditional CCTV.

1.2 Scope
The system uses ESP32 NodeMCU SoCs to record CSI values, which are transmitted to a cloud-deployed LSTM model via Flask API for real-time prediction. A responsive web app (ReactJS) and an interactive mobile app (Flutter) allow users to monitor their space, view real-time data/heatmaps, receive alerts, and integrate with emergency services.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CSI: Channel State Information
- IoT: Internet of Things
- LSTM: Long Short-Term Memory
- API: Application Programming Interface
- JWT: JSON Web Token

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Wi-Vi provides an innovative, cost-effective, and energy-efficient surveillance solution by leveraging existing WiFi infrastructure and deep learning, eliminating the need for expensive cameras and complex installations.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- CSI IoT Recorder: Record and display Wi-Fi CSI values using ESP32 and OLED screen.
- LSTM with Flask API: Deploy trained LSTM model on cloud, exposed via API for real-time prediction.
- Web Application (ReactJS): User authentication, real-time monitoring, dashboard, system configuration, historical data review, user management, emergency services integration.
- Mobile Application (Flutter): Real-time monitoring, push notifications, system configuration, event history, offline access, secure access.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Homeowners: Primary users monitoring their spaces (approx. 80% mobile use).
- Organizations: Monitor business premises (approx. 150 adopting organizations, primarily web use).
- Emergency/Law Enforcement Agencies: Interact during security incidents via integration.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Channel State Information IoT Recorder
FR1: The system shall record Wi-Fi Channel State Information (CSI) values after a specific time interval.
FR2: The system shall transmit Channel State Information (CSI) data to Flask API endpoint after a specific time interval.

3.2 Long Short-Term Memory with Flask API
FR1: The model shall communicate with ESP32 through Flask API.
FR2: The model shall process Channel State Information (CSI) data in real-time.
FR3: The model shall detect variations in Channel State Information (CSI) data.
FR4: The model shall classify the presence of human activity or objects based on variations in CSI data.
FR5: The model shall predict for each event with a confidence score.
FR6: The model shall trigger an event in case of detected anomalies.

3.3 Web Application in ReactJS
FR1: The system shall have an interactive and robust user interface.
FR2: The system shall allow users to securely login to access their security system.
FR3: The system shall allow users to upload the architectural plan of their premises.
FR4: The system shall display real-time human monitoring and object detection data.
FR5: The system shall provide heatmaps for visualization.
FR6: The system shall provide a dashboard to display system status and analytics.
FR7: The system shall allow users to configure their system settings based on their preferences.
FR8: The system shall allow users to access surveillance data for the previous two days period.
FR9: The system shall provide a role-based access control to users for permissions.
FR10: The system shall provide an option for integration with emergency services.

3.4 Mobile Application in Flutter
FR1: The system shall allow users to securely login to access their security system.
FR2: The system shall send push notifications in case of security incident.
FR3: The system shall display real-time human monitoring and object detection data.
FR4: The system shall provide heat maps for visualization.
FR5: The system shall provide a dashboard to display system status and analytics.
FR6: The system shall allow users to configure their system settings based on their preferences.
FR7: The system shall provide users with cached surveillance data to access it even when offline.
FR8: The system shall provide a role-based access control to users for permissions.
FR9: The system shall provide an option for integration with emergency services.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: The system shall maintain a reliable architecture with a 99.9% uptime.

4.2 Scalability
NFR2: The system shall be scalable to monitor larger spaces up to the transmission range of WiFi signals.

4.3 Usability
NFR3: The system shall provide usable and interactive web and mobile applications.
NFR4: The mobile app shall have support for offline usage.

4.4 Performance
NFR5: The system shall meet real-time requirements for monitoring, recording CSI values at regular intervals.
NFR6: The model shall have a simple input to process data and predict the pattern.

4.5 Maintainability
NFR7: The system shall be developed using industry line development techniques to provide a modular architecture.
NFR8: The system shall be documented properly.
NFR9: The applications shall be containerized.

4.6 Availability
NFR10: The system shall be available all the time with maximum uptime.

4.7 Security
NFR11: The system shall employ industry line security to provide secure and easy to use.
NFR12: User authentication shall utilize JsonWebTokens (JWT).
NFR13: The system shall leverage Role-Based Access Control.

4.8 Interoperability
NFR14: The system shall support integration with external systems like emergency services for alerts during security incidents.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: SmartStudyCompanion - AI-Powered Adaptive Learning Platform for Programming
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for SmartStudyCompanion, an AI-powered web application that enhances programming education through personalized learning experiences using knowledge graphs and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

1.2 Scope
The system creates personalized knowledge graphs for learners, visualizing programming concepts and their relationships. It uses a RAG-powered chatbot for context-aware answers, an adaptive assessment system that adjusts difficulty based on performance, and a recommendation engine for personalized learning paths. The system initially focuses on fundamental programming concepts.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
- LLM: Large Language Model
- KG: Knowledge Graph

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SmartStudyCompanion addresses the challenges of traditional programming education by providing an adaptive, personalized learning journey that visualizes complex concept relationships and offers one-on-one tutoring-like support through AI.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data collection and preprocessing: Collect and clean programming books, perform chunking.
- Model building (Knowledge graph): Extract entities and relationships, create hierarchical graph.
- Model building (RAG): Generate graph embeddings, implement similarity search.
- Information retrieval: Fetch relevant subgraphs, visualize graphs, provide concept details, chatbot using RAG.
- Quiz and Assessment: Adaptive testing, track user progress, suggest topics based on performance.
- User interface: Intuitive UI, interactive visualizations, chat interface, quiz interface.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Student: Primary user, typically a university student looking to understand programming concepts (20-30 test users).
- Educator: Project supervisors and faculty evaluating system effectiveness.
- Administrator: The three team members who develop and maintain the system.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Collection and Preprocessing Module
FR1: Collect programming books.
FR2: The system shall clean the collected data by removing irrelevant information and formatting inconsistencies.
FR3: The system shall perform text chunking on the cleaned data.
FR4: The system shall identify and remove duplicate data.

3.2 Model Building (Knowledge Graph) Module
FR1: The system shall select or train a model capable of extracting knowledge from preprocessed text data.
FR2: The system shall create a knowledge graph structure by identifying entities and relationships.
FR3: The system shall organize the knowledge graph in a hierarchical manner.
FR4: The system shall store the created knowledge graph data in a database.

3.3 Model Building (RAG) Module
FR1: The system shall generate graph embeddings for efficient retrieval.
FR2: The system shall implement a similarity search function on the graph embeddings.
FR3: The system shall store the generated embeddings in a database.

3.4 Information Retrieval Module
FR1: The system shall fetch relevant subgraphs based on user expertise or skills.
FR2: The system shall provide a visualization of the retrieved subgraphs.
FR3: The system shall display detailed information about concepts when a user selects a specific node.
FR4: The system shall implement a chatbot using RAG to answer user queries related to programming.

3.5 Quiz and Assessment Module
FR1: The system shall provide a quiz after the completion of each learning topic.
FR2: The system shall implement adaptive testing that adjusts question difficulty based on the user's previous answer.
FR3: The system shall present quiz questions one at a time.
FR4: The system shall track the number of consecutive incorrect answers during a quiz.
FR5: The system shall terminate the quiz if the user provides a specified number of consecutive incorrect answers.
FR6: The system shall recommend that the user revisit the topic if the quiz is terminated.
FR7: The system shall store quiz results and use them to update the user's knowledge graph.
FR8: The system shall use quiz performance to suggest relevant topics for further study.

3.6 User Interface Module
FR1: The system shall provide an intuitive and user-friendly interface for all functionalities.
FR2: The system shall implement a user login system and store login information in a secure database.
FR3: The system shall create interactive visualizations for displaying knowledge graphs.
FR4: The system shall design and implement an interface for chat interactions with the RAG-powered chatbot.
FR5: The system shall provide a dedicated interface for quizzes, including section to display one question at a time, mechanism for user input, "Next" button, immediate feedback, and quiz termination screen.
FR6: The system shall display a recommendation to revisit the topic if a quiz is terminated.

3.7 Testing Module
FR1: The system shall include a mechanism for identifying and fixing bugs.
FR2: The system shall perform unit testing to evaluate individual components.
FR3: The system shall conduct integration testing.
FR4: The system shall provide a mechanism for gathering and incorporating user feedback.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Reliability
NFR1: Our system should work without any major issues for at least 14 days straight.
NFR2: A major failure is defined as users unable to login for more than 5 minutes, knowledge graph not loading/updating properly, or chatbot not responding for more than 2 minutes.
NFR3: To protect from failures, we will automatically save quiz answers every minute and keep a daily backup of the knowledge graph.
NFR4: For error detection, we'll setup monitoring that alerts us if any page takes more than 60 seconds to load, chatbot fails to respond to more than 5 consecutive queries, or more than 10 users report the same issue within an hour.

4.2 Usability
NFR5: The system shall display an explanation of a concept on the side panel when a user clicks on a node in the knowledge graph.
NFR6: The knowledge graph visualization shall provide zoom capabilities.
NFR7: The system shall use color-coding for nodes to indicate familiarity (Red = doesn't know, Green = knows).
NFR8: The system shall provide clear error messages for incorrect quiz answers.
NFR9: The web interface shall be responsive across different desktop browser window sizes.
NFR10: The quiz interface shall allow users to easily navigate between questions and submit answers with minimal clicks.
NFR11: The chatbot interface shall be easily accessible from any page.

4.3 Performance
NFR12: The system shall retrieve and visualize relevant subgraphs within 10 seconds of a user request.
NFR13: The chatbot shall provide responses to user queries within 10 seconds for 90% of queries.
NFR14: Quiz questions shall be generated and displayed within 10 seconds of the previous question being answered.
NFR15: The system shall update the user's knowledge graph within 30 seconds of completing a quiz.
NFR16: All database operations (storing and retrieving user data) shall complete within 20 seconds.

4.4 Security
NFR17: The system shall ensure that each user can only access their own learning progress, quiz results, and personal information.
NFR18: The system shall implement access controls to prevent unauthorized users from viewing or modifying the knowledge graph and learning content.

Project: WebCodeGen - LLM-Based Automatic Code Generation for Web Apps and APIs
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the research problem and proposed approach for WebCodeGen, a project focused on automating template and code generation for web applications and APIs using class diagrams.

1.2 Scope
The research aims to develop an automated tool that generates templates and code for web applications and APIs. It uses synthetically generated XML and template datasets as a case study.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- LLM: Large Language Model
- CRUD: Create, Read, Update, Delete
- ORM: Object-Relational Mapping
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
The research addresses the inefficiency and inconsistency of manual code and template generation, which slows down development and can compromise quality.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Class Diagram drawing interface.
- Class Diagram parsing to XML.
- Template Generation Module.
- Code Generation Module.
- Testing and Validation.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Developers: End-users who will use the tool to automate code generation.

2.4 Operating Environment
Web-based tool.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Class Diagram Drawing Interface
FR1.1: The system shall provide a facility to draw UML diagrams, mainly class diagrams, within the application.
FR1.2: The drawing feature shall provide elements such as relationships, classes, functions, and attributes.

3.2 Class Diagram Parsing
FR2.1: The provided diagram shall be parsed into XML format.

3.3 Template Generation Module
FR3.1: Based on the XML input, the system shall generate foundation code for the web application and API.
FR3.2: The system shall generate model functions, classes, controller actions, views, and API endpoints.
FR3.3: The system shall allow users to provide their own templates.

3.4 Code Generation Module
FR4.1: The system shall generate actual runnable code and main business logic based on user requirements.

3.5 Testing and Validation
FR5.1: The system shall test and validate the generated code for both templates and business logic.
FR5.2: The system shall automatically check generated templates for consistency, correctness, and adherence to coding standards.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Accuracy
NFR1: The system shall parse class diagrams and generate code with high accuracy.
NFR2: The system's performance will be evaluated using count of missing classes/functions, F1 score, and accuracy.

4.2 Efficiency
NFR3: The system shall significantly reduce the time and effort required to generate consistent and high-quality templates.

4.3 Usability
NFR4: The system shall provide an intuitive interface for drawing class diagrams and generating code.

1. INTRODUCTION

This document outlines the requirements for Structify, a Gamified Intelligent Tutoring System (ITS) designed to help learners grasp complex data structure concepts (linked lists, stacks, queues) through an interactive, engaging, and personalized platform.

The system uses an initial assessment to create a personalized learning plan. Learning is delivered via gamified lessons with dynamic code visualization. Features include real-time feedback, reward mechanisms (points, badges), user profile management, performance analytics, and customizable settings.

- UML: Unified Modeling Language
- ERD: Entity Relationship Diagram

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
Structify addresses the challenges students face in learning abstract data structure concepts by blending educational content with game-based elements, adaptive learning, and immediate feedback.

FR2: The system shall continuously assess users' progress throughout the learning journey.

3.2 Personalized Learning Path
FR1: The system shall generate a personalized learning plan based on the user's initial self-identification.
FR2: The system shall adapt the content and sequence of learning materials in real-time based on user performance.
FR3: The system shall ensure users build foundational knowledge before progressing to more complex topics.

3.3 Gamified Learning
FR1: The system shall offer interactive games and hands-on exercises for each data structure topic (linked lists, stacks, queues).
FR2: The system shall use gamification to bridge the gap between theory and practical application.
FR3: The system shall provide a variety of games to cater to different learning styles.

3.4 Reward Mechanism and Engagement
FR1: The system shall incorporate a reward mechanism that tracks points, badges, and achievements earned by users.

3.5 Real-Time Feedback and Support
FR1: The system shall provide real-time feedback to users during games and assessments, highlighting errors and explaining correct solutions.

3.6 Dynamic Code Visualization
FR1: The system shall dynamically generate and display corresponding lines of code as users interact with exercises and games.

3.7 User Profile Management
FR1: The system shall allow users to create an account, log in, and manage their profiles securely.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: NewsNet - GraphRAG leveraging KGraphs for Deep News Analysis using LLMs
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for NewsNet, a system that uses GraphRAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and causal knowledge graphs to analyze news, detect causal relationships between events, and provide users with a deeper understanding of news stories.

1.2 Scope
The system scrapes unstructured news text, extracts named entities and events (NEER), identifies causal relationships, constructs a causal knowledge graph, and uses GraphRAG for enhanced information retrieval. Features include category-based filtering, timeline generation, personalized recommendations, trend analysis, anomaly detection, and a chatbot interface.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
- GraphRAG: Graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation
- LLM: Large Language Model
- NEER: Named Entity and Event Recognition
- KG: Knowledge Graph

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
NewsNet addresses the difficulty of understanding cause-effect relationships in news events by building a causal knowledge graph and using it to enhance LLM retrieval, providing users with a coherent narrative and deeper insights.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Collection and NEER: Web scraping, data cleaning, Named Entity and Event Recognition.
- Causal Knowledge Graph: Identify causal relationships, construct and update KG.
- GraphRAG: Fine-tune LLM, use causal KG to enhance retrieval.
- GraphRAG vs Baseline RAG: Implement baseline RAG, compare performance.
- Chatbot Creation: Chatbot interface, personalized KG visualization, timeline layout, article references, trend analysis.
- Quality Assurance: Performance metrics, unit testing, system testing.
- User Experience Optimization: Category-based search, user-tailored recommendations.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- News Viewer: Regular consumer wanting current updates on topics of interest.
- History Learner: Studies past events and backgrounds, uses timelines and resources.
- Researcher: Academia/industry needing trustworthy, detailed information for work/publications.
- Media Enthusiast: Loves media trends, production, communication analysis.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Data Collection and Named Entity and Event Recognition (NEER)
FR1: The system shall collect data from news websites through web scraping tools.
FR2: The system shall pre-process and clean the data.
FR3: The system shall implement Named Entity and Event Recognition model on the news data.

3.2 Causal Knowledge Graph
FR1: The system shall identify causal relationships among entities in the data.
FR2: The system shall construct a causal knowledge graph (KG) using the causal relationships.
FR3: The system shall update and add new relationships to the knowledge graph as more data is available.

3.3 GraphRAG
FR1: The system shall use the data from the news to fine-tune a Large Language Model (LLM).
FR2: The system shall use the causal knowledge graph to improve and enhance information retrieval using Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation (GraphRAG).

3.4 GraphRAG vs Baseline RAG
FR1: The system shall implement a baseline Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
FR2: The system shall utilize the baseline RAG to perform a performance comparison between RAG and GraphRAG.
FR3: The system shall test the system performance of GraphRAG using predefined metrics.

3.5 Chatbot Creation
FR1: The system shall create a chatbot interface that provides options to users to select news.
FR2: The system shall create and provide a personalized knowledge graph to the user according to their query.
FR3: The system shall display the news results according to the relative timeline.
FR4: The system shall provide references for the articles in which the news was provided.

Project: AutoWise - Car Maintenance and Fuel Efficiency Tool
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for AutoWise, a mobile application designed to help car owners manage vehicle maintenance, monitor fuel efficiency, and improve overall driving experience by integrating real-time OBD-II data, machine learning, and data analytics.

1.2 Scope
The app connects to OBD-II adapters to capture real-time vehicle performance metrics (engine health, fuel consumption). Features include personalized maintenance reminders, diagnostic trouble code (DTC) explanation, fuel efficiency optimization (driving behavior analysis, tips), route planning and optimization (real-time traffic, driver communication), driver log, and machine learning for predictive maintenance and insights.

- DTC: Diagnostic Trouble Code

2.1 Product Perspective
AutoWise empowers car owners with actionable insights from their vehicle's data, enabling proactive maintenance, improved fuel efficiency, and better driving decisions, reducing operational costs and preventing breakdowns.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User Interface Module: Dashboard, notifications, settings, driver log.
- Vehicle Diagnostics Module: Real-time data monitoring, DTC detection and explanation, performance metrics.
- Predictive Maintenance Module: Maintenance reminders, historical data analysis, custom recommendations.
- Fuel Efficiency Optimization Module: Consumption tracking, driving behavior analysis, fuel efficiency tips, historical trends.
- Route Planning and Optimization Module: Real-time traffic updates, route optimization, driver communication, alternative routes.
- Machine Learning and Data Analytics Module: Model training, behavior analysis, performance insights.

Project: TracknRetrieve - Lost and Found Item Recovery Platform
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for TracknRetrieve, a mobile application that simplifies the process of reporting and retrieving lost and found items by using description-based image retrieval, secure real-time messaging, geo-location tagging, voice-to-text, and proof of ownership verification.

1.2 Scope
The platform allows users to report lost items via description (system retrieves matching images from Google) or found items via upload (system generates description). Features include geo-location tagging, in-app messaging, notifications for matches, proof of ownership verification, reporting to authorities, privacy protection for finders, donation center for unclaimed items, rewards, community alerts, multilingual support, and reviews/ratings.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- API: Application Programming Interface
- GPS: Global Positioning System
- UI: User Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
TracknRetrieve provides a secure and user-friendly alternative to traditional lost and found systems by allowing description-based reporting, privacy-preserving matching, and facilitating communication between finders and owners.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User Management: Profile management, view history, notifications.
- Lost Item Reporting: Provide detailed description, image retrieval based on description, image modification.
- Found Item Reporting and Description Matching: Upload image, system generates description, match with lost reports.
- Voice-to-Text Conversion: Verbally describe lost items.
- Geo-Location Tagging: Tag last known location of lost items.
- In-App Messaging: Secure real-time communication between users and finders.
- Notification System: Notify users of matches.
- Proof of Ownership Verification: Verify item ownership, store ownership documents.
- Reporting to Authorities: Report stolen/sensitive items directly to authorities.
- Privacy Protection for Finders: Anonymous reporting, secure messaging.
- Donation Center: Donate unclaimed items to charity, track donations.
- Rewards: Offer rewards for return of lost items, track reward offers.
- Community Alerts: Send alerts to nearby users.
- Multilingual Support: Support multiple languages.
- Provide Reviews and Ratings: Leave feedback on experience.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Item Owner/User: Reports lost items, receives notifications, communicates with finders, provides reviews.
- Finder/User: Reports found items, uploads images, may remain anonymous, may receive rewards.
- Admin: Manages system, user accounts, lost/found reports, Donation Center, handles disputes.
- Authorities: Receive reports of stolen or sensitive items.
- Donation Center: Processes unclaimed items for donation to charities.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 User Registration and Authentication
FR1: The system shall allow users to register with unique credentials.
FR2: The system shall authenticate users using email and password combinations.
FR3: The system shall enforce password security measures.

3.2 Report Lost Item
FR1: Users shall be able to report lost items by providing item details (description, category, last seen location).
FR2: The system shall store lost item details securely in the database.
FR3: The system shall allow users to update or remove their lost item reports.

3.3 Tag Geo-Location
FR1: Users shall be able to tag the geo-location where the item was lost using a map or GPS data.
FR2: The system shall save the tagged location to the lost item report.

3.4 Report Found Item
FR1: Users shall submit details and an optional image of a found item.
FR2: The system shall store found item information securely in the database.
FR3: The system shall notify the finder if there is a potential match.

3.5 Receive Found Item Notifications
FR1: The system shall notify users via email or in-app notifications when a potential match for their lost item is found.
FR2: Users shall be able to configure notification preferences.

3.6 Manage Profile
FR1: Users shall be able to edit their profile, including contact information and preferences.
FR2: The system shall securely store user profile information.

3.7 Provide Reviews and Ratings
FR1: Users shall be able to submit reviews and ratings based on their experience.
FR2: The system shall display aggregated ratings and reviews.

3.8 Submit Found Item to Store
FR1: The system shall allow finders to submit found items to a designated store for safekeeping.
FR2: The system shall update the store's inventory when new items are submitted.

3.9 Manage User Accounts
FR1: The Admin shall be able to create, update, or deactivate user accounts.
FR2: The system shall securely store all user data.

3.10 Manage Lost and Found Items
FR1: The Admin shall be able to update the status of lost and found items (claimed, unclaimed, donated).
FR2: The system shall ensure accurate tracking of item details.

3.11 Handle Donation of Unclaimed Items
FR1: The Admin shall be able to designate unclaimed items for donation.
FR2: The system shall track the status of items through the donation process.

3.12 Send Notifications
FR1: The system shall automatically send notifications to users when item matches are found.
FR2: The system shall allow users to view and manage their notifications.

3.13 Process Item Matches
FR1: The system shall match lost and found item reports based on description, category, and geo-location.
FR2: The system shall notify the users involved in the match.

3.14 Hold Unclaimed Items
FR1: The store shall hold unclaimed items in inventory until claimed or designated for donation.
FR2: The system shall update the status of held items as needed.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS

NFR2: Notifications for found items should be delivered promptly.

4.2 Security and Privacy
NFR3: User data must be securely encrypted and stored.
NFR4: The system should provide privacy settings to manage profile and item report visibility.

4.3 Reliability
NFR5: The application should be accessible at all times, with minimal downtime.
NFR6: Reporting processes must be reliable, ensuring data integrity and accuracy.

4.4 Scalability
NFR7: The application should accommodate a growing number of users, reports, and notifications.
NFR8: Future enhancements and integrations must be feasible.

4.5 Compatibility
NFR9: The application must work seamlessly across major platforms (iOS, Android, web browsers).
NFR10: Compatibility with various devices should be ensured.

4.6 Usability
NFR11: The user interface must be intuitive and easy to navigate.
NFR12: Clear instructions should guide users through tasks.

4.7 Maintainability
NFR13: The codebase should be well-structured and documented.
NFR14: Regular updates should address bugs and security vulnerabilities.

The app uses AI models (SVM, CNNs, ResNet) to analyze images of the eye captured by smartphone cameras. 3.3 Data Management
FR1: The application should be capable of storing images for further boosting the dataset.

3.4 Map Module

3.5 AR in Camera Module

3.8 ToolTip Module
FR1: The application shall provide guidance via tooltips and tutorials for new users.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: FraudLock - Developing Robust Deepfake Detection Methods for Face Recognition
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for FraudLock, a research project focused on developing a robust deepfake detection system using a multi-modal approach that synchronizes eye, lip, audio, and cheek muscle movements to identify manipulations in facial videos.

1.2 Scope
The research aims to create a deepfake detection system that analyzes inconsistencies between lip movements and audio, as well as the correlation between lip and cheek muscle dynamics. The proposed method uses CNNs for feature extraction, attention mechanisms for synchronization, and an MLP for classification. The goal is a cross-platform optimized app for mobile devices.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CNN: Convolutional Neural Network
- MLP: Multi-Layer Perceptron
- DCNN: Deep Convolutional Neural Network
- MCMC: Markov Chain Monte Carlo
- DAG: Directed Acyclic Graph

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
FraudLock addresses the limitations of existing deepfake detection methods (which often focus on isolated features) by using a multi-modal approach that correlates multiple facial and audio cues, aiming to reduce false positives and improve reliability.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Input Video Stream: Accept video with audio.
- Pre-Processing: Face detection (DLIB), feature localization (mouth, cheeks), audio extraction (MFCC, Mel Spectrogram), frame alignment.
- CNN for Lip Movement Feature Extraction: Extract lip movement features.
- Attention Mechanisms for Lip-Audio Synchronization: Self-attention and cross-attention to identify audio-lip mismatches.
- CNN and Cross-Attention for Lip-Cheek Muscle Correspondence: Correlate lip and cheek movements.
- Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) for Classification and Feature Fusion: Combine outputs from modules for final real/fake classification.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- (The primary "user" is the system itself, designed to be integrated into applications. The research paper outlines the methodology and does not define specific user classes for a final application.)

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Proposed Methodology (Functional steps)
FR1: The system shall accept an input video stream with audio.
FR2: The system shall perform pre-processing including face detection (DLIB), feature localization (mouth, cheeks), audio extraction (MFCC, Mel Spectrogram), and frame alignment.
FR3: The system shall use a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to extract lip movement features from the pre-processed video data.
FR4: The system shall employ attention mechanisms (self-attention, audio self-attention, cross-attention) to analyze lip-audio synchronization and detect inconsistencies.
FR5: The system shall use a separate CNN and cross-attention layers to correlate lip movement features with cheek muscle movement features.
FR6: The system shall use Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) for classification from both the lip-audio and lip-cheek modules.
FR7: The system shall fuse the outputs from both MLPs to generate the final classification decision (real or fake).

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance (implied from methodology)
NFR1: The model should be efficient enough for real-time detection on mobile devices.
NFR2: The system should have high accuracy in detecting deepfake manipulations, with low false positive and false negative rates.
NFR3: The system should be robust to varying video conditions (lighting, camera angles, compression).

4.2 Security (implied)
NFR4: The system should be resilient against adversarial attacks designed to bypass detection.

The system analyzes user images using CNNs for skin condition detection, provides dietary and product recommendations based on diagnosis, tracks user skin health over time (predictive analytics), and offers a user-friendly conversational interface. SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: IMUPDN - Reward-Incentivized Framework for dApps on IOTA Tangle
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for IMUPDN, a research project focused on designing and implementing an efficient, fee-free, and reward-incentivized framework for decentralized applications (dApps) on the IOTA Tangle network.

1.2 Scope
The research aims to modify the IOTA Tangle consensus protocol to integrate a reward mechanism for users validating transactions and to facilitate node participation for dApp users without requiring significant computational resources.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- dApps: Decentralized Applications
- DLT: Distributed Ledger Technology
- DAG: Directed Acyclic Graph
- MCMC: Markov Chain Monte Carlo
- PoS: Proof-of-Stake

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
IMUPDN addresses the need for scalable, feeless, and user-engaging dApps by leveraging IOTA's Tangle and introducing reward mechanisms to incentivize node participation and network security.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Incorporate Reward Mechanisms: Develop incentives for users validating transactions.
- Facilitate Node Participation: Make it feasible for dApp users to participate as nodes in the Tangle network.
- Consensus Protocol Modification: Adapt the IOTA Tangle to include lightweight nodes and a dynamic reward system.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- (The primary "users" are dApp developers and end-users who would benefit from fee-free transactions and rewards. The document focuses on the research methodology.)

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Consensus Protocol Modification
FR1: Adapt the existing IOTA Tangle to include lightweight nodes with low computational overhead.
FR2: Introduce a dynamic reward system that compensates participants for validating transactions.
FR3: Ensure the reward mechanism operates seamlessly with the IOTA Tangle's fee-free nature.

3.2 Node Validation Mechanism
FR1: Users participate as nodes by validating transactions. Each transaction must approve two previous transactions in the Tangle.

3.3 Reward System
FR1: Nodes that validate transactions receive rewards based on their contributions.
FR2: Rewards are generated dynamically based on network activity and the amount of computational power contributed.

3.4 Lightweight Node Implementation
FR1: Enable resource-constrained users to participate as nodes by minimizing computational requirements.
FR2: Provide a simplified interface for these users to validate transactions.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: The system should maintain IOTA's scalability and low-latency characteristics.
NFR2: The reward mechanism should not introduce significant overhead.

4.2 Security
NFR3: The modified consensus protocol should remain resilient against attacks like Parasite Chain attacks.
NFR4: The reward system should incentivize honest behavior and discourage malicious actions.

4.3 Scalability
NFR5: The framework should be scalable to handle a large number of nodes and transactions.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: NeuroGen - 3D Brain MRI Inpainting for Glioma-Affected Regions
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for NeuroGen, a research project focused on using advanced inpainting techniques (Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models, GANs, VAEs) to generate healthy 3D brain tissue in regions affected by glioma tumors, thereby improving the reliability of MRI analysis for clinical tasks.

1.2 Scope
The research involves training models on the BraTS dataset (T1 MRI modality) to synthesize healthy tissue in tumor-affected areas. The goal is to enhance data integrity for downstream tasks like brain tissue segmentation, anatomical parcellation, and tumour growth modelling. The project explores various models (DDPMs, GANs, VAEs, LDMs) and evaluation metrics (SSIM, PSNR, MSE).

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- MRI: Magnetic Resonance Imaging
- DDPM: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model
- GAN: Generative Adversarial Network
- VAE: Variational Auto-encoder
- LDM: Latent Diffusion Model
- SSIM: Structural Similarity Index Measure
- PSNR: Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
NeuroGen addresses the challenge of analyzing brain MRIs with gliomas, where standard algorithms (trained on healthy brains) underperform. By inpainting tumor regions with realistic healthy tissue, it enhances the performance of clinical analysis tools.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Data Preprocessing: Rescaling to consistent voxel size (1x1x1 mm³), normalization (Z-score, percentile), quality control.
- Model Training: Train and compare inpainting models (DDPMs, GANs, VAEs, LDMs) using T1 MRI scans from BraTS 2024.
- Inpainting: Generate healthy brain tissue within the masked tumor-affected region.
- Evaluation: Assess model performance using SSIM, PSNR, MSE.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- (The primary "users" are researchers and clinicians who would use the inpainted images for better diagnosis and analysis. The document focuses on the research methodology.)

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Verifying the BraTS Challenge Dataset
FR1: Download the BraTS dataset and confirm the presence of ground truth segmentation masks for tumor regions.
FR2: Perform visual inspection on a sample MRI volume to check alignment and identify corrupted files.
FR3: Calculate statistical measures (mean, variance, intensity histograms) to find anomalies.

3.2 Workflow for Rescaling, Normalization, and Ensuring Consistent Voxel Size
FR1: Rescale all MRI volumes to a consistent voxel size of 1x1x1 mm³ using 3D interpolation.
FR2: Apply Z-score normalization (subtract mean, divide by standard deviation) for each modality to standardize intensity ranges.
FR3: Verify that all MRI volumes have consistent voxel sizes by checking metadata; resample mismatched images.
FR4: Perform quality control checks (visual, statistical) to ensure preprocessing did not distort data.

3.3 Model Training (implied)
FR1: The system shall train inpainting models (e.g., DDPMs) to generate healthy tissue in tumor-affected regions.
FR2: The system shall use evaluation metrics (SSIM, PSNR, MSE) to assess the quality of inpainted images.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Performance
NFR1: Models should be computationally efficient enough to handle 3D volumetric data, potentially using techniques like pseudo-3D or memory-efficient architectures.
NFR2: Inpainting should produce high-quality reconstructions with high SSIM and PSNR scores.

4.2 Scalability
NFR3: The methodology should be scalable to handle large datasets like BraTS.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: DashGrab - Automated Shopping System for Retail Clothing Stores
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for DashGrab, an innovative project that automates the shopping experience in retail clothing stores by integrating Computer Vision, RFID technology, and automated billing.

1.2 Scope
The system integrates with existing CCTV infrastructure to track customers' shopping behavior (picking up/returning items). It uses RFID tags for seamless item tracking and associates products with customers automatically. Features include automated billing upon exit and security monitoring to detect shoplifting or fraud.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- CCTV: Closed-Circuit Television
- RFID: Radio-Frequency Identification
- AI: Artificial Intelligence

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
DashGrab aims to streamline retail operations by eliminating manual checkouts and reducing security risks through real-time activity tracking and automated billing using existing CCTV and RFID technology.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User Identification: Identify customers upon entry using CCTV and registered profiles.
- Item Detection: Automatically detect RFID-tagged clothing items as the customer moves in the store.
- Automated Checkout: Process payment automatically when the customer exits.
- Security Monitoring: Continuous monitoring for suspicious activity (shoplifting, insufficient balance).

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Customer: Main user identified upon entry; actions tracked; account billed based on shopping.
- Staff: Manage the system, ensure accurate detection, resolve issues.
- Admin: Oversee system operation, monitor performance, security, manage user permissions.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Functional Requirements
FR1: User Identification: The system identifies customers upon entry using camera surveillance and registered profiles.
FR2: Item Detection: RFID tags attached to clothing items are detected automatically as the customer moves within the store.
FR3: Automated Checkout: Once the customer exits the store, the system automatically processes payment through the registered payment method.
FR4: Security Monitoring: Continuous monitoring for suspicious activity, such as shoplifting or insufficient balance during checkout.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Real-Time Processing
NFR1: The system must process transactions and monitor security in real-time.

4.2 Security
NFR2: The system should detect and prevent fraud, including monitoring for attempts to steal items or checkout with insufficient funds.

4.3 Availability
NFR3: The system should have high availability and minimal downtime to support continuous operations.

Project: SoulSync - AI-Powered Digital Persona Creation from Digital Legacy
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for SoulSync, an AI platform that creates an artificial persona ("digital twin") based on a person's digital footprint (social media posts, emails, photos, videos), enabling loved ones to interact with and preserve the legacy of a deceased individual.

1.2 Scope
The system collects user-provided digital data (text, images, video, audio), processes it using NLP and CV, and creates an interactive persona. Features include data aggregation and categorization, analysis of communication style and emotional resonance, persona simulation (text, voice, video), and an engagement module for interactive conversation and memory sharing.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- CV: Computer Vision
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
SoulSync addresses the growing need to preserve digital legacy meaningfully, using AI to replicate an individual's communication style, wisdom, and emotional resonance, providing comfort and connection for loved ones.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Legacy Data Aggregation: Collect and categorize user data (text, media) using AI.
- Data Analysis and Modeling: Process text/audio (create memory bank, speech style) and visual data (face, speech style).
- Persona Creation: Generate dynamic persona, provide communication interface (voice, text, video).
- Engagement Module: Allow simulated conversation, enable sharing of personal memories.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- User: Primary user who needs therapy, knowledge, or wants to connect with deceased loved ones.
- Admin: Controls which models can be deployed publicly and monitors users.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Legacy Data Aggregation
FR1: The system must allow users to add (text, media) type of data.
FR2: The system must categorize data using artificial intelligence.

3.2 Data Analysis and Modeling
FR1: The system must process text in a meaningful manner to make the memory bank by storing meaningful data.
FR2: The system must process images and videos to make the persona feel real by duplicating the real person's style.
FR3: The system must process audio to store data in the memory bank and also to make the persona's voice.

3.3 Persona Creation
FR1: The system must generate a persona based on the data processed and analyzed.
FR2: The system must allow users to interact with the artificial persona using a user-friendly conversational interface.

3.4 Engagement Module
FR1: The system must allow users to initiate and interact with the persona using text and voice.
FR2: The system must enable users to share personal memories for better interaction.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Legacy Data Aggregation
NFR1: The aggregation process should be efficient.
NFR2: The aggregation process can handle large data within a few (1-4) hours.

4.2 Data Analysis and Modeling
NFR3: The system should support audio and visual media in different formats like MP4, etc.
NFR4: The system must ensure privacy for user models.

4.3 Persona Creation
NFR5: The artificial persona's speech should be natural.
NFR6: The artificial persona should provide a response in real-time with lag time < 1 second.

4.4 Engagement Module
NFR7: The artificial persona's emotional responses should feel natural to users.
NFR8: The user interface should be user-friendly and accessible on all mobile devices with Android version >= 8.0.

Project: AGIT - Automated Game Genre Identification Using Text and Image Classification
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for AGIT, a system that automates game genre identification by integrating both textual (game summaries) and visual (gameplay images) classification using advanced deep learning techniques and transformers.

1.2 Scope
The system aims to classify games into 23 diverse genres using a multimodal approach. It will leverage the comprehensive MSR PlayMyDataset (containing game summaries and images) and employ transformers (BERT, RoBERTa) for text analysis and CNNs/YOLO for image analysis. The goal is to create a robust multilabel classification model.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- NLP: Natural Language Processing
- CNN: Convolutional Neural Network
- LLM: Large Language Model
- SVM: Support Vector Machine

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
AGIT addresses the limitations of previous studies that focused on single-modality classification or a limited set of genres by combining text and image analysis to provide a more comprehensive and accurate genre identification model.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- User-friendly web portal.
- Integration of gameplay images and textual summaries for classification.
- Deep learning and transformer-based approaches for multilabel pipelines across 23 genres.
- Input field for game summaries.
- Classification scores and criteria for authentic result evaluation.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- (The document focuses on research problem, methodology, and literature review. User classes are implied to be researchers, developers, and potentially game platform users, but not explicitly detailed.)

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Functional Requirements (from SRS section)
FR1: The system will provide a user-friendly web portal with proper orientation so that users can easily navigate through webpages.
FR2: The system shall integrate both gameplay images and textual summaries to classify the 23 genres.
FR3: The system will incorporate deep learning algorithms and transformers-based approaches for developing multilabel pipelines across 23 genres.
FR4: The system shall include an input field to input game summaries.
FR5: The system will include classification scores and criteria for authentic result evaluation.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Non-Functional Requirements (from SRS section)
NFR1: The system shall include prompt response time for output generation.
NFR2: The system shall be interactive and user-friendly.
NFR3: The system shall protect user-sensitive information (passwords, email, etc.).

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION (IEEE 830)
Project: FloodVis - Deep Learning with Aerial Imagery for Flood Assessment
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Problem Statement
The 2022 floods in Pakistan left 1,739 dead and over 8 million displaced. Traditional ground-based and satellite remote sensing equipment faces challenges: optical satellite images hindered by cloud cover, and survivor identification is time-consuming and labor-intensive.

1.2 Scope
The system integrates deep learning with SAR satellite and UAV imagery for flood detection, mapping affected areas, and detecting humans and animals in flood zones. Features include semantic segmentation for flood exposure zones, custom object detection for affected individuals/livestock/damaged buildings, approximate coordinates for rescue teams, and a web dashboard with analytics.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- SAR: Synthetic Aperture Radar
- UAV: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle
- API: Application Programming Interface

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
FloodVis aims to enhance flood disaster response by providing real-time data and improving detection accuracy and speed for flood detection, survivor identification, and damage assessment.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Flood detection and mapping using semantic segmentation
- Human and animal detection in flood zones
- Counting of critically affected individuals and cattle
- Submerged infrastructure detection
- Dashboard with flood area size and severity
- Pre- and post-flood image comparison
- Report export functionality

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- Emergency Responders: Acquire counts and locations of stranded humans/animals
- Disaster Managers: Make key decisions regarding resource allocation
- System Administrators: Maintain application, ensure security and data integrity

2.4 Operating Environment
Web application with backend data storage, model deployment, and cloud scalability.

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
FR1: The system shall classify regions as flooded or non-flooded.
FR2: The system shall detect and count critically affected and non-critically affected humans.
FR3: The system shall detect and count critically affected and non-critically affected cattle.
FR4: The system shall detect number of submerged infrastructure.
FR5: The system shall provide quantitative data via dashboard (flood area size, severity).
FR6: The system shall allow users to export reports with flood analysis and detection summaries.
FR7: The system shall allow comparison of pre- and post-flood imagery.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
NFR1: The system shall process images with varying levels of noise.
NFR2: The system shall support medium-resolution satellite and UAV images.
NFR3: The system shall process images captured from different angles.
NFR4: The system shall provide accurate flood segmentation and detection of cattle, humans, and infrastructure.
NFR5: The system shall process large amounts of data quickly for emergency insights.
NFR6: Interface shall be user-friendly for decision-makers and rescue teams.
NFR7: The system shall ensure confidentiality and integrity of satellite/UAV data and results.

Project: INS(P)ECT - Optimized Flea Beetle Counting using Edge Devices
================================================================================

1. INTRODUCTION
1.1 Purpose
This document outlines the requirements for INS(P)ECT, a research project focused on developing and optimizing a deep learning AI model for detecting flea beetles on sticky traps, optimized for deployment on edge devices such as mobile phones.

1.2 Scope
The project aims to create an accurate, energy-efficient, and readily accessible pest detection solution for farmers. It involves developing an AI model, optimizing it for mobile devices (reducing inference time and energy consumption), and integrating it into a mobile application. The app features image capture, automatic insect counting, offline functionality, results summary, data visualization, and cloud backup.

1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- FB: Flea Beetle
- SIT: Smart Insect Trap
- YOLO: You Only Look Once
- SVM: Support Vector Machine

2. OVERALL DESCRIPTION
2.1 Product Perspective
INS(P)ECT addresses the gap in accessible, automated pest detection by moving from expensive, specialized hardware (like the SIT with NVIDIA Jetson Nano) to a solution that works on commonly available mobile phones, enabling small-scale farmers to benefit from AI-driven pest management.

2.2 Product Functions (Summary)
- Image Capture and Upload: Capture images of sticky traps.
- Automatic Insect Counting: AI model processes images to detect and count flea beetles.
- Edge-AI: Model inference runs on the mobile device itself.
- Easy-to-use User Interface: Simple and intuitive UI.
- Offline Functionality: Detection and counting possible without internet.
- Results Summary and History: Compile results and keep history.
- Data Visualization: Visualize data for better understanding.
- Cloud Backup: Backup data to the cloud for storage.

2.3 User Classes and Characteristics
- (Primary user is the farmer, though not explicitly detailed in a table, the context indicates the farmer as the end-user who captures images and receives results.)

3. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
3.1 Functional Requirements (from SRS section)
FR1: The application will allow users to capture images of sticky traps which can be analyzed for the detection of flea beetles.
FR2: The application will have an AI model process these images to accurately identify and count the flea beetles captured on the sticky trap.
FR3: The application will have a user-friendly and intuitive user interface for ease of navigation and use.
FR4: The application will generate reports for the analysis of the population of flea beetles present in the field and provide information about the trend of population increase or decrease.
FR5: The application will allow these reports to be stored on the cloud where they can be easily accessed and stored.

4. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Non-Functional Requirements (from SRS section)
NFR1: The application will inference the model to provide the results in a minimal time span.
NFR2: The application will be optimized for low battery consumption to prolong battery life.
NFR3: The application will have a user-friendly and intuitive user interface for ease of navigation and use.
NFR4: The application will be compatible with a range of mobile devices to ensure wide availability.
NFR5: The application will handle data securely, only allowing access to authorized users.
